You should be very careful not to make the Bible say something it does
not say. This garbling of passages is the equivalent of taking a message
written to the twelve tribes of Israel (James 1:1), and making it apply
to a New Testament, born‑again Christian in the Body of Christ (1 Cor.
12), which, of course, it does not.

So let us observe how to handle the passages against the people who will
use them as though they were doctrinally applicable to this age, though
in actuality they are doctrinally applicable only to local churches in
the Tribulation.

Revelation 2--3 are, in type, a perfect picture of the "Church Age." In
type, for example, the first church mentioned in Revelation 2 is a
purposed church, working hard and doing the right thing. Their only
fault is that they have begun to get a little cool in their personal
relationship with Jesus Christ. (The last church in Rev. 3 is a church
in total and complete apostasy, and yet it professes Orthodoxy. That is,
it is not an Atheistic, Communistic church. It is a church that
subscribes to the "Apostle's Creed," but its bishops are all dressed up
in robes, and everybody is rich. This church has plenty of everything,
and they are bragging about what they have. God says their spiritual
condition is the worst condition of any church in the Book of
Revelation. Their spiritual condition is poor, wretched, miserable,
naked, and blind.)

2:1 "Unto the angel of the church of Ephesus write; These things saith
he that holdeth the seven stars in his right hand, who walketh in the
midst of the seven golden candlesticks."

Notice that these messages are all aimed at the angel of the church. The
Scofield note defines the word "angel" as "messenger," and then applies
the "angel" of each church to the pastor of that local church. This is
an error and in no way can be true, for we are told in Revelation 3:16
that God will "spue" the angel out of His mouth! Now, a born‑again
pastor is not in Jesus Christ's stomach, and cannot be spit out! He is
part of Christ's body. The messages in Revelation 2--3 are to the angels
of particular churches and to the churches (plural), as though God might
spit out the local assembly and not individuals in the Body of Christ.

So to begin with, we notice a supernatural situation in Revelation 2
that cannot be explained by any theologian from A.D. 30 to A.D. 1966.
Although the meaning of the Greek word for angel in the New Testament
may be "messenger," an angel is more than a messenger. A "messenger"
only defines one of the meanings of the word. An "angel" (if you want to
reduce him to his general function) is an "appearance." He is an
appearance whether he carries any message or not. To make the word
"angel" apply only to a "messenger" is to shortchange the word of God.
There is an angel in Luke 4 that is not a messenger: he is a helper.
There is an angel in Daniel 12 that is not a messenger: he is a fighter.
There is a group of angels in Matthew 24 that comes at the Second Coming
of Jesus Christ. They are not messengers: they are witnesses.

When you reduce "angel" to its basic essentials, the all‑inclusive Bible
meaning is "appearance." An "Angel of God" is an appearance of God. An
"angel of light" is an appearance of light. The "angel of the Lord" is
the appearance of the Lord. This definition will meet every verse in the
Bible. The meaning that confines the word "angel" to "messenger" will
not meet about twenty verses; therefore, it should be discarded
immediately and ignored. Although some angels may carry messages and be
messengers, angels are not primarily messengers. They are appearances.
This meaning will fit every place. For example, Matthew 18:10 says,
"Take heed that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto
you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my Father
which is in heaven." It is not their messengers that do always behold
the face of the Father which is in heaven; it is their appearances.

But this produces a doctrine too hard for twentieth-century theology to
lay hold on. This doctrine augments the great truth found in Daniel
9--11, which teaches that everything on this earth has an appearance in
the third heaven. The statement about the little children in Matthew
18:10 was not that "their guardian angel takes care of them on this
earth." The statement was that their angel is up there in heaven where
God can look at him. The statement is that everything on this earth has
a representative in heaven. (This is what one discovers when one studies
demonology in Eph. 6; Dan. 8--11, and other passages.) Let it suffice to
say that an angel is always an appearance, although he is not always a
messenger.

So Revelation 2:1 is written to the appearance of this church; that is,
God has before His face (in heaven) a representative spiritual condition
of every local church on the face of this earth.

Revelation 2 pictures the early Apostolic church in type. This early
Apostolic church runs from A.D. 33 to A.D. 200. Its foundation is the
Apostles, and when the Apostles are dead, it is continued by the
"Apostolic Fathers." In the history of this church (in the ministries of
Polycarp, Ignatius, Justin Martyr, and Papias), we begin to see the
seeds which produce the death of the church later on. Ephesus begins
with that group now known as "Apostolic Fathers." Although the Apostolic
"Fathers" may have been saved men, they carried within them the seeds of
Greek philosophy and ecclesiastical tradition which eventually destroyed
the Biblical faith "once delivered to the saints."

2:2 "I know thy works, and thy labour, and thy patience, and how thou
canst not bear them which are evil: and thou hast tried them which say
they are apostles, and are not, and hast found them liars."

This early church, you will notice, is commended for intolerance, a
rather archaic commendation in this day and age. God commends this local
church for being intolerant, and using discrimination against people who
profess to be something they are not.

"Thou hast tried them which say they are apostles, and are not, and hast
found them liars."

This church did not believe everything it heard; it checked with the
Scriptures and obeyed the admonition "to prove all things." This church
was a good church.

"I know thy works, and thy labour, and thy patience, and how thou canst
not bear them which are evil."

It was a working church. It was a laboring church. It was a church that
"sweated" in carrying out its tasks.

In the passage, we find a group of people professing to be "Apostles,"
who were not Apostles. This is one of the first characteristics of the
early church. Up to A.D. 200 we have some church leaders who profess to
be coming from the Apostles by Apostolic succession, and by the "laying
on of hands." This bunch of people continue in the church until they
take over the church. They profess to be "one true Apostolic line,"
receiving their "Apostolic succession" from Simon Peter. These people
are not Apostles; they are liars. It is not hard to find out if they
have really descended from the Apostles. The Apostles have "Apostolic
signs and wonders" (2 Cor. 12:12; Mark 16:17--18). When a man professes
to be an Apostle, or to have "Apostolic succession," you can check his
gas in a hurry. If he doesn't have "the signs and wonders" given to an
Apostle, he is not an Apostolic believer; he is not a believer going
back to the Book of Acts; he is not a man "contending for the faith once
delivered to the saints." He is a liar (Rev. 2:2).

When you find a man always going back to Acts 2 to try to prove that he
has the "one Apostolic faith," you are dealing with a religious crook.
When you find a man that is always going back to Matthew 16 to prove
that by the "laying on of hands" that he comes from the Apostles, and
has Apostolic authority, you are dealing with a religious crook. You can
try them out very quickly, and see immediately what they are. They may
be Campbellites (Church of Christ) or Jehovah Witnesses or Mormons or
Roman Catholics, but they are not Apostles, and they have no Apostolic
authority. They are not in the line of the Apostles, and the only
authority they have is the money, stocks, bonds, collateral, interest,
and political lobbies of their denominational organizations. That's the
power they have. They have the same power that Adolph Hitler and
Khruschev had; their spiritual power is totally defunct. They only
control the minds and consciences of men by threats or by forcing them
to attend church or to take "sacraments," under fear of going to hell.
These people are not Apostles. They are liars. The early church knew it
and tried them out and got rid of them. They didn't write magazine
articles about them, and brag about them over the radio and television!

2:3 "And hast borne, and hast patience, and for my name's sake hast
laboured, and hast not fainted."

This church not only worked, it labored.

2:4 "Nevertheless I have somewhat against thee, because thou hast left
thy first love."

Then, the first criticism of the early church is not that they are not
orthodox or that they don't exercise church discipline. This church is
just as straight as a gun barrel. (You can be straight as a gun barrel
theologically, and empty as a gun barrel spiritually.) This church was
sound in its orthodoxy, and it was a working church and a separated
church. But, it was a church that had begun to get a little ritualistic,
formalistic, and leave its first love. This is the hardest thing for any
Christian to do---to keep his first love for Jesus Christ. The death of
Jesus Christ and His crucifixion means something to you as a Christian.
It touched your heart when you first heard about it. When you were first
a "newborn babe" in Christ, it was precious to you. But the older you
get, the longer you live, the more you grow in grace, the more danger
you have of losing that first warmth and appreciation of what He did for
you.

This is the same trouble in marriages. The hardest thing you have to do,
as a man, is to keep that strange fascination and infatuation for your
wife that you had for her in the days when you began your engagement.
And the hardest thing a woman ever has to do is to keep the admiration
and respect she had for her husband before he said "I do."

2:5 "Remember therefore from whence thou art fallen, and repent, and do
the first works; or else I will come unto thee quickly, and will remove
thy candlestick out of his place, except thou repent."

(Notice, this is addressed to the angel of the church and to the church
itself, as a group. It is not addressed to individuals in the Body of
Christ.)

Remember---to get back to your first love, you ought to remember when
you were saved. You ought to remember your separation and how you began
to live for the Lord. You ought to remember your service and how you
began to work for Christ.

Repent---this church must repent of its indifference, indecision, and
indulgence.

Remove---God will remove its power and influence if it does not repent.

As a man said, "If you are happier about anything right now than
salvation, you are in a backslidden condition." A church ought to be up
and around and going and doing, but at the same time its love and its
attitude toward Christ should be warm and fervent in heart.

"From whence thou art fallen" is not a reference to an individual
falling from grace. The teaching that a man can "fall from grace" is an
abortion or perversion from a passage in Galatians 5:4. (The majority of
the pastors who use the passage don't even know where to find it in the
word of God.) Galatians 5:4 is the only place it occurs in the Bible,
and that reference, in the context, is to unsaved people who are trying
to get saved by the law. This is a perfect example of how preachers will
pervert the word of God without knowing where it is or what it says!

2:6 "But this thou hast, that thou hatest the deeds of the Nicolaitans,
which I also hate."

This church needs to get back to its first love and repent, but they
have one thing about them that is very good---they hate the "deeds of
the Nicolaitanes."

(The Nicolaitanes are found in Rev. 2:15 also.)

The word "Nicolaitane" is a Greek word that has not been translated. For
example, the Greek word "petra" means rock. (Our word "petrified" comes
from it.) Now, if you translated "petra," you would write "rock." But if
you transliterated it, you would write "petra." That is, in
transliteration, you take literally what it says in the language and
transfer it to English letters. When you translate, you take the word
and say what it means in the other language. We have this several times
in the new "Bibles." Instead of translating the word "hades" as "hell,"
they simply write "hades," which is not a translation. "Hades" is simply
the Greek word spelled out with English letters; it has not been
translated into English. In a translation, you tell what the word means;
in a transliteration, you just spell the word out in English letters
instead of Greek letters.

"Nicolaitanes" is not a translation, but a transliteration. To
understand the word, you almost have to get a concordance and run down
the Greek. (I very seldom refer to the Greek, if ever. It has been used
time and time again to prove a lie, so I stick to the English which
sticks to the truth. I only use the Greek to back up what the English
says. Greek scholars use the Greek to prove that they are right, no
matter what the English says.)

Nicolaitanes---"Nikao" means to conquer. "Laos" (Laity) means common,
ordinary people. For example: we talk about "clergy" and "laity." We
talk about Methodist "laymen." Now, the average Methodist doesn't know
where the word comes from any more. It comes out of the Book of
Revelation. A "layman" is a common, ordinary man. A distinction is made
between "clergy" and "laity," but the Bible does not make this
demarcation. Hence, the doctrine of the "Nicolaitanes" means the
doctrine of how to conquer the common people! The doctrine of the
Nicolaitanes is a doctrine set up whereby the clergy becomes a special
priest‑class in a special position, ruling the average ordinary
Christian. Of course, this is a heresy and a pagan perversion of the
Christian Bible.

In the word of God, every Christian is in the Body of Christ; every
Christian is anointed. God is no respecter of persons, and the only
difference between "clergy" and "laity," in the word of God, is that the
ordinary, common, everyday Christian has not, perhaps, been set apart
for a full‑time official position in the Body of Christ, as a leader.
However, every Christian in the Body of Christ has been called to
preach, called to witness, and called to work for the Lord Jesus Christ.
God knows nothing about a "ruling class" of people who rule the "common
people." The only power that a bishop or an elder or a deacon has in the
New Testament to rule is spiritual power.

This word "Nicolaitanes" doesn't mean to rule spiritually; it means to
conquer. You are not supposed to conquer the common people and get the
upper hand and "lord it over them" (1 Pet. 5:1--4). You are supposed to
lead them, guide them, and direct them. Your spiritual power as a
bishop, deacon, elder, or minister is spiritual only. The "doctrine of
the Nicolaitanes," then, is the doctrine that a certain class of
Christians should conquer another class of Christians. It is a doctrine
which God hates (see the text).

2:7 "He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the
churches; To him that overcometh will I give to eat of the tree of life,
which is in the midst of the paradise of God."

Here we have the first promise to the "overcomer." (If you are going to
deal with someone who thinks the passage applies to this age, you will
have to show him 1 John 4:4, which says that every Christian in this age
is an "overcomer.")

The truth of the matter goes a little deeper, doctrinally. Undoubtedly,
Revelation 2:7 refers to someone in the Tribulation, doctrinally. I say
this because the promise in verse 7 is that the overcomer will eat of
the tree of life. Now, every place you find this "tree of life" in the
Bible, it is a means of obtaining eternal life; and its "partaking" is
based on works, and not on grace. For example, look at Revelation 22:14.
Here you are told that a man has to "keep the commandments" to partake
of the tree of life. (Right away one can see where the Seventh-day
Adventists get all fouled up. A Seventh-day Adventist is just dumb
enough to think that he doesn't have eternal life unless he gets that
tree, and he thinks he can't get that tree unless he keeps the Old
Testament Ten Commandments!)

Do you see why people teach salvation by works? They get all balled up
on the Second Coming of Jesus Christ. If a man is not Premillennial, he
can't even get the plan of salvation straight in this age. Look at
Revelation 22:2 and notice that nations partake of the tree of
life---not Christians. No Christian ever had to get his eternal life by
eating of the tree of life. Every Christian that ever lived got his
eternal life by believing on the Lord Jesus Christ and trusting Him as
Saviour. The "tree of life," as you find it in Revelation 2, 21--22, is
always a reference to a tree that people get life out of by coming to it
and eating. They receive this right to come and eat by works, "by doing
the commandments." Therefore, Revelation 2:7 cannot apply doctrinally to
a Christian. However, if you are asked about it, you can point out that
in 1 John 4:4 and 1 John 5:4 it is very clear that every Christian is an
"overcomer." These comparisons point out the difference between
Christian New Testament doctrine in the Age of Grace, and doctrine for
people who get eternal life by partaking of a tree during the
Tribulation or the Millennium or eternity (as you find it in Rev. 22).

In this particular period of church history---the Ephesus period (A.D.
33 to A.D. 200)---we have the eleven Apostles, and then the twelfth
Apostle, Matthias, and then the thirteenth Apostle, Paul, a special
Apostle, set apart for a special ministry to the Gentiles (Gal. 2; Eph.
3). In this same period, we have a man showing up named Irenaeus, A.D.
180, right at the close of the period, and a man named Tertullian, A.D.
150, right at the close of the period, also. Irenaeus and Tertullian
have within their writings the hints of the coming apostasy, which would
drive the church from fidelity to the Bible before A.D. 500. Now, these
men were both professing Christians. Irenaeus was the source of a dozen
or more heresies, one of which was that he thought the book called the
Shepherd of Hermes was a Canonical book. Irenaeus and Tertullian have in
their writings little hints that water baptism is essential for
salvation. (These writings are what the Roman Catholic Church falls back
on and uses when they try to prove their man‑made traditions. They try
to prove that the earliest Church Fathers believed in baptismal
regeneration and used it.)

Tertullian, before he died, left the Latin situation that was shaping
up, and became what is known as a "Montanist," a Premillennialist in
early days. The words "Montanist," "Chiliast," and "Premillennialist"
are nearly the same word. These were people who believed in the Second
Coming of Jesus Christ to set up a reign on this earth. If Tertullian
had not left the Latin group and joined this group, he probably would
have wound up as one of the first "popes," but he left the group and
became a "radical," a Premillennial "fanatic," and, as a consequence,
the Roman Catholic Church did not canonize him and today they can't use
his writings very much. But they do use them occasionally, and they use
Irenaeus'.

When you hear the so‑called "Catholic Church" refer to the "Church
Fathers" to prove the superstitions of their traditions, they are
referring to Clement, Ignatius, Irenaeus, Papias, Cyprian, and
Tertullian. They pick a certain Church Father to quote for a certain
heresy that they wish to believe. They are always very careful to appeal
to a "Church Father," instead of a Pauline Epistle, when proving a
"private interpretation." The way the popes have always gotten their
heresies across is by simply going to the Book of Matthew---twenty‑six
chapters to Old Testament Jews under the law; then they go to the Book
of Acts---before the Pauline revelation---and they quote Simon Peter
preaching to Israel---and then they go to the "Church Fathers" instead
of the Pauline Epistles. When they get all through, of course, they have
proof, among other things, that their church is the one true church. In
so doing, the popes have blasphemed the Holy Spirit for eighteen
centuries, and have contradicted the Bible in more than 500 passages.

At this time (when these Church Fathers are coming along), the Roman
bishops pick a number of men to prove "Apostolic succession," during the
Ephesus period. These men are the liars referred to in Revelation 2:2,
who claim Apostolic succession. They are as follows: Linus, now called
St. Linus; Cletus, now called St. Cletus; Clement, Evaristus, Alexander,
Sixtus I, Telesphorus, Hyginus, and Pope Pius I, who have all been "made
saints" by the Catholic Church. The Catholic Church likes to "make
saints" out of its own superstitious idolators to try to prove that they
are an unusually holy political organization; not realizing, of course,
that in the New Testament every Christian is a saint. All Christians are
addressed as "saints" in the Pauline Epistles, and not just one or two
are picked out to be special displays.

These people who claim "Apostolic succession" are said to be elected as
"popes," in an unbroken chain of the popes, found in the book printed by
the Knights of Columbus---No. 11, A Short Story of the Popes, published
by the Knights of Columbus, 4422 Lindale Blvd., St. Louis, Missouri,
page 41. These early "saints," who profess to come from the Apostles and
to have Apostolic succession to the chair of St. Peter, are either
liars, or more likely, the politicians in the Vatican have smeared and
slandered their good name by connecting them with such a monstrosity as
the Roman Catholic Vatican State, which has about as much in common with
the Bible as Al Capone had in common with David Livingston.

In this first church period, the seeds of false doctrine are found among
the "Church Fathers" themselves. Even Ignatius and Papias have things in
their writings that indicate they are going to believe in baptismal
regeneration and a church political hierarchy---a Nicolaitane setup.
Although this is not pronounced until later (until the time of
Augustine, Ambrose, Jerome, and the Roman Catholic Popes), yet very
early---Revelation 2:1--7---the seeds of degeneracy have been planted.
They are sowed, first of all, by turning away from personal love for
Jesus and putting the church ahead of Him, and then putting a
clergy‑laity class as the final authority instead of the Holy
Scriptures.

In this early period, A.D. 33 to A.D. 200, there were some genuine
Church Fathers who believed the Book and went by it. Polycarp was one of
these---a man who was burnt at the stake in the arena for Jesus Christ.
He was a contemporary of the Apostle John. He was very sound in his
doctrine; consequently, the Roman Catholic Church does not do too much
quoting from Polycarp.

2:8 "And unto the angel of the church in Smyrna write; These things
saith the first and the last, which was dead, and is alive."

The word "Smyrna" means "myrrh." Myrrh is indicative of bitterness and
death. This church is a persecuted church. This is the church from A.D.
200 to A.D. 325, during the ten official Roman persecutions. These ten
official Roman persecutions kill out the saints by the thousands, but
the more they kill, the more the saints multiply; they multiply like
rabbits. You will find a detailed list of the kind of things that took
place in this period in Fox's Book of Martyrs. From A.D. 200 to A.D. 325
pagan Rome carries on a desperate warfare to wipe out every Christian on
the face of the earth, but they only multiply. When Constantine takes
over Rome around A.D. 313, he takes over a situation where he has to get
the Christians to join up with the world in order to shut their mouths.
This period is found typified in Revelation 2:8--11.

2:9 "I know thy works, and tribulation, and poverty, (but thou art rich)
and I know the blasphemy of them which say they are Jews, and are not,
but are the synagogue of Satan."

The main trouble with the first church in the first period was that some
of its leaders claimed "Apostolic succession." Now these
Nicolaitanes---"the conquerors of the common people"---are claiming
something even wilder. They are claiming that because the Christian is a
spiritual Jew (Rom. 2), that he is also a physical Jew, and therefore
gets all the promises given to the Jew, and therefore God is all through
with the descendants of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. This is the beginning
of Amillennial and Postmillennial theology. And these two pagan
speculations are taught by the "Ante‑Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers," in
this period and later. They are Cyprian, A.D. 200; Jerome, A.D. 345;
Origen, A.D. 182; Clement, A.D. 200; Didymus, A.D. 313; Asthanasius,
A.D. 297; and Cyril of Jerusalem, A.D. 380.

Here we have Amillennialism and Postmillennialism coming in, which teach
that God is all through with the Jew forever, the Christian has replaced
the Jew, the church has replaced Israel, and the Kingdom of God and the
Kingdom of Heaven are the same. Therefore, the spiritual kingdom of "the
gospel" is composed of mortgages, stocks, bonds, collateral, indemnity,
interest, lobbies, parochial schools, tax grants, etc., to carry out its
work of "spreading the kingdom." Further, all the Old Testament promises
to Israel are spiritual and to be applied to the Christian, that God is
all through with Israel and you can persecute the Jew and get away with
it, and the church has replaced Israel. Therefore, the Gentile Roman
"Catholic" is now "top dog" and can drive the Jew out and take his land,
even if he has to send Crusaders to capture the city!

Of course, this is blasphemous foolishness of the worst kind. The Holy
Spirit says that these people "say they are Jews" and they are not, and
He calls this B‑L‑A‑S‑P‑H‑E‑M‑Y (Rev. 2:9). The most blasphemous
religious outfit on the face of the earth---according to the Author of
the Bible---is an outfit that professes to replace, or supplant or
supersede, Israel, the Jew. God is not through with the Jew (1 Thess. 2;
Rom. 9, 11), and you are told not to be "wise in your own conceits" and
think that you have replaced him, because you have not. You may be a
spiritual Jew, but the literal, physical, earthly, visible twelve tribes
of Israel have yet to come into their own; they will at the Second
Coming of Jesus Christ.

In the Smyrna period, the Postmillennial teaching begins to come to the
fore. In A.D. 354, Augustine, the Roman "Catholic," North African
bishop, wrote a book called The City of God, in which he fully develops
this theology. He even goes so far as to say that Rome has replaced
Jerusalem, and this is one of the main sources from which the United
States gets its blasphemous, anti‑Biblical "Christianity." We get it
from the North African Latin Church (which may account for their
interest in several social problems going on right now!).

"I know thy works, and tribulation, and poverty, (but thou art rich)."
Notice how differently God looks at things. There's a lot of difference
in saying you are poor and being poor. There's a lot of difference in
saying you are rich and being rich. Notice in Revelation 3:17, where a
church says it is rich and God says it is poor. In Revelation 2:9 the
church says it is poor, and God says it is rich! This is a witnessing,
martyred church. This is the church that goes into the Colosseum and is
eaten by lions. This is the church that is tied to the stake and soaked
in tar and burned. This is the church that is put in bags with
rattlesnakes and thrown into the river. A detailed account of this
church is found in Fox's Book of Martyrs, which every Christian should
buy.

(Every Christian should have---if he has nothing else---four books. He
should have an Authorized Bible \[1611\], which is the word of God;
Young's Concordance, which will help him find things in the Bible;
Dispensational Truth by Clarence Larkin, which will lay it out where he
can "get it"; and Fox's Book of Martyrs, which will give him the zeal to
do the work. There are other books to buy, but these are basic.)

"I know the blasphemy of them which say they are Jews, and are not, but
are the synagogue of Satan." These people, who claim to replace Israel
and to replace the Jew, are of the Devil according to the text. They are
not merely backslidden Christians; they are not even professing
Christians, according to the text; they are not "Church Fathers," but
are (to quote the Holy Spirit) "children of hell" (Matt. 23; John 8).

The Smyrna period is in sharp contrast to our church period today. Folks
stand up and sing, "It Pays to Serve Jesus." It does pay to serve Jesus,
but it doesn't always pay off in the coin of this world every time. John
the Baptist was paid for serving Jesus by having his head cut off,
Stephen was stoned, Christ was crucified, Paul had his head cut off,
James was thrown in jail and had his head cut off. This modern
Christianity where you think that if you "live for God" everything is
going to be just hunky‑dory is a twentieth-century Hollywood version (2
Tim. 3:12).

2:10 "Fear none of those things which thou shalt suffer: behold, the
devil shall cast some of you into prison, that ye may be tried; and ye
shall have tribulation ten days: be thou faithful unto death, and I will
give thee a crown of life."

The "crown of life" is not promised to somebody who gets saved. The
passage does not say that if you are faithful unto death that you will
receive eternal life. The statement is that if you are faithful unto
death you will receive "a crown of life." The crowns, of course, are
rewards. It's strange how people read the Bible. They'll pray, "Lord,
help me to be faithful unto the end," or "Lord, help me to endure until
the end," or "Lord, help me to hold out until the end, and save us all
in heaven at last." They pray this not knowing that when a man is born
again, God promised to keep him up to the end (1 Cor. 1:7--9). The
promise in Revelation 2:10 is not that God will give you eternal life if
you are "faithful unto death," but that He will give you "a crown of
life."

These crowns are rewards given at the Judgment Seat of Christ, which
will take place on your chart between the eight‑inch mark and the
ten‑inch mark. While the Tribulation is taking place on this earth, the
Judgment Seat of Christ is taking place in heaven. This is where the
Christian will be rewarded and receive these "crowns." (These crowns are
found in James 1; 1 Pet. 5; 1 Thess. 2; 1 Cor. 9; 2 Tim. 2; Rev. 2.)

"Fear none of those things which thou shalt suffer." (Jesus said the
same thing in Matt. 10:28.) "The devil shall cast some of you into
prison." This happened a great deal in this early period.

2:11 "He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the
churches; He that overcometh shall not be hurt of the second death."

Again, there is an implied warning that if you don't overcome, you will
be hurt of the second death. (Use 1 John 4:4 and 1 John 5:4 and show
that every Christian has already overcome.)

The next period of church history is the most momentous and
consequential period of church history through which the church has ever
passed. To find a period like this period, you would have to come up to
1952 and Papa Johnny's "Ecumenical Council." This is the period where
the church goes to pot so fast and so quick that, at the end of the
period, the church no longer bears any resemblance to the New Testament
Christian faith delivered to the Apostle Paul. What takes place in this
period is of terrific consequence to subsequent church history.

The Pergamos period is the period in which Church Fathers, apologists,
and writers all begin to spend their time defending the faith, instead
of preaching it. They spend all their time arguing about it instead of
propagating it. The martyrs who propagated it in the Smyrna period were
killed; the ones who did the real work in that period were the practical
Christian witnesses. They had such a tremendous effect upon the Roman
Empire that, at the end of that age (around A.D. 300 or A.D. 325) when
Constantine came into power as a Roman Chief, he saw immediately that
his only hope of keeping the Eastern division of his empire and the
Western division of his empire together was to amalgamate the
Christians, give them rights and liberties, swallow them up, and make
them part of Pagan Rome---which he did. The Smyrna period (A.D.
200--325) is the most dangerous period in church history. This is when
the martyr church tires of being the martyr church, and longs to be the
Roman Church, for all practical purposes.

Now, during this time, there were scores of heretical sects teaching
contrary to the word of God. The first and foremost group were the
"Judaizers," who continued to teach that the Christian is the Jew, that
he is saved by works, that the Old Testament is spiritual, and that
salvation is by faith and works. Eusebius (264--340) propagates this
foolishness, along with the fleshly teachings that the Christian is not
to do this or that or he will lose his salvation. The Greek "Gnostics"
show up in this period. They are the "scientists" of the first couple of
centuries and profess superior knowledge to the average person.

Tatian (160), a learned Greek scholar who had been converted, spent a
lot of time writing against these groups. Basilides (138) was a man who
professed to be a Christian, but was actually a pantheist. Valentinus
(160), a Greek-speaking Egyptian philosopher, wrote at this time;
Marcion (160), called the "heretic," wrote at this time. A number of
sects and organizations appeared, blaspheming and contradicting the word
of God, teaching heresies, and were refuted by various Church Fathers
who wrote against them. This "dialogue" gradually developed into writing
and arguing instead of preaching the gospel.

It is in this period that the individual writings of people begin to be
stuck into the Bible, as though they were an equal authority with the
Bible (like the "Epistles of Clement," "Shepherd of Hermes," "Epistle of
Barnabas," etc.). These writings begin to pop up, and the Roman bishops
hold them to be equal in authority with the word of God. In the next
period---Pergamos---"Sinaiticus" and "Vaticanus" are written---the two
Roman Catholic, North African manuscripts that are used as a basis for
the RSV, ASV, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Centennial, Amplified, etc.
And in both periods, Smyrna and Pergamos, the Alexandrian School of
Philosophers and Greek translators put out their pseudo‑Christian trash.
It is in this period that Christianity gradually gets philosophical
(arguing with the heretics and Greek philosophers), and gets soft, and
finally compromises with Rome.

The men who lead the church into this apostasy are Irenaeus, Origen, and
Eusebius (the bishop of Caesarea who idolized Emperor Constantine), a
man who sells out to Constantine, and delivers the church over to the
Roman emperor. This Royal Merger later becomes the Roman "Catholic"
Church, which, of course, is neither Christian, Biblical, Scriptural,
nor true to the New Testament in any way, shape, or form. Clement of
Alexandria (200) lived in the Smyrna period, and he set up the "school
of Alexandria," which began to revise the Bible and change it and write
the Greek manuscripts that are used for such modern fiascoes as the ASV
and RSV.

The "Hesychian" form of Greek text, called the Westcott and Hort text
(used for all the new Bibles), originates in the Smyrna period. Origen,
born in A.D. 185, does his work in this period. Although Origen was a
professing Christian, he studied the Bible scientifically and
critically, and he tore it apart. He mended it, changed it, revised it,
and while doing this, he taught universal salvation, baptismal
regeneration, soul sleep, and God knows what. Origen believed in the
"allegorical method" of interpreting the Bible. He believed when a
little child died, it had to be baptized to be saved. (Of course, the
"Catholics" picked this up.) Origen's method of Scripture interpretation
was adopted throughout the church, except in Antioch. Antioch, by the
way, was where (in Acts 11) the disciples were first called
"Christians." Origen's method went on to a "spiritual" method of
interpretation, which finally made the Bible of no effect and thus made
the Roman bishop the final authority instead of the word of God.

The men who write in the Smyrna‑Pergamos period of time are Origen (the
worst), A.D. 184 to 254; Clement of Alexandria, A.D. 200; Didymus, A.D.
313 to 398; Cyprian, A.D. 200 to 258; Jerome, A.D. 345 to 429; and
Augustine, immediately after this period. These are the "Alexandrian
fathers" and "Post-Nicene Fathers," and of course, Augustine was a
"Western" father. In Antioch, in the Scriptural line, we have Ignatius
(107), Lucian (312), and Chrysostom (345). The ones to whom Catholics
will appeal are Eusebius, Jerome, Irenaeus, Papias, Cyprian, Augustine,
and Clement. (Origen is the source of most corrections in the RSV, RV,
and ASV over your Authorized Version. There's nothing new about these
corrections. They were made in the third century by Origen of
Alexandria, Egypt, in North Africa. They were preserved by Greek
scholars who did not believe the Bible was the word of God.)

Constantine makes peace with the church, at the end of the Smyrna
period, and during this period one should notice three movements:

1\. An outbreak of heresies and tremendous opposition to the word of God
by philosophers, scholars, and Pagan Rome.

2\. A huge body of Christian martyrs, dying for the faith, being killed
by Rome.

3\. A rising body of Church Fathers, who think that they are more
authoritative than the word of God, and who try to set up an
authoritative church to run things, instead of an authoritative Bible.
These men stop at nothing to prove their point. They revise and change
the original Greek Scriptures, make their own Scriptures in Alexandria,
Egypt (in North Africa); and they are the root and source of what takes
place in the Pergamos period, which is, of course, the beginning of the
Roman Catholic Church---beginning at the Council of Nicaea in A.D. 325.

No matter how many Apostolic successors the pope may claim before A.D.
325, the fact remains that the entire list of so‑called "popes" from St.
Linus to St. Melchiades in A.D. 311 are either fabricated or
plagiarized.

The only real Christians in this period were born‑again, Bible‑believing
martyrs, whom Rome killed. Rome had nothing religious at this time but a
populace of God-rejecting, Emperor-worshipping, Christ-denying,
Bible-contradicting persecutors.

Smyrna, therefore, is the "myrrh" period of the church---the period when
the true Christians are being killed, burned, tortured, and murdered.
The "Church Fathers" are writing polemics, tracts, and treatises, trying
to compete with worldly scholarship and worldly philosophy, in order to
set up a church organization as powerful as pagan Rome. In the next
period we will see how Constantine took this powerful church
organization and married it to pagan Rome, and put the two in the same
yoke and developed the great pagan Roman, non‑Christian Church, today
called "Catholic."

2:12 "And to the angel of the church in Pergamos write; These things
saith he which hath the sharp sword with two edges."

We now come to the Pergamos church, which represents that period of
church history from A.D. 325 to A.D. 500. This period is marked by the
rise of the Roman "Catholic" Church, and the subsequent Dark Ages.
(Wherever you have the Roman Catholic Church in power, you have moral,
social, intellectual, and religious disintegration. "The Dark Ages" are
rightly named. They begin in A.D. 500 with Pope Gregory, and do not
really end until A.D. 1520 with Martin Luther. This is a matter of
history and not a matter of Protestant prejudice, as Roman Catholics
would have you believe. Since the Roman Catholic Church is the most
bigoted, dogmatic, intolerant, superstitious, religious organization on
the face of the earth, it is not surprising that they accuse everybody
of being anti-Catholic when they present any facts.)

"These things saith he which hath the sharp sword with two edges."
Notice that the message to the church is given on the appeal that the
one giving it has the two‑edged sword, which is defined in Hebrews 4;
Ephesians 6; and Revelation 1 as the "word of God."

The word "Pergamos" means "much marriage." This church is characterized
by a joining of itself with the world. The message is aimed at a church
that is married to the world in A.D. 325.

2:13 "I know thy works, and where thou dwellest, even where Satan's seat
is: and thou holdest fast my name, and hast not denied my faith, even in
those days wherein Antipas was my faithful martyr, who was slain among
you, where Satan dwelleth."

This church is said to dwell where Satan dwells. An interesting side
note, historically, is that the high Babylonian mystical priesthood,
which taught the religion of Semiramis, Tammuz, and Nimrod---the
teaching of the virgin‑born Tammuz, who had the sign of the cross, born
on December 25th (Christmas), with the holly, mistletoe, yule log,
rings, beads, Easter bunnies, candles, etc.---was moved from Babylon to
Pergamos in 133 B.C. This mystical Babylonian priesthood is found in
Pergamos at the time of the writing of John's Book of Revelation, and
yet it pictures the subsequent removal of this high Babylonian
priesthood to Rome itself in A.D. 313.

At the Council of Nicaea, when Constantine integrated the Christian
Church with the World and brought the church and state together (as you
find it in the Vatican today), he took up the mystical Babylonian
religion, which is found exposed in the Old Testament. If there is any
doubt about this religion, we will find out when we get to Revelation
2:20 that this religion of black‑robed priests, called "father," is as
old as 1800 B.C.! It is no more "Christian" than a burlesque show.

This group dwells where Satan has a seat. He speaks out of the
seat---excathedra---out of the chair.

The Pergamos group of Christians was living earlier under pagan Rome,
and now they came under papal Rome. (The Vatican has killed more than
ten times as many Christians as pagan Rome ever thought of doing.) This
church period introduces the martyrdom of faithful Christians by a papal
church that is wedded to the world.

2:14 "But I have a few things against thee, because thou hast there them
that hold the doctrine of Balaam, who taught Balac to cast a
stumblingblock before the children of Israel, to eat things sacrificed
unto idols, and to commit fornication."

The main thing that is against this church is the fact that it holds the
"doctrine of Balaam." If you want to see what the "doctrine of Balaam"
is, you will have to go back and review Numbers 22--25.

To be very brief, the "doctrine of Balaam" came about from the fact that
he could not curse the children of Israel. God would not let him curse
them, and threatened to kill Balaam if he tried to curse them. So Balaam
found a way to put the children of Israel in a situation where God
Himself would kill them. He taught Balak, the king of Moab, this method:
he had the Moabites intermarry with the Jews, and then God killed them
Himself. This is "the doctrine of Balaam," found in Numbers 25:1--8.

Notice in this religious worship of Balaam---this is a religious
doctrine, not a political movement---three things are outstanding:

1\. He taught the children of Israel to eat things sacrificed to idols
(Rev. 2:14).

2\. He taught the children of Israel to commit fornication (Rev. 2:14).

3\. The clergy is over the laity (Rev. 2:15).

Notice in Balaam's worship, taught to Balak, things are sacrificed to
idols, and immorality is part of the worship service. This Babylonian
mystical religion incorporates immorality as true worship of God. This
is arrived at by teaching:

1\. God is love, and love is God.

2\. God is life, and life is God.

3\. Since man is a creator and creates physical life, man is his own
God, and thus the worship of life is the highest worship, and reverence
for life is the highest act of worship. Therefore, man's physical act of
creation is the highest act of worship!

(If this seems farfetched to your ears, you have not been a careful
student of Harry Emerson Fosdick, E. Stanley Jones, A. J. Cronin, Lloyd
C. Douglas, and the writing of the National Council of Churches found in
the Interpreter's Bible.)

This Pergamos church is wedded to the world, and has fornication and
idols as part of the worship, and also a
clergy‑over‑the-laity‑situation.

2:15 "So hast thou also them that hold the doctrine of the Nicolaitanes,
which thing I hate."

See Revelation 2:6.

2:16 "Repent; or else I will come unto thee quickly, and will fight
against them with the sword of my mouth.

17 He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the
churches; To him that overcometh will I give to eat of the hidden manna,
and will give him a white stone, and in the stone a new name written,
which no man knoweth saving he that receiveth it."

The verse is entirely obscure to the commentator. Spiritually, you can
say to a man that when he is saved that he is acquitted, that he is
found "not guilty." Therefore, according to the ancient custom, he is
given a white stone instead of a black stone. You can say that the stone
is Jesus Christ, that you "are in Christ," and that you have a "new
name" in Christ. This is spiritual application, but it doesn't solve all
the problems. (I don't profess to be able to solve all of them. I don't
profess to know all the Bible; I only know where the verses are. It's
like Sam Jones said, "If I understood everything there was in the Bible,
I'd know somebody wrote it that didn't have anymore sense than I have.")

This Pergamos period, A.D. 325, marks the rise of the Roman "Catholic"
Church. Constantine, the Roman Emperor, becomes the head of the church
with the bishops under him, and the church "goes to pot" so quickly that
you can't even pick up the pieces. First, Constantine gave money to the
bishops to convert the basilicas into "churches," then he dressed the
bishop up in fine linen, gold, and silver, and sat him on a throne of
marble instead of making him stand behind a wooden pulpit!

With this joining of Church and State, the Christians were allowed to go
to school, and were given liberties and rights. The backslidden Church
Fathers, like Augustine (perhaps the dumbest), Jerome, and Clement, and
the rest of the carnal Christians of their day (who were definitely out
of fellowship with the Lord), thought that because the persecution had
stopped (and they were now worshipping in big buildings with gold,
silver, and jewels on them, and were no longer being thrown to the
lions), that the Millennial reign of Christ had come and the "Kingdom
had come" on the earth! This, of course, is the teaching of
Postmillennialism today taught in every major seminary in the United
States!

There are actually people in America today with Ph.D., M.A., and Th.D.
degrees who are so imbecilic and lazy and just good‑for‑nothing, that
they actually think if all the churches join an Ecumenical Council it
would mean the "Kingdom" was coming!

Human nature doesn't change. Men are just as stupid in the twentieth
century as they were in the fourth century and the fifth century. All
that happened in A.D. 325 was that the church lost its testimony and
lost its Bible for nearly one thousand years. The monks, popes, priests,
and bishops took over, and the martyrs' church became the Roman Church.
In this Postmillennial, Amillennial situation, the pagans and Christians
were united, and Constantine consolidated his kingdom and held it
together. The day of worship of the "sun god" (Dec. 25) was instituted
as the birthday of Jesus Christ. Sun‑Day---the day of the "sun
god"---since it was the first day of the week and the day the Christian
met, was held sacred, hallowed, and set apart as a special day of
observance all over the kingdom. With this monkey‑shine came wedding
rings, parades, Easter bunnies, candles, earrings, makeup, plays,
theatricals, mistletoe, elevation of the Virgin Mary, the teaching of
baptismal regeneration, souls in purgatory, salvation by works, and
every godless, depraved, dirty, rotten, blasphemous thing that Rome has
tolerated and promoted for fifteen centuries.

It began by bishops professing to be orthodox and professing to believe
the Apostle's Creed---A.D. 325. If you will read the Apostle's Creed
carefully, you will notice that there is no detailed statement in the
Apostle's Creed on the Second Coming of Jesus Christ. The key that opens
the Jewish Bible, written by Jews, is the Jew. The key to the Jew is the
coming of Christ to give to the Jew what belongs to him at the Advent.
If you do not get the Second Coming of Jesus Christ right, you cannot
get your Bible right. Consequently, in A.D. 325, when the Apostle's
Creed was formulated and there was no detailed statement put in it about
the Second Coming of Jesus Christ, there was no foundation for any kind
of a church to stand on, and the whole thing just fell apart while
professing to be orthodox. Nicaea was not a victory for orthodoxy---it
was a religious calamity.

The men found in this period are the Latin "Church Fathers," or
"Post-Nicene" Church Fathers; they lay the foundation for the popes. We
find among them Augustine, North Africa, A.D. 354 to 430; Didymus, A.D.
323 to 398; Athanasius, A.D. 297 to 373; Cyril of Jerusalem, A.D. 380 to
444. We have some that are fairly orthodox, but in the main, they are
corrupt. In this period you have the controversies over the Trinity,
while worshipping the Virgin Mary. You have controversies over the
Person and Nature of Christ, while "Catholics" are trying to baptize
people to get them saved. This is one of the funniest and most tragic
parts of church history, judged by the Bible.

In this period you will find the Roman Church setting itself up as an
equal authority to the Bible. While professing to be orthodox, it is
teaching, as doctrine, the most wild, idiotic, pagan, superstitious,
morbid things the Devil has ever foisted on the minds of men. (There is
a statement on this kind of religious idiocy in Mark 7:1--14.)

In this period of church history, we find Clement, Ambrose, Basil, the
two Gregorys (who do some fair preaching), and Theophilus. We find the
Council of Nicaea in 325, the Council of Constantinople in 381, the
Council of Ephesus in 431, and the Council of Chalcedon in 451.

During this time the Apocrypha is incorporated into the Roman Catholic
Bible as inspired literature! (It is no more inspired than Pogo or Li'l
Abner.) During this time we find Augustine (354--430) writing, and
Jerome (340--420) making a Vulgate translation of the Bible. We have
Pope Innocent, Pope Leo, Pope Gregory, and all similar deluded Italian
princes, running around in Halloween costumes, looking like spooks,
flipping their fingers, twiddling their beads, lighting their candles,
pretending to be someone holy, when they were no more holy than Fidel
Castro or Oral Roberts. These men take over the church, and set up an
ironclad Fascist, ecclesiastical system which winds up with a "pope"
being head of a so‑called "Holy Roman Empire."

The pope begins to put two crowns on his head---later he puts on three.
One crown is for the "Kingdom of God," a spiritual, moral kingdom; the
other crown is for the "Kingdom of Heaven," an earthly, visible,
physical, literal kingdom. From this time on you are taught they are
both the same---exactly as they teach it in every Southern Baptist
Seminary today. You'll find that this is taught in every Southern
Baptist, Methodist, Presbyterian, Lutheran, and Episcopalian seminary in
the world---that the Kingdom of Heaven and the Kingdom of God are the
same. (They are not even the same word in the English. You don't have to
be a third-grade student to know that God is not heaven, unless you are
a pantheist or a materialist!)

We have now come to the end of the Pergamos period in A.D. 500. In the
midst of all this filth, rot, apostasy, and false doctrine, there are
lights which shine in a dark place. There is Chrysostom, "The Golden
Mouth," a Greek preacher, who lived in A.D. 407. (He preached and taught
out of an entirely different Bible than the one that was approved by
Eusebius at the Council of Nicaea. Eusebius and Constantine had a Bible
called "Vaticanus." It was written on vellum scrolls, in an Hesychian,
Egyptian type text. This Vaticanus contained the Apocrypha, and it is
the official Bible of the Roman Church in the United States, commonly
known as the RSV or the ASV. Both Bibles are from the same Vatican
manuscript.)

Now, Chrysostom did not use this manuscript. Instead of coming up
through North Africa into a Latin Church, Chrysostom came up through
Antioch into a Greek Church. Since the New Testament is written in
Greek, and not in Latin, Chrysostom had the right Bible. It was a very
odd Bible, and it was hated by Roman Bishops. It was a Bible written in
a "Syrian type, Byzantine text" called later a "Textus Receptus." (It is
the Bible that appears on the dime store counters of America as the AV
1611.) Chrysostom preached the gospel and had people saved. He preached
hell fire and damnation, and the new birth. He was outlawed, thrown in
jail. He prayed his way out of jail. He was exiled, and spent the
remaining years of his life on the Black Sea making translations of the
Greek into Russian from the Textus Receptus. This means that Russia had
the Authorized Version nearly a thousand years before the King James
Bible was made! That is, it was the same text as the King James Bible.

We have, in this dark period, John of Antioch (441), who preached the
truth. We have that marvelous saint, St. Patrick of Ireland (389), who
was nothing but "Patrick." (If he had known they would call him "Saint,"
he would have kicked someone in the britches, including the Pope!) Pat
was not "St. Patrick's Day" and the "wearin' of the green." St. Patrick
was a hell‑fire and damnation, new birth, Premillennial preacher. He
went out into the woods of Ireland, beat on a drum, and when the natives
gathered, he got on top of that drum and yelled at them, "You must be
born again," and preached to them John 1:12 from a Latin Textus
Receptus, not from a "Vaticanus" or a Jerome's "Latin Vulgate."

We have in Scotland and England---Columba, Alban, Cuthbert, and Finan.
We have Peter of Ravenna and Elgius preaching in France. We have in
Norway and Sweden---Ansgar, Rimbert, Nithard, Wilibrord, Egbert,
Baubert, Haarfagr, and Trygveson. These men were great preachers, who
preached out of a Latin Textus Receptus, known to you as a 1611 King
James AV. The King James, 1611 AV is the English translation of the
Greek Textus Receptus, exactly as the Norwegian, Swedish, British,
Scotch, Irish, German, Spanish, French, Italian, and Russian Bibles were
copies of the Textus Receptus which the popes tried to burn.

These men preached the gospel and won hundreds to Christ. Salvian, the
monk, preached and rebelled---against the Catholic Church. Adda, Lebuin,
Cedd, Cuthbert, and Adan preached. These early missionaries were hated
by Rome, and they hated Rome. They fought all the time. They fought and
fought and fought and cussed each other out. The pope had no use for
them, and they had no use for the pope. When they died, the pope made
saints out of them. (Christ referred to these people in Matthew
23:29--33.) In the Pergamos period there were great preachers who
preached the gospel in the early part of the Dark Ages.

2:18 "And unto the angel of the church in Thyatira write; These things
saith the Son of God, who hath his eyes like unto a flame of fire, and
his feet are like fine brass."

The word "Thyatira" means "odor of affliction." In this church period,
under the religious Fascism of the Catholic Church, the Body of Christ
is persecuted as it was never persecuted before in history. The
persecutions of pagan Rome, before the year A.D. 300, were absolutely
nothing beside the persecutions that began under papal Rome along about
A.D. 500 and came through to A.D. 1500---the Reformation. This period is
also thoroughly covered in Fox's Book of Martyrs (which has been banned
by the Roman Catholic Church, as a textbook, in the public school system
in the United States). No teacher in a public school in the United
States can teach Fox's Book of Martyrs as a history; it is absolutely
forbidden because it reveals what the papal Catholic Church did to the
true Body of Christ in this period.

The Thyatira period takes us from A.D. 500 right into the very heart of
the Dark Ages, bringing us up to the Crusades that take place from A.D.
1000 to 1200. In this period we have the "Council of Constantinople." In
this period we have the Goths, the Huns, Gauls, Charlemagne, the
Vandals, Clovis, Pepin the Short, and the Visigoths. Charlemagne
(742--814) comes into power and consolidates the Holy Roman Empire. One
finds the "popes" fighting to take over kings in order to get control
over Europe, and the kings fighting popes in order to wrest political
power from them. We find the popes crowning kings as the "head of the
church," making political speeches and carrying on with all the typical
baloney that you will see in America in the next twenty years. During
this time, Mary is raised to a position of deification, and baptismal
regeneration is taught as absolute truth. Every rotten perversion that
ever began in the first three centuries A.D. was thrown upon the true
Bible‑believer in this age (from A.D. 500 to 1000). The real
Bible‑believing Christians---among them some Catharists, Dominicans, and
Franciscans---who actually came out of the Roman system, were persecuted
relentlessly by the pope and his henchmen.

2:19 "I know thy works, and charity, and service, and faith, and thy
patience, and thy works; and the last to be more than the first."

The Thyatira church had not only left its first love, but its first
works. This church had not only parted from Christ inwardly, but had
departed from Christ in what Christ told it to do.

2:20 "Notwithstanding I have a few things against thee, because thou
sufferest that woman Jezebel, which calleth herself a prophetess, to
teach and to seduce my servants to commit fornication, and to eat things
sacrificed unto idols."

This verse shows the cause of the church leaving its first works. This
woman, in the local church historically, is called "Jezebel." Any real
Bible student understands exactly who she is. It is a reference to the
"Jezebel" of the Book of Kings; she was a prophetess, a religious
leader, who followed the Babylonian worship of Baal with its attendant
"queen of heaven" and the whole pagan works.

This woman of Thyatira is teaching things that are wrong, and of course,
as Jezebel, we can immediately identify her doctrine by what it typifies
in the Old Testament. Let the Holy Spirit give you the references very
briefly; it will be the eye‑opener and shocker of your life. Notice:

Genesis 49:17---The tribe of Dan is called a serpent.

Deuteronomy 33:22---Dan is called a lion's whelp.

The tribe of Dan is like unto a lion and a serpent. If you know your
Bible, you know who this typifies. Satan is called a serpent in Genesis
3 and a lion in 1 Peter 5.

Judges 18---The tribe of Dan gets a young man to be a priest for its
tribe.

Judges 18:19---This priest is called "father."

Judges 18:20---This "father" takes care of idols and uses them as an aid
to worship.

Judges 18:28---The tribe of Dan settles up near Zidon and Tyre on the
Phoenician coast.

Judges 18:30--31---They (Dan) are in total apostasy for nearly one
thousand years. Up by Zidon, where the Phoenicians live, we find the
tribe of Dan mixing with the heathen, and with priests called "father,"
who use idols as an aid to worship. Nobody has any doubt about Zidon. In
Genesis 10:19 you are told that Hamites (Canaanites) settled in this
part of the country, and set up Baal worship.

1 Kings 16:29--33---Ahab takes over, marries the daughter of the king of
the Zidonians, and serves Baal, the Phoenician god. Here is Jezebel
herself in the same passage.

1 Kings 18:26---If that weren't enough, in this Baal worship carried on
in Phoenicia, connected with the tribe of Dan, we find that these people
worshipped from 10 (or 11) in the morning to 12 noon.

1 Kings 18:24--25---They worshipped the fire of the sun; they worshipped
the sun god, on his day---"Sunday."

1 Kings 18:28---These black‑robed priests called "father," mutilate
themselves and do penance to pay for their sins. They mutilate their
bodies to try to get what they want from God.

2 Kings 10:22---These black‑robed, Baalite priests, called "fathers,"
who are in the tribe of Dan, who conduct the Phoenician Baal worship for
Ahab and Jezebel, wear long robes called vestments. Consequently, they
have vestries and vestrymen for the vestments.

Jeremiah 10:1--5---These people, who worship Baal, the sun god (on Dec.
25 as his birthday), worship him with a tree cut out of the forest by
the hands of the axeman, and "deck the halls with boughs of holly, tra
la la la la la la la la."

Jeremiah 44:17--19, 25---They bake cakes to a female deity whom they
call, quaintly (exactly what Papa Paul calls her!), "the queen of
heaven," clearly identifying the Phoenician Baal worship for what it is.

Phoenician Baal worship from North Africa, through the sons of Ham, with
its black‑robed priests called "fathers," connected with the tribe of
Dan, worships Sunday from 10 or 11 a.m. to 12 noon and observes
Christmas. These are the original worshippers of the Antichrist. They
are the Devil's crowd. This is not a Dark Age superstition or Protestant
bigotism. This is Scripture with Scripture, without anybody interpreting
anything. All you have to do is read the passages. This is the woman,
Jezebel.

"To teach and to seduce my servants to commit fornication, and to eat
things sacrificed unto idols." Notice that she teaches what Balaam
taught Balak. (Compare vs. 14 with vs. 20, and notice the comparison is
identical.)

2:21 "And I gave her space to repent of her fornication; and she
repented not.

22 Behold, I will cast her into a bed, and them that commit adultery
with her into great tribulation, except they repent of their deeds."

(The space of repentance was A.D. 325--500.) Notice that people who hang
around with her get in the same trouble she does. You are warned to get
out of this outfit and leave it alone (Rev. 18:1--8)!

2:23 "And I will kill her children with death; and all the churches
shall know that I am he which searcheth the reins and hearts: and I will
give unto every one of you according to your works."

Applying the passage to the Church Age, from A.D. 500 to A.D. 1000, this
"death" is called the "Black Death" in Europe when it comes.

2:24 "But unto you I say, and unto the rest in Thyatira, as many as have
not this doctrine, and which have not known the depths of Satan, as they
speak; I will put upon you none other burden."

Many people do not know that the present "Catholic" Church is the
twentieth-century duplicate of the original North African, Hamitic, Baal
worshippers of Phoenicia. Many "Catholics" do not know this because they
will not study the Bible. God says that He will put no burden on them
other than necessary.

But if this is being read by somebody who will not find this truth out
because you are afraid of man, then, my friend, you have sinned against
God, and you will get what you have coming to you. If you don't know
that what you've just read is the truth, because you are wilfully
ignorant of the word of God, or because you are afraid you won't get to
feed your tummy regularly next year, then, my friend, you have sinned
and "Be sure your sin will find you out." Don't think for a minute that
"ignorance" is "innocence." Just because you are stupid does not mean
you are justified! God knows your motive for not finding out, and He
will surely judge you concerning this matter (James 4:17).

2:25 "But that which ye have already hold fast till I come.

26 And he that overcometh, and keepeth my works unto the end, to him
will I give power over the nations:

27 And he shall rule them with a rod of iron; as the vessels of a potter
shall they be broken to shivers: even as I received of my Father.

28 And I will give him the morning star.

29 He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the
churches."

"Rod of iron" (Rev. 12:3--6).

"Shivers." We say "splinters."'

In this fourth church period, we have covered the centuries from A.D.
500 to A.D. 1000. This is the period of the greatest nighttime in the
history of Christianity, for it is that period of time when nearly
everybody believes that the "Catholic" theologians are right, and the
Bible is too hard to understand.

The only real preachers in this age are Bernadino of Sienna, Henry of
Lausanne, Tanchelm, Leuthard, Robert of Brescia, Bruno of Segni, Rudolph
of Saxony, Vincent Ferrer, and the Nestorians. Arnold of Villanova comes
along a little later with Joachin of Floris, Berthold of Rumania (1210),
and others; Tanchelm and Leuthard are good ones, and of course, Raymond
Lull (1235) was a great missionary.

In this period of time, there is one fact that is always foremost. It is
the fact that the popes who use Jerome's Latin Vulgate Bible, preserved
in the monasteries by the monks, never win any souls, and spiritually
never amount to a hill of beans.

The men who are doing the preaching on the street and among the poor,
and doing the missionary work in Africa and India, and who are winning
the souls, are using either the Greek Textus Receptus of the Greek
Eastern Branch of the Orthodox Church or the old Latin from the same
text. This is the Byzantine text, which, of course, is the source of the
Authorized Version (King James) 1611.

CHAPTER 3

We now come to the "Sardis period" of church history. The word "Sardis"
means "red ones." These saints are not called the "red ones" for
nothing, because this age is typified by martyrdom and bloodshed. This
is the age that spawns forth the murderers of Bible‑believing Christians
by thousands, under the encouragement of the Roman Catholic priests and
popes. This is the age that brings forth the Spanish Inquisition. This
is the age that is headed‑up by the massacre of the Huguenots, at the
St. Bartholomew Day Massacre, where the Pope gave a medal to armed
Catholics, who were able to kill unarmed Protestants.

Although these things seem to be afar off, hundreds of years ago, you
must remember that similar events will take place again. As we have said
before, "The only thing that men learn from history is that men never
learn from history." The first time Jesus Christ came, Rome was in
power---not Russia.

The Sardis Period, "the red ones," begins about A.D. 1000, right in the
middle of the Dark Ages; it begins with the Crusades and with the
invasions of Saladin and Ghengis Khan. In this period of time we also
find the One Hundred Years War between England and France (1337--1450).
We find the beginnings of the Renaissance and the writings of Dante,
Boccacio, Balzac, and Omar Khayyam; we find the influx of Asiatic
culture and civilization going back into Europe, by means of the
Crusades, and Hindu and Moslem philosophy and learning are coming up
into Spain and up through Greece.

Above all, we find in this period the multiplication of the Greek
"Textus Receptus" manuscripts (used by the King James translators),
found in the Greek Orthodox Church, and traveling up through Hungary,
Bohemia, and Bulgaria into Martin Luther's Germany. We noticed that in
the 1,000 years previous to this time that God never used the Latin
(Jerome's Vulgate) of the Roman Catholic Church for any kind of a
revival. The line of revival is not Augustine and Origen (in Alexandria,
Egypt), up through a Latin Church in Italy and over into the Spanish
Inquisition. That is the line of spiritual degeneration and Scriptural
corruption.

The line of revival is from Antioch (where the disciples were first
called Christians---Acts 11), up through Asia Minor to the seven
churches we are studying---which were founded and set up by the Apostle
Paul---up through the Greek Church. When the Greek Church went into
apostasy, the line of revival went up through Hungary, Bohemia,
Bulgaria, Austria, and into Germany under Martin Luther (and the
Gutenberg Bible in 1468), and from there to the English‑speaking people,
and out to the ends of the earth under the reign of Queen Victoria. This
is the line of the Holy Spirit's "Textus Receptus. "

(The line of the RSV and ASV is a Roman Catholic, North African line
that God has never used and never will use for anything but religious
confusion, political trouble, bloodshed, and coercive politics.)

The Sardis Period has three of the greatest names in church history in
it---Wycliffe, John Huss, and Savonarola.

3:1 "And unto the angel of the church in Sardis write; These things
saith he that hath the seven Spirits of God, and the seven stars; I know
thy works, that thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead."

He knows the works of this church exactly as He knew the works of the
churches at Ephesus and Pergamos. This church is a dead church. It has a
reputation of being alive, yet it is not alive---it is dead. While the
membership in this church was growing, the members were not growing!

This church (Sardis) is the believing remnant in the Roman Catholic
Church who had a name for "orthodoxy." They had a name for believing the
fundamentals of the faith, the "Apostle's Creed," the Virgin Birth,
etc., but they were "stone cold dead in the market." By A.D. 1000, the
Roman Catholic Church is as dead spiritually as it ever will get, and it
has been a corpse since that time. It has a name that it lives, and yet
it doesn't live at all. (The Popes say they can trace their church back
to Jesus Christ. This is a bad error; Jesus Christ is the "Way, the
Truth, the Life." "He that hath the Son hath life"---not death. The true
church is not a dead, ecclesiastical organization, lying to people to
get them to join, forcing people into membership before they are born,
by intermarriage. The true Church of the Lord Jesus Christ is a living
body, and the situation in Revelation 3:1 in Sardis is the description
of a dead outfit.)

This is true of many organizations. The Methodist Church has a name. It
has a great name, a fine old name---but it is dead. It was founded by
John Wesley, and we hear Methodists brag about their founder and what a
tremendous work he did in the Social Revolution in England. Yet, if John
Wesley were to come back today and visit the average First Methodist
Church, he would drop dead with a heart attack before he got past the
third ashtray. The organism has become a mere organization. (Back in
1850--1890, when a man said he was a Methodist, they loaned him money on
his membership. All a man had to do at the bank was to say that he was a
member of the Methodist Church, and they would loan him any amount of
money he wanted to borrow. Now, you can go to any bank in the world and
try to borrow money on your church membership, and they will laugh you
out of town. It doesn't mean anything anymore.)

This church, in Revelation 3, is like that. Many of the churches today
are like an ailing lung with only a few cells doing the breathing. The
real life is a few Spirit‑filled people who keep it from becoming an
animated corpse. In the Roman Catholic Church from 1000 to 1500, there
are just a few lights that keep the thing going. As a church
organization, it is dead. Catharists, Huguenots, Waldensians, and
Albigenses keep it from being buried.

3:2 "Be watchful, and strengthen the things which remain, that are ready
to die: for I have not found thy works perfect before God.

3 Remember therefore how thou hast received and heard, and hold fast,
and repent. If therefore thou shalt not watch, I will come on thee as a
thief, and thou shalt not know what hour I will come upon thee."

Notice, this is a definite reference, doctrinally, to the Tribulation
(although it can have spiritual application to the Church Age). This
passage actually goes with Revelation 16:15, which is a reference,
definitely, to the Tribulation. It also goes with Matthew 24--25 and the
"coming as a thief in the night."

3:4 "Thou hast a few names even in Sardis which have not defiled their
garments; and they shall walk with me in white: for they are worthy."

These white robes He speaks of are "the righteousness of saints" (Rev.
3:18, 16:15; Psa. 45). The "white robes" are earned works. They are not
"white robes washed in the blood of the lamb" (Rev. 6). These robes are
the righteousnesses of the saints themselves (Rev. 19); they are the
good works which a Christian does after he is saved, with which he will
find himself clothed at the Judgment Seat of Christ (2 Cor. 5:1--10).
This verse (Rev. 3:4) shows that there are still a few people standing
for the right thing and preaching the truth. In church history, these
people are called "Anabaptists," "Waldensians," "Albigenses,"
"Lollards," "Paulicians;" and they are branded as heretics by the
official Roman Church, which, of course, is a dead corpse.

3:5 "He that overcometh, the same shall be clothed in white raiment; and
I will not blot out his name out of the book of life, but I will confess
his name before my Father, and before his angels."

This verse is often shown to prove that a man can lose salvation. (This
is typical of the wresting of the scripture.) If you will read
Revelation 3:5 carefully, and look at it closely, you will see that it
is not a threat at all; it is a promise. "He that overcometh\...I will
not blot out his name." This is how people get all messed up. They say
that if you don't overcome, that He will blot your name out! Again, go
back to 1 John 4:4 and 1 John 5:4 and learn immediately that the
Christian, by believing on Jesus Christ, has already overcome.
(Therefore, if somebody gives you a "hard time" in the passage, you
point out Scripture with Scripture. Rev. 3:5 is a promise that by
believing on Jesus Christ you will not be blotted out of the book of
life.)

3:6 "He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the
churches."

This period is the period of the bloody persecutions. The great
preachers in this period are Militz, Peter Waldo (who founds the
Waldensians), Conradt, John Tauler Matthias of Janow, Tyndale, Wycliffe,
Huss, Savonarola, John Colet, William Rubrick, Andrew of Perugia,
Vincent Ferrer, George Candinas, Robert Junius, David Givias. These men
preached in Germany, Switzerland, France, and Spain. They filled the
Holy Roman Empire's stomach so full of growling and acid indigestion
that, in the year 1200, we find two popes calling each other
"Antichrist"! (This is rather amusing because we have noted before that
the main theme in the Bible is not salvation, baptism, or church
membership---it is the Second Coming of Jesus Christ. "The Antichrist"
is a figure associated with Christ's Second Coming \[Rev. 13 \]; not
sacraments and "churches.")

As I have said before, and will say again, no one will ever get this
Jewish Bible right until he gets the Jew in his proper place. You will
never get the Jew in the proper place until you realize that when God
promised to send him a Messiah and make his nation top, God was not
lying. Until you recognize the events associated with the Second Coming
of Jesus Christ---the setting up of an earthly, literal, visible,
Davidic, Messianic Kingdom over Israel---you cannot even locate the
Antichrist.

So, we find two knuckle‑headed popes in the Sardis period (in 1200)
calling each other "the Antichrist." Neither of the idiots knew enough
about the Bible to know that the Antichrist doesn't show up until after
the Body of Christ is gone! If either of them was the Antichrist, both
of them were lost! This is typical of the powerful, authentic,
"ex‑cathedra," holy, "infallible leadership" of the pope! He is no more
"infallible" than a three‑year‑old Ubangi.

In this particular period, we have street preachers going all over
Europe, preaching the Book of Revelation and exposing Rome for what she
is. If there is any doubt in your mind, and you think that this is a
dogmatic or bigoted statement, let us turn to the Bible and see what
they were preaching.

(For one time in your life, swallow your intolerance and your prejudiced
outlook on things. Forget for awhile what you have learned from Life,
Look, and Reader's Digest, and that television set that has been
brainwashing you for the last ten years. Look at the word of God. We
shall not interpret these passages. Let the Holy Spirit Himself show you
something you have never seen before, and open your eyes to something
that you need to see. The Lord is going to do it; I'm not going to fool
with it.)

When the late President Kennedy was shot, Christians were sitting around
TV's by the thousands, eating up things that supposedly were religious,
and they were so brainwashed that they actually saw black‑robed Roman
priests dump water on the American Flag, and not a single protest was
made. Think about that! You talk about Communists! Do you realize that
black‑robed priests, who have sworn loyalty to a foreign power and
recognize the Italian pope as the supreme ruler of their lives, dumped
water all over the Stars and Stripes? "Us boys" in the infantry were
told not even to let that flag touch the ground---let alone dump water
on it. Somebody said it was "holy water." That shows you how brainwashed
the American people are. Who ever heard of such a thing as "holy water"?
The only time you find that kind of thing is in the Old Testament in the
signs and wonders and miracles given to Israel. There's no such thing as
"holy water," and twentieth-century, Bible‑believing Protestants ought
to have better sense. All this "holy water," "holy Mary," "holy
cross"---HOLY BALONEY! In the word of God, the only things "holy" are
the Holy Spirit, the temple of the believer, the word of God, and God
Himself! There is no such thing as "holy" points of contact. God is a
Spirit. He doesn't fool with tables, radios, blankets, baptismal pools,
and sanctuaries.

Now, turn to Revelation 17 to see what "heretics" in 1200--1400
preached. Revelation 17:1--2 says, "And there came one of the seven
angels which had the seven vials, and talked with me, saying unto me,
Come hither; I will shew unto thee the judgment of the great whore that
sitteth upon many waters: With whom the kings of the earth have
committed fornication, and the inhabitants of the earth have been made
drunk with the wine of her fornication."

Notice how closely this description matches the references you studied
in Revelation 2 on Jezebel, the prophetess, who taught the church false
doctrine (Rev. 2:20). But there can be no doubt about the identification
of this lady in Revelation 17. For example, Revelation 17:18 says, "And
the woman which thou sawest is that great city, which reigneth over the
kings of the earth."

Now, isn't it wonderful how the AV King James 1611 is self‑interpreting?
Isn't it strange how all these new "bibles" have changed verses so that
