you can't find the interpretation? Isn't it a blessing how the King
James describes what it means, by what it means, where it says what it
means, the way it says it, as it meant it? You don't have to guess who
this woman is; you don't have to "interpret" anything.

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city." You understand the
word---your trouble is that you don't want to believe it! The trouble
people have with the Bible is not that it is hard to understand---it
simply hits them too hard. Here is a woman who is said to be a "city,"
and a "great city," who reigns "over the kings of the earth." Do you
know of any city like that?

Revelation 17:9---"And here is the mind which hath wisdom, The seven
heads are seven mountains, on which the woman sitteth."

Do you know any great city that reigns "over the kings of the earth,"
that is built on "seven mountains"? It is not a matter of
interpretation; it is a matter of your either knowing of a city that
reigns over people, which is built on seven mountains, or not. Now, do
you, or don't you?

Revelation 17:6---"And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the
saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus: and when I saw her,
I wondered with great admiration."

Do you know of any city that is a "great city," that is built on "seven
mountains," which reigns "over the kings of the earth," that has killed
"martyrs of Jesus Christ"?

Revelation 17:4---"And the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet
colour, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having a
golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her
fornication."

Do you know of any "great city" that "reigns over the kings of the
earth," that is built on "seven mountains," whose colors are "purple and
scarlet," whose symbol is a cup, who is guilty of the "blood of the
martyrs of Jesus Christ"? You don't? Well, forget it then. But if you
are lying, you've got problems, for the Holy Spirit wrote this Book and
revealed this city, named her, and identified her, in such certain
terminology that the only way Rome could ever get rid of the passage was
to claim that the book was written before A.D. 70 and referred to pagan
Rome!

Revelation 17 is what the street preachers were preaching in the
European "Holy Roman Empire." By 1500 the Holy Roman Empire had such
acid indigestion it needed a dose of "Tums." In 1252, popes called each
other Antichrist; the Roman Catholic Church split all to pieces. It
changed its "doctrine" fourteen times---and they are still changing it
every five years! When the Roman Catholic Church teaches a dumb
Protestant that it is always consistent in doctrine and has "uniform
teaching," and that the Protestants are "all split up," it is telling
you the biggest cock‑and‑bull story since Aesop's Fables. The Roman
Catholic Church has changed its political doctrines five times a year
for the last three hundred years and its ecclesiastical convictions at
least fifty times in 600 years. The only place it is consistent is that
all its members agree that you have to do what their church tells you to
or you'll go to hell, because they've got the authority to put you there
or to keep you out. (If this is slander, the Knights of Columbus haven't
written a very honest set of books on "what a Catholic believes!")

In the Sardis period, we have some great pre-Reformation preachers. We
have the great John Wycliffe (1324), the great John Huss (1369), the
great John Tauler, and the great Savonarola of North Italy (1452), and
near the end we have Tyndale, the translator.

One of the most effective of the pre‑Lutheran reformers was Peter Waldo
(1176), a wealthy merchant in Lyons, France. He put great emphasis on
the Scripture and Scriptural methods. He translated portions of the
Bible into the vernacular, that is, the common language of his people.
He also stressed preaching by laymen on the street, and he taught that
the Roman Catholic Church was not infallible. With this teaching the
papacy became more hostile to his followers, and finally they were
tortured by "Catholics," murdered, shot, drowned, hung, stabbed, and
killed by any means possible.

John Wycliffe, 1324--1384, was a Biblical reformer, bringing the
teaching of Scriptures to bear on practices of the Roman Church. He was
branded as a heretic. (The modern practice is exactly the same. Where
any man tries to turn light on the Roman Church with Scripture, that man
is branded a "Communist" or a "heretic." That is exactly how they
branded the Reformers. In a pamphlet by the Knights of Columbus called
"Reformation or Revolt," the Roman Catholic Church tries to prove that
every man who returned to the Scripture was a rebel against the
authority of God given to Rome.) Wycliffe was educated at Oxford and was
able to reach the upper class of English people because of his
education. He opposed the infallibility of the pope. He emphasized the
invisible church and stressed the responsibility of the clergy to preach
the word of God, rather than rule. He was a good evangelical Christian
and trained scores of preachers called "Lollards."

John Huss, 1369--1415, was a Professor of Philosophy at the University
of Prague. (It is generally held that Huss was influenced by Wycliffe,
and his approach was much the same as Wycliffe's.) He preached around
Bohemia. (This, it will be noticed, is in the line of the revivals,
coming up from Germany to England, via the Balkans, not from Italy to
Spain. When a man returns to Italy for his authority, he proves that he
is nothing but a medieval, archaic, superstitious pagan. Italy has never
had anything in its religions which was Scriptural, or near the
Scripture. There is nothing about Italy or Rome, in any way, shape, or
form, that resembles Biblical Christianity, other than a profession to
believe certain correct statements. Of course, the Devil believes all
the correct statements!)

One of the most remarkable figures in Europe during the latter part of
the fifteenth century was Girolamo Savonarola, 1452--1498. He was a
Catholic monk who became a street preacher. He preached sermons in
Florence, Italy, and led a reform movement there. He preached
Premillennialism. Savonarola attacked the corruption in the Roman
Catholic Church, including the authority of the pope. Subsequently, he
was martyred. First, the pope tried to shut him up and said, "I'll give
you a red hat"---meaning a Cardinal's hat. Savonarola replied, "I'll
take a red hat of blood"---meaning a martyr's hat of death. He got it.
They burned him at the stake. Before they burned him at the stake, the
"Catholic" priest said, "I separate thee from the church militant (the
one down here) and the church triumphant (the one up there)." Savonarola
replied, "You can separate me from the church militant (down here), but
not from the church triumphant (up there), that is beyond thy power."

On the eve of the Reformation (1500), the "Holy" Roman Empire was like a
seething kettle, ready to boil over. Economically, socially,
politically, intellectually, and religiously, the time had come for an
explosion. The Catholic Church had become as rotten as a thing could
get.

It was Martin Luther, the son of a German coal miner, who gave direction
to the "explosion" which we now call the Protestant "Reformation." He
was, perhaps, hated more than any man in the world, outside of King
Henry VIII. Martin Luther is hated today because he took Germany away
from the pope. King Henry VIII is hated because he took England from the
pope. The pope lost his power to control the earth largely through these
two men---for he lost the German and British Empires, which would have
included the United States, Australia, and Canada! Because of this
appalling loss, the Roman Catholic Church has spent the last 500 years
trying to bring England and Germany back into the fold. Consequently, we
wouldn't be surprised to find that Adolph Hitler and Joseph Goebels were
both Roman Catholics!

In the period we are about to enter (around 1500), we will find Columbus
discovering America (1492). We will find the full tide of the
Renaissance has rolled in, rediscovering the literature and thought
patterns of the classical age. (It also brought with it a secularization
of human life.) The greatest of all of the ancient documents began to be
recovered at this time, and this, of course, involved the Bible. The
Bible that was getting printed all over the country was the "Textus
Receptus" of the Greek Byzantine Eastern Church. It was no longer the
Latin Vulgate of Jerome, kept in the monasteries by the monks, who stole
them and hid them, and kept people from understanding them. It was the
true word of God propagated by street preachers.

3:7 "And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write; These things
saith he that is holy, he that is true, he that hath the key of David,
he that openeth, and no man shutteth; and shutteth, and no man openeth."

The word "Philadelphia" is from two Greek words meaning "brotherly
love," or "love of the Brethren." This represents the church period from
1500 to 1900. This is the greatest period in history (as far as the
church goes) for the true Body of Christ. This is the only church of the
seven churches that is not told to repent! This is the church that gets
up and goes the way God intended for it to go. This church brings with
it the Industrial Revolution, the Agricultural Revolution, the
Scientific Revolution, and every major progressive step of man since the
world began to that time.

The movement of this church forward is so powerful and so consistent
that unsaved people think the world is getting better, so this is the
age that teaches the theory of evolution as a fact! (It is, in reality,
nothing more than the King James Bible being preached to the ends of the
earth under the Victorian British Empire.) Darwin and other Englishmen
thought that because of this evangelistic reformation, with its fruits
of civilization (prophesied in the King James Bible---in which
everything got better because it was the church of the "Open Door" and
the "Open Bible"), that man must be evolving upward from animals and man
was his own god moving upward to godliness by his own effort! Some of
the Christians were dumb enough to believe this, and they began to teach
the same old Postmillennial theory that they learned in A.D. 325 when
they hooked up with Rome (Rev. 2:12) under Eusebius and Constantine!

Since they had so many missionaries going out---Carey, Judson, Goforth,
Taylor---and because the Gospel was being preached to the ends of the
earth, Christians actually thought they were going to "bring in the
Kingdom." (Of course, the Southern Baptist Convention and the Roman
Catholic Church still believe this today!)

3:8 "I know thy works: behold, I have set before thee an open door, and
no man can shut it: for thou hast a little strength, and hast kept my
word, and hast not denied my name."

This church is the church of the "open door." (The next church, the
Laodicean Church, is the church of the shut door, Rev. 3:20). But
Philadelphia is the church that keeps the word of God. This is the
church that publishes the 1611 Authorized Version. "Hast kept my word,"
is not said about any other church in any other period.

The Philadelphia Reformation Church from 1500--1900,  with the English,
1611 Authorized Version, is the only church that is said to keep the
word of God. With the RV (1885), ASV (1901), and with the switch from
the King James Bible for the RV in the British and Foreign Bible Society
in 1904, you enter the church of the shut door which rejected His word.

3:9 "Behold, I will make them of the synagogue of Satan, which say they
are Jews, and are not, but do lie; behold, I will make them to come and
worship before thy feet, and to know that I have loved thee."

We found this same bunch of liars in the Smyrna period, A.D. 150 to A.D.
325 (Rev. 2:9). Smyrna had this bunch of people in it who claimed that
they were really Jews, and that God was all through with the physical
Jews, and they claimed to be the true successors to Israel, and taught
Postmillennial doctrine. These "birds" pop up again in Revelation 3:9,
in the Reformation period. They claim that they are Jews, when they are
actually nothing but Romans. They claim that they replace Israel, when
they have done nothing but steal from Israel. They claim that the
Kingdom of Heaven and the Kingdom of God are the same, when they are
different. They are the same old clique of Postmillennial robbers who
have been perverting the word of God since the Acts of the Apostles.

From 1500--1900, the Roman Catholic Church gradually "poops out," and
drops out of prominence until it nearly collapses altogether. If it
weren't for the subtlety of the popes in working out politics
undercover, the Catholic Church would have been completely obliterated
by 1900. (Although the Catholics claim that the "gates of hell shall not
prevail" against their church is the reason why the Catholic Church
survives, it is not because the gates of hell don't prevail against it.
The reason the Catholic Church survives is because it always adapts
itself and compromises to meet the contemporary political situation.)
From 1500--1900 the Vatican goes "underground" and does not pop up again
until after 1930 to be recognized as the one "true church," when it is
the same Mother of Abominations that God is speaking of in Revelation
3:9 and Revelation 17:1--9. It is in league with "the gates of hell."

But in this period (1500--1900), Protestant Bible Christianity spread so
rapidly and so thoroughly, that up to 1900 it looked for a minute like
the popes were all through. But the popes had other plans, and they went
behind closed doors into the revision committees of the word of God, and
substituted the Westcott and Hort Roman Catholic Text for the Textus
Receptus. Consequently, every Bible published since 1885 is basically a
Roman Catholic Bible! Rome will come back in the driver's seat in 1980,
because as Rome was in power at the first coming of Christ, it will be
in power at the Second Coming of Christ.

3:10 "Because thou hast kept the word of my patience, I also will keep
thee from the hour of temptation, which shall come upon all the world,
to try them that dwell upon the earth.

11 Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast which thou hast, that no man
take thy crown."

These verses imply that the crowns, or rewards, can be lost (2 John 8).

3:12 "Him that overcometh will I make a pillar in the temple of my God,
and he shall go no more out: and I will write upon him the name of my
God, and the name of the city of my God, which is new Jerusalem, which
cometh down out of heaven from my God: and I will write upon him my new
name.

13 He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the
churches."

The Philadelphia period of the church is represented by that period of
history found between 1500--1900, when Protestant Biblical Christianity
reaches its peak, and then goes back into total apostasy and returns to
Roman Catholicism. (The next church \[Laodicea\] goes right back to the
Roman monstrosity from whence it came out, and by 1950, we find the
church in the same condition it was in during the Pergamos period, in
A.D. 325.)

The Philadelphia period is by far the most interesting period of church
history. This is the church of the open door. This is the church that
has all the great soul‑winners, all the great evangelists, all the great
missionaries. Consequently, this is the part of church history that
people always refer to when they want to prove that God is still the God
of revival. But this is the church that believed that the Authorized
Version was the word of God.

(One of the most asinine things you ever heard in your life is a modern,
present‑day evangelist, standing in the pulpit, talking about great
revivals yet to come, when he no longer believes the Authorized Version
is the word of God. Martin Luther's German Bible is the same text as the
King James 1611. Subsequently, all Bibles in Europe and the United
States, from these translations, are Textus Receptus, Greek Byzantine
text, Syrian type text; and they are not the text of the North African
Latin Church.)

This period begins in 1500. Christopher Columbus discovers America in
1492. At this time, we see the paintings of daVinci, Michelangelo, and
Raphael (in the Renaissance). We observe the great discoverers Balboa,
Magellan, Cortez, and Henry Hudson. We see the great painter, Rembrandt.
We can study with Copernicus and Galileo. We note the Napoleonic Wars.
We listen to the greatest musicians who ever lived---Bach (1750), Brahms
(1897), Beethoven (1827), Wagner (1893). We have, in this period, all
the great revolutions in art, science, music, industry, travel, and
discovery. This was the day of the "open Bible," and every blessing this
world had in these 400 years came from the fact that Martin Luther's
Bible and the Authorized Version (1611) were all over the world, with
multitudes being "born‑again" outside the "Catholic" church.

In this same period is the Civil War, the American Revolution, and the
War of 1812. In this period are all the great discoveries and all the
great inventions.

Also in this period is the most overwhelming wave of atheism, Communism,
and Christ‑rejecting scholarship that the world has ever seen. Spinoza,
Leibnitz, Descarte, Hegel, Rousseau, Astruc, Kant, Ewald, Delitzsch,
Wellhausen, Semler, Neuman, Schleirmacher, the Jesuits, Lessing,
Tregelles, Tischendorf, Freud, Huxley, Bauer, Marx, Darwin, Strauss,
James, and all their Mafia---Darwin, a God‑forsaken monkey with his tail
cut off, and Sigmund Freud, the sex‑neurotic, who wrote books on dreams.

When Martin Luther, Storch, Stubner, Munzer, Staupitz, Melancthon,
Hubmaier, Peter Bohler, Spener, and Zinzendorf cut through the papacy
(1500--1600), they tore the country up for God. But no sooner were they
"dead and gone," than up pop Rauschenbusch, Strauss, Astruc, Wellhausen,
Leibnitz, and all the others, who are Christ-rejecting, Bible‑defying
humanitarians, and who are dedicated to the overthrow of the word of
God.

Martin Luther was a son of a German coal miner, born in 1483. He was
raised in a little town in Germany, and was a coal miner himself. As a
boy he sang hymns on the street, and later became a monk. He went to the
University of Erfurt to study law, and then entered the Augustinian
monastery at Erfurt. When Luther went to Rome, he saw what a mess things
were in. In 1512, he received his Doctor of Theology Degree, and
succeeded Staupitz as Professor of Theology, and held this position
until 1546. He nailed his famous Ninety‑five Theses on the church door
at Wittenburg, Germany, on October 31, 1517, in protest against the sale
of indulgences. Printed copies of this Thesis went out all over Europe,
and Luther even sent a copy of it to Rome. Subsequently, Luther was
hauled up before a Mafia of priests, popes, and bishops at the Diet of
Worms (1521); and there he told the entire group of Bible‑rejecting
Roman Catholics, "I cannot and will not recant anything, since it is
neither safe nor honest to act against one's conscience. My conscience
is bound by the word of God, and so, God help me, here I stand."

After this Martin Luther and his followers turned the world into an
uproar. (Luther was a popular, dynamic leader in an age that looked for
leadership. He could sing, write music, and was a great preacher; he
could preach in German or Latin. Martin Luther's translation of the
Bible is a monument in the German language, exactly as the King James
Bible is a monument in the English language.) Martin Luther addressed
the pope as "Your Hellishness," and called him "Most Hellish Father,"
instead of "Most Holy Father."

Martin Luther's followers took over, and there follows the "Peasant's
War" under men like Zwingli, who was a little more radical than Martin
Luther. Hoffman, Munzer, and some of the radicals went out and burned
monasteries, killed a few priests and monks, took a few nuns and tore up
their clothes, burned up their candles, and busted their beads. (Martin
Luther didn't approve of all this, but it went on.)

Ulrich Zwingli, 1484--1531, had a part in the Reformation in the
German‑speaking part of Switzerland. (He studied in Bern, Vienna, and
Basel.) Zwingli was "born-again," and he began to preach the word of
God. He broke with the pope, got married, and preached openly against
celibacy. Gradually, the Reformation which he started triumphed, and
John Calvin picked up his work in 1509--1564.

(John Calvin is often called the "Protestant Pope." He was a
dead‑orthodox theologian, and had some pretty wild theological ideas. He
was Amillennial---as are all Roman Catholics. However, Calvin was fairly
fundamental in some beliefs.)

Along with this Continental Reformation, the Church of England moved to
"mend its ways." It produced some great preachers. The Reformation
proceeded in Scotland under the work of John Knox. Farel was a great
preacher. Bernadino was a converted Roman Catholic who preached. Menno
Simons, who founded the Mennonites, was a great preacher. Storch,
Stubner, Munzer were great German preachers. Staupitz, Grebel, Hubmaier
were sound in their preaching. Peter Bohler (a German) led John Wesley
to Jesus Christ. Zinzendorf (a German) founded an orphanage (Halle,
Prussia), and took care of a group called Moravians, who went out all
over India, China, and Japan preaching the Gospel. Franke (a German)
preached the Gospel and aided Pietists and Moravians in their missionary
enterprises.

When John Wesley got saved, England was having a social upheaval. George
Whitefield and John Wesley pitched into it, preaching the gospel and
turning England upside down. John Wesley would pray two to four hours a
day. (He was preaching three hours at a time when he was in his
seventies.) He and Whitefield preached in the open fields. They preached
the new birth, and they preached repentance. They preached that the
Roman "Catholic" Church was pagan, unholy, anti‑Christian, archaic, and
worthless. Wesley even broke with the Episcopal Church because it was
too formal and too much like the Catholic Church. He said to his
Episcopal priest, "The world is my parish," and began to preach
outdoors. George Whitefield did the same thing. These two men were
persecuted, shot at, and threatened. Both of them lived to a ripe old
age, and preached right on up to their dying day.

Whitefield had some messages that were three hours long, and he would
often preach until his throat bled. (Wesley preached in Dublin, Ireland,
and when the Catholics tried to stone him there and push him down from a
statue he was standing by---a ten foot statue of the Virgin Mary---one
Catholic fell and broke his leg, another one got hit in the mouth with a
stone thrown at Wesley, and one man got his hand split open across the
knuckles, when he tried to muzzle Wesley's mouth.)

Once George Whitefield had a gun pointed at him about two feet behind
his back by a man who said, "If you don't shut up, I'm going to blow
your head off." Whitefield kept on preaching, and the man walked around
him for ten minutes, putting the gun against his nose, then in his ear,
then on his eyes, then in his stomach, saying, "You blankety‑blank, I'm
going to blow your head off." Finally, the man stopped about ten feet
behind Whitefield, pulled the trigger, and the gun exploded and blew the
fellow's hands off, and just blew Whitefield's shirt off his back.

Whitefield came to America and preached in Georgia. The "old time
religion" went right up through the Carolinas, and on up to Boston with
him. When Whitefield got to Boston Common, Professor Stidger of Boston
University met him "on the green" in Boston and said, "I'm sorry to see
you here, Brother Whitefield." Whitefield said, "So's the Devil," and
began to preach. (Wesley and Whitefield probably led over two million
men to Christ during their time.)

A.D. 1500--1900 is the great period of the "open door," when Roger
Williams came to America and became a Baptist missionary among the
Indians. Roger taught "separation of Church and State," knowing that the
Roman Catholic Church would eventually try to take over in America.

(When the pope finally moved in, he moved into Baltimore, Maryland,
twenty miles from Washington, D.C. The first Archbishop's Diocese in
America was in Baltimore in Maryland. Did you get that? Our "black
capitol" is located in Maryland.)

At this time, around the 1700's, we have Jonathan Edwards preaching. We
have Thomas Webb and William Strawbridge, great Methodist preachers. We
have Darby a little later, in 1800. We have Phillip Embry, a great
Methodist preacher. We have Munhall and Culpepper in the 1800's. We have
great missionaries in this period like William Carey who went to India,
David Brainerd who went to the Indians, Judson who went to Burma, Hudson
Taylor of the China Inland Missions, C. T. Studd, Livingstone, Gordon.
All the great missionary activities in China, Japan, and India take
place in this period, between 1800--1900.

One will hear great pulpit preachers in this period. We have Talmage,
Baxter, Payson, Culpepper, Jacob Knapp (a traveling evangelist), Peter
Cartwright (the Methodist circuit rider) who would knock a man down, sit
on him, and tell him to trust Christ or he would bust his teeth out! We
have James McGready, a backwoods preacher in Tennessee, and Lorenzo Dow,
a half‑breed Seminole Indian, who preached in Alabama and Georgia. We
have Sheldon Jackson, Billy Bray, and Charles Guthrie, and the great
Methodist Welsh preachers.

This is the period which saw Charles G. Finney's great revivals, and the
revivals under Spurgeon and Dwight L. Moody. Moody, who led millions to
Christ, talked so fast it took two shorthand stenographers to write down
what he said. Once a man came up after Moody had finished a message and
said, "I counted eighty‑four grammatical errors in your message." Moody
stuck out his tongue and said, "You see that," pointing to his tongue.
The man said, "Yes." Moody said, "I'm using that for the glory of God,
what are you using yours for?"

Moody founded the Moody Bible Institute, which is still fairly
fundamental today after one hundred years, and has perhaps stayed true
to the word of God longer than any other institution on the face of the
earth. Moody was succeeded in his preaching by R. A. Torrey, a great
evangelist and militant theologian, who studied under Franz Delitzsch.
(Torrey inherited some of his Bible criticism from Delitzsch, and he
occasionally used the RV or the ASV, but Torrey was true to the word of
God, and was a Premillennial, fundamental, hell‑fire‑and‑damnation,
new‑birth preacher.)

We have Joseph Parker and Simpson (Missionary Alliance) at this time,
putting out the word of God; they were both great preachers and great
souls.

As we come nearer to the 1900's, we find Sam Jones, a circuit riding
Methodist preacher, who was born in Alabama. (He had a circuit in
Cartersville, Georgia, and his preaching salary for a year was $80.00.)
Sam Jones was a drunken lawyer, and he was saved at his father's
deathbed. He preached all over Alabama, Georgia, Tennessee, California,
Texas, the Carolinas, Mississippi, and Arkansas, as a circuit riding
Methodist preacher. He later became a great evangelist, another truly
great soul. (Half of what Will Rogers and Dave Gardner knew, they got
from the cracks and side remarks which Sam Jones made.) He was a great
wit, and he led thousands of people to Jesus Christ.

We have in this period the great George Mueller, who founded the
orphanage in Bristol, England, and prayed in more than four million
dollars. We have the Haldane brothers, converted from godless sea
privateers and pirates to great men of God. We have David Livingstone,
who died out in Africa and had his heart cut out of his body and buried
in Africa. (They found him dead by his bed, kneeling in prayer for the
heathen.) We have also C. T. Studd, Gordon, A. J. Cronin, and the great
Presbyterian preacher Earle, who traveled up and down across the western
seaboard.

In the Philadelphia period are Taylor, Morgan, Goforth, Judson, Fiske,
General Booth, Captain Bliss, Fannie Crosby (the great hymn writer),
Havergal, another great hymn writer, Payson, Andrew Murray, who wrote
books on holiness, Baxter McClendon (Cyclone Mac), a circuit riding
Methodist preacher, H. C. Morrison, a great Methodist preacher and a
great soul, and Wilbur Chapman, who was in a measure responsible for the
success of Billy Sunday.

We have the men born who edited the Scofield Reference
Bible---Gaebelein, Pettingill, and C. I. Scofield.

As we come up toward 1900, we see the tremendous impact of the Methodist
Church which preached, "Ye must be born again." (Before the Methodist
Church went modernistic in 1910--1920, it won literally thousands of
people to Jesus Christ in the United States and England.)

During this time (Philadelphia), we run into Billy Sunday, a converted
baseball player (who still holds the fastest base‑running record of any
man in the American League---Billy Sunday ran it in his bare feet!). He
got saved outside Mel Trotter's Rescue Mission in Chicago, while two of
his "buddies" laughed at him. Yet, Billy Sunday led more than a million
souls to Christ. In response to the last sermon he preached before he
died, eighteen grown men walked the aisles for Jesus Christ and were
converted. (Billy Sunday is the evangelist whom Sinclair Lewis makes fun
of in his dirty book, Elmer Gantry. Lewis was a chronic alcoholic, and
he hated Billy Sunday's "guts" because Billy preached about liquor. So
Lewis wrote a dirty book, and from this came a dirty movie called "Elmer
Gantry," which dirty people like to see, and especially drunken people
who like to drink and don't like to listen to preachers talk against
liquor. It was voted an "Academy Award" winner a few years ago in the
United States by the same kind of dirty people who subscribe to that
particular kind of dirt.) Billy Sunday was a clean, honest man. Homer
Rodeheaver knew him for twenty years and said, "I never heard Billy
Sunday tell a dirty joke offstage or on stage. He lived what he
preached---one of the cleanest men that ever lived." He was a
hard‑hitting evangelist and a great soul, who won literally thousands,
perhaps millions, to Jesus Christ,

This is the period in which Mordecai Ham was born, the man who led Billy
Graham to Jesus Christ. (Although a personal worker in Ham's meeting led
Billy Graham to Jesus Christ, it was during one of Ham's meetings in
Charlotte, N.C.) Mordecai Ham was raised in Kentucky and Arkansas. He
was a great soul and a great soul‑winner. He was an iron man of God.
Catholics threatened him, thugs threatened to kidnap his son, and on one
occasion they knocked Mordecai down in the street with a car, and
dragged him a good ways on the bumper. Before Billy Graham was saved, a
man stopped Mordecai Ham outside of his hotel, put a .45 pistol in his
stomach, and told him to get in a cab, and that "they" were driving him
out of town. Ham said, "I'll give you five seconds to put up that gun,
or I'll call a cop." The man put up the gun and left himself. Later,
Billy Graham was saved.

Along about this time we have a man showing up who was born back in the
little town of Brannon's Stand near Dothan, Alabama---Bob Jones Sr. We
also have a man raised in Texas, J. Frank Norris, who later attended
Louisville Seminary.

This is the great heyday of the true church, 1500--1900. While Darwin,
Huxley, Marx, Bertrand Russell, Rauschenbusch, Bacon, Brunner, Ritschl,
and all the lost, humanitarian, self‑righteous Bible-rejectors were
trying to destroy and overthrow the word of God, the gospel was going
across America, Canada, the United States, and Asia like a prairie fire,
and people were getting saved by the thousands. Wherever the Bible went,
educational standards came up. Wherever the Bible went, God blessed with
material wealth and health. The power of Bible Christianity was so great
that unsaved educators actually thought that man was evolving from a
monkey! They figured he had come so far that he just must have come up
all the way! A greater mistake could not have been made.

Under cover, the old depraved scholars worked away, and hammered at the
foundations of Christianity. The Seventh-day Adventists started in 1833
and tried to teach salvation by works. The RV was translated in 1885,
and tried to replace the King James Bible. Mary Baker Patterson Eddy,
1879, came out with a lot of "chittlens" and tripe not worth talking
about. Then, of course, we find a group of spiritual degenerates called
"Latter Day Saints" and "Mormons" who thought they were pretty smart.
They professed to have discovered some gold plates. They put on a big
show and got real upset when people criticized them (as they do today),
and they blasphemed the word of God and contradicted the Holy Spirit and
began a century‑long trip to hell out in Salt Lake City in a big
religious way with a lot of show that doesn't amount to anything
Scripturally.

In the Philadelphia period, the Catholic Church goes undercover into the
Bible revision committees. The Council of Trent in 1546 had officially
pronounced a curse on all Bible‑believing Protestants. (At the Council
of Trent, you will find the Roman Catholic Church officially cursed
every man who doesn't believe the Apocrypha is inspired, every man who
teaches salvation without water baptism, and every man who teaches you
can be born again of the Holy Spirit without subscribing to the
sacraments of the Roman Catholic Church! Religious bunk!) The Catholic
Church went underground in 1700 and began to work on the Westcott and
Hort revision committee.

So, when the RV comes out in 1881--1885, it comes out not as a new
translation of the Bible, but as a completely new book from a different
set of manuscripts. The RV (1885), ASV (1901), and RSV (1952) are
nothing but the recovered corrupt Roman Catholic text of A.D. 325. They
are resurrections of the text that Eusebius and Constantine used to
destroy the Apostolic church! This text is "Vaticanus," written in A.D.
350, and Sinaiticus, written in A.D. 331. This is basically the text
from which all Roman Catholic Bibles are taken. (In A.D. 325, this was
the type of text that was adopted at the Council of Nicaea.)
Consequently, we are not surprised to find the Federal Council adopting
this text in 1900, the National Council adopting it in 1940, and the
Ecumenical Council adopting the same text in 1962.

The year 1900 brings us to the end of the greatest, most wonderful, and
most powerful period of church history. This is the Philadelphia period,
the period of the church of the open door---when people were saved, when
the gospel was worldwide, when the doors were open.

No matter what scholars think about it, the door stayed open as long as
the born‑again Christians believed that the 1611 Authorized Version was
the word of God. The missionary doors to the gospel only closed when the
Body of Christ rejected the King James Bible and replaced it with the
Westcott and Hort Vatican text. (You can quote me on that to any Greek
professor you know, anytime, anyplace, anywhere, under any condition,
with my blessing. I will be glad to discuss it with him anytime, out of
Nestle's Critical Apparatus, containing the emendations, interpolations,
transpositions, and conjectures on all 1,600 manuscripts.)

There isn't any such thing as defeat and apostasy in the Body of Christ
until God's people reject the English Bible. But when they do, brother,
you've had it. (And you've had it, whether you know it or not.)

This brings us to 1900, the final church period before the Rapture, the
Laodicean church.

In Revelation 2--3, we saw how the Church Age began with a good church,
whose only fault was that it had left its first love. But by the time we
reach the end of the Philadelphia period and enter the Laodicean period,
the church is lukewarm and is everything except what it ought to be.

As the church was "on fire" in Ephesus and Smyrna, and "went to pot" in
Pergamos and Thyatira, the church is "on fire" in Sardis and
Philadelphia, and goes "to pot" again in Laodicea. History repeats
itself. As we have said before, the only thing men learn from history is
that men never learn from history. What you are learning in the Book of
Revelation is church history which is not taught in the public school
system. (A young man in the United States can get a college education,
and yet not learn enough about what God has done in history to make an
intelligent comment on a morning newspaper. This is why Cedric Foster,
Huntley and Brinkley, et al., are so unequipped to comment on the "news"
that their "commentaries" are almost ridiculous. No man is able to
comment intelligently on "the news" unless he knows what God has done in
history, what the Devil has done in history, and what the political
movements of the Roman Catholic Church are in history. These movements
are delineated in church history, and only in church history.)

3:14 "And unto the angel of the church of the Laodiceans write; These
things saith the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of
the creation of God."

The word "Laodicean" means the "rights or justices of the people."
Literally the civil rights of the people, as over against God's rights,
characterize the last church before the Rapture, where God (the Lord
Jesus Christ) has no "rights."

"The beginning of the creation of God." Notice how the preposition "of"
confuses things. This is not the beginning of the creation of God, in
the sense that Jesus Christ is an object. He is not the first thing God
made; He is the subject. (The Jehovah Witness finishes his theology
right here, and goes home to hell with it!) The sentence is not that
Jesus Christ is the first thing that God created. The sentence is that
Jesus Christ is the Beginning; He is the first of whatever God does. The
creation is the object, not Jesus Christ. (Jehovah Witnesses teach that
Jesus Christ is a "created god" and the first god that God created! This
is why Jehovah Witnesses distinguish between God the Father, as the one
true God Jehovah, and Jesus Christ as "a god" whom He created. This is a
matter of willful ignorance, and a deliberate rejection of plain
English, in addition to an inability to read English, because of heart
trouble, for Colossians 1:15--18 explains this verse thoroughly.)

3:15 "I know thy works, that thou art neither cold nor hot: I would thou
wert cold or hot."

The last church professes orthodoxy while going into apostasy. The last
church professes belief in the fundamentals of the faith, while it sides
with the Roman Church. It is a chameleon church; it hunts with the
hounds and runs with the hares; it fights on both sides at one time. It
is a rotten church; it is not hot---on fire for God; and it is not
cold---apostate modernism. It is dead‑orthodox, lukewarm---close enough
to God to be warmed by the Bible, and far enough away to be chilled by
the world. (You don't have to freeze in formalism just because you're
not frying in fanaticism. This church is right in the middle.)

"I would thou wert cold or hot." God is an extremist. God is not a
synthesized conservative, nor a coordinated integrationist, and He does
not believe in the "cooperative adjustment of unequal values to a true
and righteous balance of relative truths." Hot or cold, brother! Up or
down, heaven or hell, black or white, lost or saved! He says, "I would,"
and that's God's directive will about the matter.

3:16 "So then because thou art lukewarm, and neither cold nor hot, I
will spue thee out of my mouth.

17 Because thou sayest, I am rich, and increased with goods, and have
need of nothing: and knowest not that thou art wretched, and miserable,
and poor, and blind, and naked."

God says, "You make me sick." This is why He is sick of Laodicea. This
last church is marked by tremendous material assets. It is bragging
about its busses, properties, holdings, how much the property is worth,
how much the offerings are, how much missionary offering was raised. It
says, "I am\...increased with goods, and have need of nothing." But God
says, "You are wretched, you're miserable, you're poor, you're blind,
and you're naked." They are burdened with money instead of debt!

3:18 "I counsel thee to buy of me gold tried in the fire, that thou
mayest be rich; and white raiment, that thou mayest be clothed, and that
the shame of thy nakedness do not appear; and anoint thine eyes with
eyesalve, that thou mayest see."

"Buy of me gold tried in the fire." See 1 Peter 1:7.

"Thou mayest be clothed." See Revelation 16:15.

"Anoint thine eyes with eyesalve, that thou mayest see." See
Ecclesiastes 9:8; John 9:8; 2 Corinthians 5:3.

3:19 "As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten: be zealous therefore, and
repent.

20 Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and
open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with
me."

At the end of the Church Age Christ is outside the door, and the church
has slammed the door on Him. If any individual in that church hears His
voice and opens the door, Christ will come in to that particular man and
have fellowship with that particular man.

This is the only place in the Bible where the local church is spoken of
as a literal building. The only place in the Bible where the local
church is spoken of as a building, the congregation has Jesus Christ
shut outside, the door locked, and unless a man opens the door from the
inside, He can't get in.

3:21 "To him that overcometh will I grant to sit with me in my throne,
even as I also overcame, and am set down with my Father in his throne.

22 He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the
churches."

This winds up the messages to the seven churches. As we have said
before:

Historically, they refer to seven local churches in Asia Minor, at the
time of John the Apostle in A.D. 90.

Doctrinally, they apply to local churches in the Tribulation.

Spiritually, they refer to the history of the Church Age, from the Book
of the Acts to the Rapture of the Church.

Before we leave the Laodicean Period, let us review what happened
historically during this period. This is the period of the great
"falling away" mentioned in 2 Thessalonians 2. It begins around 1900. It
begins with the publication of the American Standard Version in 1901.
This blasphemous, dishonest book is still recommended by the Greek
department of every fundamental seminary in the United States, including
the best ones. If you were to write letters to the Greek Department at
Bob Jones University, Dallas Theological Seminary, Tennessee Temple, and
the best Bible institutes in America, you would find the Greek faculties
of these colleges recommending the ASV above the AV 1611. The ASV is the
official Jehovah Witness Bible; it has the word "Jehovah" in the Old
Testament, and the word is not mentioned one time in the New Testament,
thus making a distinguishing between "Jesus" and "Jehovah." (If this
sounds like I am picking a fight or being "radical," let the gullible
reader turn to John 9 and look in the margin of an ASV, and he will find
that it says that Jesus Christ was a creature and not the Creator! If
this seems a little hard, read Luke 2:33, and if this seems hard, turn
to 1 Tim. 3 and find the word "God" left out of the statement in vs. 16.
Then let the reader turn to Titus, where the reader of the ASV is left
to wait for the glory of God instead of the appearing of Jesus Christ in
2:13. If this isn't enough to make a real Christian sick at his stomach,
then maybe he had better go ahead and adopt it as his Bible.)

The American Standard Version of 1901 is a clumsy, naive, and irreverent
translation of the word of God. Although it is said to be the most
accurate by Greek professors in the fundamental seminaries, it most
certainly is not. It is a corrupt translation. When they say that it is
"more true to the original" than any other translation, they are lying
flatfooted. The ASV is not even a Bible. It is the American translation
of the Vatican text. It is the Westcott and Hort, Vatican, North African
Latin Text, used by Eusebius and Constantine in A.D. 325, used by the
Monks in the Dark Ages around A.D. 1000, used by the Roman Catholic
Church at the Council of Trent in 1546, and today is the official Bible
of the Ecumenical Council (1962). (The RSV and the RV are from the same
text, as is the Williams, Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed,
Amplified, Fenton, Gobey, Montgomery, Berkeley, Reims, Douay, Lamsa, and
the rest of the spurious nonsense.)

In the Laodicean period of great apostasy, we still have great
individual Christians "holding forth the word of life." There are Bob
Jones Sr., J. Frank Norris, Charlie Fuller, Dr. DeHaan, Theodore Epp,
Percy Crawford, Jack Wyrtzen, R. R. Brown, J. Harold Smith, Hyman
Appleman, John R. Rice, Oswald Smith, Gray, Scofield, Pember, Larkin,
Sauer, John Peter Lange, Macon, Strong, Carrol, Truett, Bullinger, and
many other great defenders of the faith.

Some of these men have little "scholastic" leanings. You'll notice that
Dr. DeHaan, Theodore Epp, and Charlie Fuller have spent the last ten
years correcting the King James Bible. As they've spent their time
correcting it, they've lost their radio time. God doesn't bless any man
who tears up the Authorized Version. I don't care if he's the greatest
soul winner in America, and has led you to Christ with a dozen others;
God will not bless people who destroy the Reformation Bible, the
Reformation Text, by which this world was put back on its feet after the
Dark Ages. Too many martyrs died to preserve this text for any Christian
to "mess" with it. One of the tragedies of our age is to hear men like
Dr. DeHaan---he's a great man---and Charlie Fuller and Theodore Epp (who
are consecrated Christians), wasting their time trying to correct the
King James Bible with a Vatican, Westcott and Hort Text, found in
Nestle's Greek Testament; it's a shame and a disgrace. These men were
great soul winners, and they preached the Authorized Version, and
thousands of souls were saved by their preaching.

Oswald J. Smith, the great missionary leader from Toronto, Canada,
raises something like $300,000 a year for missionaries, and has
literally hundreds of missionaries on the field "full time."

Bob Jones Sr. (1882--1968) preached the Gospel and founded a university.
(It went bankrupt during the depression, and reestablished itself in
Cleveland, Tennessee. It is now in Greenville, South Carolina.) Bob
Jones Sr.'s influence, which God used, lasts and will last until the
Second Coming of Christ. Hundreds and thousands of "preacher boys" have
gone out from Bob Jones University and led souls to Christ all over the
world. Bob Jones Sr. preached a King James Bible all his life. He was
Premillennial, although he was a Methodist. (He had his "letter" in Bob
Schuler's church in California.)

Bob Schuler, being a great Methodist of the old era, is now retired; he
preached at Trinity Methodist Church in Los Angeles, and he has several
sons in the ministry. Bob Schuler was a great, old‑time, Methodist
preacher, one of the old "Peter Cartwright school" of preachers.

We have Charlie Fuller holding forth in California for years over the
"Old Fashioned Revival Hour," which is broadcast all over the world; it
has saved hundreds.

We have Theodore Epp's "Back to the Bible" Broadcast out of Lincoln,
Nebraska.

We have Dr. DeHaan's broadcast out of Grand Rapids, Michigan, with the
Doctor also publishing a great deal of good literature. Dr. DeHaan is a
very simple, childlike type of teacher, but he is very effective. Many
of his books are excellent works on the fundamentals of the faith.

Jack Wyrtzen and Percy Crawford work with young people up in New York,
in the Laodicean period; both do an excellent work.

J. Harold Smith is a great preacher, who went from Knoxville, Tennessee,
to Greenville, South Carolina (where he was from originally). He
preaches now in Fort Smith, Arkansas. He is a great fighter and crusader
for righteousness, and a great preacher of the word. He is a Southern
Baptist, but a great soldier of the cross, like R. G. Lee, who also
stood for the Authorized Version against the RSV.

Hyman Appleman, a converted Russian Jew, is a great evangelist, who
still wins many people to Jesus Christ.

John R. Rice, editor of the Sword of the Lord, has published hundreds of
tracts and booklets. He has led many souls to Christ and helped many
Christians grow in grace. He has not spent very much time correcting the
word of God. Where he's spent his time preaching the King James Bible,
God has tremendously blessed his work.

We have the great J. Frank Norris (some people would rather spit than
say his name). Many preachers, who knew him intimately, don't think that
he was even saved! This comes from the true dictum "familiarity breeds
contempt." The greatest of the great had their faults and holes in their
armour, and J. Frank had his; but he was a great crusader, a man who
took two churches out of their respective conventions, and a man whose
influence kept the Southern Baptist Convention preachers Premillennial
until he was dead. The Southern Baptist Convention didn't go
Postmillennial strongly until he was gone. He kept their "feet to the
fire" and stayed on their trail. (The reason why you have hundreds of
Premillennial preachers in individual Southern Baptist Churches today is
because of the ministry of J. Frank Norris, and the publication of the
Scofield Reference Bible, which J. Frank Norris strongly recommended.)
Norris had a tremendous influence in the ministry of Mordecai Ham,
Beauchamp Vick in Detroit, Harold Henniger in Canton, Ohio, and John
Rawlings in Cincinnati, Ohio. A whole group of preachers, too numerous
to list, now make up the "Bible Baptist Fellowship" and the "World
Baptist Fellowship." These are the only large groups of local churches
doing anything for God these days in a strictly Premillennial,
Scriptural way.

In this group we have Harold Henniger, in Canton, Ohio; Rev. Hodges,
formerly in Jacksonville, Florida, at the Beaver Street Baptist Church;
Rev. Bob Ingle in Jacksonville, Florida; and hundreds more across the
Gulf Coast and into Texas. J. Frank Norris probably had as great an
influence on young preachers of his day as any two evangelists who ever
lived. The Bible Baptist College and the Arlington Baptist College
(which came from a church split) were the later works of J. Frank
Norris. These works in Springfield, Missouri, and Arlington, Texas, plus
the work of Bob Jones Sr., in Greenville, South Carolina, and the work
of Lee Robertson in Chattanooga, are the hotbeds of New Testament
Christianity and fundamental evangelism today (1962). They are hated
with a purple passion by modernists, liberals, and Catholics. They
continue to put out Bible‑believing preachers, and some of these
preachers have built tremendous churches that are still true to the 1611
AV Bible and the Premillennial Coming of Jesus Christ. This is the key
to "staying on the ball" in the twentieth century. You can't go into
apostasy, as a local church, believing the AV and being Premillennial.
You may have your troubles and your problems, but there isn't a single
case in the history of the church where anybody who was Premillennial
and stuck with the AV 1611 got seriously messed up fooling around with
the "original Greek" or the "better translations" or John Calvin or
going Amillennial or Postmillennial. You can't get off base with an AV,
King James Bible, teaching the Premillennial coming of Jesus Christ. God
keeps you on the base.

You should know this period well; you were raised in it. It is the
period that saw the two greatest wars that were ever fought, World War I
and World War II. It is the period that saw the Korean War. It is the
period that saw the rise of Adolph Hitler, Lenin, Franco, Stalin, and
Castro. It is the period which saw the Roman Catholic Church get back on
its feet, and literally take over the world. By putting African
cardinals in the Vatican setup, it stirred up conflict which is going on
today in the heart of the Congo. By putting the pinchers on Viet Nam and
Laos, it had Buddhist monks burning themselves. Every dictator raised up
in this century was a Roman Catholic. We have Lenin, Trotsky, and Stalin
coming out of the Eastern Orthodox Church in Russia, rebelling against
it, but of course, raised in the Eastern Branch of the Catholic Church.
Then, we have Batiste, Trujillo, Castro, and Francisco Franco coming up
as good Roman Catholics on the other side of the water. We have Hitler
and Mussolini, two of the best Roman Catholics in Europe, turning the
world into a holocaust that cost 22 million lives. Every man named is a
Catholic, baptized and confirmed. That's Adolph Hitler, Mussolini,
Joseph Goebbels, Castro, Batiste, Trujillo, Francisco Franco, Trotsky,
Lenin, Stalin, and right on down to the last one. The only thing that
men learn from history is that men never learn from history.

While Rome prepares to take over the fundamental schools in the United
States by the text of the ASV, she also prepares to use the Federal
Government to jail anyone if he preaches the Scriptures which deal with
Rome. And while Rome prepares to take over the Treasury and Justice
Departments of the United States to get your tax money to support its
schools, the stupid, backslidden Christians sit and worry about
Communist Russia, the "Jets" and the "Colts," East and West Germany, and
try to find a better translation of the Bible! It is so tragic a man
could almost weep thinking about it!

This is the age that saw Cardinal Spellman and Dean Weigle (of Yale
Divinity School) misuse the Bible. This is the age that saw Fulton Sheen
make a clown out of himself over television, and put on a religious
horse show, unequalled by anybody outside of Oral Roberts. It saw Nels
Ferre denying the Virgin Birth of Jesus Christ, and being paid an
honorarium by Louisville Southern Baptist Seminary to come up and talk
to young ministers. It saw McConnell, Harkness, Van Deusen, Oxnam,
Grant, Buttrick, Lippman, Chappell, Jones, Sloan, Stockwell, and Kagawa
teaching salvation by works, "the Fatherhood of God and the Brotherhood
of Man," and all the religious-humanitarian trash promoted by
Rauschenbusch, George Bernard Shaw, Hemingway, Fosdick, E. G. Blake,
Steinbeck, Dahlberg of St. Louis, and all these other little one‑world,
humanitarian, communistic, socialistic, watered‑down, Bible‑rejecting,
Christ‑denying infidels, who profess to be "Christians." It saw the
rankest acts of hypocrisy, and the most terrible acts of religious
apostasy that a century has ever produced since A.D. 325.

Yet, through it all, the voice of the glad tidings sounded out! We have
Haldeman, pastor of Calvary Baptist Church, New York; William Ward Ayer,
who took over after Haldeman; Harry Ironside; Bob Schuler; Jimmie
Johnson (a great evangelist still preaching today); Fred Brown; Jesse
Henley; E. J. Daniels of Orlando, Florida (who has gone along so far
with the Convention that he can't get loose!); Cotton Mercer; Monroe
Parker; Hugh Pyle; Beauchamp Vick, formerly of Detroit (organizing and
conducting tremendous Sunday Schools and great Christian camps in the
summer with hundreds of young people getting saved and young men called
to preach). The student of history will discover the work of Homer
Ritchie out in Fort Worth, and Lester Roloff, a great evangelist, and
Wendell Zimmerman, formerly in Kansas City. There is Dawson Trotman (now
deceased), who led thousands of soldiers and sailors to the Lord during
the Second World War, through the work of "The Navigators," on board the
ships, and Harold Henniger, a great fundamental pastor formerly in
Canton, Ohio. These men, and men like them, are called "Separatists,"
"extremists," "Dispensationalists," and "Bereans." They are marked by
their faithfulness to the English Bible of the Reformation, and by their
soul‑winning zeal and their desire to propagate the preached word of God
as against the traditions of Rome, and against the liberalism of the
National Council of Churches, and against the socialistic philosophy of
the dead, dehydrated humanitarians, who have no solution for this
world's problems but a lot of academic hot air.

We have the work of General Howard early in the century; General Booth
of the Salvation Army; the Rescue Mission work of Mel Trotter, Jerry
McCauley, Ernest Reveal, and Jimmy Stroud (who was in Memphis,
Tennessee). We have the great radio ministry of the Lutheran
fundamentalist, Walter Maier. We have the work of A. T. Pierson, Simpson
of the Christian Missionary Alliance, Bishop Cannon, and J. R. Stratton,
who were the great old‑time, fundamental Methodist bishops in the early
part of the twentieth century. We have the work of Wilbur Smith, Harry
Rimmer, and Arthur W. Pink, who wrote great books about the Bible, along
with Stam and Bullinger. We have the work of Joe Henry Hankins, Jack
Hyles, and Billy Graham (who still holds great United Campaigns).
Although Billy Graham lets modernists come into these campaigns and is
guilty of compromise in many directions, the Lord is still using him,
and he still preaches, to a great extent, the Authorized Version and has
"decisions for Christ."

We have Merv Rosell, an evangelist. We have Anthony Zachello, W.
Montano, and Carrera, converted Roman Catholics who write books and
preach. (When Rome "lowers the ax," they are going to get rid of
Zachello and Carrera as quickly as they can, along with Paul Blanshard,
Avro Manhattan, and some of the other boys, who have been "behind the
curtain" and come back out again!) We have Eddie Liebberman, a converted
Jewish evangelist, Noel Smith, who writes with a very caustic pen---a
man very good at analyzing world trends in relation to the Premillennial
Coming of Christ. We have also the work of the Gideons, the Christian
Businessmen's Association, the Pocket Testament League, Wycliffe Bible
Translators, Baptist Mid‑Missions, and many other missionary groups
still preaching the word of God.

In the midst of all the apostasy of the Laodicean period---the National
Council of Churches, the Ecumenical Council, the socialization of the
government, the dirty work of the NAACP, the anti‑Biblical,
non‑Christian, pro‑Darwinian decisions put out by the Supreme Court, and
all the other carnal foolishness, one can still find men of God holding
forth the Word of Life. There is Carl McIntire, still attacking
modernism and liberalism, and consequently hated by the National Council
of Churches; D. G. Barnhouse (now deceased), who preached for many
years; McGinley (now deceased), a great Scotch evangelist; Ockenga of
Boston, a man who preached the truth and led many souls to Christ. We
have Lee Roberson of Chattanooga, who is training many young men for the
ministry.

There is Keith Brooks and E. L. Maxwell---a great devotional writer. Up
north is Dallas Billington, who has a tremendous work in Akron, Ohio. (I
must stop here because there are so many more, and there isn't time to
go into the details in the lives of all these men, the things they have
done, and how God has used them. Neither is there time nor space to tell
the wonderful story of their faults, trials, tribulations, testings,
weaknesses, failures, successes, and victories.)

As the Church Age closes (1990), it closes with the church (as a whole)
lukewarm, and concerned only about "civil rights," the "social class
problems" of the age, and the newspaper problems of a press that has
gone Roman Catholic. It closes with the Body of Christ heading into
apostasy. When I say "apostasy," I mean born‑again, saved people are
rejecting the word of God and turning to civil rights for a gospel; and
I mean just what I said, the way I said it. But even as the Body of
Christ turns from the word of God given to it and goes into apostasy,
there are still a few voices crying out, like John in the wilderness,
"Repent, prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
Behold, he cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see him"!

In the close of this twentieth century, we know the path that lies
ahead; we don't have any illusions about it. If someone is deceived
about the future, a Bible‑believer is not the least bit. I don't say
that boastfully. (It might sound that way to some of you Amillennial,
Postmillennial, little liberals that haven't been in your Bibles enough
to even know what I'm talking about!) But as a born‑again, saved,
Bible‑preacher, going into the last half of the twentieth century, I
have no illusions about the future. The real Bible‑believer is in a
lukewarm church that is neither hot nor cold; a church that brags about
its material goods and is actually wretched, miserable, poor, blind,
naked, in the sight of God; and the Bible‑believer in it represents the
most staggering minority in church history since A.D. 33.

As a man called of God to preach, I just pray that some of my faith
that's been tried in the fire "might be found unto praise and honour and
glory at the appearing of Jesus Christ." I pray too that I will have the
white raiment which He counselled this church to buy, and that He will
anoint my eyes with salve, so that as this last church goes blindly back
to Rome, I'll have my eyes open and turn my back on that great old
religious whore of Revelation 17, and face the rising sun "in His
strength"---my Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ (Mal. 4:2).

I have no prayer in my heart that is any more weighty or important, at
the time of this writing, than that God will grant me in the last years
of my life, the time, grace, ability, and physical strength to stand and
boldly declare to my dying breath, or until the Lord comes, "Thus saith
the Lord" from a King James, 1611, Authorized, Christian‑loved,
Pope‑hated, God‑honored, Scholar‑despised, Reformation Bible, which I
received by Divine appointment from the Philadelphia Church Period---the
greatest period the church has ever seen, or will ever see.

CHAPTER 4

We have reached the end of the Church Age, typified by the seven
messages to the seven churches in Asia Minor. Although these messages
apply historically to seven local churches in A.D. 90, and doctrinally
to local churches in the Tribulation, they represent, in type, the
history of the Church Age, which can be seen by a comparison of the
first church (the church at Ephesus) with the last church (the church at
Laodicea). The word "church" disappears in Revelation 3:22 and is not
mentioned again from Chapters 4--21. There is something else, too. The
Holy Spirit is spoken of as being in the midst of the churches, on earth
with the churches in Revelation 2 and 3. Yet, the Spirit (spoken of as
seven Spirits) is found up in the third heaven in Revelation 4:5,
indicating there has been both a change in the churches' position and in
the position of the Spirit.

4:1 "After this I looked, and, behold, a door was opened in heaven: and
the first voice which I heard was as it were of a trumpet talking with
me; which said, Come up hither, and I will shew thee things which must
be hereafter."

A door opens in heaven. Heaven only opens two times in the Book of
Revelation---once when somebody goes up, and in Revelation 19 where
somebody comes down. Notice that when the door is opened (Rev. 4:1),
there is a voice and a trumpet. If you will compare this passage with 1
Thessalonians 4:15--18, there will be no doubt in your mind as to whose
the voice is or what the trumpet is. This is the Lord calling somebody
(in both passages), and they respond by ascending through the clouds.

4:2 "And immediately I was in the spirit: and, behold, a throne was set
in heaven, and one sat on the throne."

"I was in the spirit." Notice the expression again. "In the spirit," as
in Revelation 1:10. This is not in the sense of a person being in the
Holy Spirit, as a born‑again believer is in Christ. This is being "in
the spirit," in the sense of a remarkable, miraculous transformation of
a shape or structure into another dimension. (Compare this with 1
Corinthians 15:49--52, and you will see that it matches the "Rapture,"
or the "catching out" of the Body of Christ. Paul says that this mystery
will take place "in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye," at the
"sounding of a trumpet." We are told in 1 Thessalonians 4 that it takes
place "with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God." Here
in Revelation 4:1--2 we have all the elements present---a voice, a
trumpet, a calling up, a change into a spiritual condition, "in a
moment.")

John is transferred immediately up into the third heaven. I say "third
heaven" because 2 Corinthians 12:1--4 clearly distinguishes three
heavens. (The "seven heavens" of Milton are Roman Catholic, and have
nothing to do with Bible revelation.) The Bible delineates three
heavens.

The first heaven is located from the ground to the clouds, as in the
statement, "He cometh in the clouds of heaven."

The second heaven is located up where the sun, moon, and stars are, as
"the sun, moon, and stars are in the heaven."

The third heaven is located beyond the constellations, nebulae,
galaxies, and star clusters, into the presence of God Himself, which, by
the way, is north, as we shall learn later.

If there is any doubt as to whether or not Revelation 4:1--2 is a
picture of the Rapture of the Church, let the reader observe that John,
who writes here of himself and his own experience, turns out to be a
type of the Body of Jesus Christ! If there is any doubt in your mind,
note the following references and study them carefully.

Notice, first of all, in Ephesians 5, the Lord Jesus Christ's body, "the
Church," bears the same relationship to Him that a wife bears to her
husband. Notice further, that the original relationship of wife to
husband is the relation of Eve to Adam (Gen. 2). Therefore, Eve, and a
man's wife, stand in relation to their husbands as the Body of Christ,
the Church, stands in relation to Christ. Now, we need to grasp this
truth for several reasons. When Adam saved his wife, he had to die to
save her. Christ had to die to save the Church. Adam's wife was part of
his body; Christ's wife is part of His body---"bone of His bone, flesh
of His flesh" (Eph. 5:28--33). Adam's bride was taken from below his
heart, and his side was pierced so that a rib could be extracted. Jesus
Christ was crucified, and was pierced below the fifth rib, when He died
on the cross for sinners. The Bible said, "Christ also loved the church,
and gave himself for it" (Eph. 5:25). Adam had to die, or sleep the
sleep of death, in order to get his wife; so Christ dies (and is in the
ground three days and three nights) to get His wife. Adam's wife is
"bone of his bone, and flesh of his flesh." She is his body. Christ's
wife is "bone of His bone, and flesh of His flesh." She is His body.
Adam's bride is obtained by the shedding of blood. Christ's bride is
obtained by the shedding of blood.

Now, all of these things point indirectly to John the Apostle. The Lord
Jesus Christ loved John in a special way that He didn't love the others,
and John knew it. Now, John 3:16 tells us that God loved everybody, but
the Bible points out that He "loved the church, and gave himself for
it"---a special kind of love. The same is true of John. Although Jesus
loved all the disciples, and "loved them to the end" (John 13), John is
loved in a special way---notice John 21:20. This "disciple whom Jesus
loved" is John. Notice John 13:23, 25. John is lying right on the Lord's
side, below his chest, exactly where Eve, the type of the bride of
Christ (the church), came from, below the heart. To repeat: John is
loved in a special way and knows it, as the church is loved in a special
way. John is lying, at the Lord's Supper, right where the church is
going to come from---the heart.

That isn't all. There is a statement about the future of the Apostle
John which clearly indicates that he is a type of the Body of Christ.
Notice John 21:21--24. John the Apostle is told that he will "tarry"
until the Second Coming of Christ. This can only be true in the sense
that John was alive in Revelation 1, when God caught him up in the
Spirit and dragged him clean through nineteen hundred years of time, set
him down in the Tribulation, and showed him the Second Coming of Christ.
John did die, but he did see the Second Coming of Jesus Christ before he
died. This leads the Amillennial and Postmillennial expositors to think
that Christ came back before John died! Of course, this is a booby trap.
John, in A.D. 90, is caught forward "in the Spirit" to the "day of the
Lord," and sees the Second Coming of the Lord, and writes about it in
the Book of Revelation. So John is plainly a type of the Body of Christ,
the bride of Christ, the Church. The word "John" means "beloved." So
when John is caught up in Revelation 4:1--2, he pictures the catching up
of the Church, the Body of Christ.

There is something else; in Revelation 4:1 we have the expression, "Come
up hither." This expression occurs three times, and three times only, in
the word of God. These three times are important to notice, as these
three times signal a "catching out," or a "catching up," of saints in
various dispensations. The first reference is here---Revelation 4:1. The
second reference is Revelation 11:12. The third reference is in Proverbs
25:7.

Now, lest we get too complicated, too deep, or too "far out," return
again to your simple diagram that I asked you to draw at the beginning
of this series of studies. Now, this expression occurs three times in
your Bible---"Come up hither." It occurs one time at a "catching up" of
Old Testament saints. This takes place at the two‑inch mark, at the
resurrection of Christ. It occurs again at the "catching up" of New
Testament saints. This occurs at the eight‑inch mark, the end of the
Church Age. It occurs the third time to represent the "catching up" of
Tribulation saints. This occurs at the ten‑inch mark, simultaneously
with the Advent of Jesus Christ. (Draw a line, going up, alongside the
crucifixion of Christ. This will represent a Rapture of Old Testament
saints \[Prov. 25:7\]. Draw a line going up at the eight‑inch mark---the
Rapture of the New Testament Church \[Rev. 4:1,2\]. Then draw a line
going up just before the ten‑inch mark, going up to meet the Lord coming
down---the Rapture of Tribulation saints \[Rev. 11:12--15\].) This is
one of the great, hidden mysteries in the word of God, found only in the
writings of Pember and Larkin. Scofield didn't quite catch it, I don't
believe, nor did Gaebelein, Pettingill, Ironsides, or many of the other
great Christian expositors.

The long and short of it is that the Bible teaches three raptures:

1\. The Rapture of the Old Testament saints at the resurrection of
Christ (Matt. 27:50--54; Eph. 4:8--12).

2\. The Rapture of New Testament saints of the Church Age (1 Cor.
15:49--53; 1 Thess. 4:13--18).

3\. The Rapture of Tribulation saints at the end of the Tribulation
(Matt. 24; Rev. 11).

This means that "the first resurrection" is the first resurrection of
saved people, and has three parts to it. This is justly so, as all
harvests have three parts. To illustrate this resurrection, Paul likens
it to a crop that you would plant in the ground. Notice that in the
great chapter on resurrection, 1 Corinthians 15, he clearly indicates
the Resurrection has three parts to it. Notice, in 1 Corinthians
15:22--24, that the first resurrection has three parts to it, just like
a crop has three parts to it. Every crop has:

1\. Firstfruits---the first part which you pick when it gets ripe.

2\. Harvest---the part that ripens later on.

3\. Gleanings---the part that ripens at the end.

First Corinthians 15:22 says, "For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ
shall all be made alive." And further in verse 23, "But every man in his
own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ's at
his coming." (You go out and pick a few of the first fruits of the crop
which gets ripe, to start with; then there's the harvest---New Testament
saints.)

First Corinthians 15:24, "Then cometh the end" (after you harvest you
always have some gleanings left), "when he shall have delivered up the
kingdom to God, even the Father; when he shall have put down all rule
and all authority and power."

First Corinthians 15:25--26, "For he must reign" (Millennium), "till he
hath put all enemies under his feet. The last enemy that shall be
destroyed is death" (Rev. 20---at the end of the Millennium).

The first resurrection, then, has three parts. This is why John does not
say in Revelation 20 that "this is the first resurrection," until all
three parts are over. The first part is Old Testament saints; the second
part is New Testament saints; the third part is Tribulation saints.
Therefore, there are three "catching ups":

1\. "Firstfruits"---Old Testament saints at the resurrection of Christ.

2\. "Harvest"---New Testament saints at the Rapture of the church at the
end of the Church Age.

3\. "Gleanings"---Tribulation saints at the Second Advent.

All three parts of this resurrection are mentioned in Deuteronomy 16:16,
and they are marked by three Jewish feasts, given to the Jews. (I'll
stop here, as we are getting dangerously close to trying to date the
Rapture, which, of course, I'll not do. But, the three raptures are
mentioned there, and they are marked by the feasts which God gave to
Israel.)

Now, returning to our text in Revelation 4:1--2, we plainly have a
picture of the resurrection, and the Rapture of the body of New
Testament saints, the church, in the resurrection of John, the beloved
(a type of the Body of Christ). He is "caught up," and he observes the
events that take place in the second part of the Book of
Revelation---the events that will describe the Tribulation. Therefore,
you can be absolutely certain that any event found in Revelation 4--19
occurs in the great Tribulation. Events that occur after Revelation 4
will not be found in the Church Age. This is important because
Seventh-day Adventists, Mormons, Roman Catholics, and many others have
the habit of taking passages found between Revelation 4 and Revelation
22 and making them apply to the Christians in the Church Age. Of course,
this is a case of "wrongly dividing" the word of truth. You are told to
"Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to
be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth" (2 Tim. 2:15). This is
wrong division. The Book of Revelation is clearly divided into three
parts, and we are now in the second part, beginning with Chapter 4 and
ending with Chapter 19.

"And immediately I was in the spirit: and, behold, a throne was set in
heaven, and one sat on the throne." This, of course, takes John up into
the third heaven. (Here he writes about the things that Paul was
forbidden to write about in 2 Corinthians 12:1--4.)

4:3 "And he that sat was to look upon like a jasper and a sardine stone:
and there was a rainbow round about the throne, in sight like unto an
emerald.

4 And round about the throne were four and twenty seats: and upon the
seats I saw four and twenty elders sitting, clothed in white raiment;
and they had on their heads crowns of gold."

The "crowns" can be connected with the crowns of rewards. The white
raiment can be associated with the Judgment Seat of Christ and the
marriage of the Lamb, in Revelation 19, where we are told that "fine
linen is the righteousness of saints." If this is so, the twenty‑four
elders represent saved people from the Church Age, who are saved,
"washed in the blood," and have earned rewards. However, these
twenty‑four elders are almost impossible to identify. Some people say
that they represent the Old Testament saints; some people say that they
represent the "Twelve Patriarchs" and the "Twelve Apostles." But when
they occur again in Chapter 5, they are saying that God has "redeemed
them by His blood out of every kindred, and tongue, and people, and
nation," implying that they are representative of saints, saved in this
age. Of course, they would be up in heaven in Chapter 4 because the
Church Age ended in Chapter 3. Why there are twenty‑four of them is
impossible to tell. Twenty‑four is the Jewish number (12) doubled. It
may mean "Jew" and "Gentile" in Christ. It may be typical of the Twelve
Apostles that began the Body of Christ, and the Gentiles who were saved
later. The references that may help you to identify them are Psalm
107:32;  
1 Chronicles 24:1; 1 Peter 5:1; Exodus 3:18; Hebrews 11:2. They are
undoubtedly men of some kind, as they appear to be in Revelation
4:9--10. (Although we are not able to identify every detail of the
things we are reading about, we do know that they are so. As I have said
before, it is not hard to understand the Book of Revelation, it is hard
to believe it.)

So John is caught up to the third heaven; he sees the throne with the
Lord on it, and it is surrounded by twenty‑four elders on thrones, and
four beasts (cherubim) around the throne. It's a wild scene, but it is
so. The problem, of course, is to believe it, and receive it.

Before going on and finishing Revelation 4, we should understand that
John was "caught up" to a real, literal place in the universe, and that
place is up there now. The Scripture has much to say about this
"dwelling place" of God, its location, distance, size, nature, and
inhabitants; and it is very clearly defined. Since the revelation on
this place is far in advance over the latest scientific findings of Cape
Kennedy or Redstone Arsenal at Huntsville, let us study the verses which
relate to it; it is the abode of "God the Father" in the third heaven,
on the throne, above the sea of glass, northward.

Now, these verses that we are about to pick up reveal something that is
unknown to "modern science," so these verses will come as a shock to
many people reading this. But these verses, as they stand, where we find
them, in the context, plainly teach that the essence of God is located
in a direction in the universe, and that direction and place are clearly
given and described. (The concept that God is "spirit," or shapeless,
formless, and bodiless, of course, is the absolute concept of God's
essence. The Bible says, "God is a spirit," and as a spirit God cannot
be located in any one particular place. But God, being God, is able to
manifest Himself in different places, at different times, in different
ways. (The Liberal carries this idea to the extreme and says that God is
as much manifested in the writings of Shakespeare or the music of
Beethoven or the sayings of Confucius or the meditations of Buddha, as
He is in the revelations given through the Paulie Epistles. Of course,
this is incorrect. The orthodox Jew, and some other groups, bend over
backwards to say that since "God is a spirit" and has no form and has no
shape, that He never manifests Himself in any form, in any visible
shape, in any way, but is always a vague, neuter essence---a nonentity
that kind of "fills the universe" in a sort of naturalistic, pantheistic
way. Neither of these concepts are Biblical, Christian revelations,
given by God Himself. God's revelation of Himself in the New Testament
is a Father, Son, and Holy Spirit---a Trinity, able to manifest Himself
in thousands of ways, but choosing to manifest Himself in certain ways,
and these ways are revealed in the Holy Bible.)

Although God is everywhere---David said, "If I ascend up into heaven,
thou art there: if I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there," God
is not indwelling the body of an unsaved man in the sense that He
