indwells the body of a saved man. Again, the Holy Spirit did not indwell
the bodies of Old Testament saints in the sense that He now indwells New
Testament saints. Again, the Holy Spirit is not on this earth in the
Tribulation in the sense that He is on this earth in the Church Age. To
"get the sense," you must be born again (1 Cor. 2:14).

So, when the Bible speaks about the location of God in the third heaven,
it is talking about the "theophany," the appearances of God in bodily
shape, and the center (or location) of God's operation in the universe,
as He is in the manifestation of His glory. Of course, God is manifest
in other senses, but this is the sense of His absolute glory, above and
outside the material creation. Here are the references.

Job 26:5--13---In this passage, notice a number of things. Notice that
"dead things are under the water." The things that are "under the water"
are connected with hell. The area that is over the water is called "the
north" and "the empty place." Over this north, or "empty place" is a
solid covering that keeps a human being from seeing "the throne of God."
(Remember that John was caught up to the throne.) This location is
beyond the solar system, where "day and night have an end" (Job 26:10).
Notice too that the "water" mentioned between this throne and hell below
it is divided (vs. 12). In this water is Satan (vs. 13).

The next passage is Job 37:9--10. Observe the connection of the
direction "north" with God. "Cold out of the north. By the breath of God
frost is given: and the breadth of the waters is straitened." Notice,
again, the connection of "the waters" with north, and the "throne of
God" in connection with north. Next, in Job 37:18, we are told that the
sky is a solid object---strong like a "molten looking glass." That is,
it is like frozen crystal or ice---molten, poured, or as a frozen
looking glass. It is not transparent, but reflects back. This cannot be
the sky over your head, because you can see right through the sky over
your head. This sky (Job 37:18) is a good deal higher!

Read, in Job 37:22, the connection of north with God again. (The reason
why we should study the quotations from the Book of Job is that the Book
of Job is the oldest book in the Bible. The events recorded in it are
not as old as the events recorded in Genesis, but the date of the
writing of the Book of Job precedes the date of the writing of the Book
of Genesis. Consequently, if you want information on the formation,
foundation, and the structure of the universe, you do not go primarily
to Gen. 1, you go to Job.)

Notice Job 38:30. The waters are said to be "hid" where you can't see
them. (You can see the ones in the Atlantic and Pacific!) "The face of
the deep is frozen." This is not the face of some lake in Palestine, nor
is it Lake Michigan. This is the face of the D‑E‑E‑P. It is frozen. The
"face of the deep," then, is like a strong molten looking glass, and it
is frozen, and it is north. If there is any doubt in your mind about
what the "face of the deep" is, go to Genesis 1. In Genesis 1:2, we
learn that the "face of the deep" is not the Atlantic or the Pacific
Oceans, or the Baltic or the Black or the Dead Seas.

Genesis 1:2 says, "And the earth was without form, and void; and
darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon
the face of the waters."

What follows is the re-creation of the universe. Now, notice in Genesis
1:6--9 that in the re‑creation of the universe, it is composed of an
open, hammered‑out, flat space called "a firmament." Above this
firmament is a body of water, and below this firmament is a body of
water. The Bible (being much more accurate than twentieth-century
physicists or Russian and American atomic and nuclear scientists) is
very careful to tell you in Genesis 1 that this earth and heaven is not
"the ancient Jewish traditional concept of a flat turtle with a world on
his back" (or an "elephant"). This universe is constructed in a flat
open space, which contains the stars, sun, moon, galaxies,
constellations, nebulae, and star clusters; and it is called "heaven"
(Gen. 1:14--17). Above this heaven is water (vs. 7), and below this
heaven is water. There are waters above and below the second heaven.

Turn now to Psalm 148. Here, the Holy Spirit tells us that the waters
above the heaven (whose top going northward is frozen) are not the
clouds. The Scofield Reference Bible tells you that these waters above
the firmament are "clouds," or the "vapor." This is a gross error that
comes---as all errors do---from changing the plain English and trying to
prove something that is in line with science. (If you ever get in the
place where you begin to switch the Bible around to meet the demands of
"scientific discoveries," you will quickly forsake the "faith once
delivered to the saints." The Bible never has to be twisted to meet the
demands of modern science. "Science" must always alter its statements to
meet the demands of a 1611 AV. The Bible tells us the earth is round,
that it hangs upon nothing \[Isa. 40:22\], that day and night can be in
the earth at the same time \[Luke 17:31, 34\], and so many other
scientific statements that I don't have room to list them all.) Wherever
you find "modern science" stating one thing and the 1611 AV, Protestant,
Reformation Bible stating the opposite, you can bet your last dollar on
the bottom of the heap that science is always, absolutely, 100 percent
dead wrong every time. In the Louvre in Paris, there are five and
one-half miles of shelves of books whose "scientific facts" are now
considered obsolete! Don't worry about changing the Bible to meet the
demands of "modern science."

Psalm 148:1---"Praise ye the Lord. Praise ye the Lord from the heavens
(notice the plural---three heavens, 2 Cor. 12:1--4): praise him in the
heights." The heights of what? It is not the "heights" of a mountain,
for the context is speaking of something a good deal higher than Mt.
Everest.

Psalm 148:4---"Praise him, ye heavens of heavens, and ye waters (plural)
that be above the heavens" (plural).

Psalm 148:5---"Let them praise the name of the Lord: for he commanded,
and they were created." Notice the context is the creation---Genesis 1.
God made a firmament between the two bodies of water.

Psalm 148:6---"He hath also stablished them for ever and ever: he hath
made a decree which shall not pass." In plainer words, these waters
don't move out of place. They are held in suspension above the universe,
and below the universe, by God's decree on atomic structure, nuclear
fission, and sustaining power (Heb. 1:2--4).

Psalm 148:7---"Praise the Lord from the earth, ye dragons, and all
deeps:" (Did you notice how the Scriptures come down to earth from the
universe? Now you are down to the ground, but in vss. 1--6 you were
clean over your head!)

Psalm 148:8---"Fire, and hail; snow, and vapours; stormy wind fulfilling
his word." Then the "vapors" are on this earth, and they are not the
"waters above the firmament" spoken of in Genesis 1:4--6! The waters
above the heavens are not vapors and clouds. Vapors and clouds are on
this earth, and the "waters" are above the second heaven, which means
that the "face of the deep" is frozen---going northward. The temperature
up there is absolute zero, going up. There is water over your head that
would make the Pacific and Atlantic look like a spittoon! If you are a
Christian, someday you will be caught up to heaven; you will be caught
up through this celestial body of water exactly as the children of
Israel came out of the land of Egypt and passed through the Red Sea, or
as Joshua went through the Jordan River, coming into the promised land.
The temperature of this third heaven is "absolute zero," where all
molecular action ceases, and hence, all passage of time ceases.

Above that place in heaven, going northward, is eternity. If you
travelled northward at 150,000 miles a second, it would possibly take
you a good 500 years to get up there.

Isaiah 57:15---"For thus saith the high and lofty One that inhabiteth
eternity, whose name is Holy; I dwell in the high and holy place."
(Brother, it's high! That place is up!) Now, turn to the Scriptures
which deal with this empty place over the north.

Isaiah 14:12---"How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer, son of the
morning! how art thou cut down to the ground, which didst weaken the
nations!"

Isaiah 14:13---"For thou hast said in thine heart, I will ascend into
heaven, I will exalt my throne above the stars of God: I will sit also
upon the mount of the congregation, in the sides of the NORTH."

Isaiah 14:14---"I will ascend above the heights of the clouds; I will be
like the most High."

The passage plainly teaches that God's abode is straight north, over the
empty place, through the firmament, out through the waters, up through
the door, into the throne room, over the "empty place over the north;"
and this direction is north. It is indicated by the Polar Star. God's
one fixed direction in this universe is straight north.

Psalm 48:2---"Beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth, is
mount Zion, on the SIDES OF THE NORTH, the city of the great King." If
there is any doubt as to whether this is heavenly Jerusalem or earthly
Jerusalem, look at the expression "the sides of the north." (Compare
this with Isa. 14:13.) "I will ascend into heaven, I will exalt my
throne above the stars of God: I will sit also upon the mount of the
congregation, in the sides of the north." Now, Satan has a great deal
more sense than Von Braum or Einstein or H. G. Wells or any of these
other limited human beings, like ourselves. Satan would never say that
he was going to ascend and put his throne above the throne of God at
Jerusalem in Palestine! When Satan used the expression "sides of the
north" above the heights of the stars, he was talking about taking a
trip somewhere, and he was going a good bit beyond where man will send a
rocket before Christ comes back. This is the expression in Psalm
48:2---"the sides of the north, the city of the great King."

Now, as if to wrap it up and make it infallibly plain, the Holy Spirit
has made a substitution in Psalm 75:6--7 where He lists four directions,
and then, for a touch of humor, He leaves out one direction and puts the
word "God" in as a substitute for the direction!

Psalm 75:6---"For promotion cometh neither from the east, nor from the
west, nor from the south." What you should expect next would be "but
from the north." But the Holy Spirit knocks out one direction and makes
this substitution: "But God is the judge: he putteth down one, and
setteth up another" (Psa. 75:7). Now, this is no accident. "God" has
been substituted for "north."

(If someone is reading this who thinks that this type of exposition is
fanatical or being "too literal" with the word of God or "going off the
deep end," then let it be called to his attention that the facts of
history contradict any man who takes any other position. In case you
think the Bible is stretching a point, don't forget that Egypt was
conquered by Babylon, north of Egypt; Babylon was conquered by Persia,
north of Babylon; Persia was conquered by Greece, north of Persia;
Greece was conquered by Rome, north of Greece; Rome was conquered by the
Huns, Visigoths, and Vandals in Germany, north of Rome; Germany has been
beaten in two World Wars by England, north of Germany. A man who doesn't
believe that the direction of God and glory is north is a man who cannot
read history or a compass. You know who won the Civil War! Don't act so
innocent about it.)

From these passages we have learned a number of facts. We've learned
that although God is everywhere in the sense of Spirit, even His Holy
Spirit is not manifest everywhere in the same sense. For example, the
Holy Spirit is in hell manifesting God's judgment. The Holy Spirit deals
with a sinner in the sense of convicting him of sin. The Holy Spirit
indwells the heart of the believer, forming in him the Holy Ghost---the
bodily shape of Jesus Christ (Gal. 4:19). Yet, God in His glory (and
manifestation of power from His center of operation), is "on the throne"
in the throne room, located over your head (going north), at several
million light years, up into the firmament, out through the water, in
through a frozen sea of glass, which is like a molten looking glass. On
that throne, between four supernatural beings called "cherubim,"
surrounded by twenty‑four elders, is God, the Father, and our Lord Jesus
Christ, sitting at the right hand of the Father in the heavens. That is,
the Bible literally means what it says, exactly as it says it, where it
says it. When we Christians say that we believe that Jesus Christ died,
was buried, and rose from the dead, and that He is now "seated at the
right hand of the Father," do you know what we mean? We mean that He
died, was buried, and rose from the dead, and He is seated at the right
hand of the Father. We Bible‑believers know where we are going, and we
know how to get there (John 14:1--6).

4:5 "And out of the throne proceeded lightnings and thunderings and
voices: and there were seven lamps of fire burning before the throne,
which are the seven Spirits of God."

For the "seven Spirits," see Isaiah 11:1--3.

4:6 "And before the throne there was a sea of glass like unto crystal:
and in the midst of the throne, and round about the throne, were four
beasts full of eyes before and behind."

To repeat, John is up in the third heaven describing what he sees. This
throne is in the third heaven, on a sea of glass, like a molten looking
glass, frozen at absolute zero, above the waters, on the frozen face of
the deep.

"Sea of glass." Remember that Job said the sky is like a molten looking
glass? Remember that Paul said, "Now we see through a glass, darkly; but
then face to face" (1 Cor. 13:12)? This sea was "like unto
crystal"---like dry ice. When John sees the throne, it is transparent;
so, he sees the beasts right through the throne, and around the throne.

Now, in the commentaries these beasts have been made to represent
various things. I've heard them likened to the four gospels. I've heard
them likened to Judah on the east, Ephraim on the west, Reuben on the
south, and Dan on the north. But, there is no reason for not believing
that these beasts are literal beasts. These beasts are found as literal
beings in Ezekiel 1 and 10, where they are called "cherubim." They are
found in Genesis 3:24. Supernatural beasts like them, called "seraphim,"
are found in Isaiah 6. There is no reason for not believing they are
supernatural beasts (real beasts as we find them) when we remember that
replicas, or remnants, of these supernatural beasts are found in all the
Assyrian, Babylonian, Egyptian, and Hittite monuments. Greek mythology
and Chinese mythology all have their centaurs and satyrs and ogres.
People who worship demons have always had this same thing in mind---a
half‑man and half‑beast. There isn't any reason to believe that there
isn't a basis for this superstitious worship. Anybody who watches "The
Twilight Zone" and "The Outer Limits" ought to realize by now that there
is always a basis of truth for a lie.

These beasts are described in Ezekiel 1 and Ezekiel 10, and they are
called "cherubim." There are four of them present, as in Ezekiel. The
amazing thing about this passage is that when we study the Bible, we
find that there were five of them! When Ezekiel sees them in Ezekiel 1,
there are four, and when John sees them in Revelation 4 there are four.
But if you will turn to Ezekiel 28:14, you will find that there were
five. There is one Cherub missing from the four in Revelation 4. The one
that is missing is the Cherub "over the throne." In Revelation 4 they
are round about the throne and in the throne. But, in Ezekiel 28, you
read of one Cherub being "over the throne." (We are going by exactly
what it says, as it says it. When a man doesn't see these things, it is
because he doesn't believe what he is reading is the true word.) Now,
look at Ezekiel 28:14.

"Thou art the anointed cherub that covereth." None of the other four
cherubim are said to be "anointed." If you will read Ezekiel 1 and 10,
you will find that this is the only one that is said to be anointed.
This is very important because the word in Hebrew for "anointed" is
"messiah," and the word for it in the New Testament is "christ." This
cherub is a "christ"! He's not the Lord Jesus Christ. He's not the holy,
anointed One of God---the Lord Jesus Christ, but he is a Christ. (This
is very important because later on this cherub appears as the
Antichrist.)

"That covereth." This cherub was over something which was underneath
him. Look at Ezekiel 28:16.

"By the multitude of thy merchandise they have filled the midst of thee
with violence, and thou hast sinned: therefore I will cast thee as
profane out of the mountain of God: and I will destroy thee, O covering
cherub, from the midst of the stones of fire."

Notice, this is said of no other cherub. There is the "sides of the
north"---the heavenly Mt. Zion, spoken of in Hebrews 12. This is the
"covering" cherub.

This cherub (Ezek. 28) is so special that in verse 12 he is said to seal
"up the sum, full of wisdom, and perfect in beauty." In verse 13, he is
said to have been in the Garden in Eden. In verse 14, he is said to be
"anointed," and in verse 15, he is said to be the originator of
"iniquity!" There is no doubt about who he is; he is the "Lucifer"
referred to in Isaiah 14:10--13, of whom we have just read.

Then there were five cherubim, which means that one cherub has been
"kicked out"; he is no longer over the throne. The reason this is so
significant is because of the nature of the fifth Cherub, and the nature
of cherubim themselves. (Cherubim are drawn on Valentine cards with
little baby faces and wings on their backs, and little bows and arrows,
but these are not the cherubim of the word of God.) Turn to the Book of
Revelation and look at the description carefully.

4:7 "And the first beast was like a lion, and the second beast like a
calf, and the third beast had a face as a man, and the fourth beast was
like a flying eagle."

Then these four beasts have four different faces.

4:8 "And the four beasts had each of them six wings about him; and they
were full of eyes within: and they rest not day and night, saying, Holy,
holy, holy, Lord God Almighty, which was, and is, and is to come."

These four beasts, in their stationary position, have six wings for each
cherub and no "wheels." (The cherubim in Ezek. 1 had "wheels," and they
were moving and travelling.) The cherubim in Revelation 4 are stationary
around the throne. Look carefully at the different faces---lion, calf,
man, and eagle. Go back now to Ezekiel 1 and pick up these cherubim with
a slightly different description. (You understand that when Ezekiel sees
them that he sees them a long time before the birth of Christ \[around
588 B.C.\], and when John sees them, it is A.D. 90.)

Ezekiel 1:5 says, "Also out of the midst thereof came the likeness of
four living creatures. And this was their appearance; they had the
likeness of a man."

Then a cherub looks like a man when you see him. That is, he has a face,
head, shoulders, arms, stomach, legs, back, chest, neck, feet, etc.
(Hence, the Devil, who is said to be the "anointed cherub," is often
drawn as a man. Hence, he appears as "an angel of light." An angel, of
course, in the Bible is a man (see The Bible Believer's Commentary on
Genesis, 1970, Gen. 6:1--6).

Ezekiel 1:6, "And every one had four faces, and every one had four
wings."

This is a little different description. It can mean that each one had
all four faces. (The chances are that when he says "every one had four
faces," he means that every one of the four had four faces.)

Ezekiel 1:7, "And their feet were straight feet; and the sole of their
feet was like the sole of a calf's foot: and they sparkled like the
colour of burnished brass."

Although all appear as men, and although all have wings, and although
four different faces are present, please notice that all four have the
feet of a calf. In plainer words, all have a "split foot." (Hence, the
Devil, the anointed Cherub, is often referred to by ignorant people as
"old split foot.") Notice, too, that a calf walks on its toes, as do all
animals. The bottom part of an animal's foot, the hoof, is actually a
toenail. All animals walk on their toes. This is why a horse can run
like lightning. The part of the horse that is his foot is the part from
the ground to the first joint, which we would call a leg. This means
that these beasts walk on tiptoe, as a calf. Strangely,
twentieth-century women, who like to slenderize their bodies and give
their bodies a sleek, smooth appearance, nearly always put their legs up
on three‑inch heels, walking on the toes!

Ezekiel 1:8, "And they had the hands of a man under their wings on their
four sides; and they four had their faces and their wings."

Ezekiel 1:10, "As for the likeness of their faces, they four had the
face of a man, and the face of a lion, on the right side: and they four
had the face of an ox on the left side; they four also had the face of
an eagle." This passage seems to teach that each cherub had all four
faces---see Ezekiel 1:6 and the comments.

All four of these cherubim are said to have the combination of four
faces, and each cherub, then, would have one face for each direction. If
this were true each would have eight eyes. John says "eyes before and
behind."

Ezekiel 1:18, "As for their rings, they were so high that they were
dreadful; and their rings were full of eyes round about them four." The
description is similar to a flying saucer with portholes all around the
upper disc.

These creatures, each one of them, can see in four different directions
at one time. The eagle looks one way, the lion looks another, the man
looks the other, and the ox looks the other. They are said to have four
wings, and "wheels" (Ezek. 1:15--23). Notice the "frozen sea" over their
heads (vs. 22) is as the color of the "terrible crystal"---ice. These
beings are slightly different from those in Revelation 4, but different
only because of their situation. In Ezekiel 1, they are flying, carrying
the throne (vs. 26). In Revelation 4, they are stationary in the third
heaven, by the throne.

Now, remembering that the faces are ox, man, lion, and eagle, turn to
Ezekiel 10:14, and you will discover that the fifth cherub had only one
face. (The reason why the AV 1611 Bible has changed the word here is so
that you can identify Lucifer! The reason John says lion, calf, man, and
eagle, and Ezekiel says lion, ox, man, and eagle, is found in Ezek.
10:14.) If John had said, in Revelation 4, lion, ox, man, and eagle, you
never could have identified the fifth cherub missing. By saying lion,
calf, man, and eagle, you know what the fifth cherub was. He was an ox!

Ezekiel 10:14, "And every one had four faces: the first face was the
face of a cherub, and the second face was the face of a man, and the
third the face of a lion, and the fourth the face of an eagle."

The AV 1611 (this is not found in the Hebrew or the Greek) here opens up
a secret unknown to any Hebrew scholar, alive or dead, anywhere in the
world since the beginning of Bible study. It unlocks a secret that a
cherub, as such, in essence, would have a face like an ox. You know this
by substitution, compare Ezekiel 1:10 with Ezekiel 10:14 in the English.
In Ezekiel 1:10---man, lion, ox, eagle; in Ezekiel 10:14---man, lion,
cherub, eagle. Then a cherub has a face like an ox.

To sum it up---northward, in the third heaven, over the sea of glass,
and over that throne, we have God's Person and glory seated, in a light
that no man can dwell, approach unto, or see (1 Tim. 6:16). At the right
hand of that immortal life (the soul of God), is seated the Man, Christ
Jesus. Around that throne are four cherubim. These four cherubim look
like men. They have one thing in common, they all have feet like a calf.
These four cherubim have four separate faces. They have the face of a
calf, lion, eagle, and man. When these cherubim pick up this throne and
bring it back to this earth at the Second Advent (which Ezekiel has a
vision of in Ezek. 1), they will assimilate each other's faces, so that
each one will have four faces. Each cherub will have the face of an ox,
a lion, an eagle, and a man. Originally, in Genesis 1:1, there was a
fifth Cherub who was over the throne, who is no longer present. This
Cherub looked like a man (as cherubim do), had a split foot like a calf
(which all cherubim have), had wings (hence, angels are drawn as having
wings, although no angel ever had wings---cherubim have wings), had a
face like an ox with two horns on his head. This "anointed Cherub" who
"covers" is named "Lucifer." Hence, he is drawn as a man with two horns
on his head, a split foot, and a pointed tail, as a serpent would have!

A cartoon of Satan is not a cartoon at all; it is a picture of Satan in
his original position. But, in his original position, he was not red.
The color red is a description of his present state as a "red dragon"
(see Rev. 12:3--10). Originally, the Cherub appeared in a brilliant halo
of light, covered from head to foot with precious stones, full of wisdom
and beauty, knowing just about everything that God knew, being perfect
in beauty, being over the throne---geographically, over the throne of
God---with a face like an ox, two horns on his head, four wings on his
body---a winged "angel of light." Through sin he degenerated into a "red
dragon," called a "serpent," was worshipped by Aaron in the form of a
golden calf, was worshipped by Jeroboam in the form of a golden calf,
was worshipped as an ox by a Baal or bull worshipper, and in America,
his followers are called "cow‑boys"---Baal‑boys. The word for "Baal" in
English is "bull," as in Papal Bull, as in the priests offering oxen for
sacrifice in Acts 14:13, as in somebody saying "that's a bunch of bull,"
as in a bull session, as in "the Bull of the woods," referring to a
chief superintendent, or as in the winged bull on the Assyrian monuments
(Isa. 10:5--13).

The Devil was an "anointed Cherub" with a face like an ox, with two
horns and wings. In his present condition, he is a serpent---a red
"dragon" with seven heads. When he appears, he appears neither as a
cherub nor as a serpent. When he appears, he appears as "an angel of
light" (2 Cor. 11:11--14). When Satan appears, he appears as a
thirty-three‑year‑old, wingless, male "christ"!

Notice in Revelation 4:8 the threefold doxology: "Holy (God the Father),
holy (God the Son), holy (God the Holy Spirit), Lord (God the Father),
God (God the Son), Almighty (God the Holy Spirit)."

4:9 "And when those beasts give glory and honour and thanks to him that
sat on the throne, who liveth for ever and ever,

10 The four and twenty elders fall down before him that sat on the
throne, and worship him that liveth for ever and ever, and cast their
crowns before the throne, saying,

11 Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honour and power: for
thou hast created all things, and for thy pleasure they are and were
created."

Here, we have the scene in heaven where God Almighty is receiving the
worship of the beasts and the four and twenty elders. This is the scene
which John beholds when he is caught up to the third heaven, and his
journey is a type of the Rapture of the church.

CHAPTER 5

5:1 "And I saw in the right hand of him that sat on the throne a book
written within and on the backside, sealed with seven seals.

2 And I saw a strong angel proclaiming with a loud voice, Who is worthy
to open the book, and to loose the seals thereof?

3 And no man in heaven, nor in earth, neither under the earth, was able
to open the book, neither to look thereon.

4 And I wept much, because no man was found worthy to open and to read
the book, neither to look thereon."

John sees in this vision a form like a man on the throne. In his right
hand he has "a book written within, and on the backside, sealed with
seven seals." Now, this book is unidentified. It could be the Book of
Revelation itself (as the Book of Revelation in the next two chapters
begins with the opening of these seven seals). It could refer to the
entire Bible. Some people believe it refers to the Book of Daniel,
because in Daniel 12, Daniel is told to "seal up these things until the
end time," and unseal them then. It is absolutely impossible to identify
this book, for certain, but the chances are very good that it is a
reference to the Book of Revelation itself.

(I realize that this commentary will eventually get into the hands of
people who do not believe that the chapter and verse markings of the
Authorized Version are authoritative. Their reason for this is: "they
were added later." This reason would seem plausible if it were not for a
number of factors that these people have not considered. The biggest
factor is that these people \[who do not believe the chapter and verse
markings are inspired\] have never studied them to start with. They've
taken for granted that since they were added around 1200 to 1600 that
they could not be inspired, that God would have nothing to do with them.
But this is only a half‑truth. The divisions of the Old Testament
\[Parashas\] into paragraphs was set up before the birth of Christ.
There is also a division found in the "Eusebian Canons," dividing
passages of Scripture in the New Testament off into certain sections,
which was in vogue before A.D. 400. If you study the chapter and verse
markings in an Authorized Version, you will find that there are so many
"undesigned coincidences" connected with the numbers in these chapters
and verses that a man who makes a thorough study of them will no longer
believe that God did not have a hand in their manufacture.)

Suffice it to say, the Book of Revelation has twenty-two chapters. If it
had a seal after every three chapters, it would have seven seals; and it
would be "written within and on the backside"---chapter 22 would fall on
the backside of the last seal! There is a very good chance that the
seven‑sealed book is the Book of Revelation.

There is a very good chance that the seven‑sealed book is the Holy
Bible. The older King James Bibles, AV 1611, on the front page, had a
little sketch of an open Bible with seven seals. All AV 1611 Bibles (by
the wildest stroke of coincidence!) have seven marks around the back of
the cover on the outside. If it only has five marks, you will find the
other two marks running around the border of the cover.

Whether this book is the Book of Revelation or the Bible, one thing is
certain---no man is able to open this book: no man is "worthy."

"And no man in heaven (no angel, no saint), nor in earth (no preacher,
no scholar, no pope, no bishop, no priest), neither under the earth (no
demon, no lost person, no devil), was able to open the book, neither to
look thereon."

The idea is that no man was able to make anyone understand it. This is
what the word "open" means, as it is used in other passages in the
Bible. This is not merely to open the book to look at it, but open the
book so you can understand it. Notice the comparative passage in Luke
24:45 and Acts 16:14.

Luke 24:45, "Then opened he their understanding, that they might
understand the scriptures."

Acts 16:14, "And a certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the
city of Thyatira, which worshipped God, heard us: whose heart the Lord
opened, that she attended unto the things which were spoken of Paul."

5:5 "And one of the elders saith unto me, Weep not: behold, the Lion of
the tribe of Juda, the Root of David, hath prevailed to open the book,
and to loose the seven seals thereof."

There is no difficulty about the "Lion of the tribe of Juda"---it is the
Lord Jesus Christ. Jesus Christ is from the tribe of Judah, and is
called the "son of David" in Matthew 1:1.

"Hath prevailed to open the book, and to loose the seven seals thereof."

Jesus Christ prevailed in the wilderness over the Devil. He prevailed at
Calvary over the sins of the world. He prevailed at Gethsemane,
recovering what Adam lost in Eden.

5:6 "And I beheld, and, lo, in the midst of the throne and of the four
beasts, and in the midst of the elders, stood a Lamb as it had been
slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of
God sent forth into all the earth."

In the vision, John sees Christ pictured as a real lamb, a literal lamb.
(We must always distinguish between figurative and literal. We know that
Jesus Christ is not a four‑legged, grass‑eating, horned animal; but He
can assume many appearances, as Satan does also. Satan appears as a
"serpent," "dragon," "cherub," and as an "angel of light.") The Lord
Jesus Christ is pictured as the "Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin
of the world," and the "Lion of the tribe of Juda." In the vision that
John sees, he sees Him pictured as a literal lamb, getting up, walking
to the throne, and picking up the book.

5:7 "And he came and took the book out of the right hand of him that sat
upon the throne."

Notice in verse 6, "stood a Lamb as it had been slain." The idea is a
lamb pierced for sacrifice. The indication is (if it is a literal lamb)
that the lamb has its throat cut and blood running out. Or, it is a
picture of Jesus Christ with the crucifixion scars on Him. This is
significant because it shows us that, in some sense, the Lord Jesus
Christ will bear forever in eternity the crucifixion scars as proof of
His blood atonement. The only imperfect body in heaven will be the body
of the Lord Jesus Christ! (When I say "imperfect," I don't mean with
blemish or sin. I mean that the Scriptures indicate that the
resurrection body of Jesus Christ still bears the holes and the
spear‑thrust that were a part of His crucifixion before He arose from
the dead (Luke 24; John 20--21). The glorified body of the Lord Jesus
Christ still had the marks of His crucifixion in it. This is a wonderful
thought. Out there in eternity, when several billion years have passed
by and we've long forgotten our problems, sorrows, and the troubles we
had on this earth, whenever we pass the throne, we'll still see One who
bears in His body the marks of our sin and our redemption! In case the
Christian should ever be tempted in eternity to forget what the Lord
accomplished for him when he was a sinner, whenever he sees the Lord
Jesus Christ, he will have a gentle reminder that he was a sinner, and a
sinner who was redeemed only by the grace of God.

5:8 "And when he had taken the book, the four beasts and four and twenty
elders fell down before the Lamb, having every one of them harps, and
golden vials full of odours, which are the prayers of saints.

9 And they sung a new song, saying, Thou art worthy to take the book,
and to open the seals thereof: for thou wast slain, and hast redeemed us
to God by thy blood out of every kindred, and tongue, and people, and
nation."

People who get to heaven without trusting the blood would have a time,
wouldn't they? Suppose a Campbellite got to heaven, and everybody
started singing about the blood, and he wanted to start singing about
the water! Suppose a Campbellite could get to heaven; wouldn't he be
frustrated to see John Wesley there? John Wesley (one of the most
sainted men of God that ever lived) led more souls to Christ in a month
than Alexander Campbell did in his entire lifetime, and John Wesley was
sprinkled! What do you reckon these little old tin‑horse, two‑bit Church
of Christ preachers will think when they get to heaven (if they ever
do!) and find Martin Luther, sprinkled; Savonarola, sprinkled; and John
Wycliffe and John Huss, who were baptized, not in the "Church of
Christ," but in Anabaptist groups, independent groups, and even Catholic
groups! What a joke! If a "Church of Christ" preacher could get to
heaven, he would find saints there with more rewards than he ever
thought of having, and those saints weren't even immersed in water. (If
a Campbellite got to heaven, he would be miserable; he would have to go
to hell to be happy!) In heaven they don't sing about anything but the
blood. They don't sing about the church; they don't sing about the
sacraments; they don't sing "Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with
thee, blessed art thou among women." (They don't go around imitating
angels' messages to Old Testament Jewish women, given before the
crucifixion!) They sing about the blood! (And I don't mean a jug of wine
that some priest said was blood. They sing about the blood!) They don't
sing about a jug of some fermented hootch that some black‑hooded priest
is holding up and saying, "Fi, fie, fo, fum" over, and pretending it's
Christ's blood. When Christians get to heaven, they sing about the
B‑L‑0‑0‑D! "What can wash away my sins? Nothing but the BLOOD of Jesus.
What can make me whole again? Nothing (nothing, NOTHING, man---no
commandments, no works, no Golden Rule, no repent, believe, confess, and
be bup‑tized) NOTHING BUT THE BLOOD OF JESUS!" Imagine a man getting to
heaven and saying that he got there because he repented, believed,
confessed, and was bup‑tized, and kept the commandments and "endured
until the end!" Somebody would say to him, "Shut your self‑righteous
mouth, you egomaniac, we are worshipping Jesus Christ up here." "For by
grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the
gift of God: Not of works, lest any man should boast."

5:10 "And hast made us unto our God kings and priests: and we shall
reign on the earth."

The American Standard Version, 1901, one of the more irreverent and
deceptive perversions of the Word of God, says, "And we reign on the
earth." This puts the reign in the present tense. The Jehovah's Witness
group, as the translators of the ASV---the dead, orthodox, Presbyterian,
Congregational groups, who put out the ASV and thumbed it off on the
Louisville Seminary and the Southern Baptists---would subscribe to this
change of tense. They believe the Kingdom is here now, and there is not
going to be any literal Kingdom. These Bible "twisters" believe that the
only kingdom is a spiritual kingdom; and therefore, the Christian is
reigning now. This same group of carnal backsliders has succeeded in
getting the crowns for themselves without carrying the cross. (Quite a
trick, if you can do it.) The Lord Jesus Christ bore His cross the first
time to get His crown the second time. If you are trying to wear a crown
now, "spreading the kingdom," you've got the wrong dispensation!

The Amplified Version (put out by a group resembling the Reformed Church
of Holland, Michigan, and other Christian "Reform" groups who believe in
"covenant sprinkling" and Amillennial theology), says, "And THEY shall
reign on the earth," trying to make it apply to somebody else. Now both
of these attempts to pervert the plain English are the attempts of a
group of people who have rejected the Premillennial coming of Christ,
and have tried to turn the Bible around and make it fit the Amillennial
and Postmillennial theologies of dead, orthodox Presbyterianism, and
tradition‑worshipping Roman Catholicism. Augustine, Eusebius, and Origen
did the same thing in A.D. 100--400. But the Word of God stands:

"And we shall reign on the earth." This is future---the Second Coming.
We will reign on the earth, not "in heaven." This is not a heavenly,
spiritual reign, but a reign on the earth.

5:11 "And I beheld, and I heard the voice of many angels round about the
throne and the beasts and the elders: and the number of them was ten
thousand times ten thousand, and thousands of thousands;

12 Saying with a loud voice, Worthy is the Lamb that was slain to
receive power, and riches, and wisdom, and strength, and honour, and
glory, and blessing.

13 And every creature which is in heaven, and on the earth, and under
the earth, and such as are in the sea, and all that are in them, heard I
saying, Blessing, and honour, and glory, and power, be unto him that
sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb for ever and ever."

All the animals praise God. Just like God opened the mouth of Balaam's
ass to give him an evangelistic message, at this time the mouths of the
squirrels, fishes, birds, creeping, crawling, and running beasts are
opened to praise the Lord.

5:14 "And the four beasts said, Amen. And the four and twenty elders
fell down and worshipped him that liveth for ever and ever."

CHAPTER 6

To repeat what we have learned, the church in Revelation 4 has been
raptured up into heaven, and it is up there with the Lord Jesus Christ.
The Lord Jesus Christ has stepped forward and taken the book with the
seven seals. He now begins to open the seals in the book. As each seal
is opened, the events which follow take place on the earth below. (What
follows in Rev. 6--19 is called "Daniel's Seventieth Week" in Dan. 9,
"the time of Jacob's trouble" in Jer. 30:7, and the "time of trouble" in
Dan. 12:1. This is that unparalleled time in which Israel will be
persecuted, and the race will be wiped off the earth, down to 144,000
people.) These unparalleled, catastrophic events and cataclysmic
judgments which are going to come upon this earth begin after the
Rapture of the church. The Body of Christ is caught out, and sometime
thereafter (we're not given the exact time element), these events begin.

6:1 "And I saw when the Lamb opened one of the seals, and I heard, as it
were the noise of thunder, one of the four beasts saying, Come and see."

Then follows "seven seals." Now, you should make another chart. Draw a
line twelve inches long. Draw an arrow coming down at the one‑inch mark.
This arrow coming down, which will not touch the line, represents the
Lord descending "from heaven with a shout," coming down in the air to
catch up His saints. Then, at the one‑inch mark, draw a line going up.
This line represents New Testament saints being caught up. Make this
line go in an arc up over the top of the chart, clear across and down to
the ten‑inch mark. From the ten‑inch mark to the twelve‑inch mark is the
1000 year reign of Christ. (You now have a broader space to study the
Tribulation.) Draw another arrow going up at the seven and one-half‑inch
mark. This is the Rapture of the "Tribulation saints." The space between
the one‑  
inch mark and the eight‑inch mark is the seven year period of the
"Tribulation." Divide this eight inches into seven equal parts. (This
will be one part for each year in the Tribulation.) Now, as we study
these events, we will match them on this chart.

"One of the four beasts saying, Come and see." The "one of the four
beasts" turns out to be the first beast, by what follows. The first
beast is a lion (Rev. 4:7). So, the lion said, "Come and see."

6:2 "And I saw, and behold a white horse: and he that sat on him had a
bow; and a crown was given unto him: and he went forth conquering, and
to conquer."

This "white horse rider" is the most controversial figure in Scripture.
As a matter of fact, he is so debatable that the Civil War and the
Revolutionary War were fought because of his misidentity. Here we have a
man who is indirectly responsible for more than ten major wars. (So you
can see the importance of getting the Book of Revelation right!) By the
way, the Southern Baptist seminaries and the Roman Catholic Church still
teach a false identification of this rider so that "Christianity"
continues to cause wars. You can look forward to "wars and rumors of
wars" until somebody gets Revelation 6:2 right.

Now, if Revelation 6:2 is a reference to Jesus Christ---which the
majority of Baptists, Methodists, Presbyterians, Lutherans,
Episcopalians, Church of God, Assemblies of God, Roman Catholics,
Mormons, Seventh-day Adventists, Jehovah Witnesses, etc., teach that it
is---then He is now a king (there's the crown in the middle of the
verse); He is now conquering (there it is at the end of the verse); and
this means that "the kingdom " is gradually spreading as Jesus Christ
conquers this earth! This is the teaching known as "Postmillennialism,"
taught by Augustine (5th century), Eusebius (4th century), and Origen
(3rd century), between A.D. 200--500, and it is the same Roman Catholic
ecclesiastical falsehood that has corrupted and defiled the face of this
earth for 1,500 years. It is the teaching that "things are getting
better." It is Darwin (19th century) in the church. This teaching is the
cause of more wars than you can count, for if man is "getting better"
and "the kingdom" is coming on this earth, then no matter how big a war
you have, you can always say that man is getting better from these wars
and learning more and becoming more "human" all the time. You can also
say that the Kingdom is nearer now than it was and that these wars have
helped to bring it about. But mainly, you can say that "Christ's church"
should be getting more and more power over the earth, since Christ is
gradually conquering the earth as a king. This is the official attitude
taken by the Vatican State, and it is the prime cause of every major war
in Europe and Asia between A.D. 500 and A.D. 1970. If Christ is a king
conquering the earth, then the church with Him (like the Vatican State,
with its so‑called "king"---he has two crowns and he "reigns," and he's
called the spiritual ruler of all Catholics) is gradually conquering the
earth. This is ex‑cathedra "infallible" Catholic doctrine, exactly what
is taught by the Vatican State! And this is the doctrine which justifies
political shenanigans, lobbyists in Congress, false dealing with the
United Nations, and Roman Catholics getting into government agencies to
accomplish their end. For if Christ is gradually conquering and
"Catholics" are His one true church (which they claim to be), then they
are gradually conquering with Him! This is the twentieth-century,
Postmillennial theology of Augustine, Origen, and Eusebius (and
incidentally, Louisville Southern Baptist Theological Seminary). Very
odd bedfellows, when you find Southern Baptists agreeing with Roman
Catholics! But, we are living in strange days! A Baptist and a Catholic
can't be comfortable in the same bed unless one of them is asleep.

If the rider on the white horse is Jesus Christ, then this world is
getting "better and better" as Christ "gradually conquers" it. If it is
not Jesus Christ, then 98 percent of all our theological schools are
teaching a bald‑face lie about a major Christian doctrine. Now, is it,
or isn't it? Somebody says, "Who cares?" You'd better care! The correct
attitude toward modern "Christianity" will be determined by whether the
rider is the Lord Jesus, or whether it is someone else.

Why would anybody think that this rider was Jesus Christ? Every
theological seminary that teaches that it is Jesus uses another passage
in Revelation to prove that it is. Turn to Revelation 19, and you will
find the passage that is used by  
A. T. Robertson, Alford, Berkhof, Dabney, Kuyper, Hodge, et al., to
prove that the white horse rider of Revelation 6 is Jesus Christ.

In Revelation 19 there is a rider on a white horse, and about this white
horse rider, there can be no doubt; this rider has a name which is
"Faithful and True" (Rev. 19:11). He is called the "Word of God" in
verse 13. He is "King of kings, and Lord of lords," in verse 16. There
is no doubt about the Lord Jesus Christ in this passage. Since He is on
a "white horse" (Rev. 19:11), He is naturally lined up with the rider in
Revelation 6:2, and our religious leaders say that they are both the
same. (This is typical of the careless exegesis for which Greek scholars
are famous. Dr. A. T. Robertson, who wrote a Greek Grammar about three
inches thick, and who is one of the most profound Greek scholars the
world ever produced, was also one of the most ignorant men that ever
lived when it came to reading plain English. Dr. A. T. Robertson was an
Amillennialist, as are all Presbyterian, Congregationalist, and Southern
Baptist seminaries and he believed that the white horse rider of
Revelation 6:2 was Jesus Christ, exactly as you will find the
cross‑reference in the "Amplified New Testament." Why these men are so
proficient in Greek and cannot read English is a problem that is going
to have to be analyzed at the Judgment Seat of Christ.)

In Revelation 19:11 you are told that this "white horse" comes out of
heaven. In Revelation 6:2 you are told nothing of the kind. In
Revelation 19:12 you are told that the white horse rider has "many
crowns." In Revelation 19:15 you are told that the white horse rider has
a "sharp sword." In Revelation 6:2 you are told that this white horse
rider has "a bow." In Revelation 19:14 you find the white horse rider
followed by the armies of God. The white horse rider in Revelation 6:2
is followed by "Death," "Hell," and "famine"! What do you suppose would
possess a man (with the ability to take the prefixes and suffixes off a
Greek word, and decline it or conjugate it, and talk about what part of
speech it was and what it modified) to pick up a book with sixth‑grade
English and be unable to read the print? Yet, that is exactly what has
happened. There isn't one major accredited denominational seminary in
the United States that is able to teach sixth‑grade English, when it
comes to the Book of Revelation. This accentuates the fact that the Book
of Revelation is not hard to understand, it is hard to believe.

Now, there is only one man in the Bible who is so much like Christ that
he could fool 100 seminaries into thinking that he was Jesus Christ.
(You people who know the Bible would have no trouble telling who this
is!) If there is a man in the Bible who is so much like Christ that some
people think that he is Christ, do you know who that would be? Well, it
wouldn't be Paul. Paul preached to Gentiles, and Jesus preached to Jews.
If there is any man in the Bible that would really "imitate Christ," it
would be Satan. I suppose this explains why all the Greek scholars are
so muddled when they come to the passage. Most people think that Satan
is a two‑horned, red‑tailed being. They don't realize that in spite of
his real nature which God knows and sees, he appears to people as "an
angel of light." In the Bible, he is the highest power in the universe
outside of God. He is such an exact reproduction of Jesus Christ that
you couldn't tell them apart if they were standing side by side. Now to
understand this, look at the Scriptures:

1\. Jesus has a city who is a bride (Rev. 21).

​Satan has a city who is a bride (Rev. 17).

2\. Jesus said, "I am the light of the world" (John 8:12, 9:5).

​Satan is called an "angel of light" (2 Cor. 11:14).

3\. Jesus Christ is called a "King of kings" (Rev. 19:16).

​Satan is a "king over all the children of pride" (Job 41:34).

4\. Jesus Christ is called the "Prince of Peace" (Isa. 9:6).

​Satan is called the "prince of this world" (John 14:30).

5\. Jesus Christ is called "my Lord and my God" (John 20:28).

​Satan is called "the god of this world" (2 Cor. 4:4).

6\. Jesus said to Satan, when tempted by him, "It is written" (Luke 4).

​Satan quotes the Scripture right back to Him, "It is written," and
quotes part of Psalm 91:11 (Luke 4).

7.​Jesus is called "the Lion of the tribe of Juda" (Rev. 5:5).

​Satan is called "a lion" (1 Pet. 5:8).

In plainer words, the most perfect imitator of the Lord Jesus Christ is
Satan himself, not Sheldon or Thomas á Kempis!

Coming back to Revelation 6:2, this white horse rider has some
connection with Satan; there is no doubt about it. War follows him in
verse 4; famine follows him in verses 5 and 6; Death and Hell follow him
in verse 8. In verse 9, he is found killing people who believe the word
of God. His ministry winds up in verse 17 with the great day of God's
wrath. What do you suppose could possess some poor, blinded, scholar
(with a Ph.D.!) to believe that Revelation 6:2 was a reference to Christ
"gradually conquering" when the rest of the chapter refers to Death,
Hell, famine, and judgment? Aren't scholars strange? (The Bible has
messed up more men who professed to be intellectual scholars than any
other book. The most intellectually dishonest man you ever met in your
life was a preacher or teacher who would teach you that Revelation 6:2
is a reference to Jesus Christ. Before it could be a reference to Jesus
Christ, it would be a reference to Castro or Santa Claus.)

Now, notice that this man (Rev. 6:2) comes in with a crown. If he has a
crown, he is a king. The Latin for "king" is "Rex." Notice, he has a bow
in his hand. He shoots a bow. When you shoot a bow, you lay the arrow
across the wood and pull it back, and if you look at it very carefully,
you find that the arrow is at right angles to the bow, making a cross.
The Latin word for "cross" is "crux." This unnamed, unidentified king
with the bow is a mystery man. (Let's call him "Mr. X.") Did you ever
study words that end in "x"? This man is a mystery. He's "X." (See book,
The Mark of the Beast.) And, he has a bow. When you shoot a bow, you
pull back the string and release it. Now, look at your forefinger and
index finger when you are through releasing the string; you'll find that
the thumb is forward and the two fingers (forefinger and middle finger)
are straight out. Hold it up in the "V" for victory sign made by Winston
Churchill, and you'll see what I mean. This "sign of the two fingers" is
one of the most remarkable signs that the Devil ever slipped into man's
use. When the Roman Pontiff blesses the multitude at St. Peter's, he
holds his hand with the forefinger up, the middle finger slightly
dropped---a two‑fingered blessing. When a Latin Catholic crosses (crux)
himself, he uses these two fingers. "Cross your heart and hope to die."
A "double cross" is when a man is betrayed. Folks say, "I told a lie,
but I had my fingers crossed." These two fingers are found in Protestant
and Catholic paintings of Jesus Christ. If you'll look in some old
Bible, you'll find them in every picture of Jesus Christ and John the
Baptist, the forefinger up straight and the index finger slightly bent.
It is a two‑fingered salute. The Pope's private Swiss body-guard salutes
the papal flag with this salute, and has for 300 years!

Now the Pope would manufacture some remarkable fairy story about how
this all means "the Father, Son, and Holy Ghost," but, ignoring the
Pope's duplicity, let's just slip back about 1,000 years before any Pope
was born. Let's go way back to when there weren't any "popes," and get
some authentic information, instead of believing everything we hear.

First of all, may I call your attention to the fact that from time
immemorial, two fingers raised over the head and moved forward has been
the sign of an attack. In the twentieth century this is the sign in the
infantry for an assault with a bayonet. It also is the sign in the tank
corps for an attack. This is the sign of Nimrod, the bowman, to kill.
These two fingers, placed behind the front teeth and pushed forward,
make up one of the most insulting gestures you can ever deliver (in
Italy). These two fingers are remarkable. Did you notice that people
hold a cigarette between these two fingers? Did you notice that when a
man wants a shot of liquor, "a jigger," he says, "Two fingers"? Today
when any man wants to give the sign of victory, he holds up these two
fingers. In an AP photo you'll find thirteen men exhibiting this
two‑fingered salute. These men passed the Civil Rights Bill, the bill
that will eventually lead to national socialism. You'll find
politicians, candidates for office, holding up these two fingers. I have
pictures in my "Doomsday Book" of Alvin Barkley, President Eisenhower,
President Truman, President Kennedy, Eddie Arnold, Pope John, and God
knows who, with these two fingers up in the air. Churchill says, "It's
just 'V' for victory." But a man can be ignorant of his own definitions.
Don't think that because Churchill held up two fingers, "V" for victory,
that he suddenly got a bright idea. There's nothing new "under the sun."
The Devil leads people (Eph. 2:1--4). One of the conceits of unsaved
men, and sometimes saved men, is to think that they can tell what
certain signs mean, and identify them as meaning positive things in line
with Darwin's theory. But, the Bible is the final textbook on what signs
mean. If you want to know what a "black cat" or a "13" means or "two
fingers," you don't go to Churchill or Pope Johnny to find out; you go
to the word of God. From time immemorial, in the Bible (and out of the
Bible), two fingers means death. That's what it means. Snake eyes! The
double cross! Two of them, as on the split tongue of a serpent (Gen.
3:1--2). (See The Mark of the Beast, 1969.)

This man is a mystery man. He is a "Mr. X." He represents something
terrible because when he comes, famine, Death, and Hell come after him.
He's an imitation of Jesus Christ so he has some connection with Satan.

6:3 "And when he had opened the second seal, I heard the second beast
say, Come and see."

The second beast is the calf (Rev. 4:7). The calf said, "Come and see."

6:4 "And there went out another horse that was red: and power was given
to him that sat thereon to take peace from the earth, and that they
should kill one another: and there was given unto him a great sword."

These people, who believe that the world is getting better and better
all the time and that Christ is "gradually conquering," have had a
little trouble in verse 4, so they just leave it out. The fact is that
the white horse rider is followed by bloodshed.

6:5 "And when he had opened the third seal, I heard the third beast say,
Come and see. And I beheld, and lo a black horse; and he that sat on him
had a pair of balances in his hand."

The third beast is the man (Rev. 4:7), and the colors are all
significant. White is usually a symbol of purity or innocence.
Consequently, people think the rider of Revelation 6:2 is Jesus Christ.
Don't forget that the Devil is an "angel of light"! Red is always a
symbol of blood wherever you find it. This may be Red Russia or Red
China, but it is not identified. There is one thing for certain, that
with this red horse comes war. Then we find the black horse, and famine
follows in his footsteps.

Look at your chart. These events should take you from the one‑inch mark
to the two‑inch mark, from the two‑inch mark to the three‑inch mark, and
from the three‑inch mark to the four‑inch mark. This will put you into
the third year of the Tribulation.

"And he that sat on him had a pair of balances in his hand." This
"balance" is the old‑time way of measuring food. It's simply a scale set
up with two pans on either side of it to balance each other. You put the
proper weight in one pan, and the goods in the other pan. When they
balance out level (which is determined by an arrow in the center), you
have the proper weight. The "balances" are for the purpose of weighing
food.

6:6 "And I heard a voice in the midst of the four beasts say, A measure
of wheat for a penny, and three measures of barley for a penny; and see
thou hurt not the oil and the wine."

When this food is divided out, it is divided out a measure at a time.
The weight for a measure is put in one pan, and the barley, or the
wheat, is put in the other cup.

"A measure of wheat for a penny, and three measures of barley for a
penny." The passage is obscure, and nobody has written a good comment on
it yet. In Homer's "Odyssey" it is said to be the daily wage of a Roman
soldier. (This is found in Herodotus, chap. 8, p. 637.) Hence, we might
learn from this passage that in the Tribulation people are going to get
to eat no more than a Roman soldier gets in one day. However, "Hurt not
the oil and the wine" doesn't put any light on the passage. "Oil and
wine" cost money. The idea may be that you persecute the poor folks and
let the rich folks go. You'll find a companion verse for this in Job
24:11. There are a few other verses that may shed some light on the
passage---2 Chron. 2:15; Hosea 12:1. All these passages come to one
pretty solid conclusion. In the Tribulation, following the Antichrist
and following the war, we have a very definite famine. This is a time
when the barley and the wheat have to be weighed out a measure at a time
(see Ezek. 4:11, 16).

Now, going on over a little further on your chart, around the four‑inch
to the five‑inch mark, we come to the fourth seal.

6:7 "And when he had opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the
fourth beast say, Come and see."

This fourth beast is an eagle (Rev. 4:7).

6:8 "And I looked, and behold a pale horse: and his name that sat on him
was Death, and Hell followed with him. And power was given unto them
over the fourth part of the earth, to kill with sword, and with hunger,
and with death, and with the beasts of the earth."

So "all hell breaks loose," as the expression goes. (Such expressions as
"come hell or high water," or "all hell busting loose," of course, come
from the Book of Revelation. The "high water" occurs in Rev. 12, and
"hell breaking loose" occurs here.)

"Pale horse: and his name that sat on him." Notice, this is the first
rider who is named. This is a particular type of person because,
although people are getting killed in Revelation 4, the name of the one
on this pale horse is "Death." Then this is a definite person. Notice,
Death personified with a capital "D." Then in the Bible, you'll find an
angel whose job is "killing" or "death," an angel to whom this power has
been delegated. God delegates the power of death to Satan in Hebrews
2:14. Sometimes God gives permission for Satan to take a life, and
sometimes He does not. The actual execution is carried out by one of the
angels of Satan, who has a special name; in Revelation 9:11 you will
find that the name of this angel is "the Destroyer." (It is indicated by
two words, one in the Hebrew---"Abaddon," and one in the
Greek---"Apollyon." Both words mean "perdition" or "destroyer." The word
"Apollyon" is the same word found in 2 Thess. 2:3 for the "son of
perdition," exactly as you find it in John 17:12.) This is a particular
death angel entrusted with this job, Death personified, and hence, he is
often cartooned as an old man with a sickle, riding on a pale horse.

"And his name that sat on him was Death, and Hell followed with him."

Now, this is rather hard to take literally. Yet in the passage, "Hell"
is capitalized and personified, as though the inhabitants of hell were
spued out on this earth during this last half of the Tribulation. (I
don't teach that as a fact. That's too wild of a shot. There are not
enough Scriptures to confirm this---to teach that in the Tribulation the
inhabitants of hell come up to roam across this earth!) But, the
indication is rather strong. For example, in Revelation 9:1--13, it is
quite apparent that the inhabitants of the bottomless pit come up. And,
if that weren't all, the inhabitants of the bottomless pit are in
various stages of human and animal decomposition when they come up.

When a soul goes to hell, it seems as though that soul begins a gradual
deterioration which winds up in an animal form! And at the Great White
Throne Judgment, the unsaved receive animal forms instead of human
forms. Hence, the shape of their soul is destroyed. (Now, there is not
enough Scripture to substantiate this fully. So, don't make me say
something that I didn't say! But there are passages in the Bible that
are fairly clear about this.) Notice in Isaiah 28:15, the Jew makes a
covenant with "Death and Hell" in the Tribulation, and this covenant is
broken in the middle of the Tribulation by the Antichrist. Speaking of
this half‑animal, half‑beast type of thing which you see and hear about
in the "Twilight Zone," "Outer Limits," and those other imitations of
the word of God, notice in Isaiah 34:8--17 a group of animals who
populate hell. Isaiah 34:9--10 is the lake of fire. Yet, the inhabitants
are pictured as animals instead of human beings!

This brings up the weird thought---if a Christian is like his Father and
receives a body like his Saviour at the Second Coming (which he will,   
1 John 3:1--6), it stands to reason that the unsaved will receive a body
like their father at the Great White Throne Judgment. Their father,
however, is a red dragon, a serpent, a red worm (John 3:14; Rev. 12:9).
This red worm ("their worm shall not die, neither shall their fire be
quenched") is in either language a red maggot. It brings upon one's mind
the fearful apprehension that the final state of the wicked is, perhaps,
Darwin in reverse! Instead of Darwin being right in thinking that man
came up from amoeba, planaria, and paramecium into the jellyfish forms,
and the fish, serpent, and worm forms, into the mammalia and vertebrae
forms, that man is returning back to the lower forms by degeneration.
Man's ultimate and final state, after bragging about becoming like God,
getting to heaven and conquering the sun, moon, stars, Venus, and
Jupiter (and putting on 6,000 years of a lying burlesque show about how
great and powerful and wonderful he is getting up to take over the
heavens), will possibly wind up as a red maggot in a lake of fire (Psa.
22:6; Isa. 66:24)! (I would not teach this as an absolute doctrine from
the word of God, but it is strongly intimated.)

In Revelation 6:8 you are told that Death and Hell are set loose during
the Tribulation. When they come out, in Revelation 9 (which is another
account of it), they are in half‑animal and half‑human form. Hence, the
"satyrs," "centaurs," and "ogres" of Greek, Hebrew, and Phoenician
mythology have a Bible basis. The horror tales on the news stand, and
the Frankensteins, the Draculas, have a Scriptural basis. (This, of
course, is the main purpose of the Book of Revelation---to open up
types. By that I mean the Book of Revelation so opens the Bible that you
can get a dictionary on what anything means that you don't understand
outside the Bible. Revelation is the greatest book in the Bible for
establishing the Bible as the absolute, final authority on what things
mean, which men think mean something that they do not mean.)

Now, look at your chart on the Tribulation. You are now coming down to
the six‑inch or seven‑inch mark, near the end of the seven years.

6:9 "And when he had opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the
souls of them that were slain for the word of God, and for the testimony
which they held:

10 And they cried with a loud voice, saying, How long, O Lord, holy and
true, dost thou not judge and avenge our blood on them that dwell on the
earth?

11 And white robes were given unto every one of them; and it was said
unto them, that they should rest yet for a little season, until their
fellowservants also and their brethren, that should be killed as they
were, should be fulfilled."

Now, coming into the second half of the Tribulation down on earth, John
sees under the fifth seal "an altar," and under this "altar" the "souls"
who have been slain for the word of God, for the "testimony which they
held." (Compare this with John's own position in Rev. 1:9, which would
indicate that the Book of Revelation is primarily to the people in the
Tribulation.) These martyred people, who have been killed, are pictured
as "souls." Notice that they have voices, memories, and a body which can
wear a white robe. This brings up one of the most radical, revolutionary
Bible doctrines found anywhere in the word of God; and it is a doctrine
so far in advance of anything that has ever been printed, that an AV
1611 is the only place you could ever get it. The passage reveals that a
"soul" is not like a croquet ball or a tennis ball or a baseball, stuck
away in your body somewhere. A soul is a bodily shape which is shaped
exactly like the "body of flesh." The soul, being the seat of the
emotions, is a bodily shaped, invisible type of "ghost." Hence, the Lord
Jesus Christ in the believer would be "the Holy Ghost." Paul says,
"Christ be formed in you" (Gal. 4:19). The soul has arms, legs, eyes,
ears, nose, throat, mouth, lips, teeth, tongue, back, jaw, stomach, and
"the works." In Luke 16, the rich man in hell had a tongue, eyes, voice,
and yet his physical body was buried in the grave. There is no argument
at all about "the grave." The Bible clearly teaches that the physical
body goes to the grave and rots. Hell is not the grave, and the grave is
not hell. In Luke 16, the body is not in hell; the body is in the grave.
The soul is in hell.

This soulish body (with a bodily shape) is an invisible body that cannot
be seen. It is referred to as an "Astral body" among Rosicrucians. The
psychiatrist refers to it as the "ego" or the "I am." But we must never
forget that the word "psychology" is a Greek word compound---"psyche"
meaning "soul," "logia" meaning "knowledge" or "wisdom." The greatest
"psychology" book on knowledge of the soul is the AV 1611. (The works by
Marx, Freud, Pavlov, Jung, and Menninger are very naive and shallow
dissertations alongside an AV 1611!)

The "soul," in the Bible, has a bodily shape, and it leaves the body at
death. (You'll find this reference in Gen. 35:18 when Rachel died; her
soul "was in departing.") It was what Paul referred to when he said,
"The time of my departure is at hand" (2 Tim. 4), and "Having a desire
to depart, and to be with Christ; which is far better" (Phil. 1:23).

The Bible teaches that man has a body, soul, and spirit. The body is a
physical thing which you can see (Greek---soma). Inside this is a
soulish body that fills out the regular body and is invisible. This soul
is filled with the "spirit of man," which is a dead spirit derived from
Adam, and it has no life in it until it is breathed upon by the Holy
Spirit---"That which is born of the Spirit is spirit." Before you were
saved, you were "dead in trespasses and sins" (Eph. 2:1--4). All this
tallies everywhere with the word of God. For example, John said, "No man
hath seen God at any time," referring to the soul of God. Then Jesus
said, "He that hath seen me hath seen the Father," referring to the
body. We find all three of these apparent in Matthew 3 when the Lord
Jesus Christ is baptized. In the last three verses you will find the
Father in heaven (the soul), the Son on the earth (the body), and the
Spirit (the Holy Spirit) communicating between the two. God, being God,
is a trinity who can divide Himself without dying. Man, being a trinity,
cannot split himself without dying. This is why James says, "For as the
body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also"
(James 2:26).

We have a case in the Old Testament of a soul leaving and then coming
back into a man (l Kings 17:22). The child was actually declared dead
and then he returned, as we find it in "modern science." (The case in
the Old Testament is several thousand years ahead of the best medical
science.) The Bible then reveals something unknown to the physical
scientists, and there is nothing in parapsychology, logotherapy, or
psychosomatics that could come anywhere near the correct truth of the
matter. The truth of the matter is that every man is built like a
volleyball. He has an outside leather cover (his body), he has an inside
rubber innertube (his soul---the same shape as the body), and if he is
saved he is filled with air (the Spirit). See 1 Thessalonians 5:23. He's
a "flat tire" if he is unsaved. He is not a complete man until he is
"born of the Spirit." Look at Genesis 2:7.

"And the Lord God formed man of the dust of the ground (body), and
breathed into his nostrils the breath of life (spirit); and man became a
living soul (soul)." (See commentary on Genesis 1--2.)

The soul, then, is a spiritual body---a spiritual body within a physical
body. (If you threw a sheet over it, it would appear as a "ghost."
Hence, the translation "Holy Ghost" in the King James Bible is the
correct translation, and you should ignore the readings of the
Amplified, RSV, and the ASV in making "pneuma" uniformly "Spirit,"
because it is not uniformly "Spirit.") The Spirit is like air or wind
(John 3:6--8), and only refers to that Spirit which fills a container.
The soul (or the "ghost") is the container, and the Lord Jesus Christ
dwells bodily within the believer, as well as spiritually. The "Holy
Ghost" is the correct term, as you find it in the King James, 1611,
Authorized Version.

Now, John saw these souls that were slain, under the altar. These souls
had a voice, they could wear clothes (they had a bodily shape), and they
were killed in a certain manner. Notice:

"Their fellowservants also and their brethren, that should be killed AS
THEY WERE." It is a particular kind of a death. This death is called
"the death" in Revelation 12:11. This particular kind of death is
clearly given in Revelation 20:4, as follows:

"And I saw thrones, and they sat upon them, and judgment was given unto
them: and I saw the souls (Rev. 6:9) of them that were beheaded." They
had their heads cut off! Decapitation is the final form of capital
punishment under Papal Rome, exactly as it was under pagan Rome in the
time of Paul. James, the brother of John, had his head chopped off with
the sword (Acts 12). In Romans 16:4, Paul said some saints "have for my
life laid down their own necks." Paul himself was beheaded at Rome.
Capital punishment in the final days will be by decapittion by a sword
or an ax. This is the standard Roman method, and the Roman lictor had
the ax there where you could see it, for the purpose of capital
punishment---the rod was for beating them and the ax for cutting off
their heads. My! How civilized we are getting! Wasn't Darwin great?
Isn't it strange that the last form of capital punishment isn't the gas
chamber? Or hanging? Or the electric chair? Or any such humane measures?
The last form of death on this earth is decapitation. Exactly like the
Chinese would do it in Viet Nam.

"And when he had opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls
of them that were slain." This altar is plainly a literal temple (as a
passage is always literal if it can be literal). You are unwise to
spiritualize any passage, or make it figurative, if it can be literal.
For example, turn to Revelation 11:1--2. Here you will find there is a
literal temple, a literal court, and a literal altar on this earth at
the Second Coming of Jesus Christ. In Matthew 24:15 you will find the
"holy place" in the literal temple and something standing in it. In 2
Thessalonians 2:3--8 you will find a literal temple with a literal "holy
of holies," and somebody sitting in that temple professing to be God.
All attempts by Catholics to spiritualize these passages are attempts to
attack the word of God and to fight the Holy Spirit. The Bible clearly
teaches that a literal temple will be on this earth at Jerusalem, with a
literal "holy place" and "holy of holies" in it, with a literal Devil
seated in there professing to be God, with a literal altar outside this
temple, and (horror of horrors) people being beheaded as sacrifices at
this altar for the worship of Satan. If this seems wild to you, you
should stop and think a minute. Over one‑fourth of the professing
Christians in the United States profess to believe in a bloody sacrifice
every Sunday morning at eleven o'clock. They profess that their priest
continues the actual sacrifice of Jesus Christ in an "unbloody fashion,"
and then he drinks fermented liquor, pretending that it is the real
blood of Jesus Christ. That is, we have cannibals in America, who came
over here after the Puritan forefathers fled England, and (professing to
be Fifth Amendment people while pleading the rights of the Constitution)
they began to take over the government of the United States. These
people drink blood at eleven o'clock Sunday morning and eat corpses.
They profess that the bread which they eat is the literal corpse of
Jesus Christ! They do not believe that it is a memorial of His death,
but a continuation of His sacrifices. (Any black-robed, Baalite priest
on the face of this earth will tell you that the Roman Catholic mass,
supported by one‑fourth of the professing Christians in America, is a
literal act of eating somebody's literal corpse. So don't be surprised
at the Book of Revelation. It is not too "far out." It is easy to
understand, but it's hard to believe.)

These souls have been sacrificed at an altar, near a temple, by having
their heads cut off. Now, if there is any doubt about this
interpretation, check Leviticus 1 and 2. Here you will find that all
sacrifices have their heads cut off (Lev. 1:8, 15). They are
decapitated. This altar (Rev. 6) is not the earth itself. This is a
literal altar (Rev. 8:5, 11:1--2; 2 Thess. 2:3--8). These souls have
been sacrificed to the Antichrist, who at this time will be in the
temple "showing himself that he is God." The martyrs are on the earth
under the altar, in an intermediate state, as we find in the Old
Testament (Luke 16:22). (In the next chapter, we find them up in
heaven---Rev. 7:9--14.) The same thing applies to another group. In
Revelation 7 we come upon 144,000 who are on the earth; the same company
is found up in the third heaven in Revelation 14:1--4. This means that
people who are killed in the Tribulation get raptured sometime before
the end of the Tribulation. (As I pointed out before, the first
resurrection has three parts:

1\. The "catching up" of the Old Testament saints at the resurrection.

2\. The "catching up" of New Testament saints at the Rapture.

3\. The "catching up" of the Tribulation saints just before the Second
Advent at the end of the Tribulation.)

The martyrs who have their heads cut off in Revelation 6 are up in the
third heaven in Revelation 7, and the 144,000 that are on the earth in
Revelation 7 are up in the third heaven in Revelation 14, clearly
indicating a Post-Tribulation Rapture. This is not a Post‑Tribulation
Rapture of the church. This is a Post‑Tribulation Rapture of Tribulation
saints, specifically Jewish saints, as you'll find it in Matthew
24:29--31.

6:12 "And I beheld when he had opened the sixth seal, and, lo, there was
a great earthquake; and the sun became black as sackcloth of hair, and
the moon became as blood;

13 And the stars of heaven fell unto the earth, even as a fig tree
casteth her untimely figs, when she is shaken of a mighty wind."

Here we have the events that precede the end of the Tribulation. These
events are connected with heavenly phenomena in the sun, moon, and
stars, and they match the warnings of the Lord Jesus Christ in the
gospels (Matt. 24:29; Luke 21:25; Mark 13:24--26; Acts 2). (The reason
why the Postmillennialists and Amillennialists think that the Old
Testament prophecies of a literal, visible Coming of Christ were
fulfilled at the first coming, is that they are blind enough to think
that what Peter quotes in Acts 2 \[referring to Joel, the prophet\] is a
reference to first advent fulfillment. If you read the passage in Acts
2:16--21, you will see that it is a Second Advent promise.)

The references in Acts 2 are dealing with the Second Coming, but at that
time the Lord is about to come back. After Acts 7 He delays, and hence,
all the promises quoted up to Acts 7 have a future fulfillment at the
Second Advent. (But you cannot teach this to a Greek scholar. He is too
dumb to get it. So most Greek scholars have spiritualized the Old
Testament promises to Israel and claimed that they all took place at the
first coming of Jesus Christ. This is a blatant lie, and a denial of
three‑quarters of the Bible.) The heavenly phenomena (Acts 2:16--20)
does not occur until the Second Coming of Christ (as you find it in Rev.
6). Simon Peter quotes it in Acts 2 because, for all practical purposes,
the Lord is about to return; but you cannot teach this to a man who does
