not believe the English.

6:14 "And the heaven departed as a scroll when it is rolled together;
and every mountain and island were moved out of their places.

15 And the kings of the earth, and the great men, and the rich men, and
the chief captains, and the mighty men, and every bondman, and every
free man, hid themselves in the dens and in the rocks of the mountains;

16 And said to the mountains and rocks, Fall on us, and hide us from the
face of him that sitteth on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb:

17 For the great day of his wrath is come; and who shall be able to
stand?"

Notice the word "wrath." If you believe that the heavenly phenomena took
place in Acts 2 because Peter quoted it in Acts 2, you've got problems.
When these heavenly phenomena take place in Revelation 6:12--13, it is
accompanied by the "day of the Lord's wrath," which did not take place
in Acts 2. In Acts 2 the Gospel is preached (as much as they know it to
be at that time), and an offer of salvation is given to Israel, if they
will accept Jesus as their King and Messiah. But the context of
Revelation 6 is the day of God's wrath, and obviously it is not the
first coming. At the first coming, "God so loved the world, that He gave
His only begotten Son," but at the Second Coming (Isa. 63:1--6), you
have the "day of vengeance," the Advent of the Lord to come in wrath (to
tear up the United Nations and the Ecumenical Council!).

"Fall on us, and hide us from the face of him that sitteth on the
throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb." This is not Jesus Christ dying
on a cross and offering salvation! This is the Lamb on a throne, in
wrath, at the Second Advent; it is not the destruction of Jerusalem,
because this was written twenty years after Jerusalem was destroyed!

Revelation 6 takes you clear through the Tribulation. It takes you from
the first year to the last year. (As you get up near the sixth year or
the seventh year, around the eight‑inch mark on your chart, you are
approaching the day of God's wrath.) The Book of Revelation takes you
through the Tribulation several times. Contrary to the common way of
teaching the book, taught by DeHaan, Gaebelein, Pettingill, Scofield,
Larkin, Pember, Sauer, and all Premillennial expositors, the Holy Spirit
takes you clear through the Tribulation four times. The Book of
Revelation gives four accounts of the Second Coming of Christ, exactly
as Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John give four accounts of the first coming
of Christ.

The first account of the Tribulation is Revelation 5--6. (Chapter 7 is
parenthetical, as we will see shortly.) The next complete account of the
Tribulation is found in Revelation 8--11, ending in Revelation 11:15.
(Chapter 10 is a parenthesis.) We have another account of the
Tribulation beginning in Revelation 12, and running through Revelation
14, ending in Revelation 14:20. The last account of the Tribulation is
Revelation 15--19. Each time the Holy Spirit takes you through the
Tribulation, He puts the emphasis on a different thing. For example, in
Revelation 5--6, He takes you through the seals; in Revelation 8--11 He
takes you through the trumpets; in Revelation 12--14, He takes you
through the activities of the Antichrist; and in Revelation 15--19, He
takes you through the seven vials and the destruction of Babylon the
Great.

CHAPTER 7

Now, when we say that Revelation 7 is parenthetical, we mean that in
Revelation 7 there is no mention of the seals; up to here you have been
dealing with the seals. You come through the sixth seal in Revelation
6:12, and yet, the seventh seal does not occur until Revelation 8:1.
Then the events recorded in Revelation 7 are a gap telling about
something that takes place during the Tribulation. It must be something
that takes place during the seals because when you get to Revelation
6:12--17, the Tribulation is over and the wrath of God is coming on the
"day of his wrath." But when we go to Revelation 7, we have a place
where, before all these events take place, 144,000 Jews are "sealed."
So, Revelation 7 is telling you something that takes place during the
six seals. The chronology then closes with the seventh seal at the
beginning of Revelation 8.

7:1 "And after these things I saw four angels standing on the four
corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, that the wind
should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree."

Notice the expression "four corners of the earth," which has led people
to believe that the Bible is unscientific. They say that it is
old‑fashioned. They say, "Look at that dumb nut, John. He spoke about
the 'four corners' of the earth. He believed in the pagan Hebrew concept
of an earth that was flat with four corners." Of course, this is just
the talk of a bunch of twentieth-century idiots trying to kid themselves
because they are living like the Devil and are rejecting what the Bible
has to say about their sins. I passed by a sign at a Marine Recruiting
Station recently which said, "Uncle Sam's Marines are serving the U.S.
in the four corners of the earth." In plainer words, God uses the
expression, taking for granted that you've got enough sense to know what
He means. The four corners are north, south, east, and west. If the
Bible were written in the language of twentieth-century science, it
would be sealed to anyone up until 1890. God uses the popular language
of common ordinary people so that they won't have an alibi when they get
to the judgment. The Bible is not written for a bunch of educated,
egotistical jackasses in 1970, it is written for sinners between Adam
and the end of the Millennium. Consequently, the Bible is written in
plain language that anybody can understand, and often uses common,
ordinary language that is not "scientific," for the very purpose of
getting a scientist to attack it, disbelieve it, and bust his neck on
it, and stumble and fall flat into hell (2 Thess. 2:6--13).

These four corners are north, south, east, and west. (In Isa. 40:22 He
said the earth is a circle. In Luke 17 He says that it is day and night
on both sides of the earth at the same time! So Luke \[A.D. 50\] and
Isaiah \[780 B.C.\] are far ahead of Columbus.)

7:2 "And I saw another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of
the living God: and he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to
whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea,

3 Saying, Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we
have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads."

Then "all hell" cannot break loose against nature and the earth until
this remnant of Israel is sealed. Since "all hell has broken loose" in
Revelation 6:12--15, then Revelation 7 is obviously parenthetical,
telling about something that took place before the seals began to be
opened.

7:4 "And I heard the number of them which were sealed: and there were
sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the
children of Israel."

Now, this is one of the most important passages in the Bible, because
this is the passage used by the Seventh-day Adventists, Mormons, and
Jehovah Witnesses to bolster their absurd "theologies." All of them just
love this passage! They all just love to go beyond the Church Age and
get into a dispensation that no one is in yet, and then pervert the word
of God to teach salvation by works, because they (as all self‑righteous,
unsaved, hell‑bound, lost people) think they are smart and good enough
to make it (with a little help from Jesus Christ).

Notice in the passage that these are sealed servants (vs. 3); "the
servants of our God" is always used of Israel. The manner of their
sealing is found in Ezekiel 9:1--8. They are marked in the forehead with
ink (Ezek. 9:4--8), and they are marked with the name of God the Father,
which is "Jehovah" (see comments in Rev. 14:1). Because this 144,000 is
marked with the name of Jehovah, we have a bunch of Bible-distorting,
self‑righteous sinners in this age who pervert and wrest the word of
God, and steal promises that don't belong to them, and say that the
"grave" is "hell" and that "hell" is the "grave" (when the grave is the
grave, and hell is hell), who run around saying that since the servants
of Revelation 7 have Jehovah in mind that they are Jehovah's
"Witnesses." Therefore, these Watchtower Society people call themselves
"Jehovah's Witnesses," and claim to be the Jews in this passage. Hence,
Rutherford and Russell write, "Millions now living shall never die."
They meant that we are all now in the Tribulation, and that if we
survive until the end we will reign on earth forever in a mortal body.

"Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed
the servants of our God in their foreheads. (See Deut. 32:5.) And I
heard the number of them which were sealed: and there were sealed an
hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of
Israel" (see Isa. 49:6).

Notice, please, the 144,000 are not "Jehovah's Witnesses." Notice they
are not "Seventh-day Adventists." Notice they are not "Christians."
Notice they are not the "Israel of God." Notice, above all, that they
are not "spiritual Jews." They are 144,000 of all the "T‑R‑I‑B‑E‑S of
the children of Israel." They are not spiritual Jews. They are not
spiritual Israelites. They are not Abraham's seed, spiritually. They are
144,000 of the twelve tribes. (You can quote that to a Seventh-day
Adventist, Mormon, Campbellite, or Jehovah's Witness until you are black
and blue in the face, and the saphead will never believe that it means
what it says, where it says it, because he has been taught that it
doesn't mean what it says, as it says it, where it says it. I'm so hard
about it because my patience gave out long ago. I've dealt by the hour
with these men who profess to be authorities on the word of God, and
there is one thing that characterized every single one of them; they
\[sooner or later\] reach some passage where they say that it means
something that it doesn't say.) This passage says "144,000 of all the
tribes of the children of Israel." Therefore, it is the "tribes of the
children of Israel." It is not the "tribes of the spiritual believers."
It is not the "children of spiritual Israel." It is not the "spiritual
Jew." It is not Pogo. It is not Little Lord Fauntleroy. It is not "Gone
with the Wind." It is not "Captains Courageous." It is not Moron or
Moroni. It is not Joe Smith and his gold plates. It is 144,000 of all
the tribes of the children of Israel. That's it! You don't have to
interpret it; all you have to do is read. You don't have to understand;
all you have to do is believe. The trouble is that people have been fed
so much religious slop, tripe, chittlins, and garbage that they can't
believe what they read. The passage says that it is "144,000 of all of
the tribes of the children of Israel," and if the Holy Spirit says that
it is the "tribes of the children of Israel," do you know what it is? It
is "the tribes of the children of Israel!" Do you know how I know that?
Because it says it!

7:5 "Of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of
Reuben were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Gad were sealed
twelve thousand.

6 Of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of
Nepthalim were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Manasses were
sealed twelve thousand.

7 Of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of
Levi were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Issachar were sealed
twelve thousand.

8 Of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of
Joseph were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed
twelve thousand."

There were twelve thousand from each tribe---Juda, Reuben, Gad, Aser,
Nepthalim, Manasses, Simeon, Levi, Issachar, Zabulon, Joseph, and
Benjamin. There are 144,000 literal, visible children of Israel, from
each one of the literal, visible twelve tribes, and they are named.

Now in the passage, observe that there have been several substitutions.
In verse 8, we have the "tribe of Joseph" given. Yet Joseph had two
sons, Ephraim and Manasseh---Manasses is found in verse 6, but Ephraim
is omitted---so we find the word Joseph substituted for Ephraim. We also
find in the passage (in vs. 7), Levi as one of the twelve tribes. Yet,
Levi was the thirteenth, or the priestly tribe; Levi has been
substituted for another tribe that is missing. The tribe that is missing
is Dan. Two tribes are missing in the list---Ephraim and Dan. (You can
study about Ephraim in the Book of Hosea.) The "Mormons" or "Latter Day
Saints" claim to come from Ephraim, which is rather peculiar (Hosea
11:12). Ephraim is not sealed in the Tribulation; their name is blotted
out under heaven in the Book of Hosea. They are said to be "joined to
idols" and God says, "Leave them alone!!" (Hosea 4:17). So we find
Joseph replacing Ephraim and Levi replacing Dan. (The references which
explain this are Amos 8:14; Deut. 29:20; Lev. 24:11.)

Very briefly, let us study why the tribe of Dan has a place in the
prophecies of God that no other tribe has. (It is definitely a bad
place.) The first time in the Bible that there is any mention of the
tribe of Dan is Genesis 14:14. Here you are told about the location of
his city. (Gen. 14:14 is one of the supposed proofs that Moses did not
write Genesis. Here is the reasoning. The twelve tribes of Israel did
not settle the land of Palestine until the Book of Joshua, after Moses
was dead, so Moses could not have known where the city of Dan was. In
Genesis 14:14 you are told that the city of Dan was located in north
Palestine, up near Damascus \[vs. 15\]. The scholars say that Moses
could not have known, and therefore, Moses did not write Genesis; but
this overlooks two things. It overlooks the fact that Joshua was Moses'
minister and had the same Spirit on him and the same commission to
continue what Moses had started. It also omits the fact that before
Moses died he was taken up on Pisgah, east of Jordan, and was shown all
the promised land by God, "even to Dan!"

Deuteronomy 34:1, "And Moses went up from the plains of Moab unto the
mountain of Nebo, to the top of Pisgah, that is over against Jericho.
And the Lord shewed him all the land of Gilead, unto Dan."

Therefore, Moses knew where the place was. The only problem which comes
up is whether he wrote the passage in Genesis 14:14 after God showed it
to him. If he did, he wrote it right before he died. Be that as it may,
whether the Mosaic authorship includes every single word and verse in
Genesis or not, Moses is still the author of the book, and any additions
to it are going to be taken care of by Joshua, because Joshua had the
same commission. In Deuteronomy 34:9 you find this where he continues,
and also in the Book of Joshua.)

"Dan" is first mentioned in Genesis 14:14, and the context is the
rebellion of nine kings (with the number 13 appearing the first time in
your Bible (verse 4). This is where the "giants," the remnant of "the
Sons of God" (vs. 5) are found, the "Rephaims" and the "Emims" (and
where Lot is also found, who is said to be present at the Second Coming
of Christ, in type \[Luke 17:28--30\]). So Dan's first appearance is
here. Of course, here he is mentioned as a city, which is not founded
until later, but here the name is just given as a location reference.

Dan is mentioned, by birth, in Genesis 30:6. Dan is the first son that
Rachel has. She has this son by "proxy."

She has this son by her handmaid, out of the will of God. Bragging,
because she is jealous of her sister (vs. 1), she says, "God hath judged
me" (which He had), "and hath also heard my voice" (which He had), "and
hath given me a son," which He did not. (Rachel makes the same mistake
that Eve makes when Eve said, "I have gotten a man from the Lord." She
has a man physically, but this man is wrong spiritually.) The first son
that Rachel has is Dan, which means "judge" (Gen. 30:6).

The first prophecy found on Dan is given when Jacob is dying. Jacob
says:

"Dan shall judge his people, as one of the tribes of Israel" (Gen.
49:16). Not saying that he is one of the tribes, but that he will judge
as one of the twelve tribes of Israel.

"Dan shall be a serpent by the way, an adder in the path, that biteth
the horse heels, so that his rider shall fall backward" (Gen. 49:17).

This tribe has a rough future. In Genesis 49:17 Dan is called a
"serpent," and in Deuteronomy 33:22 he is called a "lion." The only
person we know in the Bible who is like unto a lion and a serpent is
Satan. (Jesus is called "the lion of the tribe of Juda," but is never
likened to a serpent, except in His death on the cross, John 3:14.) The
Devil is called a "roaring lion" in 1 Peter 5:8, and is called a
"serpent" in Revelation 12:9. This is Dan's connection. If this seems
farfetched, let us continue our study of Dan and see just how farfetched
it really is.

In the Book of Leviticus, which deals with all the priestly rules and
regulations, we have an incident mentioned in detail concerning a man
who cursed, and then his punishment is given.

"And the son of an Israelitish woman, whose father was an Egyptian
\[Notice the Hamitic influence. It's a mixed marriage, where the father
is from Ham and the mother is from Israel.\] went out among the children
of Israel: and this son of the Israelitish woman and a man of Israel
strove together in the camp; And the Israelitish woman's son blasphemed
the name of the Lord, and cursed. And they brought him unto Moses: (and
his mother's name was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of
Dan:)" (Lev. 24:10--11).

This is the first actual case mentioned in the Bible of cursing. The one
case of cursing under the Mosaic Law (that is mentioned by name) is a
case of a man from the tribe of Dan, blaspheming the name of the Lord.
(We'll get into the significance of this when we get into Rev. 13, where
the Antichrist lifts up his voice "to blaspheme" those that dwell in
heaven.)

We find, therefore, that the tribe of Dan has a rather peculiar place
among the twelve tribes. We notice that the one judge of Israel who
really "messed up" in a big way, and finally committed suicide, was from
this tribe (Judg. 13:2). You know the man as Samson, and his history is
found in Judges 13--16. But we are not through with the tribe of Dan
yet. In Judges 17:1 the tribe of Ephraim pops up. (Remember, in the list
we are studying in Rev. 7, Ephraim has been replaced by Joseph, and Dan
has been replaced by Levi.) Now, watch Levi, Ephraim, and Dan all pop up
together right in the middle of Judges 17--18.

"And there was a man of mount Ephraim, whose name was Micah" (Judg.
17:1). His mother cursed (or swore) about a vow she had made---Judges
17:2. This was because she had dedicated money to "make a graven image
and a molten image," as an "aid to worship"---Judges 17:3. This house
where Micah lived was a "house of gods." (It had a "Via Dolorosa" with
images all around, as kind of an "aid to worship"!)

"And there was a young man out of Bethlehem-judah of the family of
Judah, who was a Levite, and he sojourned there\....And Micah said unto
him, Dwell with me, and be unto me a FATHER and a PRIEST, and I will
give thee ten shekels of silver by the year, and a suit of apparel, and
thy victuals. So the Levite went in" (Judg. 17:7, 10).

Here, more than 1,200 years before Christ, the outstanding pawn in the
Roman Catholic system suddenly pops up! Don't ever let this bunch of
black‑robed, Halloween "hoods" kid you into thinking that they are the
official priesthood which Christ established on earth. Christ
established no priesthood on earth but a priesthood of believers. (Simon
Peter confirmed this in 1 Peter 2:1--8 with words so clear that you
don't need any interpretation; all you have to do is read. If there is
some Catholic reading this, I realize, of course, that you have your
problems. I was Episcopalian and Catholic before I was saved, but I
reached the place where I could either believe the Bible, or believe the
priest; I chose the Bible. So if you think what I am giving you is
private interpretation, I know why. You're scared! You've been put
"under the Anathema." You're afraid if you leave the "church" you are
going to get cursed. You're afraid that if you listen to me you are
going to get a Protestant's "interpretation." I know your position.
You've been taught that your salvation is connected with the Roman
Church, and you're afraid of your own church. If I was afraid of my
church, I would get out of it.)

In this passage, in Judges 17:10 (I'm reading. I'm not "interpreting"
anything.), Micah says, "Dwell with me, and be unto me a father and a
priest." Is that clear? The first time that a priest was ever called
"father," the first time that a "father" was a priest, the first time a
man ever asked a priest to be a "father" for him, and the first time
that a man ever called a priest by the name of "father," is Judges
17:10. You don't find anything like this in the Book of Acts, Romans,
Matthew, Mark, Luke, or John, except where Jesus said, "And call no man
your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven"
(Matt. 23:9). (Jesus was correcting these black‑robed, Baalite
"priests.") You never read in the New Testament that you were to call
some priest "father," but you find it in Judges 17:10. Also in the
context, is a museum of idols as an "aid to worship." The chapter,
strangely enough, has thirteen verses in it, and concludes with Micah
saying, "Now know I that the Lord will do me good (not because I believe
the word of God, not because I have assurance of eternal life, based on
God's word, but) seeing I have a Levite to my priest." The fellow
thought surely God would do him good, since he found somebody to dump
the blame on, and bear the load for him, and argue the Bible in his
place, and answer the "heretics" for him so that he wouldn't have to
study the Bible himself!!

Now watch Dan enter.

"In those days there was no king in Israel: and in those days the tribe
of the Danites sought them an inheritance to dwell in; for unto that day
all their inheritance had not fallen unto them among the tribes of
Israel" (Judg. 18:1).

Along come the children of Dan, travelling northward, and they come
right up to Micah's house (Judg. 18:3--4). They talk with this priest
called "father." They go their way, and they go up by the Zidonians
(Judg. 18:7). (Zidon is up on the western coast of Palestine, north of
the Sea of Galilee.) Then they come back (18:11), and in going to battle
(18:13), they come the third time to Micah's house, go in, and talk to
the priest again.

"And they said unto him, Hold thy peace, lay thine hand upon thy mouth,
and go with us, and be to us a father and a priest: is it better for
thee to be a priest unto the house of one man, or that thou be a priest
unto a tribe and a family in Israel?" (Judg. 18:19).

So this priest goes with the tribe of Dan, and they go up, and they
dwell in the city called "Laish" (18:27) and call it "Dan" (18:29), and
the tribe of Dan is in total apostasy from this date (1300 B.C.) up to
800 B.C., when the northern tribes go into captivity! For more than 400
years this tribe is in apostasy and is in fellowship with a bunch of
Zidonian Baal‑worshippers; and they establish the world famous "Micah
religion."

"And he said, Ye have taken away my gods which I made, and the priest,
and ye are gone away: and what have I more? and what is this that ye say
unto me, What aileth thee?" (Judg. 18:24).

You can always tell "Micah religion." It was the religion of Adolph
Hitler, Johnny Kennedy, Mussolini, Bloody Mary, DeGaulle, and Castro.
The way you spot a "Micah worshipper" is that you can steal his
religion. (If there is some Roman Catholic reading this, I am going to
be just as dead serious with you as I can for a minute, and you can get
just as mad as you want to, and just blow your top, and strip your
little gears, and blow out a valve, and burn out your clutch plate, or
blow a gasket. But here is one of the straightest propositions that you
ever had in your life, and if you don't at least listen to the
proposition, you are nothing but a lowdown scalawag. Here is the
proposition; if someone threw your candles in the garbage, and burned
your rosary, and dumped your "holy water" out, and killed your priest,
and burned your cathedral, and dumped your fermented liquor in the sink,
and took your Latin Vulgate \[or RSV\] and put it in the furnace, and
took your missal and threw it in the ocean, where would your religion
be? Now, you stop and think about that! If all your idols and candles
and beads and priests and popes and cardinals and Bibles and missals
were gone, what would you have left? Now, don't duck and use sophistry
and casuistry and try to make us think that you are real smart. Let me
ask you something, what would you have left? Suppose someone took the
cup away permanently that you take your "mass" in, and took away the
"host" you eat, what would you have left? You believe that that liquor
in that cup and that bread is Jesus Christ. Suppose someone pours that
fermented liquor down the sink, throws the bread out the window, and
shoots the priest. \[I'm not going to, but just suppose someone did.\]
Tell me something---what have you got left? You know what would be left?
You'd have nothing but a poor, godless, civilized pagan, going to hell
so fast that he would slide into it like a greased pig on ball bearings,
and he'd bust a hole in hell so wide it would take the Devil six months
to get the thing patched up after he came through!)

Now, listen to me. If you burn my hymn book, I have the songs memorized,
and I have a "song in my heart" (Col. 3:16). If you burned my Bible,
I've hid it "in my heart that I might not sin against Him" (Psa.
119:11). You can't kill my priest, because He is in heaven (Heb.
3:1--2). If you burned down my church building, I would worship God "in
spirit and in truth," on the street corner or in my backyard (John
4:24). You cannot burn my candles because I have none to burn. You
cannot destroy my rosary because I'm not interested in one. You cannot
break my images because I don't have any, and (If I had any I'd hang
them upside down by their heels from my dashboard!) you cannot take my
"cardinals," I don't have any. You cannot kill my "pope" because to me a
pope is just another liquor‑head over in Rome, trying to act like a
"little Jesus," and taking advantage of ignorant people like you. You
can't steal my "religion" from me; my Saviour is inside me. The only way
you can take my religion from me is to kill me, which is exactly what
Roman Catholics have done to Bible believers for one thousand years, and
will start doing again when they take over! You see, someone can steal
your "religion" because your "religion" is just made of bones, beads,
sinners, candles, trash, marbles, and matches. But you can't take mine.
The only way you can get rid of my religion, you old Baalite, is to kill
me! (And I'll bet some of you would like to do it!) And I know why some
of you would like to kill people like Ruckman, Paisley, Montano,
Blanchard, Manhattan, and Zachello. You see, you have a little old
"stick‑horse religion" that anyone can burn in the fire, but you can't
burn my religion in the fire unless you burn me in the fire, because my
Saviour is inside me! Why don't you stand up on your hind legs like the
sinner you are and receive Jesus Christ as a Person instead of a piece
of bread? Why don't you buy a Bible, believe it, and get to work for
God, instead of running around hiding behind Micah's "father"? He's no
"father" of a Bible believer. We Christians have one Father, and He's in
heaven.

Returning to our text, the last time you left the tribe of Dan (in
Judges), they were up near Zidon loafing around with a pack of
Phoenician Baal‑worshippers. However, when you get up to the Book of 1
Kings, you really pick them up in all their glory. Here we begin to
learn about the kind of people the tribe of Dan was "hanging out" with,
and we find ample reason why Dan should be omitted in the Book of
Revelation.

"And in the thirty and eighth year of Asa king of Judah began Ahab the
son of Omri to reign over Israel: and Ahab the son of Omri reigned over
Israel in Samaria twenty and two years. And Ahab the son of Omri did
evil in the sight of the Lord above all that were before him. And it
came to pass, as if it had been a light thing for him to walk in the
sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, that he took to wife Jezebel the
daughter of Ethbaal king of the Zidonians, and went and served Baal, and
worshipped him. And he reared up an altar for Baal in the house of Baal,
which he had built in Samaria. And Ahab made a grove; and Ahab did more
to provoke the Lord God of Israel to anger than all the kings of Israel
that were before him" (1 Kings 16:29--33).

In this passage, all Israel (the northern tribes) adopts Baal worship,
whereas before, it had only been Dan, one of the ten northern tribes.
Israel gets "in cahoots" with a Baal‑worshipping king, married to a
Zidonian Baal worshipper, Jezebel. There is no doubt about what kind of
religion this is; it is Baal worship, and is warned against throughout
the Bible. Notice "the grove" which Ahab builds; it is forbidden in
Deuteronomy 16:21--22. This kind of Baal‑worship is characterized by
little statues set up with groves and bushes growing around them. (My!
My! I wonder who would do a thing like that!)

In 1 Kings 18, let us mark the other characteristics of this
Baal‑worship. In 1 Kings 18:10, notice that the black‑robed priests who
are called "father" (the prophets of Baal) are in close fellowship with
the king and queen. They are primarily a political group. (Elijah is the
true prophet in the passage, but he is not in good with "the powers that
be" at all.) Vatican Imperialism in the Twentieth Century, by Avro
Manhattan, Zondervan Publishing Company, New York, 1964, will show you
this relationship that the Catholic Church has with politics. (The
favorable relationship of the Catholic Church with Nazism began in Rome,
with a succession of military victories by Hitler in 1938 and 1939. The
pope ordered the entire Roman Catholic hierarchy to stop criticizing the
German Third Reich and to offer "prayers for the Fuhrer." By the way,
the present "pope," Montini, from 1938--1948, was doing his ground work
in Germany as Secretariat for the Vatican in preparation for the work
that he is going to do now to get Germany back under the fold of the
Roman Catholic Church. Hence, both leaders of Germany, Erhardt and
Adenauer, were Roman Catholics.)

These Baal‑worshippers (1 Kings 18) were in good with the king and the
queen. As "priests," they were not primarily religious; they were
political. Their religion was merely "a means to an end." Notice that
they worship Baal (1 Kings 18:25), and their proof of being "the one
true church" is fire coming down from heaven to answer their prayers,
supposedly. Baal, then, is the "god of fire," in most religions the sun
god. (Astaroth, in the Old Testament, is the moon goddess, "the queen of
heaven." Where have I heard that before? Then, there is Baal, the god of
thunder or the god of fire.) The word "Baal" in English eventually
becomes "bull." In 1 Kings 18:26, we read that the worship services of
the Baal‑worshippers last until 12 o'clock noon. Hence, all Protestants
today hold Sunday morning service from 11 a.m. to 12 noon to honor the
sun god, Baal! Sunday, 11 a.m. to 12 noon. In the text, the priests
mutilate themselves to get their prayers answered, they do penance, and
they cut themselves to try to get an answer to prayer. (This is a common
practice in the Philippines, Spain, and Italy to this day, and it is
characteristic of demon possession in Mark 5:1--5.)

There can be no doubt in the Bible at all about the identification of
Baal‑worshippers, and we can locate them early in history. They have
priests who are called "father," they worship the sun god on Sunday
until 12 o'clock noon, and they wear a particular kind of robe called a
"vestment" (2 Kings 10:22).

"Call now, if there be any that will answer thee; and to which of the
saints wilt thou turn?" Job 5:1.

You know how this verse applies. Someone is praying to the "saints."
(Job was written 1,500 years before Christ, and here we have this same
group of godless Baal worshippers; so when Job gets into trouble, one of
his friends asks Job, "To which of the saints wilt thou turn?" This
implies that ancient people did pray to dead saints.)

"Behold, he putteth no trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not
clean in his sight" Job 15:15.

Here, we find that this doctrine of "calling on the saints" is
corrected. This is a passage that every Catholic should get a hold of as
soon as possible. God puts no trust in His saints. God wouldn't trust
St. Jude or St. Christopher as far as you could kick your house with an
arthritic left foot. If He had trusted Simon Peter, He would have been
disappointed because Simon Peter cursed and swore. If He had trusted
Paul, He would have been disappointed because Paul got out of the will
of God by going to Jerusalem. If He had trusted Noah, He would have been
disappointed because Noah got drunk. If He had trusted Matthew, Mark,
and John, He would have been disappointed because they all forsook Him
and fled. If He had put His trust in Joseph, He would have been
disappointed because Joseph lied about the cup. And don't you think for
a minute that God Almighty trusts "blessed John," "blessed Mary,"
"blessed Joseph," or "blessed John, the Baptist" any further than you
could throw a freight train. He doesn't, brother. "Behold, he putteth no
trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not clean in his sight."

The Bible has scores of verses dealing with this Baal‑worship. Of
course, they are mainly in the Old Testament. Since the Roman Catholic
Church teaches that the Old Testament "is fulfilled in the New
Testament," and that the promises to Israel have been "spiritualized"
for the Christian, nobody reads the Old Testament anymore. But, in the
Old Testament is found the beginning of the so‑called "Catholic" Church.

Baal practices are found in Jeremiah 10:1--5, where we have the
Christmas tree! (The giving of gifts every December 25th, of course, is
from the Roman, pagan celebration, "Saturnalia," which was a drunken
orgy, commemorating the first of the year.) The Druids called the
mistletoe, used at Christmas, "all heil," or "all hail," as in "Heil
Hitler," or "Hail Caesar." They called it "all heil" after the Romans'
victory laurel wreath placed on the head. Edmond Sears, a Unitarian
minister of western Massachusetts, was the man who wrote "It Came Upon a
Midnight Clear, That Glorious Song of Old," and Edmond Sears didn't even
believe in the Deity of Christ or the Virgin Birth or that Christ was
God. In the Catholic Church (the "Holy Trinity Church" of Boston),
"Catholics" had the first Christmas pageant. St. Francis had the first
"nativity" scene in 1223, and an Irish Catholic brought laurel wreaths
to his church and decorated it. "Sante Klaas," a Dutch Catholic bishop,
is the man for whom "St. Nicholas" is named; he gave presents to the
poor on December 5th, and this date was moved up to December 25th to
match the birthday of the sun god. December 25th is the winter solstice
when the earth is farthest from the sun.

This Baal worship includes not only a male deity, but a female deity.
This female deity is called the "queen of heaven" (Jer. 44). (In 1845
they found a temple built to Diana in Marseille, France, and they found
the inscription in it: "Hephizi‑Baal is the judge of the holy queen. The
holy queen receives the cakes baked for the royal worship.")

In The Glories of Mary (published in 1931 by the Roman Catholic Rev. A.
B. Koonz, CSSR, Bensinger Bros., 78 Park Place, New York), the author
says on page 32, "All graces are dispensed by Mary. She is a mediatrix
of peace between sinners and God. Sinners receive pardon by the
intercession of Mary alone. At the command of Mary, all obey---even God.
There is no salvation outside of Mary, who is the gate to heaven." (See
page 169, page 160, page 83, page 81.) This book is "Nihil Obstat," with
the imprimatur of a cardinal, that is, it contains "no objectionable
material"! You Catholics, who have hidden hatred in your heart against
Protestant Christianity and Bible‑believing Christians, should know that
in 1931, in an official publication of the Roman Catholic Church,
officially endorsed by its censoring official, God obeys Mary and that
there is no salvation outside of Mary, "who is the gate to heaven"! As
far as a Christian is concerned, Mary was no one but an Old Testament
Jewish woman who needed a Saviour. I am no more counting on Mary to get
me to heaven than I am counting on a caterpillar taking me to Berlin by
way of Belfast. Mary was a sinner (Luke 1:47), who needed a Saviour
(Luke 1:47), and needed a sacrifice (Luke 2:24), as a purification for
her sins (Luke 2:22). If you don't know this, it is because you don't
want to know it; the reason why you don't want to know it is because of
sin and wilful ignorance.

John 3:19, "And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the
world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds
were evil."

John 3:20, "For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither
cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved."

History presents another picture of Baalism in America. There was an
image for Fatima in a public school stadium in New Jersey (about ten
years ago) made out of clay, and one million Catholics came into that
stadium and kissed the clay toes on that thing until they fell clear off
it! And that hasn't been ten years ago!

In Jeremiah 44:17--19, 25, this goddess is called the "queen of heaven."
The Romans call her "Venus." The Greeks call her "Diana." The
Phoenicians call her "Astaroth." The Assyrians call her "Astarte." The
Greeks call her "Minerva," "Juno," or "Hera," but the Bible calls her
that "Harlot," "the Mother of abominations" (Rev. 17). The Roman "popes"
call her "Mary," and identify her with the woman of Revelation 12:1--2,
which is not Mary at all, but Israel (see comments on Rev. 12:1--2).

We have now gone briefly through the reasons why the tribe of Dan is
omitted from the sealing that takes place in the Tribulation in
Revelation 7.

Now, reading again over the list, we find all the tribes are present
except Dan and Ephraim. Joseph is the substitution for Ephraim, and Levi
is the substitution for Dan. Let us repeat, this is 144,000 of all the
tribes of the children of Israel (Rev. 7:4). (I keep repeating this
because it is disgusting and nearly intolerable, in these last days, to
hear men \[who believe the Bible\] changing it and changing it, with no
right or authority to change it. Every false doctrine that a man ever
got into, every phony leading which he did not get from the word of God,
came from changing what it said.) What it says is that these people in
Revelation 7:4--8 are 144,000 literal children of Israel from the
literal twelve tribes who are named. They are not Seventh-day
Adventists; they are not Jehovah's Witnesses; they are not Latter Day
Saints; they are not Mormons; they are not Christians. They are what
they are, according to what it said they were, and I don't care if the
Roman Catholic Church interprets them as "spiritual Jews" or as
"Buddhists." If any man says they are not what it says they are, that
man is a liar, and I wouldn't care if he was "Pope On Too Long," the
215½.

7:9 "After this I beheld, and, lo, a great multitude, which no man could
number, of all nations, and kindreds, and people, and tongues, stood
before the throne, and before the Lamb, clothed with white robes, and
palms in their hands."

The "I," of course, is John, speaking in the first person, and he is in
the third heaven when he says this.

Notice, the first group which he saw was the tribes of the children of
Israel. The second group is of "all nations, and kindreds, and people,
and tongues"---Gentiles. (This throne is in the third heaven, and so is
the Lamb, as in Rev. 5--6.) We cannot help but connect these "clothed
with white robes" with those mentioned in Revelation 6:11. We also
identify these with Revelation 3:4 and Revelation 3:18. So these white
robes are given, evidently, to martyrs. They represent human
righteousness, as in Revelation 19:8, where it says that white linen is
the "righteousness of saints." Therefore, the robes have to do with
works after salvation, and not the righteousness of Jesus Christ
Himself. In Revelation 7:9, then, God allows us to see a great multitude
of people who have been killed, and they are under "the altar" in
Revelation 6, but in chapter 7 they are pictured in the third heaven.

7:10 "And cried with a loud voice, saying, Salvation to our God which
sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb.

11 And all the angels stood round about the throne, and about the elders
and the four beasts, and fell before the throne on their faces, and
worshipped God,

12 Saying, Amen: Blessing, and glory, and wisdom, and thanksgiving, and
honour, and power, and might, be unto our God for ever and ever. Amen."

There is no doubt about the change in position of these martyrs from
earth to heaven. The location is the same as you found it in Revelation
4:1--7---the third heaven. In Revelation 4:1--7 you have the elders, the
four beasts, and the throne.

7:13 "And one of the elders answered, saying unto me, What are these
which are arrayed in white robes? and whence came they?

14 And I said unto him, Sir, thou knowest. And he said to me, These are
they which came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes,
and made them white in the blood of the Lamb."

"Sir, thou knowest." In plainer words, "I don't know, but you know."

This passage could be applied to a Christian in the Church Age,
spiritually, but it cannot be applied to him doctrinally. The
Christian's righteousness is the Lord Jesus Christ, and the Christian's
"white robes" are his own righteousnesses which he earns after he is
saved, and his salvation is not conditional on works. In Revelation
7:14, their robes were made "white in the blood of the Lamb." It isn't
that they were saved by the blood of the Lamb, or that they were "washed
in the blood of the Lamb." They have washed their robes, and made them
(robes) "white in the blood of the Lamb."

Notice, "these are they which came out of great tribulation"---a special
time. Paul says, "We must through much tribulation enter into the
kingdom of God" (Acts 14:22), and "To you who are troubled rest with us,
when the Lord Jesus shall be revealed from heaven" (2 Thess. 1:7). But,
this (Rev. 7) is "great tribulation," and to make this tribulation refer
to the trials and testings and tribulations of the Christian's life in
the Church Age is a Scriptural perversion. This (Rev. 7) is a definite
period of time, spoken of in a dozen places, and it is never connected
with the tribulations through which God's people go as Christians. It is
always connected with a period of time in which Israel (144,000) is
saved.

Matthew 24:21, "For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not
since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be."

Again, you cannot make this refer to the tribulations through which the
Christian passes. The "this time" is the Second Coming of Christ.

Matthew 24:29, "Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall
the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the
stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be
shaken."

This is the heavenly phenomena which we read about at the end of
Revelation 6. Again, the context is not the Church Age.

Matthew 24:30, "And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in
heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall
see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great
glory."

The context of Matthew 24 is the context of Revelation 7. We have "a
great multitude out of every nation, and tongue, and people," and we
have the "t‑r‑i‑b‑e‑s." These people are saved out of a period of time
called "the great tribulation," which precedes the Second Advent of
Jesus Christ, and recognition of this truth establishes the
Premillennial system of interpretation as the only correct Bible system.
(The Amillennial and Postmillennial Catholics, Presbyterians, Baptists,
and Methodists can bawl until they are blue in the face about "conceited
people" thinking "their interpretation is the only one," and "making fun
of real Christians" by claiming to have the only true system of
interpretation, but the bloody fact remains that Scripture with
Scripture, without changing the words \[or changing the verses\], verse
with verse, as they occur, where they occur, as you find them, where
anybody can read them, teaches that the Second Coming of Jesus Christ is
preceded by a period of time called the "great tribulation," which deals
with Israel, the twelve tribes, and that a multitude of people that are
saved out of this Tribulation \[in addition to Israel\], are not
Church‑Age Christians.)

"Have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the lamb."
My robes have not been washed and been made white in the blood of the
Lamb; I've been washed in the blood of the Lamb (Rev. 1:5). "Study to
shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be
ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth" (2 Tim. 2:15). This thought
brings out another great truth about Bible study; it is that the
difference between correct Bible exegesis and incorrect Bible exegesis
is respect and reverence for the English text, as it is found. The only
way you can ever get it right is to believe what it says, as it says it,
where you find it, exactly as it said it. The difference in the
Christian method of learning and teaching the Bible and the standard
systematic theologies taught in the schools, is that the men who teach
the standard theologies do not believe what they are reading and
teaching is the word of God. If He said in one place, "Unto him that
loved us, and washed us from our sins in his own blood," and another
place, "And have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of
the Lamb," then these are two different references. The reason I believe
this is because they are two different sets of words, in two different
places, referring to two different sets of people, at two different
times.

7:15 "Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve him day and
night in his temple: and he that sitteth on the throne shall dwell among
them."

There is another reason why this "multitude" cannot be Church‑Age
Christians. These saints serve Him "day and night in his temple." Now
the Christian's body is the temple of the Holy Ghost in this age, and
when he gets to heaven, to New Jerusalem, there is no temple. The Bride
of Christ, the church (2 Cor. 11:1--4; Eph. 5) is called "New Jerusalem"
in Revelation 21, and this city is said to be without a temple (Rev.
21:22). Therefore, this multitude saved in Revelation 7:14--15 cannot
possibly be Church‑Age Christians; they are saved Tribulation saints.
(On the second chart that you've drawn, these would be saved and
raptured somewhere around the seven and one-half‑inch or the eight‑inch
mark.)

7:16 "They shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more; neither shall
the sun light on them, nor any heat."

The cross references for this verse are Isaiah 25:10 and Isaiah 49:10.
You will find that in both of these references, the message is to Israel
in the Millennium not to the Christian in eternity.

7:17 "For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them,
and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe
away all tears from their eyes."

This is the first mention of the fact that God is going "to wipe away"
any tears from anybody's eyes; it is not mentioned again until
Revelation 21:4. Notice that there are two times when tears are
mentioned as "being wiped away"---both occur after the Church Age is
over. (You may die and get to heaven, but the old song "No Tears in
Heaven" is a lie. There are tears in heaven in Rev. 7, and there are
tears in heaven in Rev. 21.) Now, there are no tears in eternity.
Eternity is just beginning in Revelation 21, and it hasn't started in
Revelation 7. In Revelation 21:4, when God wipes away tears, the context
is immediately following the judgment of the unsaved dead (Rev.
20:12--15). God does not wipe away all tears for the final time until
after the Great White Throne Judgment.

In Revelation 7:17 (which is the seven‑inch or the eight‑inch mark on
your chart), you will notice that these tears are not wiped away until
the end of the Tribulation. (Right above your chart, somewhere around
the five‑inch mark, draw a throne and call it the "Judgment Seat of
Christ." Notice, at the one‑inch mark you had an arrow going up to that
throne, picturing the Rapture of the Church at the end of the Church
Age.) This means that at the end of the Church Age, the Body of Christ
is going to be "caught out" and "caught up," and, while the Tribulation
takes place down on this earth, the Body of Christ is going to be judged
at the Judgment Seat of Christ in heaven. The tears are not "wiped away"
until the end of the Tribulation (Rev. 7:17), and this occurs at a time
when a company of post‑Tribulation saints have been "caught up" to the
throne. So, there are two times when tears are wiped away, and these two
places occur:

1\. Following the Judgment Seat of Christ.

2\. Following the Great White Throne Judgment.

This means that there is going to be weeping and wailing among the
born‑again Christians at the Judgment Seat of Christ and at the Great
White Throne Judgment; there is going to be weeping and wailing of
Tribulation and Millennium saints. When you get to heaven, your tears
are not over, as a Christian, until the Judgment Seat of Christ is
passed. (And there may be some "bawling" at the Great White Throne
Judgment when you see unsaved loved ones going out forever into hell.)
Now, it is true that in Revelation 20 you'll be like Christ and you'll
have "the mind of Christ," but Christ wept over sinners, and Christ wept
over Jerusalem.

Matthew 23:37--39, "O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the
prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I
have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her
chickens under her wings, and ye would not! Behold, your house is left
unto you desolate. For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth,
till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord."

There shall be weeping in heaven on two occasions. One time when you see
what you could have done for Christ and didn't do---this will take place
at the Judgment Seat of Christ; secondly, when you see unsaved people to
whom you could have witnessed, perishing and going to hell because you
didn't witness to them. In plainer words, God's people aren't through
with tears until Revelation 21. There's going to be weeping at the
Judgment Seat of Christ, when we see our loss of rewards, the crowns we
could have won that we didn't win, the time we lost that we could have
redeemed and the wasted opportunities. There is going to be crying again
at the Great White Throne Judgment when we see unsaved loved ones,
friends, relatives, and neighbors cast into the Lake of Fire, when some
of us did not do our very best to win them to Jesus Christ. The moral of
these two passages is very clear, practically. Do the very best you can
to win people to Jesus Christ. When you have prayed for them all you
can, witnessed to them all that you are able to, given them tracts as
occasions are provided, given them the whole testimony and the clear
counsel of God and the plan of salvation as clearly as you can give it,
then don't worry about it; turn it over to God and forget it. But, until
you have done these things, remember that there are "tears in heaven."
The song "No Tears in Heaven" is just like the song "Wait a Little
Longer, Please Jesus." They are both non‑Scriptural, non‑Christian
doggerel. There will be weeping at the Judgment Seat of Christ and at
the Great White Throne Judgment.

CHAPTER 8

8:1 "And when he had opened the seventh seal, there was silence in
heaven about the space of half an hour."

We are now about to start through the Tribulation for the second time.
This time it is described in Revelation 8--11 under the figure of
"trumpets."

There has been no satisfactory interpretation written on this verse
because there has not been found yet any other verse in the Bible to go
with it. Some people believe that the seventh seal is opened at the end
of the Tribulation, and that the "silence in heaven" is because the
conquering hosts are on their way back down to earth, with Christ at
their head (Rev. 19:11--15). But this is pretty loose interpretation.
There is no way to locate whether this seal is at the beginning or at
the end of the Tribulation. But it is certain that we have come clear
through an account of the Tribulation in Revelation 6--7. So, wherever
the seventh seal opens, it starts the whole thing over again, as far as
the narrative is concerned.

8:2 "And I saw the seven angels which stood before God; and to them were
given seven trumpets.

3 And another angel came and stood at the altar, having a golden censer;
and there was given unto him much incense, that he should offer it with
the prayers of all saints upon the golden altar which was before the
throne."

Now, this points out clearly a temple in heaven. Connecting back with
Revelation 7:17, we find the throne mentioned, and also the temple in
Revelation 7:15. Now in Revelation 8:3 we find an altar before the
throne which was not mentioned by John before. This indicates that in
heaven there is a tabernacle, or temple, built after the pattern of that
which was showed to Moses on the mount. (This is why God told him to
build it just like the pattern \[Heb. 8:5\].) This heavenly temple is
mentioned again in Revelation 11:19, and it is evidently in heaven until
eternity begins; but when eternity begins and the New Jerusalem comes
down from God out of heaven (a city 1,200 miles square), this city no
longer has any temple in it.

Revelation 21:22, "And I saw no temple therein: for the Lord God
Almighty and the Lamb are the temple of it."

This golden altar---Revelation 8:3---is an altar of incense; it is
different from the brazen altar. It is that altar "which had the golden
censer, and the ark of the covenant overlaid round about with gold"
(Heb. 9:4). The "prayers" are offered with the "golden censer." The
other altar referred to is the one found in the Old Testament, standing
outside the tabernacle, and overlaid with brass. (A brazen altar is for
sacrifice, and a golden altar is for the offering up of incense.) The
altar for burnt‑offering, outside the tabernacle, is covered with brass
(Exod. 38:1--7). The altar of incense, inside the tabernacle, is covered
with gold (Exod. 37:25--28).

"And another angel came and stood at the altar (the golden altar of
incense), having a golden censer (what the incense is offered
in)\...which was before the throne." (The "throne" has here taken the
place of the "Ark of the Covenant," in the Holy of Holies found in the
earthly tabernacle.)

8:4 "And the smoke of the incense, which came with the prayers of the
saints, ascended up before God out of the angel's hand.

5 And the angel took the censer, and filled it with fire of the altar,
and cast it into the earth: and there were voices, and thunderings, and
lightnings, and an earthquake."

The "fire" of the altar can be the fire from the golden altar, or from
the brazen altar outside. It is fire taken out of one of these altars,
and put into the censer to make the incense burn.

In this passage, we have, in type, the prayers of saints coming up
before God, as incense (Psa. 141:2; Luke 1:10). These prayers come up as
a "memorial before God" (Acts 10:2--4), and the indication is that the
prayers return to earth with a vengeance, as though they were answered.
There is not much doubt about whose prayers they are. In Revelation
6:9--10 were the "souls" of those who were martyred for their testimony
(and sacrificed to the Devil on the earth during the Tribulation),
praying for vengeance. This vengeance comes in Revelation 8:5.

8:6 "And the seven angels which had the seven trumpets prepared
themselves to sound.

7 The first angel sounded, and there followed hail and fire mingled with
blood, and they were cast upon the earth: and the third part of trees
was burnt up, and all green grass was burnt up."

In the first plague that falls, plants shrivel, perish, and
disintegrate. (Of course, many years ago if a man were to preach this
kind of thing, the people would laugh and make fun of him. But with
atomic and nuclear fission now operative, it could happen very easily.
As someone has so aptly said, "A hundred years ago the preachers were
saying that the world was coming to an end, and the scientists were
laughing at them. Now the scientists say that the world is coming to an
end, and the preachers are laughing at them." It is a strange situation
when preachers don't know as much truth about the future now as
scientists know. That's the situation we are in. The foolish "ministers"
are still preaching "the Ecumenical Council," "the United Nations," and
"bringing in the Kingdom," by Postmillennial means, where everybody gets
slowly better and better and better. Yet, the scientific facts are that
man now has something on his hands so small that he can't see it, and
yet he is about to blow himself to "kingdom come" with it.)

8:8 "And the second angel sounded, and as it were a great mountain
burning with fire was cast into the sea: and the third part of the sea
became blood;

9 And the third part of the creatures which were in the sea, and had
life, died; and the third part of the ships were destroyed."

Again, the literal fulfillment is easy, in view of nuclear fission and
atomic power. The sea is not identified here, although "the sea" usually
refers to the Mediterranean, where you find it in the passages. ("The
sea" is not a multitude of people, as it is spiritualized to be. This is
a perversion of interpretation that has been lifted from Rev. 17:15,
where the waters are said to be "peoples, and multitudes, and nations,
and tongues." But, the waters are not the sea. The difference between
correct Bible interpretation and scholastic tradition lies simply in
believing what you read, where you read it, as you read it, as God said
it. If He said "the sea," it certainly is not "waters." "The sea" is
"the sea," and "waters" are "waters.") Where "sea" is referred to in the
Bible, it is always something like the Dead Sea, the Mediterranean, or
the Sea of Galilee, unless it is a reference to the "great deeps." This
"sea" (Rev. 8:8) is mentioned again in Revelation 13:1, when the
Antichrist shows up from the "sea," and this time it is plainly the
Mediterranean; so it probably is here, although we cannot locate it for
certain.

The third part of the creatures die, and the ships are destroyed, as
they were at the Bikini Atoll.

8:10 "And the third angel sounded, and there fell a great star from
heaven, burning as it were a lamp, and it fell upon the third part of
the rivers, and upon the fountains of waters:

11 And the name of the star is called Wormwood: and the third part of
the waters became wormwood; and many men died of the waters, because
they were made bitter."

The reference is directly to Jeremiah 9:15, and indirectly to Jeremiah
51:25. The waters become bitter, and people die from drinking them; that
is, they become poison. The "overcomers" in this period have the
apostolic promise of Mark 16:17--18; hence, drinking of this water does
not hurt them. These apostolic signs and wonders (which occur in the
early part of the Book of Acts) will reappear as God deals with Israel
in the Tribulation. (This truth is something which the "Holiness" and
"Campbellites" can never get, since they, as Catholics, are
Postmillennial and Amillennial. They can never see that the signs and
wonders and miracles of healing are given to Israel, and that they are
never in effect unless God is dealing with Israel. Hence, in the Book of
Acts, these signs and wonders disappear with the Acts of the Apostles
and do not reoccur until after the Rapture of the church.)

Nearly every heresy is a Tribulation truth. Every false teaching today
about "healing," "the baptism of the Holy Ghost," "speaking in tongues,"
and "baptismal regeneration" is a Tribulation truth which has been
misapplied by people who reject the word of God as it says it, where it
says it, in the plain English, as you find it. (If you take the Bible as
you find it, where it is, as God has given it to you---\[I am always
referring to the Protestant Textus Receptus translation---the AV,
1611\]---you will come out with a Premillennial system of
interpretation, which will locate for you correctly the time of the
"signs," "wonders," and "miracles" given to Israel, as they occur at the
first coming and at the Second Coming.)

Most heresies are Tribulation truth---even the Seventh-day Adventist
doctrines; for in the Tribulation, the people will observe the seventh
day (Matt. 24:20), the temple worship (Matt. 24:15), sacrifice at the
temple, and they will be baptized in "the name of Jesus for the
remission of sins" in order to receive the Holy Ghost, and the Holy
Ghost will be "poured out on all flesh in those days" (Acts 2:17--18;
Joel 2). Hence, every "tongue speaker," Mormon, Holiness, Seventh-day
Adventist, "healer," Jehovah's Witness, and Campbellite on the face of
the earth today is taking the truth and perverting it to teach a lie,
because he does not believe in the literal, physical, visible Second
Coming of Jesus Christ to reign over a literal, physical, visible
kingdom on this earth for a literal, physical, visible one thousand
years. This is the root of the trouble.

The saints in the Tribulation have protection from the waters that
become "wormwood," but the rest of the people die.

8:12 "And the fourth angel sounded, and the third part of the sun was
smitten, and the third part of the moon, and the third part of the
stars; so as the third part of them was darkened, and the day shone not
for a third part of it, and the night likewise.

13 And I beheld, and heard an angel flying through the midst of heaven,
saying with a loud voice, Woe, woe, woe, to the inhabiters of the earth
by reason of the other voices of the trumpet of the three angels, which
are yet to sound!"

(Sam Jones says, "When God says 'WOE,' you'd better stop!") We cannot
locate all these events exactly. I've often thought that the seven
trumpets, which are blown, do not begin to sound until the middle of the
Tribulation. The vengeance which the saints are praying for begins at
this point---in the middle of the Tribulation or just after the middle.
They are told "that they should rest yet for a little season," which
would imply that these trumpets would not be poured out until after the
middle of the Tribulation.

Notice in Revelation 8:12 how the heavenly phenomena would seem to match
the heavenly phenomena which takes place in Revelation 6:12, and this
takes place at the end of the Tribulation. (I don't profess to be
infallible or near infallible in knowing where all these verses apply.
The people who are afraid of the Book of Revelation not only do not
understand it, they don't believe what it says. A Christian believes
what it says, and understands most of it because it is clear, but the
exact laying of every event into the months and years in which they
occur is a little complicated. It may remain vague until the Tribulation
takes place.)

In the Book of Daniel---which ties in with the Book of
Revelation---Daniel was told to wait and not "unseal" some things until
the "time of the end."

Four angels have sounded, and three angels have yet to sound.

CHAPTER 9

9:1 "And the fifth angel sounded, and I saw a star fall from heaven unto
the earth: and to him was given the key of the bottomless pit."

Notice, this star is a personality---"to him." (Going back to Rev. 1:20,
we find that a star can be an angel.)

"Key of the bottomless pit." Again, the scholars have trouble with the
expression "bottomless pit." Some people say that it is a contradiction,
a fairy story, a legend, or fiction. But it is not. A bottomless pit is
a very simple arrangement. The Bible speaks of the devil being brought
down to the "sides of the pit" as though there was a particular pit that
had no bottom (Isa. 14:15). There is a pit which you could dig that
would have no bottom to it; this pit would be shaped like a
doughnut---hollow inside. The Bible would indicate that in the heart of
this earth there is such a pit. (You will find it drawn out for you on
geographic and geological maps, showing the core of the earth!) In this
"bottomless pit," with the temperature well above 235 degrees
centigrade, we have demoniac powers erupting in the Tribulation, to
match those mentioned in Revelation 6. When these demoniac powers come
out, they come out of a pit which has no "bottom" to it; it is literally
all "sides." The horror of living in such a place is almost
unimaginable, when we realize that any such pit in the heart of the
earth would constantly be rotating with the rotation of the earth.
Whoever was in this pit, would constantly be thrown up and down the
sides, as it turned!

9:2 "And he opened the bottomless pit; and there arose a smoke out of
the pit, as the smoke of a great furnace; and the sun and the air were
darkened by reason of the smoke of the pit.

3 And there came out of the smoke locusts upon the earth: and unto them
was given power, as the scorpions of the earth have power."

These are supernatural "locusts" because ordinary locusts are stifled by
smoke, and ordinary locusts "have no king"---(Prov. 30:27); these
locusts have a "king over them" (vs. 11), and ordinary locusts go in
swarms with no "leader." These locusts, then, are demoniac monstrous
beings---half‑monster and half‑human, as you find them in the "Twilight
Zone," "The Invaders," etc. They erupt from the center of the earth,
according to the best Chinese, Mongolian, Tartar, German, and Italian
traditions, proving that all mythology and tradition is an attempt to
either slander or to imitate the word of God. (There is a legend that
Ghengis Khan will come up out of the earth and "take over" sometime in
the future; that his hordes will roll back across Europe again. Of
course, this is a picture of something that is true. These troops of
Rev. 9 come from the East \[vss. 14--16\] ; they are "horsemen" \[vs.
16\] ; they are called the "kings of the east" \[Rev. 16:12\]. Hence,
the Mongolian hordes under Ghengis Khan are not mythology or fiction;
they are a picture of something that is going to take place literally.)
Out of this pit come demoniac "locusts" from the sides of the pit, even
as Apollo is supposed to come up from Hades in Greek Mythology, and even
as the Zohar (the mystic bible of Jewish tradition) says that the
"Messiah" is underground in paradise, awaiting to save Israel. In the
Babylonian Talmud, the Babylonian Sanhedrin, 98, Section A, it says,
"'the Messiah will appear at the gates of Rome!" This means that
unbelieving Israel will accept a Roman pope as the Messiah, at a future
period (see John 19:15, and The Mark of the Beast, 1969).

9:4 "And it was commanded them that they should not hurt the grass of
the earth, neither any green thing, neither any tree; but only those men
which have not the seal of God in their foreheads."

Revelation 7:3--4 because the earth, sea, and trees are not allowed to
be hurt until after Revelation 7:3--4. This means, too, that the events
of Revelation 8:7 cannot occur until after Revelation 7:3--4. This means
that Revelation 7 is a parenthetical chapter, as I have told you before,
which deals with events that take place before Revelation 8:7, 12 and
Revelation 9:4.

9:5 "And to them it was given that they should not kill them, but that
they should be tormented five months: and their torment was as the
torment of a scorpion, when he striketh a man."

Here we find this killing going on five months. Notice, too, that this
is the fifth angel who starts this holocaust. This brings up a very
interesting subject---the number "five." It is the "fifth" angel who
opens this pit, and in verse 5, they are tormented for five months. They
are not killed, but they are seeking death (vs. 6). (By the way,
Nebuchadnezzar destroyed Jerusalem on the fifth day of the fifth month.)
We are going to find, when we study the Old Testament, that the number
"five," always stands for "death" and never stands for grace. (You may
at sometime hear a fundamental, Premillennial preacher saying that the
number five indicates the number of "grace." This is a bad error taken
from the assumption that because "five" was connected with Christ's
death that, therefore, it is the number of grace. But the number "five"
applies to anybody's death. It is true that in the case of Jesus
Christ's death the number 5 would signify that the means of grace was
open to flow on the believer and on a sin‑cursed world. But this
occurrance in the death of one man in one instance would not locate the
meaning of the number in the Bible.)

As we study the number "five" in the Old and New Testaments, we will see
that it can only stand for one thing, and that is "death." This is very
conclusive, and the Scriptures do not leave any room for a "variety of
interpretations." (If you run into somebody who doubts what the number
"five" means, challenge them to sit down and study these references.
When they get through, they'll know the number "five" means death, only
death, and nothing but death, and there's no two interpretations about
it. It is the interpretation of the Holy Spirit Himself, as He wrote it
in the Book.)

In our study of the "cherubim" (in Rev. 4), we learned that there were
originally five cherubim. There were four cherubim around the throne,
but there was a fifth cherub "over the throne," who was an "anointed
Cherub." This Cherub was a special Cherub who "covered" the throne. The
Bible displays dozens of references which identify this fifth Cherub as
a two‑horned, ox‑faced cherub---an "angel of light," who is "Lucifer,"
or the "Devil." This Cherub was the fifth Cherub, and hence, we read in
Hebrews 2:14:

"Forasmuch then as the children are partakers of flesh and blood, he
also himself likewise took part of the same; that through death he might
destroy him that had the power of death, that is, the DEVIL."

The Devil, then, has the "power of death," because he is the "fifth"
Cherub. Since he is called "Devil" (five letters), and has power of
"death" (five letters), and was the "fifth" Cherub; this reaffirms that
the "fifth" (five letters) is connected with death.

Genesis 5:5, "And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and
thirty years: and he died."

By some wild coincidence, the first man that ever dies on this earth
dies in the fifth verse of the fifth chapter of the AV! (I know that
some say that the AV 1611 verse and chapter markings are not inspired,
but of course, that is the typical talk of a lazy, irreverent man who is
insincere, and has never checked to find out whether they are or not.)
How do you account for the fact that the first man that ever died on the
face of this earth died in Genesis 5:5? It's weird, isn't it? In Chapter
5, verse 5, is the first natural death, with 5 as the number of death,
with the fifth Cherub, the Devil, having the power of death. In Acts
5:5, you will find a man dropping dead. In Revelation 9:5, the fifth
angel heralds this torment of the scorpions, and they torment five
months, and men seek death.

That isn't all. For example, "May Day" is the warning signal for a
combat pilot in air battle. Do you know what month the month of May is?
It is the fifth month. The breakdown signal for a ship on the water is
number five (that is, the number five is the number given when a ship
goes dead in the water). The International Distress Frequency is 500
kilocycles, putting out an SOS. When a man falls overboard in the Navy,
there are five blasts of a whistle given! We are not through yet!

Genesis 35:18, "And it came to pass, as her soul was in departing, (for
she died) that she called his name Ben‑oni: but his father called him
Benjamin."

When Benjamin is born, his mother dies. Now, look what happens
subsequently, which can only be explained by what you have just read.

Genesis 43:34, "And he took and sent messes unto them from before him:
but Benjamin's mess was five times so much as any of theirs. And they
drank, and were merry with him."

Genesis 45:22, "To all of them he gave each man changes of raiment; but
to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver, and five changes of
raiment."

Somebody says, "You're putting it together." (That's the trouble. The
people who say this never even bothered to look up the verses to see
whether they were put together or not. Did you notice in the making of
the tabernacle that the altar of burnt offering, for the sacrifices to
be made upon, was five by five? \[See Exod. 27:1.\] Every drop of blood
that fell in the Old Testament worship services fell at "five by five.")

Coming across to 2 Samuel, we find (three times) a man's fifth rib
mentioned in connection with his death, for no reason at all, except to
drive home into your heart and mind, that five is death; and even Adam's
fifth rib is the cause of his death. He dies to get that wife. Men ought
to love their wives enough to die for them (Eph. 5).

Second Samuel 2:23, "Howbeit he refused to turn aside: wherefore Abner
with the hinder end of the spear smote him under the fifth rib, that the
spear came out behind him; and he fell down there, and died in the same
place: and it came to pass, that as many as came to the place where
Asahel fell down and died stood still."

Second Samuel 4:6, "And they came thither into the midst of the house,
as though they would have fetched wheat; and they smote him under the
fifth rib: and Rechab and Baanah his brother escaped."

Second Samuel 20:10, "But Amasa took no heed to the sword that was in
Joab's hand: so he smote him therewith in the fifth rib, and shed out
his bowels to the ground, and struck him not again; and he died."

We read in Genesis that when Adam had slept an unconscious sleep of
death (a type of the death of the last Adam), that God took a rib from
his side and made a woman. Adam, like Christ, had to die to get his
bride. As Christ's body is His bride (five letters), Adam's wife comes
from his body, and the part from which she comes is said to be the fifth
rib.

Ephesians 5:25, "Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved
the church, and gave himself for it."

The first husband and wife were Adam and Eve, which settles one thing
conclusively---your wife is your .fifth rib, and she is connected with
death! Adam had to die to save her.

In Christ's case, it is a case of "grace," but this is the only case
where it is the case of grace, and even here it is still death. Like the
old song says, "Five bleeding wounds he bare." Five wounds! One in one
hand, the second one in the next hand, the third one in the right foot,
the fourth one in the left foot, and the fifth one under the heart. One,
two, three, four, five! Christ suffered D‑E‑A‑T‑H for our sins.

9:6 "And in those days shall men seek death, and shall not find it; and
shall desire to die, and death shall flee from them.

7 And the shapes of the locusts were like unto horses prepared unto
battle; and on their heads were as it were crowns like gold, and their
faces were as the faces of men.

8 And they had hair as the hair of women, and their teeth were as the
teeth of lions."

That is, these beings are monsters. They are half-human and half‑beast
(Isa. 28, 24; Rev. 6).

9:9 "And they had breastplates, as it were breastplates of iron; and the
sound of their wings was as the sound of chariots of many horses running
to battle."

If these beings are not supernatural beings, they could be a A.D. 90
description of airplanes. (I hesitate to take any kind of a figurative
or spiritual interpretation where it is probably literal, but we have to
remember that when John sees these things, he has been carried forward
to 1990. He sees in 1995 and 1990 what you folks have not seen yet, and
you've seen plenty! When John writes, what he describes is a flying,
winged, humming, roaring type of thing, that comes out of the earth. I
still incline to the literal interpretation, but if it is not literal
then it is certainly a description of some kind of jet plane or rocket.)

9:10 "And they had tails like unto scorpions, and there were stings in
their tails: and their power was to hurt men five months."

It can be a description of a plane with rockets going out the back end,
or machine guns out the back end! (But it is probably a literal
description of demoniac beings because of what follows.)

9:11 "And they had a king over them, which is the angel of the
bottomless pit, whose name in the Hebrew tongue is Abaddon, but in the
Greek tongue hath his name Apollyon."

It doesn't say when this king came up; it is merely noted that they had
a king. (He is probably the "star" of 9:1.) These cannot be literal
locusts because literal locusts have no king (Prov. 30:27). This king is
the "angel of the bottomless pit." (We discussed this angel in Rev. 6:8.
This angel is the death angel, "the Destroyer," whose name is translated
"perdition" in 2 Thess. 2:3; John 17:12; Rev. 17.)

9:12 "One woe is past; and, behold, there come two woes more hereafter.

13 And the sixth angel sounded, and I heard a voice from the four horns
of the golden altar which is before God,

14 Saying to the sixth angel which had the trumpet, Loose the four
angels which are bound in the great river Euphrates.

15 And the four angels were loosed, which were prepared for an hour, and
a day, and a month, and a year, for to slay the third part of men."

On your chart, this is near the end of the Tribulation. In Revelation
6:8 you were told that a fourth part of the earth has been killed before
this army shows up; a third of the three‑quarters that are left are now
killed in Revelation 9:15--18. This army (Rev. 9:16) of satanic horsemen
come from the east (Rev. 16:12). They are led by "kings," and these
kings will have to come from China, Mongolia, or India. This army, when
it comes, is like the army of Ghengis Khan or Tamerlane. It comes from
the east to the west, across the Euphrates, headed for Palestine; it
comes at a certain date, on a certain day and hour, a certain month, and
a certain year (Dan. 7:12). It comes for the purpose of slaying a third
part of the men who are left.

9:16 "And the number of the army of the horsemen were two hundred
thousand thousand: and I heard the number of them."

Then these demoniac horsemen who come from the East will number 200
million men!

9:17 "And thus I saw the horses in the vision, and them that sat on
them, having breastplates of fire, and of jacinth, and brimstone: and
the heads of the horses were as the heads of lions; and out of their
mouths issued fire and smoke and brimstone."

Compare Isaiah 5:26 and Psalm 57:4 with this passage. These horsemen are
superhuman and are no more human than the locusts discussed earlier.
(The Lord is peculiar in that He has ordinary horses---like you see up
