and down the country---and also He has supernatural horses for His own
use, like the kind that took the chariot up to heaven with Elijah in it
\[2 Kings 2:11\], and the kind upon which you will return \[Rev. 19\];
then again He has demoniac horses and horsemen, as you find them here.)
They come tearing across the ground breathing fire, smoke, and
brimstone---brimstone is like sulfuric acid or burning sulphur. It would
be impossible to muster such an army literally. These are, evidently,
some kind of souls of unsaved dead men coming up out of hell; they are
half decomposed---half‑man and half‑horse, like the mythological
"centaur"!

9:18 "By these three was the third part of men killed, by the fire, and
by the smoke, and by the brimstone, which issued out of their mouths."

(See Isa. 24:6.) Albert Einstein said that an atomic war might kill
"two‑thirds of the earth's two billions." This cavalry (Rev. 9:18) kills
a third of the final three quarters of the population which is left on
the earth.

9:19 "For their power is in their mouth, and in their tails: for their
tails were like unto serpents, and had heads, and with them they do
hurt."

Again, the reference could be to a common language description of
twentieth-century mobilized infantry. John speaks in the first century
language so that anybody between then and now can understand what he is
saying. But, the text probably means what it says, as it says it, where
it says it. These are supernatural horsemen, cavalry that comes from the
East, demoniac satraps of Satan, coming to wipe out Israel.

9:20 "And the rest of the men which were not killed by these plagues yet
repented not of the works of their hands, that they should not worship
devils, and idols of gold, and silver, and brass, and stone, and of
wood: which neither can see, nor hear, nor walk."

That is, men kept right on worshipping devils and idols of gold (money),
silver (silverware), brass (cars, guns), stone (houses, buildings), and
wood (houses, TV sets, furniture, etc.). They were still trying to find
"security," "unity," and "togetherness," and they just ignored the fact
that God was wiping them out.

9:21 "Neither repented they of their murders, nor of their sorceries,
nor of their fornication, nor of their thefts."

The word "sorcery" is a peculiar word, coming from our word "pharmacy"
in the Greek. Literally, "sorcery" means the use of drugs. They kept
right on using drugs.

The word "worship" in verse 20 is an interesting word also; it is used
everywhere in the New Testament to refer to a form of worship where you
bow down and kiss something. In Matthew 28:9 (". . . and held him by the
feet, and worshipped him."), they kissed his feet. This form of worship
is not merely showing up and performing your perfunctory Sunday morning
duties; it is bowing down and kissing something. For example: Psalm 2:12
says, "Kiss the Son, lest he be angry." In Baal‑worship, they were told
to "kiss the calves." God said to Elijah, in 1 Kings 19:18, "Yet I have
left me seven thousand in Israel, all the knees which have not bowed
unto Baal, and every mouth which hath not kissed him."

You must keep this in mind because Judas kisses Jesus. (When the student
gets to Rev. 13, he will see the important place that this "kiss" has to
do in the Antichrist's program for the world. He will come preaching a
modernistic god of love, who is just a great big kiss. Or as Beethoven
wrote in his "Ninth Symphony," "This kiss is for the whole world!" It is
mimicked in the pocketbooks as the "Kiss of Death" or "Love Me Deadly,"
etc. This "kiss" will figure in the end‑time as the sign of the
Antichrist, and it will be the way in which he will transmit his "mark"
to his followers.) The word "worship," in verse 20, is this
word---k‑i‑s‑s.

You are told to worship Christ---"Kiss the Son"---that is, bow down and
kiss His feet. (Of course, all of us undoubtedly know of some big, old,
fat, liquor‑head---a deluded Italian prince, who actually lets people
bow down and kiss his finger. And although that is hard to imagine for
twentieth-century "intelligent" people, there are scores of people so
deluded in the twentieth century, that they would bow down and kiss a
man's finger, or a ring on the finger, when the man is nothing but a
shrewd politician! Now, you stop and think about that. "Thou shalt have
no other gods before me" \[Exod. 20:3\]. One of our presidents went over
to see one of those over‑stuffed, bloated, liquor‑heads one time, and he
went into a room and closed the door, and nobody knew what was said when
he went in there! But when he came back out of the room, he was quoting
Scripture for the Associated Press, talking about "one fold," and trying
to make his foreign policy line up with John 10! When the president came
back to the U.S.A. after talking to that old liquor‑headed politician,
he was quoting Scripture in his speeches on foreign policy! The
Scripture he was quoting was the scripture used by Pope John, Pope Paul,
and the Ecumenical Council. He was quoting John 10:16 and John 17:22!)

The lesson we learn from Chapter Nine is evident. No matter how much God
deals with men, bruises them, kills them, cripples them, if they don't
want to get right, they'll not get right; no amount of pressure
overcomes their wills. (Election is all right, but God elects those who
have their hat in the ring to be nominated.) The human will is more
stubborn than you think it is. Before you talk about God completely
"overpowering" anybody's will, just remember that the human race (Rev.
9:21) is wiped out to where there is less than two‑thirds of
three‑quarters of it left. When they get down to two‑thirds of
three-quarters of the population left, they go right straight on and say
that it was an "accident," "bad weather," "ironic stroke of fate," or a
"little bad luck"; and they simply ignore the fact that God is dealing
with them, not knowing that the "goodness of God leadeth" them to
repentance (Rom. 2:4).

CHAPTER 10

Remembering that Revelation 7 was a parenthetical chapter, we now come
to Revelation 10 and find another parenthetical chapter. (Notice in Rev.
8 the first, second, third, and fourth trumpets; in Rev. 9 the fifth and
sixth trumpets; and then there is a break. This is exactly like the
seals: in Rev. 6 there were the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, and
sixth seals, and a break. The seventh seal was not broken until Rev. 8.)
Six trumpets go off in Revelation 8--9, and now there is a break in
Revelation 10. The seventh trumpet does not go off until Revelation
11:15. Revelation 7 had no seals in it, although it is given at that
time, and Revelation 10 has no trumpets in it, although it is given at
that time. Therefore, Revelation 10, as Revelation 7, is a parenthesis.
It is an additional statement "stuck in" with no time element attached
to it.

10:1 "And I saw another mighty angel come down from heaven, clothed with
a cloud: and a rainbow was upon his head, and his face was as it were
the sun, and his feet as pillars of fire."

The "cloud" is usually found in connection with Jesus Christ (Rev 1:7;
Acts 1:9). There is little doubt about this Angel; this is the angel
found in Revelation 1:14--15. This is the Angel of the Lord; this is the
Lord Jesus Christ.

10:2 "And he had in his hand a little book open: and he set his right
foot upon the sea, and his left foot on the earth.

3 And cried with a loud voice, as when a lion roareth: and when he had
cried, seven thunders uttered their voices."

Jesus Christ is called "the Lion of the tribe of Juda" in Revelation
5:5, so this is apparently the Lord Jesus Christ appearing. We repeat,
this is a parenthetical record of an appearance in the Tribulation. This
means that sometime before the end of the Tribulation, the Lord Jesus
Christ is going to make an appearance. The Bible plainly teaches that
sometime during the Tribulation Christ is going to manifest Himself to
Israel, and Israel is going to be "born in a day," converted as a man,
and then the nation is going to bring forth fruit, as a "woman in
travail" brings forth a "man-child." These figures are found in scores
and scores of passages in the Old Testament, and this Mid-Tribulation
appearance is typified in a number of verses (Isa. 66:7--8; Micah 5:3).

In Acts 9:6 we hve the conversion of Paul. (There can be no doubt about
it because Paul says he saw Christ "as of one born out of due time" \[1
Cor. 15:8\].) Paul is a picture of the remnant of Israel that is saved
during the Tribulation. Paul is not a type of the church; Paul is a Jew,
saved "out of season." Paul is not "one of the twelve" apostles; he is
an additional apostle. (He is beyond the twelve.) Paul will not sit on
one of twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes, as in Matthew 19:28.
Therefore, Paul has a special ministry to Gentiles, as did Jonah; both
of their ministries are representative of the Jewish remnant that will
be saved in the Tribulation (144,000), who will be commissioned to
preach to Gentiles---not to Jews.

In Genesis, when Joseph revealed himself to his brothers, it was in the
second year of a seven year period. (But of course, some will say that
you can't teach doctrine by types!) Joseph is a type of Christ in 152
particulars. Joseph reveals himself to his brethren, as Christ will
reveal Himself to His brethren. Joseph is rejected by his brothers the
first time, and accepted the second time; Christ was rejected by Israel
the first time, and will be accepted the second time. When Joseph's
brothers reject him, and then receive him, a seven year period of
tribulation takes place, in the form of a famine.

Genesis 45:1, 4--6---"Then Joseph could not refrain himself before all
them that stood by him; and he cried, Cause every man to go out from me.
And there stood no man with him, while Joseph made himself known unto
his brethren\....And Joseph said unto his brethren, Come near to me, I
pray you. And they came near. And he said, I am Joseph your brother,
whom ye sold into Egypt. Now therefore be not grieved, nor angry with
yourselves, that ye sold me hither: for God did send me before you to
preserve life. For these two years hath the famine been in the land: and
yet there are five years, in the which there shall neither be earing nor
harvest."

We may not teach as infallible truth that Jesus will appear the second
year in the Tribulation because of these verses, but the type is very
clear.

The Book of Job is another perfect type of the Tribulation. There are
forty-two months in the Great Tribulation---the last half of the seven
years---and there are forty-two chapters in the Book of Job. The word
"Job" means "one persecuted," as Israel will be persecuted in the
Tribulation. Job is in the land of Uz, which is south of the Dead Sea in
the land of Edom (Gen. 46:13; Lam. 4:21). This is where Israel will be
in the Tribulation.

Job 42:10, "And the Lord turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for
his friends: also the Lord gave Job twice as much as he had before."

"Turned the captivity." This expression is never used anywhere in the
Bible in reference to the restoration of Israel to the land of
Palestine. (Compare Job 42:10 with Deut. 21:17.) Job is persecuted by
Satan as Israel will be persecuted by Satan (Rev. 12:1--4). In the Book
of Job, with 42 chapters, the Lord appears to Job in Chapter 38,
indicating 38 months into the Tribulation; this would be about four
months before the end of the Tribulation.

One thing is certain, the Lord Jesus Christ does make a Tribulation
appearance to Israel, and they are converted by seeing Him, exactly as
Paul was converted by seeing Him. There is a brilliant light, brighter
than the sun (exactly as the transfiguration), when Jesus appears
sometime in the Tribulation (Ezek. 43:2; Psa. 50:2). We do not know
whether this takes place before the middle of Daniel's Seventieth Week
or in the middle or at the post‑Tribulation Rapture right before the
end. The exact date is obscure, because some of these great truths are
"sealed" right up until the Tribulation. God will probably not reveal
the exact dates unless He reveals them to a man in the Tribulation. When
these truths are finally revealed in the Tribulation, they will be
revealed through a 1611 Authorized English Version, as English will be
the universal language. The Ecumenical Council and Rome are going to
take over and put their "mass" into English.

(Rome has good sense. They know that when the English people have the
money, they've got to speak English, and give their Italians English
names! They know that if the English speaking people have the money
\[which God gave them because they propagated the English Bible\], Old
Papa Paul has better sense than to call himself Pope Montini or Pope
Frederick or Pope Spinoza; he'll call himself "Paul" or "John" or
"Peter" or "Bill"! He'll take an English name. You know perfectly well
that a man named "Montini" wouldn't take a name like "Paul" unless he
was after a buck! There was a saying in the old days in the army---"If
it doesn't make sense, there's a dollar bill in it." There is more truth
than fiction in that saying. When you find something that doesn't make
sense in the religious world, social world, political world, music
world, or anywhere else in the world, there is a dollar bill in it. If
you find something so wild and confused and snarled up that you can't
make heads or tails out of it, there is a buck in it every time. And
when you find some Italian‑talking, pizza‑eating wino adopting a
criminal "alias" and calling himself "Paul" or "John," you know he is up
to something! If you are an honest man, you don't have to go under an
alias. The only kinds of people who go under aliases are gangsters,
movie stars, and popes! If Russia got control the next pope would be
Pope IVAN!)

If God ever shows anybody the time element we are discussing, He'll show
them through a Reformation Bible, 1611, AV. However, I will put one
qualification on that statement. Since the Old Testament is written in
Hebrew, and since God deals with the Jews and they have the blessings,
and since every writer in the Bible is a Jew, and since Israel is going
to be restored in the Tribulation, it is highly possible that when these
truths are revealed, they will be revealed through the Hebrew Old
Testament text of the AV 1611. That text will be Jacob Ben Chayim's,
which is the "Textus Receptus" Hebrew of the 1611 King James Bible. This
Hebrew manuscript is found in the Complutensian Polyglot, edited by
Cardinal Ximenes, 1514. It is not the text of the Septuagint, used in
Vaticanus and Sinaiticus for the RSV and ASV. (Let me say, again, that
the ASV, RV, RSV, New English Bible, Amplified, Weymouth, Pogo,
Phillips, Mickey Mouse, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Little Red Riding Hood, Gone
with the Wind, and these other translations are not Bible manuscripts.
Their Old Testament is a Greek Septuagint, written by Origen, Eusebius,
and Hesychius in Alexandria, Egypt, North Africa, for the Latin Church.
Their New Testament is the perverted and aborted Greek of Alexandrian
Greek scholarship, written by gnostics and infidels. Their Old Testament
text is not the "Textus Receptus," containing the Hebrew text of Jacob
Ben Chayim, acknowledged by all orthodox Jews as the correct Old
Testament text nor is their New Testament the Greek text of the Syrian,
Byzantine type used by Lucian in Antioch. All modern "Bibles" are
corrupt from Genesis to Revelation.)

10:4 "And when the seven thunders had uttered their voices, I was about
to write: and I heard a voice from heaven saying unto me, Seal up those
things which the seven thunders uttered, and write them not."

And that is the end of the matter. Judge Rutherford and Russell tried to
interpret these "seven thunders" of Millennial Dawnism, and tried to
tell Christians what these seven thunders were going to do and what they
said. (It is typical of Satan to begin with an obscure passage.) What
these voices uttered is never written down because God told John not to
write it down. The only help we can find is Isaiah 58:1 and Psalm 29.
The Devil has a habit of beginning with obscure verses like this one
when he wants to teach a lie. There isn't a Campbellite, Catholic,
Lutheran, Episcopalian, Seventh-day Adventist, Mormon, or Jehovah's
Witness on the face of the earth that doesn't begin his theology with a
verse of Scripture about which there is a difficulty (see Acts 2:38 and
Matt. 16:16--18). If ever there was a difficulty, there is one in
Revelation 10:4, where "seven thunders" utter something which nobody
wrote down! That would be a perfect place to start a new religion. (If I
were the Devil, I would know what verses to teach you. If I were the
Devil, I would know what Bible to use and how to use it to get you
messed up and get you home to hell. If I were the Devil \[and thank God
I'm not\], I would preach to you the "sermon on the Mount" \[salvation
by works\], "the Golden Rule \[salvation by works\], "1 Corinthians 13"
\[which applies to no unsaved person on the face of the earth\], "Acts
2:38" \[given to Israel as a nation instead of you as an individual\],
"Matthew 16:16" \[given to eleven apostles with signs, wonders, and
miracles that you don't have\], "Matthew 25" \[salvation by works\],
"James 2" \[justification by works\], "1 Peter 3:21" \[only half the
verse so you would think water baptism could get you to heaven\], and "1
Corinthians 15:29" \[get somebody to get baptized for you after you
blanked out\] ! If I were the Devil, I would know how to sew you up
good, even if you had an I.Q. of 170. The Devil has more brains than any
ten people you ever met in your life, and his greatest instrument with
which to destroy your soul is the word of God. If he wants to damn you
to hell, it would be just as easy for him as rolling a golfball off a
log. He would know just what verses to quote, where to find them, and
how to use them. He would always use a verse about which there was a
doubt, or a verse that was complex, or a verse that was difficult.

He would never come at you with John 3:16, 1:12, 3:36; Romans 10:9--10;
Revelation 3:20; or 1 John 5:10--12. He would come at you with Acts
2:38; Mark 16:16; 1 Peter 3:21; Acts 22:7--8, the Sermon on the Mount, 1
Corinthians 13, and all the verses that have no application to you
whatsoever. That's his method.)

10:5 "And the angel which I saw stand upon the sea and upon the earth
lifted up his hand to heaven."

Notice, only two elements are given---the sea and the earth. There are
several seas and oceans; this is a specific sea. As we have said before,
where the sea is referred to in the Book of Revelation before Chapter
21, it is the Mediterranean Sea. (There is one exception, in Rev. 21:1,
it says, "There was no more sea," and here the reference is to a
different sea. The reference in Revelation 21:1 is the sea we discussed
in Rev. 4.) The picture in Revelation 10:5 is probably that the Lord
Jesus Christ has one foot on the earth, Palestine, and the other foot on
the Mediterranean Sea. Palestine is God's piece of ground. It is between
the three continents that He gave to Shem, Ham, and Japheth (see
commentary on Gen. 10). The Angel, then, is taking possession of a piece
of land in the proclamation He is about to announce. This fits fairly
well because in the passage as it occurs, He is speaking about what is
going to take place at the end of the Tribulation. In Revelation 10:7
you have an Old Testament prophecy. If you run that prophecy to
Revelation 11:15, you will see that this proclamation has something to
do with the possession of earth and sea. Therefore, it is probably the
Lord Jesus Christ standing with one foot in Palestine, and the other
foot on the Mediterranean Sea. Of course, every kingdom in the world
comes from this area.

Revelation 10:7 and Revelation 11:15 refer to a literal, physical,
visible coming of Christ to take over the literal, physical, visible
kingdoms of this world. This thing (absolutely and positively) did not
happen at the first coming of Christ. History shows that it didn't
happen, and you couldn't believe it happened unless you were immoral in
your attitude toward the word of God. There is no time since 4000 B.C.
in which Jesus Christ ever took over "the kingdoms of this world." The
kingdoms of this world belong to Satan (Luke 4). They are under Satan's
dominion (2 Cor. 4:4); they always have been and always will be until
Revelation 11:15. If Revelation 11:15 is past, the newspaper reporters
today are out of their minds, because for 2,000 years they have been
recording wars, rumors of wars, bloodshed, Hitler at the head of the
government, Khruschev at the head of the government, Castro at the head
of the government, Pope Leo at the head of the government, Charlemagne
at the head of the government, Napoleon at the head of the
government---never Jesus Christ! There isn't any indication anywhere in
history at anytime, under any condition, that Jesus Christ ever
controlled anything on the face of this earth since the day that He
arose from the dead, except a "body" of born‑again people (1 Cor. 12)
who believe in Him and in whom He dwells (1 Cor. 6).

For fifteen centuries the "Catholic" Church has taken advantage of Bible
ignorance and has taught that since Jesus indwells them as a political
group, and they represent Him as "princes," that they as "head of the
kingdom" are ruling the earth; therefore, the kingdom "has come," or is
coming. But anybody who knows the true history of the Roman Catholic
Church wouldn't believe that for five minutes. You credulous
"Catholics," who don't know the history of your church (You don't or you
wouldn't be in it!), and don't know the history of your popes (You don't
or you would laugh at them!), and don't know what your church teaches
(You don't or you would repent with all your heart and soul!), you might
think that the "kingdom came" on this earth; but if you knew history and
knew the history of your church, you'd know that your church never
brought anything on this earth but political intrigue, formalistic
worship, religious misunderstandings, pagan idolatry, international
strife, over population, and "Ecumenical movements" which attempt to
force people back into the pagan mold of Baal worship. The reason why
you don't know that is because you don't read. You read the
Book‑of‑the‑Month, you go to the movies, you read the Saturday Review of
Literature, you read what Papa Paul tells you to read. You've never read
Fox's Book of Martyrs. (You haven't got the "guts" to read it!) You
never read American Freedom and Catholic Power by Paul Blanchard. (You
haven't got the backbone to do it.) You wouldn't dare read Vatican
Imperialism in the Twentieth Century by Avro Manhattan. And don't think
because you are "happy" and "contented" with your position that God
Almighty is going to forgive you for believing a lie, teaching a lie,
practicing a lie, hiding from the truth, and shutting your eyes from the
truth. If "finding the truth" will make you unhappy, you're full of the
Devil and lost. The truth doesn't make any man unhappy that is right
with the Lord (John 8:40--48; 1 Cor. 13:6).

John 3:19---"And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the
world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds
were evil."

John 14:6---"Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the
life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me."

John 16:13---"Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will
guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but
whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you
things to come."

If a "Christian" doesn't have the spiritual "guts" or the backbone to
read literature like Philip Schaff's History of the Christian Church,
then "be sure your sins will find you out."

If your alibi is, "Well, you're just giving us what is published by your
group," my answer is, "If you haven't got the integrity or the backbone
or the honesty or the sincerity to examine both sides of any group, then
you have deliberately rejected the truth, and you are living a lie" (1
Thess. 5:21).

The Bible teaches a literal, physical, visible reign of Jesus Christ
over the kings of this earth at the Second Advent, clearly taught here
in these passages. It is a thing that is going to take place in the
future that has never taken place in the past, and to reject this Bible
truth is to deliberately live a life of wilful sin because you are
content to reject the truth. It doesn't make any difference whether you
are "happy" or "contented" with your "religion" or not. If you are
living a lie, God Almighty is going to hold you accountable at the White
Throne Judgment (as an unsaved man) when "the books are opened," and
then you are going to find out that what I'm telling you right
now---that your church has never taken over the "kingdoms of this world"
except under Satan, it never will except under Satan, and Jesus Christ
has never taken over the "kingdoms of this earth, " and never will until
He comes again! The Postmillennial and Amillennial teachings of the
Reformation and Roman "Catholicism" in regards to this truth are both
blasphemous perversions of the word of God. (And if you don't like those
words, it is just because you don't like what God revealed as He
revealed it, where He gave it. You're not going to believe it until that
proud old heart gets right with God. Nothing the Bible says is going to
change you a bit.)

10:6 "And sware by him that liveth for ever and ever, who created
heaven, and the things that therein are, and the earth, and the things
that therein are, and the sea, and the things which are therein, that
there should be time no longer:

7 But in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he shall begin
to sound, the mystery of God should be finished, as he hath declared to
his servants the prophets."

The Angel places one foot on the land and one foot on the sea, and
declares that when the seventh angel sounds, this whole thing is "going
to wind up."

10:8 "And the voice which I heard from heaven spake unto me again, and
said, Go and take the little book which is open in the hand of the angel
which standeth upon the sea and upon the earth."

This voice (Rev. 10:8) comes from above the Angel. (We have this
identical thing taking place in Matt. 3, when the Lord Jesus Christ has
come down and is on this earth \[Matt. 3:16\], a voice from heaven
\[Matt. 3:17\] saying, "This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well
pleased.") In both places, then, we have a picture of the Trinity.

10:9 "And I went unto the angel, and said unto him, Give me the little
book. And he said unto me, Take it, and eat it up; and it shall make thy
belly bitter, but it shall be in thy mouth sweet as honey."

I don't know what this little book is, and neither does your church. The
little book is not identified (Rev. 5:1--5). Sam Jones said, "If a man
understood all the Bible, he would know that somebody wrote it that
didn't have any more sense than he did." Let us make spiritual
application for the time being, remembering that every Scripture has
three meanings---doctrinal, historical, and spiritual. Although we do
not know what the little book actually is, we can get a good spiritual
application out of it by saying the little book is the word of God
(Ezek. 2:9, 3:3).

10:10 "And I took the little book out of the angel's hand, and ate it
up; and it was in my mouth sweet as honey: and as soon as I had eaten
it, my belly was bitter.

11 And he said unto me, Thou must prophesy again before many peoples,
and nations, and tongues, and kings."

It could be the word of God because God says, "The Lord God hath spoken,
who can but prophesy?" (Amos 3:8). David says, "How sweet are thy words
unto my taste! yea, sweeter than honey to my mouth" (Psa. 119:103). So
there is something very true, practically, which we can apply to this
passage.

When John ate this book it was "sweet as honey" but as soon as he had
eaten it, "my belly was bitter." When man tastes that blessed word (It's
called "milk" in 1 Cor. 3, and "strong meat" in Heb. 5, and "bread" in
Luke 4, and "honey" in Psa. 119, and "apples" in Proverbs.), it tastes
good. My! My! How I love to read the word of God! My! The hours I've
spent reading it. There have been times when I've read the Bible when I
couldn't put it down. There have been times when I meant to read only
ten pages, because of limited time, and I wound up reading thirty pages
and couldn't quit, and went on until my eyes were bloodshot. There are
times (when you really begin to get a grasp on the word of God) that you
absolutely cannot put it down. (I must confess, too, that when you put
it down, it is hard to pick up again. "This Book will keep me from my
sin, or my sin will keep me from this Book." The longer you leave it
lying around, the harder it is to pick up; the more you pick it up, the
harder it is to let go of it.) That book can ruin you either way. If you
leave it lying on the shelf and let dust accumulate on it and cobwebs
come on it, you'll wind up in the Ecumenical Council, back under the
Antichrist at Rome, back under the Dark Ages and the medieval
superstitions of "cardinals" and "popes"---with twentieth-century
"scientists"---who don't have enough brains to blow their nose when they
have a cold. Your sins will catch up with you. Your old nature will take
charge of you again. The Devil will get you back in the old life, and
get you to doubting your salvation. You'll cease to bear fruit. God
won't be able to use you because He only blesses His Word. That saying
may be qualified as stating that the Word is the only thing He has
promised to bless (Isa. 55:1--11). All of this happens when any man, or
nation, leaves the word alone.

If you pick up the word and feast on it, a number of things will happen,
also. Once you digest that word it will become "bitter" because you will
become an object of persecution. (Personally, I have no "persecution
complex," so don't misunderstand me. I don't even know what real
persecution is. For preaching the word of God, standing for the faith,
and preaching the whole counsel of God from cover to cover out of the
English Bible, I've only been challenged to debates, cursed, threatened
to be "beat up," threatened with lawsuits, and carried to court on six
occasions. \[One time it cost me about $3,000 worth of property!\]
However, I've had no real persecution. I've had a .45 pistol pulled on
me and a knife threat one time, but nothing real. I don't have any
"persecution complex," and I despise these little "liberals" and
"modernists" who think that every time somebody like Harvey Springer or
Carl McIntyre or Billy James Hargis stands up and talks about them that
they are being persecuted! These little weak‑kneed sissies, who can't
stand to be talked about, bore me to tears! Very few Christians in
America have ever really been persecuted for Christ's sake.) But if you
get in that word, and stay in that Word, you will be ostracized by
contemporary society, you will be hated by Roman Catholic "priests," you
will be hated by Campbellites who teach salvation by works, you will be
hated by "Convention" Baptists and associational groups, conferences,
presbyteries, and the National Council of Churches, who are all out to
build one great big integrated church with them in charge of it (to get
your tax money instead of trying to win souls to Christ)! And you might
be opposed and persecuted by loved ones and members of your own family.
Hence, the word becomes "bitter." That isn't all. It will become bitter
because it will make you a very peculiar person; it will make you "odd."
If a psychiatrist examined you carefully, he would say that you are a
psychopathic or a neurotic or a megalomaniac, an egomaniac, a paranoid,
or a schizophrenic. (They have all kinds of big Greek words they get out
of a Greek lexicon for Bible words, and they "strut their stuff" for
about $35 an hour!)

If you get in that Bible, you'll be considered an "odd‑ball," you'll be
considered peculiar, "harsh," "unloving," "uncooperative," a
"separatist," "schismatic," "radical," "right‑wing extremist," "rebel,"
and one who does not go along with the established order. You will be
ostracized by religious people in the sense that they will look at you
as a very peculiar and odd type of person. The Bible will make you
peculiar and odd because America is no longer a Bible‑reading nation.
Now, Bible teachers study their lessons for Sunday School; preachers
refer to it to get a sermon; the NAACP uses passages which refer to
Christians to try to mix the races; but "Christians" in America do not
read the Bible. If you get in the Bible, learn it and stay with it and
propagate it, you will be an "odd ball" because you will be "one in a
million!" The things that the Lord will show you from that book will set
you apart as an eccentric type of person because the rest of the
people---including "church workers"---don't read it. If everybody in
America read the Bible like they did in 1700 and 1800, you wouldn't be
considered so queer and so peculiar, but they do not read it! (When
there is a "pastor's forum" on television or radio these days, you can't
find two preachers out of fifty who can even turn to the passages they
are talking about.)

If you get in that Bible, it will make you peculiar. It will bring about
persecution. It will ostracize you from society; it will make you feel
lonely. You won't have too many friends. The friends you do have will be
Bible‑reading people and Bible‑believing people. The type of friends you
have will be the type of Christians found in the early days of
Christianity. They will be like those ancient, extremists, those
non‑conformists who were thrown to lions because they were accused of
"not loving humanity." Don't you let the Devil ever tell you that those
Christians were ever persecuted and burned at the stake just for what
they believed! They were looked upon as enemies of the state because
they were considered to be unfriendly or unloving toward the pagan
system in which they found themselves. They were considered to be
unfriendly; that was the charge. They were asked to go along with the
ecumenical pagan rites purely for the purpose of showing that they were
friends and lovers of the system in which they were; they refused to go
along with it (1 John 2:15--17).

It is a foolproof law that the word will make you peculiar; it'll bring
persecution upon you; it will make you "abnormal"; it will separate you;
it will make you the kind of a person who will be accused of being an
"enemy of mankind," a promoter of "hate‑literature," and not having the
proper reverence for the "religions of mankind." "Religions" have always
been popular---the Bible, never! "Religions" have always been tolerated,
promoted, and supported by unsaved men---the Bible, never! "Religion" is
the greatest, most popular, wonderful thing in the unsaved world today,
outside of liquor, money, and sex. The Bible never had this kind of
rating among unsaved people, and never will.

If you eat the book as John ate it, it may be sweet to your taste (And,
God bless you, it is!), but it will be bitter when it has been digested.

CHAPTER 11

We are now about halfway through the Book of Revelation, and Revelation
11 is by far the most important chapter in the Book of Revelation. (We
might even go further and say that of all the chapters in the Bible,
from cover to cover, Revelation 11 is the greatest chapter in the Bible
for opening the Scriptures. This may seem like a wild statement, but it
will not seem so wild to you when you finish studying the chapter.)
Chapter 11 is the key to the Book of Revelation; the Book of Revelation
is the key to the Old Testament; the Old Testament is the key to the New
Testament. The reason why Catholics and Campbellites get "messed up" in
the Book of Acts, the Book of Matthew, and the Book of Hebrews---as many
Presbyterians, Southern Baptists, Northern Baptists, Methodists,
Lutherans, Episcopalians, and Protestants---is because they have refused
to believe the Book of Revelation, which opens the Old Testament
passages connected with these books. The key to understanding Revelation
11 is believing what you read.

If a man believes the Book of Revelation, as it stands, when he gets to
Revelation 11:1--3, he will have to believe in the rebuilding of the
Jewish temple at Jerusalem. This brings around a whole series of
doctrines and thoughts that are unknown and unheard of in the major
denominational seminaries of today. There isn't one major denominational
seminary in the world that teaches preachers that the temple is going to
be rebuilt at Jerusalem; yet, there it is in Revelation 11:1--2! The
exact time of this temple's existence is found in Revelation 11:2--3; it
is forty‑two months of thirty days each. (You don't have to "interpret"
because the passage interprets itself.)

Then if that weren't enough, you are told in Revelation 11:15 that there
will be time when the "kingdoms of this world," not the Kingdom of God,
are going to belong to Jesus Christ---not to the Roman Catholic Church.
In other words, if you believe Revelation 11 as you find it, where you
find it, to mean what it says, as it says it, where it says it, you have
to open your mind to a whole new series of doctrines that are unknown
and unheard of among Protestants and Catholics and Jews alike.

That isn't all. You will find something else very peculiar in chapter
11. You find a Rapture in 11:12---a "catching up" of somebody who was
killed. (This takes place long before the White Throne Judgment.) Then
in 11:8, you are told that Jerusalem is called "Sodom" and "Egypt" in
this dispensation, and yet, is going to be restored. This statement
gives the key to the Church Age, in which Israel and the Jew are in
temporary rejection, and it tells you that they are going to be restored
later and come back into political power under the Lord Jesus Christ,
the Messiah. This, in turn, gives the Premillennial system of
interpretation, and this system of interpretation opens verses in the
Bible that are completely shut to Greek scholars, translators of new
bibles, revisers, and the men who teach preachers in the seminaries, as
every major seminary in the United States is Amillennial or
Postmillennial

Also in Revelation 11: 7 you are introduced to a man who is called the
"beast." It is not the "spirit of Antichrist" or the "lawless spirit."
It is a person called "the beast." This man is the main theme of the
Bible, outside of the person and work of Jesus Christ. There are more
references in the Old Testament to this man, found in Revelation 11:7,
than there are to Paul, David, and Moses combined. Outside of the Lord
Jesus Christ, the greatest person in the Bible is "the beast" (Rev. 11),
the Antichrist (1 John 2), "the wilful king" and "king of the north"
(Dan. 11), "the man of sin" and "the son of perdition" (2 Thess. 2). He
has eighteen types in the Bible. Outside of the Lord Jesus Christ, he is
by far the most important person in the holy Scripture. (Of course, this
too is a sphinx to the National Council of Churches, and there isn't one
preacher affiliated with the National Council of Churches in more than
500 churches that has any idea at all of what we're discussing right
now.)

11:1 "And there was given me a reed like unto a rod: and the angel
stood, saying, Rise, and measure the temple of God, and the altar, and
them that worship therein."

This "reed like unto a rod" is a measuring stick. Notice too, that this
"temple of God, and the altar, and them that worship therein," to be
measured, is not the heavenly temple referred to in Revelation 8:3--5.
The temple that is mentioned in Revelation 8:3--5 is found mentioned
again in Revelation 11:19; the temple that is measured in Revelation 11:
1--2 is on the earth.

11:2 "But the court which is without the temple leave out, and measure
it not; for it is given unto the Gentiles: and the holy city shall they
tread under foot forty and two months."

If you believe what you read, as you read it, where you find it, as it
is, there will be a literal city on this earth which literal Gentiles
will "tread down" a literal forty-two months. Then this temple and this
altar is not in heaven; this is an earthly temple and an earthly altar
on this earth, and Gentiles tread it down for forty‑two months.

Therefore, if we believe what we read, we must believe that temple
worship will be restored in the future. No matter how eccentric this may
sound, notice that in Matthew 24 the Lord Jesus Christ speaks of this
time.

Matthew 24:15---"When ye therefore shall see the abomination of
desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place,
(whoso readeth, let him understand:)" (See also Heb. 9:3, 12; 2Thess.
2:4.)

Once again we see the peculiar perversity of scholars in wanting to make
Matthew 24 refer to the "destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70." The
reason for this is the desire to put the prophetic fulfillment in the
past so it will have no prophetic fulfillment in the future. (The real
reason for this desire is hatred of the word of God and hatred of the
Lord Jesus Christ. I don't care how pious or scholarly the man is, or
whether he adheres to the "fundamentals of the faith" or the "Nicene
Creed." Every man alive on the face of this earth, who wants to see the
Lord Jesus Christ come back to this earth and reign over what is
rightfully His, is willing to believe the literal passages as they
stand. Any man who would reject the Scriptures---the literal
interpretation as they are found where you find them, is a man, who in
heart, does not want Jesus Christ to come back to this earth. He has his
roots in the ground, as Lot; he's settled down in the city "by the
gate." He's "pitched his tent toward Sodom," and he is not a consecrated
man.)

The issue of the Second Coming of Christ came up in Christianity between
1800 and 1910 (just as the issue of the Trinity came up in the first
century of church history, and the issues of the Deity of Christ and the
natures of Christ came up in the next two centuries of church history,
and the issue of the sacraments came up in the next three centuries).
The issue since 1910 is not the "Virgin Birth." It is not the "Deity of
Christ." It is the Second Coming of Jesus Christ, as it draws nigh. This
is the last controversial Bible doctrine to be defined among the members
of the Body of Christ. There isn't a non‑denominational school in the
country that is not counting on its compromise about this position to
get money from its students. There isn't an evangelist anywhere in the
United States, who is still winning souls consistently, who is not a
Premillennial evangelist. And there is not one school in the country
that is Amillennial or Postmillennial that is not headed straight for
the Ecumenical Council and the National Council of Churches. I don't
care whether that school professes to believe in the Virgin Birth, the
blood atonement, the bodily resurrection, etc. If that school is not a
Premillennial school which "rightly divides the word of truth," as it is
related to Israel and the Jew, that school is doomed to go back to Rome
and to the Dark Age theology that has cursed this world for 1,000 years
before the Reformation.

The issue in this day and age is the Premillennial coming of Jesus
Christ. A man who is a Premillennialist can place the Scriptures where
they belong properly, and the man who is not Premillennial doesn't know
what to do with the Old Testament, the prophets, or the future, because
the prophets tell of the future.

In this passage, Revelation 11:1--2, the Holy Spirit speaks about a
literal temple on this earth which the Gentiles are going to "tread
under foot" forty‑two months, and it is not A.D. 70. In A.D. 70
Jerusalem was not "trodden underfoot" forty‑two months. Herod's Temple,
which was destroyed in A.D. 70, had four courts, but this one here has
one court. (God always has a way to make a liar out of a scholar if he
persists in talking like a blockhead.) The people who believe that
Matthew 24 and Revelation 11 apply to the destruction of Jerusalem in
A.D. 70 are people who desire to reject revelation of future events.
There is only one court in the temple in Revelation 11:2, and Herod's
Temple had four courts. That is a matter of history which can be checked
from Josephus or any other historian of the time.

"And the holy city." It is called the "holy city" in verse 2, and "Sodom
and Egypt" in verse 8. Notice the difference. In verse 8, Jerusalem is
spiritually "Sodom and Egypt" in God's sight because of its rejection of
the Lord Jesus Christ; nevertheless, as far as the promises go (and the
restoration of Israel at the end of the Tribulation), it is the "holy
city" (Rev. 11:2). Now, think about that. Here is a city called the
"holy city" which people say was destroyed in A.D. 70; they say God is
all through with Jerusalem; they say the "church" is the true Israel;
they say that the promises went to the Gentiles and are to be
spiritualized; they say that since Jerusalem is spiritually "Sodom and
Egypt," God is all through with it. Then, how do you account for the
fact that it is called the "holy city" in Revelation 11:2? The context
of verse 2 is when the Gentiles have finished treading it down for three
and a half years. It wasn't called the "holy city" when Titus got
through with it in A.D. 70! These people that believe Matthew 24, Mark
13, and Luke 17 refer to the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70 have
"historitis." They believe that every passage of Scripture is only
historical and has no future application. Every verse has three
applications (see chapter 1).

"They tread under foot forty and two months." How many days in forty‑two
months?

11:3 "And I will give power unto my two witnesses, and they shall
prophesy a thousand two hundred and threescore days, clothed in
sackcloth."

In the Bible forty‑two months is one thousand two hundred and sixty
days. Therefore, a Bible month has thirty days; then the last half of
the Tribulation is three and a half years long.

"And I will give power unto my two witnesses." (The One speaking is the
Angel of Rev. 10:1, 9, 11.)

11:4 "These are the two olive trees, and the two candlesticks standing
before the God of the earth."

This opens up another whole line of thought that is absolutely unknown
to the Amillennialists and Postmillennialists, and a line of thought
about which you will find a great deal of disagreement among
fundamentalists who are always going to "the Greek." (Fortunately, the
AV 1611 English Bible is much more correct than the Westcott and Hort
Greek text, which was smuggled into the Revision Committee in 1881.
Whenever you find the Greek contradicting the English, you can always be
certain that the "Greek" is dead wrong. "The Greek," with which your
King James Bible is corrected, is the Westcott and Hort "Vaticanus"
\[A.D. 350\], containing the Shepherd of Hermes and the Apocrypha. The
English AV can give you the corrections on the Greek that you need. What
is referred to as the "original Greek" in scholars' circles and
seminaries is this corrupt Hesychian text written by Origen and Eusebius
in North Africa or Italy. It is no more a Bible than a telephone book.)

Notice---the two witnesses are called "the two olive trees, and the two
candlesticks standing before the God of the earth." The Mormons believe
that the first witness is the Bible and the second witness is Joe
Smith's "Book of Mormon." This is typical of the religious clap‑trap and
lampoonery that is carried on today by religious dunces and spiritual
clowns. The thing that is wrong with this private interpretation is that
it completely ignores Revelation 11:4--7. (You can always tell a
religious dunce by what he does with the Scriptures. Any man who will
take a verse out and make it teach what he thinks is right, because what
he thinks is right is more important to him than what the Scripture
says, is nothing but a spiritual clown.) In this passage, you are told
nothing about some Joe Smith who found some golden plates---which some
demon woke him up in the middle of the night to go out and dig up---that
had part of the King James Bible written on them! (Malachi 3 in the
"Book of Mormon" is the King James text!) Whoever wrote the "Book of
Mormon" (far from writing it at the time of the American Indians 2000
B.C.--A.D. 1000), waited until 1611 to hack off those golden plates,
sonny boy!

Revelation 11:4--7 is going to yield the proper interpretation. We don't
have to mess around with it like poor Mary Baker Patterson Eddy. (She
said that she wasn't going to die and requested in her will that a
telephone be placed in her grave so she could phone up to prove she
wasn't dead! If the poor soul wasn't dead, she wouldn't need a telephone
to start with down in the hole; she could have just phoned up from the
house!) When Mrs. Eddy taught that one witness was the Bible and the
other witness was the "Key to the Scriptures," we are again reminded of
the silly, deluded Italian prince over in Rome, who sits around on wine
kegs, talking about the Bible being the first witness and the Apocrypha
being the second witness! The Christ‑rejecting Jew thinks that the Old
Testament is the first witness and the Talmud is the second witness; the
unconverted Mohammedan thinks the first witness is Abraham, Isaac,
Moses, and the Law, and the second witness is Mohammed. All of these
deluded, Bible-rejecting sinners have one thing in common---they have no
use for the passage as you find it, where it is.

Zechariah 4:11‑14---"Then answered I, and said unto him, What are these
two olive trees upon the right side of the candlestick and upon the left
side thereof? And I answered again, and said unto him, What be these two
olive branches which through the two golden pipes empty the golden oil
out of themselves? And he answered me and said, Knowest thou not what
these be? And I said, No, my lord. Then said he, These are the two
anointed ones, that stand by the Lord of the whole earth."

The two witnesses are not books. They are "two anointed ones"---present
tense, before Joe Smith was born; present tense, before Mohammed ever
kicked the desert sand out of his boots. The two olive trees are "the
two anointed ones (present tense), that stand by the Lord of the whole
earth," present in Zechariah 4 (written 530--487 B.C.). Therefore, there
isn't any chance whatsoever that either of them could be the Key to the
Scriptures or the Book of Mormon. So, the first thing we learn about
Mormon elders and Christian Science practitioners is that they are
bald‑faced liars who have no regard for the Scripture at all. (As
individuals we may love them and pray for them; as individuals we may
witness to them and try to win them to Jesus Christ. But as church
organizations, you can disregard anything that the Catholic Church,
Seventh-day Adventist, Church of Christ, Buddhist, Hindu, Mohammedan,
Jehovah Witness, Taoist, or follower of Confucius ever said about the
two witnesses in Revelation 11. You can disregard it without any
compunction whatsoever, knowing it is just another outrageous,
bald‑faced lie; unless of course, they admit that these "are the two
anointed ones, that stand by \[present tense\] the Lord of the whole
earth," 487 years before Jesus Christ was born!) The Scripture told you
what it meant, as it meant it, where it said it, the way it was written,
with no reference to the Greek or the Hebrew; you did not need a
lexicon. You did not need a teacher. You didn't have to go to school;
you didn't have to have the brains of a sixth‑grade grammar school boy.
All you had to do was to believe what you read, as you read it, where
you found it.

11:5 "And if any man will hurt them, fire proceedeth out of their mouth,
and devoureth their enemies: and if any man will hurt them, he must in
this manner be killed."

You can burn the Book of Mormon, and no fire will come out of it to burn
you; you can burn the Apocrypha, and no papal anathema will come near
you. (The AV 1611 translators translated the Apocrypha, but they made a
note by it that it was not inspired, and not to be included in the Old
Testament. A few years later, they threw it out entirely and didn't even
want it in their Bible. It has never been in the AV 1611 Bible since,
and you didn't see any fire coming out of the Apocrypha and burning any
AV 1611 translator!) You might find a pope going around trying to burn
Protestants because they don't care for the Apocrypha. But the passage
(Rev. 11) is not on the Pope, and no pope "stood by the Lord" in 487
B.C.!

11:6 "These have power to shut heaven, that it rain not in the days of
their prophecy: and have power over waters to turn them to blood, and to
smite the earth with all plagues, as often as they will."

The "two anointed ones that stand by the Lord of the whole earth"
destroy their enemies with fire, and one of them has "power to shut
heaven, that it rain not in the days of their prophecy." (Look at verse
2---forty‑two months; look at verse 3---three and a half years; compare
Scripture with Scripture---James 5:17, three years and six months.)

James 5:17---"Elias was a man subject to like passions as we are, and he
prayed earnestly that it might not rain: and it rained not on the earth
by the space of three years and six months."

Then, there is no doubt about the first witness at all. The first
witness is not the Bible, never was the Bible, and never will be the
Bible. The first witness is an "anointed" prophet that stands "by the
Lord of the whole earth," fire proceeds out of his mouth to destroy his
enemies, and he "shuts heaven" so that it doesn't rain for "three and a
half years." If there is any doubt about this interpretation, we
fortunately have the word of God and the plain English to lean upon, and
we never have to run to such a cheap opportunist as a pope, or such
superstitious, self‑righteous individuals as "cardinals" and "bishops"
to get their corrupt, private interpretations.

1 Kings 17:1---"And Elijah the Tishbite, who was of the inhabitants of
Gilead, said unto Ahab, As the Lord God of Israel liveth, before whom I
stand, there shall not be dew nor rain these years, but according to my
word."

Elijah stood before the Lord of the whole earth. He was present on the
Mount of Transfiguration (Matt. 17) on the right side of the
candlestick, and guess who was on the left---Moses.

"And have power over waters to turn them to blood, and to smite the
earth with all plagues, as often as they will."

Notice the colon in the middle of the verse after the word "prophecy,"
and that the second clause applies to the other witness. The second
witness has "power over waters to turn them to blood, and to smite the
earth with all plagues, as often as they will." There is no doubt about
who this is; this is Moses. (Don't you see how Revelation 11 opens
things? If it means what it says, as it says it, where it says it, a
Bible believing preacher can preach on the Second Coming of Christ, the
second coming of Moses, the second coming of Elijah, the second temple
to be rebuilt, and the second \[Millennial\] worship restored to
Jerusalem. A country preacher can count on history going in a complete
circle and repeating itself to the letter, without any fear of being
wrong whatsoever, knowing absolutely for certain that if the passage
means what it says, that he has an infallible key to the future which no
"pope" that ever lived knows anything about at all, as all "popes"are
Postmillennialists and Amillennialists!)

Exodus 33:20--21---"And he said, Thou canst not see my face: for there
shall no man see me, and live. And the Lord said, Behold, there is a
place by me, and thou shalt stand upon a rock."

Then Elijah and Moses both stood before the Lord of the whole earth. In
the Old Testament and the New Testament, both of them were "anointed."

Numbers 16:31, 34--35---"And it came to pass, as he had made an end of
speaking all these words, that the ground clave asunder that was under
them:\...And all Israel that were round about them fled at the cry of
them: for they said, Lest the earth swallow us up also. And there came
out a fire from the Lord, and consumed the two hundred and fifty men
that offered incense."

This is Moses speaking to the congregation of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.
Notice, the fire does its work in the case of Moses. Now, let's see if
fire had anything to do with the way Elijah destroyed his enemies.

2 Kings 1:10---"And Elijah answered and said to the captain of fifty, If
I be a man of God, then let fire come down from heaven, and consume thee
and thy fifty. And there came down fire from heaven, and consumed him
and his fifty."

Moses and Elijah were both anointed; Enoch wasn't.

Moses and Elijah stand by the Lord of the whole earth; Enoch doesn't.

Moses and Elijah both destroyed their enemies by fire; Enoch didn't.

Moses and Elijah smote the earth with plagues and kept it from raining
for three and a half years; Enoch didn't.

Moses and Elijah were on Mt. Sinai forty days and forty nights; Enoch
wasn't.

Moses and Elijah were on there forty days and forty nights with nothing
to eat or drink; Enoch wasn't.

Moses and Elijah both had ministers who took up their ministry (Joshua
and Elisha); Enoch didn't.

Moses and Elijah both appear with Jesus on the Mount of Transfiguration;
Enoch didn't.

Moses and Elijah are the last two human beings mentioned in the Old
Testament (Mal. 4), and they are mentioned in connection with Mt. Sinai
(Horeb) and the Lord Jesus Christ.

Now, let us go through the reasons why some Fundamentalists think that
they could not be Moses and Elijah.

The first reason why they think it could not be Moses is that Moses was
not "caught up without dying." (Most commentaries say that it is Enoch
and Elijah because both Enoch and Elijah were caught up without dying.
They say that since Moses died and was buried that it could not be him.
But this is an error.)

Deuteronomy 34:5--6---"So Moses the servant of the Lord died there in
the land of Moab, according to the word of the Lord. And he buried him
in a valley in the land of Moab, over against Beth‑peor: but no man
knoweth of his sepulchre unto this day."

The Lord buried Moses, but they never could find where Moses was buried
any more than they could find Enoch when they looked for him; Moses did
not stay buried!

Jude 9---"Yet Michael the archangel, when contending with the devil he
disputed about the body of Moses, durst not bring against him a railing
accusation, but said, The Lord rebuke thee."

Then, the "body of Moses" was a point of contention between Michael and
the Devil. This "body of Moses" was used. There is no doubt that the
"body of Moses" appears on the Mount of Transfiguration with Elijah.
Here, the Lord Jesus Christ appears in glory (as Simon Peter said He did
in 2 Pet. 1) with Moses and Elijah. This shows that Moses did not stay
dead; he did not stay buried; he came up from the dead. He came up ahead
of time; hence, there was a contention about the use of his body between
Michael and Satan.

Now, there is an objection that Elijah was already come in the form of
John the Baptist, and therefore, that the second witness could not be
Elijah. This interpretation is due to more than a lack of intelligence,
common sense, and education on the part of the expositor; it is just
plain carelessness in handling the word of God. Now watch the verses
very carefully, and believe what you read, as you read it, where you
find it, without referring to the Westcott and Hort Vaticanus so‑called
"original Greek."

Matthew 17:9‑11---"And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus
charged them, saying, Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be
risen again from the dead. And his disciples asked him, saying, Why then
say the scribes that Elias must first come? And Jesus answered and said
unto them, Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things."

Notice the word "restore" (restoration), as you always find it in
connection with the Second Coming of Christ (Acts 3:20--21). The
statement of the Lord Jesus Christ is that Elijah is going to come.

Matthew 17:12--13---"But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and
they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed.
Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them. Then the disciples
understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist."

Therefore, because of verses 12--13, the expositors seem in unanimous
agreement that Christ is already come, that John the Baptist was Elijah.
Therefore, all the Old Testament prophecies were fulfilled at the first
coming, and they are to be spiritualized if they refer to the Second
Coming. This is typical of the irreverent, insincere, and bungling type
of exegesis that is characteristic of all Amillennial and Postmillennial
expositors. The idea is that since Jesus Christ said that John the
Baptist was Elijah (Matt. 17:12--13), that all the prophecies in the Old
Testament which deal with the great and dreadful coming "day of the
Lord," with the forerunner Elijah, were fulfilled at the first coming of
Christ, with the forerunner John the Baptist. Therefore, any passage
that deals with the "day of the Lord" is to be applied to the first
coming.

Notice the passage carefully. "Elias truly shall first come, and restore
all things. But (apparent contradiction) I say unto you\...." What you
find here is an apparent contradiction, and yet, there is no
contradiction if you analyze it carefully. And we are going to analyze
it carefully.

Luke 9:18--20---"And it came to pass, as he was alone praying, his
disciples were with him: and he asked them, saying, Whom say the people
that I am? They answering said, John the Baptist; but some say, Elias;
and others say, that one of the old prophets is risen again. He said
unto them, But whom say ye that I am? Peter answering said, The Christ
of God."

Then there is a confusion about three people---John the Baptist, Jesus
Christ, and Elias. Although you were told by the Lord Jesus Christ that
John the Baptist was Elias, look at John's own testimony.

John 1:19--21---"And this is the record of John, when the Jews sent
priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou? And he
confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ. And they
asked him, What then? Art thou Elias? And he saith, I am not. Art thou
that prophet? And he answered, No."

Is that clear? (This is typical of the apparent contradictions that you
may find in the word of God.) Jesus says that John the Baptist was
Elias; John the Baptist says that he was not Elias! This is a perfect
apparent contradiction. (This is what makes some people throw away the
Bible and go back to reading Voltaire, Tom Paine, Rousseau, and the rest
of them!) But, this is not a real contradiction. Watch it get ironed out
as the Holy Spirit uses the plain AV 1611 English to unravel the problem
which the Greek scholars cannot unravel.

Luke 1:15--17---"For he \[John\] shall be great in the sight of the
Lord, and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink; and he shall be
filled with the Holy Ghost, even from his mother's womb. And many of the
children of Israel shall he turn to the Lord their God. And he shall go
before him in the spirit and power of Elias, to turn the hearts of the
fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just;
to make ready a people prepared for the Lord."

The apparent contradiction is unravelled by the fact that John the
Baptist comes in "the spirit and power of Elijah," and if the people had
received Jesus Christ as their Messiah and accepted the gospel in Acts
2--7, Christ would have come back, and John the Baptist would have been
Elijah! It is worked out in a double conundrum, a twin paradox, a split
enigma, so that no scholar on the face of the earth can ever find it
from the Greek; it is obscure in the "Greek." The "Greek" would yield
nothing on this truth whatsoever. "Study to shew thyself approved unto
God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word
of truth" (2 Tim. 2:15).

The first coming of Christ could have been the Second Coming (see
commentary on Matt. 1--3). Every prophecy in the Old Testament could
have been fulfilled literally, if Jesus Christ had returned in Acts 7.
Jesus Christ, foreseeing that He might have returned in Acts 7 (had the
Jews received His message), tells the Apostles when He is on the earth
before the crucifixion and their commission, "But when they persecute
you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye
shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be
come" (Matt. 10:23). The Son of man did not come; yet they did go over
the cities of Israel and to the ends of the earth, and He never came.
So, our Postmillennial and Amillennial blockheads are stuck in the same
rut they always get stuck in. They have to make the passage
spiritual---i.e., Christ didn't really mean what He was saying, or that
He was referring to something that nobody knows anything about, or that
His first coming was His Second Coming, or that He just flat lied to the
Apostles. This is very typical of the deranged and disjointed type of
teaching which is carried on continually by even orthodox, conservative,
"fundamental" Christian scholars. Any system that makes one verse
contradict another verse, or any system where you have to spiritualize a
passage that is plainly literal, or any system that makes a liar out of
Jesus Christ or a liar out of the prophets, is the wrong system.

Notice how the Holy Spirit reinforces the plain English (as we compare
Mal. 4 with Luke 1) in teaching that John the Baptist is only a type of
Elijah, who would have fulfilled the prophecy if Christ had returned in
the Book of Acts. Since Christ delays His return (upon Israel's
rejection) and calls out Paul to preach to the ends of the earth (and
the Church Age is ushered in), then Elijah's return is postponed until a
later date.

Malachi 4:4--6---"Remember ye the law of Moses my servant, which I
commanded unto him in Horeb for all Israel, with the statutes and
judgments. Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming
of the great and dreadful day of the Lord: And he shall turn the heart
of the fathers to the children, and the heart of the children to their
fathers, lest I come and smite the earth with a curse."

Luke 1:17---"And he shall go before him in the spirit and power of
Elias, to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the
disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to make ready a people prepared
for the Lord."

The commissions are not the same. They are not the same because the
plain English, in the plain passage, where you plainly read it, is not
the same wording. Therefore, since they are not the same words, and they
are spelled differently, they are not the same. It is really simple if
you believe it. You have no problem in understanding; your problem is in
unbelief, as every man's problem is unbelief (see comments in Matthew
16:16--18).

In Malachi 4:4--6 you are told that Horeb (Mt. Sinai), Israel, the Lord,
Moses, and Elijah are the five proper names that will be the main
subjects of interest at the Second Advent. Before the "great and
dreadful day of the Lord" comes, you've got to deal with Mt. Sinai, the
Lord Jesus Christ, Israel, Elijah, and Moses. Elijah comes to turn
Israel back to God lest Christ come at the Second Advent and "smite the
earth with a curse." John the Baptist comes at the first coming of
Christ to turn "the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to make ready
a people prepared for the Lord." John the Baptist comes "in the spirit
and power of Elias," and therefore, to all practical purposes, is
Elijah, and would have been Elijah, if Jesus Christ had returned in the
Book of Acts. Since Jesus Christ does not return in the Book of Acts,
the Book of Acts becomes one of the most difficult books in the entire
Bible to understand, and consequently, every unsaved teacher and
preacher on the face of the earth is eager to get his main doctrinal
material from the Book of Acts. There isn't a heretic in your city or
mine who doesn't begin his theology with Acts 2. The situation in Acts 2
is not a present day situation at all. In Acts 2, they are all
Apostles---there are none now. They all have signs, wonders, and
miracles---they don't now. (If they say they do, check them, and you
will find that they are a bunch of "liars" \[Rev. 2\].) In Acts 2, they
are connecting water baptism with salvation, exactly as John the Baptist
connected it before the crucifixion---it is no longer connected any more
(1 Cor. 1:17). They split their property and make it communal
property---this is where Marx got his manifesto---Christians no longer
do that. The Aquatic Campbellites think they are obeying the word of God
because they keep Acts 2:38, and yet there isn't a Campbellite you ever
met in your life that kept Acts 2:42--45 (in the same chapter)! Do you
know why they don't? Because in Acts 2 Israel is waiting for the Second
Coming of Jesus Christ. In Acts 3 Peter is not even telling these people
that their sins were blotted out at Calvary! He is telling them that
their sins will be blotted out at the Second Coming of Jesus Christ
(Acts 3:19)!

The key to the Book of Revelation is Chapter 11. Once you know that
Moses and Elijah are the two witnesses, you know a thousand other
things. For example:

Did you know that not only Moses and Elijah were on Mt. Sinai, but that
Jesus was on this mountain? (See Matt. 4; Luke 4.) (You say that
mountain is unidentified. Is it?) Did you know that Jesus was up there
40 days and 40 nights with nothing to eat or drink? Did you know that
Moses was on Mt. Sinai 40 days and 40 nights with nothing to eat or
drink? Did you know that when Elijah ran from Jezebel that he was 40
days and 40 nights going to Mt. Sinai with nothing to eat or drink? (See
1 Kings 19:8.) Did you know that Moses died east of Jordan on Mt.
Pisgah? Did you know that when Elijah was caught up in a whirlwind he
was caught up from where Moses died? Did you know that Elijah crossed
the Jordan where Jesus Christ was baptized? Did you know that when Moses
died he commissioned Joshua to carry out his work? Did you know that
when Elijah was caught up to heaven he commissioned Elisha to carry out
his work? Did you know that when Jesus died and went back to heaven He
commissioned Paul to preach the Gospel to the Gentiles in Romans
15:16--19? Did you know that Moses' minister, Joshua, had signs and
miracles accompanying his ministry to Israel? Did you know that Elijah's
minister, Elisha, had the same signs and wonders that Elijah had? Did
you know that Jesus Christ's minister to the Gentiles, Paul, had the
signs, wonders, and miracles that Jesus Christ had? If there is any
doubt about this, look at Romans 15:16, 18--19.

If Moses preached to Pharaoh in the Old Testament, then Pharaoh is a
type of the Antichrist in Revelation 11, yet to come. If Elijah preached
to the wicked prince of Israel, Ahab, then Ahab is a type of the
Antichrist yet to come. Since Ahab's wife was Jezebel, you will find
Jezebel referred to in Revelation 2 in connection with Baal worship, yet
to come.

The additional revelations begin to come too fast and too furious to
handle, and the Scriptures involved are too many to list. So, let us lay
this thing out where the reader can get a hold of it, even if it is
"unorthodox."

When the Lord Jesus Christ came to this earth the first time to die for
sinners, it was prophesied in the Old Testament. It was also prophesied
that He had to be buried and rise from the dead the third day.
Therefore, Jesus Christ had to come and die on the cross, be buried, and
come up again before all Old Testament prophecies could be fulfilled.
When Christ comes up from the dead and goes back to heaven in Acts 1,
what is there left in the Old Testament that yet needs to be fulfilled?
The answer is---the prophecies which deal with the Second Coming of
Christ.

There are more than 500 prophecies in the Old Testament that deal with
His Second Coming. When you make the first coming the Second Coming
(like these Amillennialists and Postmillennialists do), you spiritualize
these 500 literal promises. The Lord Jesus Christ had to come the first
time, and He will have to come the second time; the only missing
information in the Old Testament is the time element between the First
and Second Coming. This time element is so undisclosed that the prophets
of God couldn't even find the time gap between the "sufferings of
Christ, and the glory that should follow" (1 Pet. 1:9--12).

This is the problem that the modern‑day theologian has. The modern‑day
seminary professor and teacher (in every major denominational seminary
in the country) is unable to get the time element between the First and
Second Coming. That is, he is just as much in the dark as a Hebrew, Old
Testament saint was in 1000 B.C. It is one of the great jokes that God
has played on smart men. While these simpletons sit around in
professors' chairs (teaching Greek to their pupils, talking about higher
knowledge, critical research, "accredited institutions," access to
"greater knowledge than ever before," and scientific advancement of man)
and turn up their noses at the poor, superstitious, Old Testament Jews
(who "looked for a literal kingdom" when they should have looked for a
"spiritual one"), the poor, blinded fools have made the same error.

Since there was no time element given between the First and Second
Coming, the Jew was looking for the Second Coming at the First Coming.
The Jew was looking for a literal, physical, visible Kingdom on earth
when Christ came the first time. They crucified Him, then He was buried
and arose from the dead. Until Acts 7, the literal, visible, physical
Kingdom is offered to Israel. (That's the teaching in Acts 1--7. In Acts
1--7 you won't find anybody preaching the "gospel of the grace of God"
that Paul preaches in Gal. 1--2, and Eph. 2--3. In Acts 1--7 the Jew is
still waiting for the fulfillment of a literal, physical, visible
Kingdom.) The only trouble is that the Jew does not want Jesus Christ as
his Messiah for this Kingdom. So, he not only crucifies and rejects Him,
but after the resurrection, he turns down the offer of the Kingdom. On
the cross, Jesus said, "Father, forgive them; for they know not what
they do." The Lord did forgive them for they knew not what they did. In
Acts 2--7 they had a second chance to receive Jesus Christ as their
Messiah, which they turned down.

Since the time element between the First and Second Coming is not given,
the Jew thought the comings were identical. When he thought they were
identical, he rejected the Messiah that God gave him. Therefore, he puts
off indefinitely his opportunity to have a visible, literal, physical,
Davidic, Messianic Kingdom in Palestine.

Now, the average Gentile American theologian (when I talk about this
group, I'm talking about seminary professors and Greek scholars and the
Ecumenical leaders) makes the same error except he makes it backwards.
In this age he looks back to the first coming, and he says that since
there is no time element given between the First and Second Coming, when
Christ came the first time, that was the fulfillment of the literal,
physical, visible promises to Israel. Therefore, since the Jews did not
get literal, physical, visible promises, these promises are to be
spiritualized and applied to the Christian! That's how God makes fools
out of everybody. God writes a New Testament to make a fool out of an
unsaved Jew, and He writes an Old Testament to make a fool out of an
unsaved Gentile. The resultant "fool" is the product of a bad heart.
When the Gentile turns up his nose at an Old Testament Jew for making a
mistake in regard to the Kingdom, he should remember that he has already
made the same mistake. You better get the plank out of your own eye
before you take the splinter out of somebody else's eye!

The Jews thought that because there was no time element given between
the two, the First Coming was the Second Coming; but they did not want
this Messiah as a literal, physical, visible leader. So they killed Him.
He arose from the dead, and they had a second chance to receive the
Kingdom, and they turned it down. From that point on, a new era comes
in, in which a spiritual kingdom of God is preached, a man has to be
"bom again," and receive "spiritual blessings in heavenly places" until
the Second Coming of Christ. The time element between the First and
Second Coming is unknown.

This means that Jesus Christ could have come back to this earth anytime
up to Acts 7, and every promise in the Old Testament would have been
fulfilled. When Stephen is stoned in Acts 7, he looks up to heaven and
sees "the Son of man standing" up, ready to come back, and from there on
in the Bible, He is always spoken of as being seated. He did not return.
The time element is an unknown element. It means that the physical,
literal, visible promises given to Israel in the Old Testament, in
regards to the Second Coming of Christ, are going to take place later.
It does not mean that they are to be spiritualized and put into this
age. That's where the modernists, liberals, and dead orthodox
conservatives make their gross mistake. The unknown time element is the
day and age in which we are living---the Church Age; this is the age of
the "calling out" of the one spiritual body of "born‑again believers" in
Jesus Christ. It is an unknown period of time, and the end of it is not
clearly given in Scripture.

If Moses and Elijah are going to come back before the Second Coming of
Christ (and they are), then they have yet to come in the future. If
Christ had returned in Acts 7, then Elijah would have fulfilled the
promises in the Old Testament by appearing in "spirit and power" in John
the Baptist. What about Moses? There is no clear promise in the Old
Testament that Moses had to come back; the only clear promise is that
Elijah will come (Mal. 4:3--6)! You are not told that Moses will come
back until Revelation 11, which is written in A.D. 90, long after Acts
7. All that has to happen in Acts 7 is for Christ to come back and
destroy the Antichrist. (The Antichrist is present, by the way, but
we'll get into that when we get into Rev. 13.)

The Bible teaches, therefore, that before the Second Coming of Christ,
Moses and Elijah will come again, but you don't learn that until
Revelation 11. If you read just the Old Testament (which is all the
Apostles had---Genesis to Malachi), you have the second coming of Elijah
already fulfilled by John the Baptist; you have the first coming of
Christ already fulfilled in His death, burial, and resurrection. He
could return and destroy Rome (which will again be in power at the
Second Coming). He could have done it in Acts 7. This means that Acts
1--7 is a transition period, in which God is having an opportunity to
fulfill His promises to Israel. If Israel had received Christ as its
Messiah officially, and Israel's elders, priests, and scribes had
trusted Him as their Messiah, Jesus Christ would have come back to this
earth and set up a Millennial Reign at Jerusalem in Acts 8. (If you
don't believe it, look at that Hamite Gentile \[Acts 8\] coming from the
temple \[reading Isaiah the prophet\], as the Gentiles will have to come
to Jerusalem in the Millennium \[Isa. 11:10; 42:11! It's no secret that
when Christ comes back the second time, the Gentiles will have to come
to Jerusalem to worship Him \[Isa. 60:3, 5, 16\]. It's no secret that
the Gentiles are going to get in on the plan of salvation in the
