fear thy name, small and great; and shouldest destroy them which destroy
the earth."

"And the nations were angry." This passage is found in Revelation
20:8--9 where the nations gather together to wipe out Jerusalem for the
last time (12‑inch mark).

"Thy wrath is come." The fulfillment of this passage is found in
Revelation 20:9, where the fire of God comes down and consumes
everything.

"And the time of the dead, that they should be judged." This passage is
found in Revelation 20:11--12.

Now comes a very difficult passage. "That thou shouldest give reward
unto thy servants the prophets, and to the saints, and them that fear
thy name, small and great; and shouldest destroy them which destroy the
earth."

Now, you've been taught (if you've been taught at all) that there are no
saved people at the Great White Throne Judgment. You've been taught that
this is the judgment of the unsaved dead only, and there are no saved
people there. This is an error. You can see it immediately by the fact
that there are "rewards" passed out at the White Throne Judgment. (Once
again, "Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth
not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.") Revelation
11:18 is not the Judgment Seat of Christ where New Testament Christians
are rewarded; it is the Great White Throne Judgment where "his servants
the prophets" are rewarded. On your chart you have the church caught up
at the 1‑inch mark and judged at the Judgment Seat of Christ during the
Tribulation. The judgment of Church‑Age saints takes place in heaven,
during the Tribulation. But this judgment (Rev. 11) takes place at the
end of the Millennium, yet there are saved people there. (The word
"saints" throws the expositors into a panic; they think the word
"saints" applies to Church‑Age saints, as Paul uses the word in Romans
1; 1 Corinthians 1; etc.)

This is the same error I've spoken of many times before. One of the
great errors which Fundamental scholars get into is finding one truth,
and then trying to take all the Bible and make it fit that one truth.
(That's what Baptists are notorious for. They get an idea that because
they have the truth on salvation "by grace through faith" \[which is
so\], and eternal security \[which is so\], that all passages that teach
you "can lose it" should be changed, and so they go back to the Greek to
change them. They think that all passages that teach any other plan of
salvation should be rejected or twisted or distorted, and shoved into
the Church Age until the verses are forced to teach salvation by grace.)
Wherever a Baptist finds the word "saint," he just naturally puts it
into Romans 1:7 and makes it apply to a Christian in the New Testament
Church Age---which it may not. For example, in Daniel 7 (as the first
time the word "saint" is used in the Bible, 1 Sam. 2:9), it has no
reference to Church‑Age saints at all. (Don't forget that Daniel and
Revelation fit together.) Notice, the context of Daniel 7 is the
Antichrist fighting against people who have to "endure to the end" to be
saved.

Daniel 7:21--22---"I beheld, and the same horn made war with the saints,
and prevailed against them; Until the Ancient of days came, and judgment
was given to the saints of the most High; and the time came that the
saints possessed the kingdom."

See Daniel 7:18---"But the saints of the most High shall take the
kingdom, and possess the kingdom for ever, even for ever and ever."
(Compare Rev. 11:15.)

The passage is talking about Tribulation saints, who are judged at the
Great White Throne Judgment, and then inherit an eternal kingdom, in
eternity. The passage is not talking about Church‑Age saints, who are
rewarded at the Judgment Seat of Christ before the Millennium; the
passage is talking about Tribulation saints, who are rewarded at the
White Throne Judgment, and reign in eternity over a literal, physical,
visible, earthly, Davidic, Messianic, Palestinian Kingdom promised to
Israel. (Now, this is a great deal more than Scofield, Gaebelein,
Pettingill, Pember, Larkin, Darby, Bullinger, Luther, Wesley, and most
Fundamental expositors could take, so we'll "call off the dogs" for a
moment; but in Revelation 11:18, it is all very clear if you believe
what you read, as you read it, where you find it, the way it is
written.) This time has to be a time when Christ has finished reigning
(vs. 17), when "the nations are angry" (vs. 18), when the "wrath of God"
is come (vs. 18), "the time of the dead, that they should be judged"
(vs. 18), and at that time He will give reward to His servants, "the
prophets, and to the saints, and to them that fear His name, small and
great, and He will destroy them that would destroy the earth."

This brings up two interesting problems---saints at the White Throne
Judgment, whose names are in "the book of life," and the fact that
Tribulation and Millennium saints have to have some works connected with
their salvation. \[Every verse in Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews that
teaches you "can lose it," means just what it says! But since these
verses appear to contradict verses on eternal security, the error is in
the system of interpretation. If there is anything that must be an
abomination to God (outside of a man rejecting His Son), it must be a
preacher (professing to be an authority on the word of God) who is
teaching people they can "lose their salvation" by taking passages like
Revelation 11:18, 12:16, 14:12, 22:14; Acts 2:38; Mark 16:16, etc. and
sticking them into the Church Age.\]

"That thou shouldest give reward unto thy servants the prophets (not to
born‑again believers in the Body of Christ), and to the saints (the
context every place you find it, is Israel), and them that fear thy
name, small and great; and shouldest destroy them which destroy the
earth."

Notice, the text of these rewards is "fearing His name"---not "believing
on His name," nor "being born again;" not "receiving Christ" as your
Saviour, but "fearing His name." This gospel is a Tribulation gospel.

Revelation 14:6--7---"And I saw another angel fly in the midst of
heaven, having the everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on
the earth, and to every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people,
Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour
of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and earth,
and the sea, and the fountains of waters."

Notice, these people who "fear His name" are Tribulation saints. When
did Paul ever stand before an audience and say, "Fear His name"? Paul
said, "Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved." Paul
said, "And by him all that believe are justified from all things, from
which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses."

Observe, also, that the people who get saved in the Tribulation are
judged at the White Throne Judgment; they get rewards for dying for
Christ; their death is part of their salvation. They've "washed their
robes white in the blood of the Lamb." They have feared His name,
"endured until the end," and they have not taken the mark of the beast
because they did "fear His name." They feared His name because of the
coming hour of judgment (Rev. 14:6--7). This means that Tribulation
saints and Millennium saints will not appear before the Judgment Seat of
Christ. Christians have their judgment at the Judgment Seat of Christ
following the Rapture; Tribulation and Millennium saints have their
judgment at the White Throne Judgment---the same judgment where the
unsaved dead are judged. Hence, the "books" are there. Didn't you ever
wonder why those books were there? (Most evangelists say that they are
opened just to "prove your name is not in them"---to make sure your
damnation is complete. That's foolishness! People say that the "Book of
life" is opened to show and prove to you that your name was there so
that Satan can't accuse you. That's foolishness! When those books are
opened, the Christian will have already been just like Jesus Christ for
1000 years! \[See 1 John 3; Philippians 3; 1 Corinthians 15. \] Those
books are there because somebody's names are in them and somebody's
aren't. The names that are in them are the names of Tribulation and
Millennium saints. You get your name in there by works, brother, and
works can take it out! Now, you know what Revelation 3:5 means, "He that
overcometh \[works\], the same shall be clothed in white raiment; and I
will not blot out his name out of the book of life." This also applies
to Revelation 22:19, "And if any man shall take away from the words of
the book of this prophecy, God shall take away his part out of the book
of life."

There is an element of works connected with salvation in the Tribulation
and the Millennium that is not present in this age. These "books" are
for those people. Hence, you will find these "books" mentioned primarily
in the Old Testament. I know my name is in the Book of Life because Paul
mentioned it in a "Pauline epistle" \[Phil. 4\], but I'm not worried
about getting it blotted out because my name \[in the Lamb's book of
life\] is not conditional upon my "enduring to the end" of the
Tribulation or my living by the Sermon on the Mount, but because the
Lamb gave me His life. "He that hath the Son hath life; and he that hath
not the Son of God hath not life" \[1 John 5:12\]. If they take my name
out, they'll have to take the Lamb's name out too; I'm in Him!)

11:19 "And the temple of God was opened in heaven, and there was seen in
his temple the ark of his testament: and there were lightnings, and
voices, and thunderings, and an earthquake, and great hail."

This takes you right back to the narrative of Revelation 11:15 at the
end of the Tribulation. These events are the same events described at
the end of the Tribulation in Revelation 6:12--14 and Revelation 16:21.

CHAPTER 12

In studying the Book of Revelation thus far, we have learned some
amazing things, and many of these things, of course, run contradictory
to the teachings of the Roman Catholic Church, the National Council of
Churches, the Ecumenical Council, and the Lutheran, Methodist, Baptist,
Presbyterian, and Episcopal seminaries. But we would naturally expect
"the majority" to be wrong in spiritual matters, as they always have
been. We have found verses which reveal truths that are not only
contradictory to the teaching of Postmillennial and Amillennial "one
world" socialists, but we have also found many new truths in Revelation
that have never been revealed to the great Premillennial writers of
1850--1960. You will notice that these new truths have not been obtained
by inventing a quaint and novel teaching; they have not been revealed by
"going to the Greek" or the "Hebrew." They have not been revealed by
obtaining more "original manuscripts," or by doing greater research into
"original sources," nor by any of the ways by which new revelations are
supposed to appear. I stress this because all revelation comes from God.
God the Holy Spirit is the Author of Scripture, and the Scripture that
God the Holy Spirit reveals to the English-speaking people is the plain
English as you find it, where you find it. Hence, we learn many things
from the AV 1611 Bible that were hidden from Scofield, Peters,
Pettingill, McClain, Gaebelein, Pentecost, DeHaan, Epp, Kemp, Pember,
Larkin, Newell, Sauer, and other great writers. These men were great
Christian writers; many of these men were men whom God has used in the
ministry, and continues to use. But when any man, regardless of his
education or ability, past, present, or future reputation, character, or
usefulness to God---when any man, regardless of his degrees,
understanding of the Bible, faithfulness in service, or degrees of
consecration---when any man ceases to accept the AV 1611 Reformation
Text of the Textus Receptu, as the final, Divine Authority for use in
this century, God closes all future revelation to that man. He slams the
door right flat in his face! I don't mean that the man isn't saved; I
don't mean that he may not be a "soul winner." I mean that everything he
knows he must get from reading other books, and God will show him
nothing directly from the Scripture. (Now that's quite a "chew off the
quid," but that's how it goes.) If you've discovered any new or novel
things in this commentary on the Book of Revelation, you must remember
where you learned them. You learned them from the English text with the
English text, in the context where you found it, as God intended it to
be. You did not learn it from me. I am not capable of interpreting it;
all the understanding and knowledge any man has, he receives directly
from the Lord Jesus Christ, and the Holy Spirit, through simply reading
and believing the plain English text for plain, simple‑minded
English‑speaking people like you and me, in sixth‑grade language (where
any blockhead can get it if he believes what he reads!).

You have learned so far that the Book of Revelation contains four
accounts of the Tribulation, and that the Book of Revelation is divided
into three sections---Chapters 1--3; Chapters 4--19; and Chapters
20--22. You've learned in these three sections that the Church Age is
pictured in Revelation 1--3, the Tribulation is pictured in Revelation
4--19, and the Millennium and new heavens and new earth are found in the
remainder of the book. You've learned that this division is reliable, as
far as its application goes, by the fact that heaven opens two
times---once in Revelation 4 and once in Revelation 19. In Revelation 4
somebody went up, and in Revelation 19 somebody came down. John was told
to write the book in three tenses---what he had seen, what he was
seeing, and what God was going to show him. You've learned that after
the Church Age the Body of Christ is caught out (Rev. 4:1--2, with John
being a type of the Body of Christ) and the Tribulation takes place on
this earth. It is a period called "Daniel's Seventieth Week" or "the
time of Jacob's trouble." (It is two equal periods of three and one-half
years.) The Book of Revelation is four successive accounts of the events
that take place in this time. As there are four gospels recording the
first coming of Christ (Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John), there are four
accounts of the Tribulation in the Book of Revelation. One of them ends
in Revelation 6; one of them ends in Revelation 11; one ends in
Revelation 14; and the last one ends in Revelation 19. The first account
of the Tribulation is under the figure of "seals" (Rev. 4--6); the
second account is under the figure of "trumpets"(Rev. 7--11); the third
account is discussing the work and ministry of the Antichrist (Rev.
12--14); the last account is under the figure of "vials" or "bowls"
(Rev. 16--19).

Some of the odd facts that you have learned in this study are:

1\. There is a post‑Tribulation Rapture of Tribulation saints (a fact
unknown to most Premillennial scholars).

2\. There is an element of works connected with salvation in the
Tribulation that is not present in the Church Age. (This is a fact that
is unrecognized and unadmitted by every Premillennial scholar that ever
lived, due to the fact that he rejected what he read, as he read it,
where he found it in the 1611 AV, and went to the Westcott and Hort
Greek Text to prove eternal security \[where many passages do not prove
it\]. Rather than endanger one precious truth of this age \[which is
true!\], he perverted the whole Bible to make it match this age, which
it does not.)

3\. Moses and Elijah are going to come again and witness to the
Antichrist, be decapitated, and come back to life again.

4\. Saved people of the Tribulation and Millennium will be judged at the
Great White Throne Judgment. (Again, this is a fact that is unrecognized
and unadmitted by any Premillennial scholarship.)

When you get into the Bible like this, you suddenly find that you are
"way out" by yourself. The average response of worldly, ignorant, saved
people towards your enthusiasm is going to be, "He thinks he's right and
everybody else is wrong." But that is also the typical retort of a
prejudiced, lazy rascal.

We have come to Revelation 12, and again, we are talking about events
that take place in the Tribulation. The time period of three and
one-half years is given again in this chapter. In Revelation 12:6,
you'll notice that this woman is fed "a thousand two hundred and
threescore days," exactly as you found it in Revelation 11:3. You'll
also find (in 12:14) that this woman is fed "for a time (one unit), and
times (two units), and a half a time" (one-half unit), making three and
one-half. This figure is prominent in this section of the Bible (Rev.
11--13). This, then, describes events that take place in the last half
of the Tribulation. Since the end of the Tribulation was found in
Revelation 11:15, and since the two witnesses prophesy three and
one-half years before the end, it stands to reason that Moses and Elijah
begin their prophetic ministry to Israel in the middle of the
Tribulation---at the three and one-half year mark. The events which take
place in Revelation 12 take place in the same period, and the events
that take place in Revelation 13 take place in the same period.
Therefore, Revelation 11--14 is a birdseye view of the last events of
the last three and one-half years of the Tribulation. In Revelation 11,
the primary concern is about the ministry of the two witnesses; in
Revelation 12 the primary concern is over the place of Israel in the
Tribulation; in Revelation 13 you have the ministry of the "christ" who
returns to earth---a red‑haired, blue‑eyed, bass‑voiced,
Sermon-on-the-Mount monstrosity, to damn the whole world. The "christ"
who returns in Revelation 13 is not the Jesus Christ revealed in the
Bible, but the traditional, mythological, legendary "christ" of Roman
Catholic fiction.

12:1 "And there appeared a great wonder in heaven; a woman clothed with
the sun, and the moon under her feet, and upon her head a crown of
twelve stars:

2 And she being with child cried, travailing in birth, and pained to be
delivered."

Here we have another wonderful place to study how "private
interpretation" operates. If you want to find the greatest private
interpreters of the word of God, you go first to Rome, and then you go
to the Protestant seminaries and the Greek scholars. For example, in
Revelation 12:1--2 we have "a woman clothed with the sun, and the moon
under her feet, and upon her head a crown of twelve stars: And she being
with child cried, travailing in birth, and pained to be delivered." Who
could this woman be? If you went to a dime store and picked up a picture
on a counter, you would find the "virgin Mary" standing on the moon with
the sun around her and the stars over her head! This is the "queen of
heaven" to Roman Catholic painters; this is the woman who brought forth
Christ. Since Mary brought forth Jesus Christ and is His mother, the
woman of Revelation 12:1--2 must be His mother!

Now, without being involved personally (or being critical of Rome), let
me just show you how the Bible, as it is written, makes a perfect
slaughterhouse out of such ridiculous interpretations. Let's pretend,
for just a moment, that the "woman" is Mary (just like any good pope!),
and read through the text and watch what an absurd fatuity this thing
turns into.

12:1 "And there appeared a great wonder in heaven, \[Mary\] clothed with
the sun, and the moon under her feet, and upon her head a crown of
twelve stars."

The statement of Pope Pius in 1920 (the "infallible teaching" of the
Roman Catholic Church "ex‑cathedra") is that Mary is now up in heaven.
This is based on Revelation 12:1! (If you don't believe that, you're not
a good Roman Catholic.) This teaching is said to be "infallible"
Christian doctrine, the living word of the Holy Spirit Himself,
according to the popes. But you couldn't find a bigger piece of baloney
if you looked in a delicatessen.

12:2 "And \[Mary\] being with child \[Jesus\] cried, travailing in
birth, and pained to be delivered.

3 And there appeared another wonder in heaven; and behold a great red
dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and seven crowns upon his
heads.

4 And his tail drew the third part of the stars of heaven, and did cast
them to the earth: and the dragon stood before the woman \[Mary\] which
was ready to be delivered, for to devour her child as soon as it was
born.

5 And she \[Mary\] brought forth a man child \[Jesus\], who was to rule
all nations with a rod of iron: and her child was caught up unto God,
and to his throne.

6 And the woman \[Mary\] fled into the wilderness, where she \[Mary\]
hath a place prepared of God, that they should feed her \[Mary\] there a
thousand two hundred and threescore days."

Revelation 12:13--14---"And when the dragon saw that he was cast unto
the earth, he persecuted the woman \[Mary\] which brought forth the man
child. And to the woman \[Mary\] were given two wings of a great eagle,
that she \[Mary\] might fly into the wilderness, into her place, where
she \[Mary\] is nourished for a time, and times, and half a time, from
the face of the serpent."

If there is a Roman Catholic reading this and you don't believe this,
there is one of two things wrong with you: you are lying about your
religion, and you are nothing but a hypocrite; or you are so stupid that
you don't know what you believe, why you believe it, or where you got it
from. The very idea of saying that "the woman" is Mary! You talk about
private interpretation! You talk about a pope dreaming up a
cock‑and‑bull story! You couldn't whip up a better vision than that
using marijuana, LSD, cocaine, and heroin.

Now, let's take the Protestant interpretation of the Reformation---the
interpretation taught by all the major denominational seminaries.

12:1 "And there appeared a great wonder in heaven, a woman \[the
church\] clothed with the sun, and the moon under her feet, and upon her
head a crown of twelve stars:

2 And she \[the church\] being with child cried, travailing in birth,
and pained to be delivered."

12:5 "And she \[the church\] brought forth a man child, who was to rule
all nations with a rod of iron: and her \[the church's\] child was
caught up unto God, and to his throne.

6 And the woman \[the church\] fled into the wilderness, where she \[the
church\] hath a place prepared of God\...."

Revelation 12:14---"And to the woman \[the church\] were given two wings
of a great eagle, that she \[the church\] might fly into the
wilderness\...."

If this woman is the church, what is the church doing keeping the Old
Testament Ten Commandments given under the law? (See Rev. 12:17.) The
seed of this "woman" keeps the Old Testament law. The church, the Body
of Christ, is not under Mosaic Law in this age; they are under grace
(Rom. 6:14). They are under the "law of liberty" and not the Mosaic Law
(Gal. 5:13). They are given new commandments instead of the Ten
Commandments (John 14:21, 15:10--18). They are given the commandments of
Jesus Christ, not the "commandments of God." In Revelation 12:17, the
remnant of the seed of the woman "keep the commandments of God," and
have the "testimony of Jesus Christ." This is "faith and works" as you
find it in James 2:24, written to the twelve tribes (Rev. 14:12; James
1:1).

Revelation 22:14---"Blessed are they that do his commandments, that they
may have right to the tree of life, and may enter in through the gates
into the city."

This is not the church, the Body of Christ. I don't have to eat of the
"tree of life." I already have the Saviour who gave me eternal life. I
don't have to get my life off a tree! "He that hath the Son hath life;
and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life" (1 John 5:12). The
people who get life, in the Tribulation, are under Old Testament Mosaic
law. Those in the Millennium are under the Sermon on the Mount, which
would make the Mosaic Law look like a left‑wing Constitution.

"The woman" is not the church, and "the woman" is not Mary. This is the
analysis of the Scripture; this is the judgment of the Scriptures
themselves on the verse. The Scriptures interpret themselves, for they
are their own critic. The criticism that I have given is the criticism
which the Bible puts on the text. The text says that whoever the "woman"
is, that she has to bring forth Jesus Christ; she has to flee to the
wilderness; she has to fly there with two wings; she has to be fed for
three and one-half years.

(One of the beauties of the Authorized Version, 1611, is its ability to
interpret Scripture with Scripture, without ever missing a hitch.)

Genesis 37:9---"And he dreamed yet another dream, and told it his
brethren, and said, Behold, I have dreamed a dream more; and, behold,
the sun and the moon and the eleven stars made obeisance to me."

In the English Bible, the Holy Spirit has preserved the interpretation
for you, without any reference to Mary or the church. In the context,
Joseph is dreaming dreams and telling his father, "Israel," about the
dreams. (Notice, the only difference in the text of Rev. 12:1 and Gen.
37:9 is the number of stars---twelve stars in Rev. 12:1 and eleven stars
in Gen. 37:9. Let us be careful to observe things that are different.)

Genesis 37:10---"And he told it to his father, and to his brethren: and
his father rebuked him, and said unto him, What is this dream that thou
hast dreamed? Shall I and thy mother and thy brethren indeed come to bow
down ourselves to thee to the earth?"

Isn't that rich? You know it is such a blessing to see the Holy Spirit
use the AV 1611 to reveal things that Hebrew scholars can't find out! To
my knowledge, Dr. A. T. Robertson, the greatest Greek scholar that
America ever produced, never did find what Revelation 12:1--3 was about.
The poor, ignorant man, with a Ph.D. in Greek, who wrote the monumental
work on Greek Grammar, never learned to read Sixth-Grade English!

In the passage (in Gen. 37:9--10), you are told that "the sun" stood for
Israel, the father; you are told that "the moon" stood for Rachel, the
mother of Joseph; you are told that "the eleven stars" stood for his
brothers; and Joseph is the twelfth brother. In Revelation 12:1 you have
a woman clothed with the sun, the moon, and the twelve stars. You
coundn't miss the interpretation unless you were a Roman Catholic, Jew,
or a Protestant. The "woman" is Israel. Watch how the AV 1611 English
brings out the unsearchable riches of the Holy Spirit, which are hidden
to Hebrew and Greek scholars. The context is God speaking to Israel.

Hosea 2:14--15---"Therefore, behold, I will allure her, and bring her
into the wilderness \[exactly as you find it mentioned three times in
Rev. 12\], and speak comfortably unto her. And I will give her her
vineyards from thence, and the valley of Achor for a door of hope: and
she shall sing there, as in the days of her youth, and as in the day
when she came up out of the land of Egypt."

When Israel came up out of the land of Egypt, do you know how she was
brought up? Do you know what figure God the Holy Spirit uses in
reference to Israel coming up out of Egypt?

Deuteronomy 32:11---"As an eagle stirreth up her nest, fluttereth over
her young, spreadeth abroad her wings, taketh them, beareth them on her
w‑i‑n‑g‑s." (See Rev. 12:14.)

Isn't it wonderful how far ahead the 1611 Authorized Version is from
such puerile works as the RSV? Isn't it funny how men like Alford,
Buttrick, Machen, Goodspeed, Trench, and Weigle, with all their
linguistic ability, with all their ability in etymology and semantics,
have never learned how to read and believe Sixth-Grade English in an
Authorized Version? The Bible says, "Out of the mouth of babes and
sucklings thou has perfected praise." "Whom shall he teach knowledge?
and whom shall he make to understand doctrine? them that are weaned from
the milk, and drawn from the breasts." "In that hour Jesus rejoiced in
spirit, and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that
thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed
them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight"
(Matt. 21:16; Isa. 28:9; Luke 10:21).

Here we have a perfect example of how the AV 1611 reveals what others
cannot. The boards of revisers, who translated the other Bibles, didn't
believe what you just read. The men who made the other versions,
including the ASV, RV, RSV, etc., couldn't find what you just saw.
Therefore, you know one thing---the Holy Spirit is able to show you more
from the Book they hate (the AV 1611) than they can show you by
translating a "New" Bible. Now, you need to think about that. The
scholars who are handling the word and translating it can't get as much
out of it as you can reading a dime store 1611, AV. The Holy Spirit will
honor the AV, Reformation Text. He will not honor the ASV (1901) or the
RV (1885) or the RSV (1952) texts.

Here is a perfect correlation of Scripture with Scripture. Revelation 12
is dealing with Israel in the Tribulation, so you look for Israel.
Israel is called "a woman," and a woman is called "Israel." The church
is never referred to as a "woman." She is always referred to as a
"wife," "bride," or "virgin," but never a woman (see 2 Cor. 11:2; Rev.
21:9). The "woman" in Revelation 12 is called a "woman." The "woman" in
Revelation 12 goes into the wilderness. Israel is prophesied to go back
into the wilderness (in Hosea) as a "woman." When she goes she is told
to go "as she came out of Egypt." When she came out of Egypt, she came
out on two wings of an eagle. When she goes in Revelation 12, she goes
on two wings of an eagle into the wilderness. Now tell me---is the
"woman" Mary? Is the "woman" the church? (I've got a sense of humor; I
always did appreciate a good joke. But don't ask me to take "popes and
archbishops" seriously!)

(If the pope or the Ecumenical Council gets control of the government,
and they send troops to my door with summons, warrants, subpoenas, guns,
etc., I'll have to listen to them! A man will usually listen to force
and threats. If you want me to "respect" the prisons, lawyers, knives,
bullets, guns, jails, judges, and courts that they're setting up, I'll
respect them. But, if you expect me to respect these men as spiritual
leaders---tut! tut! Let's not carry the joke too far. I'll give some
respect to a man if he comes up and puts a gun in my face and says, "You
believe this or else." But any man who would say that the "woman" in
Revelation 12 is the "church" or "Mary" could not be seriously respected
as a spiritual person, or even an intelligent reader.)

12:2 "And she being with child cried, travailing in birth, and pained to
be delivered."

This "woman" brings forth the Lord Jesus Christ. (We know that Mary
"brought forth the Lord Jesus Christ," but Mary only fits verse 2. She
can't fit verses 3--4, 6, 14, or 17. So, Israel must be the one who
brings forth the Lord Jesus Christ.) The Lord Jesus Christ is not only
called the "seed of woman," but the "seed of Abraham" (Gal. 3), and the
"seed of David" (Rom. 1:1--6). Jesus is also called the "chosen one of
Israel." From His human parentage by Mary, He comes from Abraham, Isaac,
and Jacob. So Israel brings forth the Lord Jesus Christ.

12:3 "And there appeared another wonder in heaven; and behold a great
red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and seven crowns upon his
heads."

"Red" always symbolizes blood, wherever you find it. This dragon is
identified in Revelation 12:9, "And the great dragon was cast out, that
old serpent, called the Devil, and Satan, which deceiveth the whole
world\...."

He is Satan; he is the Devil; he is called a "dragon." Hence, Satan is
very careful to encourage the writers of Greek and Chinese mythology
where they talk about "dragons," so that when a sinner reads about a
"dragon" in the Bible he will laugh it off and say that it was a
"mythological monster," and therefore, "the apocalypse was written in
apocalyptic language that is figurative and obscure," etc. But Satan has
a great deal more sense than the scholars who study mythology. When you
read of a dragon in the Bible, do you know what the word means? It means
a dragon!

Right here we should take time out to find a description of this
"dragon," in detail.

Lucifer was an "anointed cherub," and he fell and became a "red dragon."
When he appears, he appears as a 33-year-old male, "an angel of light"
(2 Cor. 11:13--14). It must be understood, of course, that these
passages you are about to learn, are going to be rejected totally by
every organized religious group on the face of the earth.

Although Satan appears as "an angel of light," we should never be as
ignorant and naive as Milton (who wrote Paradise Lost) and assume that
the Devil was an angel. It is a common fable among Christians that Satan
is a fallen angel. The Devil is never an angel: he is a Cherubim and has
wings, and no angel in the Bible has wings (see commentary on Gen. 3:1).
The Devil was "the anointed cherub that covereth," and as "the anointed
cherub that covereth," he had wings. Angels do not have wings; wings, in
the Bible, are not connected with angels. The angels of Roman Catholic
fiction (as the Mary and the Jesus of Roman Catholic fiction) are the
real subjects of mythology in which most educated, professing Christians
in America believe, today. In Satan's present condition, he is not a
winged cherub, but a "red dragon." He may appear as "an angel of light"
(2 Cor. 11: 11--14), but this is a false appearance and a deception. His
actual condition is found in the Book of Job. (The Book of Job is the
oldest book in the Bible, and therefore, it deals more in detail with
the events which took place in Gen. 1:1--2 than the Book of Genesis does
itself.)

In Job 41 a strange creature is described. As a matter of fact, there
are thirty-four verses dealing with him, and these make more verses
(dealing with the reality of Satan) than there are verses in the Bible
which deal with the human appearance of Jesus Christ. That is, the most
important character in Scripture, outside of the Lord Jesus Christ, is
the "anointed cherub" who became a "red dragon." (And once again, we
will have a fine opportunity to observe how basically ignorant great
Bible scholars are when it comes to handling the word of God. In Job 41,
we find the entire Bible‑believing segment of fundamental Christianity
going completely into the ditch and never getting out.)

Job 41:1---"Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook?" The word
"leviathan" is found in Moffatt and Williams as a "crocodile," and you
will find in the International Standard Bible Encyclopedia (written by
great Fundamental, Conservative scholars) that "leviathan" is called a
"hippopotamus" or a "crocodile" (volume III, p. 1869). The general
consensus of opinion is that "leviathan" is some kind of a sea monster,
like a whale or a crocodile. Again, this proves the inability of
conservative Hebrew scholars to read Fourth-Grade English, for in Psalm
74:14 we find, "Thou brakest the h‑e‑a‑d‑s of leviathan." Here, you are
told that "leviathan" has more than one head, and this infallible truth
is found in the 1611, AV, King James Bible. Yet, the poor, deluded
editors of the I.S.B.E. are telling God's people that it is a crocodile,
whale, or hippopotamus! In Revelation 12:3 (the passage under
discussion), you will notice that the "dragon" has seven heads (plural).
In the context of Psalm 74:14 (just quoted), did you notice verse 13?
"Thou brakest the heads (plural) of the dragons in the waters." Context
with context, Scripture with Scripture, again yields riches that no
Greek nor Hebrew scholar could ever find if he studied the original
languages for 155,000 years. That is, the key to Bible revelation is
belief in what you read, as you read it.

If this were not enough, "leviathan" is described in Isaiah 27:1, "In
that day the Lord with his sore and great and strong sword shall punish
leviathan the piercing serpent \[as in Rev. 12\], even leviathan that
crooked serpent \[as in Rev. 12\]; and he shall slay the dragon \[as in
Rev. 12\] that is in the sea."

Scripture with Scripture yields the truth. Even the great German
scholar, John Peter Lange, one of the greatest Conservative scholars who
ever lived, could not get by Isaiah 27:1. When we read in Revelation 12
that Satan "deceiveth the whole world," that means the whole
"cotton‑picking" world. I don't care how bright, brilliant, intelligent,
or educated a man is, if he doesn't believe that the AV 1611 is the word
of God (I mean the King James Reformation English), he cannot go any
further than what he can read out of an ordinary book.

In Isaiah 27:1; Revelation 12; and Job 41, Scripture with Scripture, you
have the Scripture interpreting the Scripture to give you the Scriptural
interpretation of Scripture, without any reference to any set of
commentaries, directly or indirectly. The Roman Catholic Church, the
Reformation Church, and the popes are absolutely powerless and totally
defunct when it comes even to commenting intelligently on the passages.
You may reject their "interpretations" without giving it a second
thought!

Job 41:34---"He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the
children of pride."

That's why they can't get through the passage. He's their king!
Unbelievers, modernists, liberals, and psychologists say, "Do you mean
to tell me, Mr. Ruckman, that you actually believe this literally? Do
you mean to tell me that you actually believe in a seven‑headed dragon?
Ha! Ha! Man is so modern, etc." Let me tell you something---there are
operations in the spiritual realm below the atomic structure, and past
the operation of positive and minus charges in the protons and
electrons, that science knows absolutely nothing about whatsoever! The
Bible is the only final, scientific, authoritative textbook with the
infallible, authoritative statement on these operations. Since "science"
knows nothing about life beyond the grave, it would hardly be fit for
scientists even to suggest a comment in regards to the subject. These
passages, Job 41; Revelation 12; Isaiah 27; and Psalm 74, are all
dealing with a powerful, spiritual being, who operates in a spiritual
realm that was here before scientists had a material creation on which
to comment, and before there were physical phenomena present with which
to work. "Leviathan" is a being who is not interested in the
"psychosomatic relations" of the physical and emotional structure; he is
interested in damning your soul to hell. When dealing with such a being,
you'd better take the word of a higher and more authoritative source of
revelation than the poor, piddling theories of modern science, "falsely
so‑called" (1 Tim. 6:20), which is absolutely powerless to deal with
anything except temporary experiments on physical reactions of energy
and matter. That's a very limited field.

Job 41:1---"Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? (That's why they
think it's a sea monster---a hook is mentioned!) or his tongue with a
cord which thou lettest down?"

You tie the hook on the cord and let it down when you catch fish. So it
occurs to all these naive scholars that this must be a great big fish.
But as we have pointed out before, the English straightens this out. The
Hebrew here means "twisted" or "coiled," hence the being suggested is a
seven‑headed, twisted, coiled sea monster. The word for this in English
is D‑R‑A‑G‑O‑N!

Job 41:9---"Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not one be cast
down even at the sight of him?"

This being has no hope. If you saw him, the sight would make you fall
flat on your face---like any good priest taking "holy orders"!

Job 41:10---"None is so fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able
to stand before me?"

"None is so fierce that dare stir him up," and yet, God made him. If you
can't stand before Satan, you certainly can't "stand" before God.

Job 41:19--21---"Out of his mouth go burning lamps, and sparks of fire
leap out. Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot or
caldron. His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth."

Here is the original reference to cigarettes! People think that nothing
about smoking is mentioned in the Bible, but they are entirely wrong.
The first mention of "kindling a coal" by inhaling your breath, and
"blowing smoke out of your nose" is found in Job 41:19--21. Since this
custom originates with the peace‑pipe among pagan people (who dance
around the totem pole and worship the "great white father," the god of
this world---2 Cor. 4:4), there is no doubt about the reference at all.
God never intended for you to breathe smoke unless it is in hell.
Revelation 14:11 speaks about "the smoke of their torment ascendeth up
for ever and ever." The only time smoke is mentioned in connection with
God is when there is a cloud of smoke covering the holy place, and the
smoke representing the fire of God's wrath (as you find it everywhere in
the word of God). Smoke is always something off a sacrifice that is
being burned. Even where the smoke is from the altar of incense (where
the prayers of the saints are going up---Revelation 8:3--4), the saints
have had their heads cut off, and their bodies have been burned. (You'll
find another reference in Isa. 50:11 about lighting cigarettes!)

This is a joke to a modern educated man because he is a joke himself. A
man will approach Job 41 and say, "Why I never heard such private
interpretation! The idea of applying these passages in Job, written in
the apocalyptic, symbolic language of the ancient Hebrew, in the wisdom

as quickly as you can dismiss the puff of one cancer stick. Since the

that nature as being the "defense mechanism" of a burglar caught

The fact that a "modern man," in sin, resents the passages which apply
to his particular sin, is "no sweat" to the writer of the Holy Scripture
or to the Lord or to Job. The fact remains that the context of the
chapter is a description of Satan in his present condition, and his
present condition (in its description) is the picture of a man kindling
a coal with his breath, and blowing smoke out through his nose and
mouth. Christ says to a certain group of people, "Ye are of your father
the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do" (John 8:44). Like
father, like son.

Job 41:33---"Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without
fear."

This is not the description of a hippopotamus. (Hippopotami can get
scared to death.) This is not a crocodile or a whale. (Crocodiles and
whales have been scared to death.) Many a whale has run for his life and
sounded for the deep to shake off a harpoon. This monster (Job 41) here
has no fear! "The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom" (Psa.
111:10; Job 28:28). This monster has no fear and "Upon earth there is
not his like, who is made without fear." It's not a whale; a barracuda
has less fear in him than a whale does. It's not a crocodile; a piranha
has less fear in him than a crocodile does. It's not a hippopotamus;
I'll guarantee you that a lion won't chicken out like a hippopotamus
will. There is no way for you or your church to get a private
interpretation which says that leviathan is a "whale," "crocodile," or
"hippopotamus." You simply can't get that unless you just plain reject
what God says, the way He says it, where He says it! The passage is
dealing with the "king over all the children of pride," who is over all
this earth, and everything on it is given to his hand, as the "god of
this world" (2 Cor. 4:4). He doesn't fear God or anybody else. He wasn't
afraid to step up to God's Son and say, "If thou be the Son of God" etc.
He's audacious. He walks before the throne and says, "Doth Job fear God
for nought?" No fear!

Job 41:23--24---"The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are
firm in themselves; they cannot be moved. His heart is as firm as a
stone; yea, as hard as a piece of the nether millstone."

His heart is as hard as a piece of millstone, and he has scales.

Job 41:27--29---"He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.
The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into
stubble. Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a

The weapons you use against him---you little kingdom of God

use, and you'll bust your neck on it and go to hell with it. (The only
weapon you can use against him is Heb. 4:12, and if you don't use that
weapon right, you'll just cut yourself with it!)

The description is of Satan, and as far as I know, Martin Luther was the
only man in history ever to believe and realize that this description
found in Job was Satan. Martin Luther has a hymn which says, "But still
our ancient foe doth seek to work us woe. His craft and power are great,
and armed with cruel hate. On earth is not his equal" (Job 41:33). On
earth there is nothing to equal him.

Then, Job 41 is a detailed description of Satan in his present
condition. The description given in Job 41 is the description of a
coiled, twisted, serpentine sea‑monster with seven heads, and scales,
breathing fire and smoke. It is a description of what you wouldn't
believe because you have been raised to believe that such a description

would pass out cold. If you could see what a drunk sees when he gets the
D.T.'s (The doctor says it's in his brain cells; you'll just have to
pray for the doctors because they need help too!), you'd pass "clean
out." There are operations in the spirit world, in a world of beings who

structure, which are unknown to science, and only revealed in the
textbook of truth---the word of God. The proof is in the revelation.
Nothing like this is revealed anywhere or anyplace else.

Then this is a great, "red dragon with seven heads," who runs and rules
this world, as "king over all the children of pride." "The kingdoms of
the world" are his (Luke 4); he gives them to whomever he will. He
appears as "an angel of light" and he is a "christ." He is an anointed
messiah (Ezek. 28:14). Naturally, he would claim to have the one, true
"church of christ"! He desires worship as a god; he's called a prince
(John 14:30); he's called a king; he's called an angel (2 Cor.
11:10--19); he's called "an angel of light." He quotes Scripture (Luke
4), and he has a bride who is his body (as Christ's bride is His
body---Rev. 17). His sphere of activity is primarily religious. You

You find the Devil at the Ecumenical Council and the National Council of

this through their heads, but that's because they don't read the word of

12:3 "And there appeared another wonder in heaven; and behold a great
red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and seven crowns upon his
heads."

This is a seven‑headed, coiled, twisted, serpentine monster who moves up
and down in the water.

Job 41:31--32---"He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the
sea like a pot of ointment. He maketh a path to shine after him; one
would think the deep to be hoary."

(See Rev. 4:6 and comments.) You learned how the body of water above the
firmament, in Genesis 1, was a body of water which makes the Pacific and
Atlantic Oceans look like a spit puddle. You learned that there is a
body of water above the galaxies and solar systems that goes right up to
the throne of God, frozen over the north, into a sea of glass, solid on
top. Now you learn in Job 41:31--32, that Satan in his sphere of
activity, being religious, goes up and down through that water. Hence,
he is likened unto a "sea monster." Hence, the Ph.D.'s of today suppose
that "the deep" is a reference to the Atlantic or Pacific Ocean, or the
Nile River. Think about these twentieth century Ph.D.'s and Th.D.'s
going all over this country and blowing hot air (like their father---Job
41) about the "poor, ancient, ignorant Hebrews" believing that there
were sea monsters out in the deep who would swallow them up if they
sailed off the edge of the earth! These poor, deluded, twentieth-century
idiots are teaching that the "sea monster" (of Job 41) is one of these
literal fish or monsters out in a river, when the reference is to the
waters above the heavens, revealed in the Hebrew Bible (Gen. 1; Job 41;
Rev. 4--5)! The reference has nothing to do with the Atlantic or the
Pacific or the Nile or the Dead Sea or the Sea of Galilee or the Jordan.
The reference is to a body of water unknown to modern scientific
astronomers, and Satan moves up and down in it.

Isaiah 27:1---"In that day the Lord with his sore and great and strong
sword shall punish leviathan the piercing serpent, even leviathan that
crooked serpent; and he shall slay the dragon that is in the sea."

So Satan goes up and down through this water, and controls the earth by
a system of kingdoms set up under seven crowns or kings. Although the
red dragon has ten horns, he has only seven crowns---one crown for each
head (Rev. 12:3). This brings us to another great truth of Scripture:
the Bible itself encompasses all kingdoms from the beginning to the end
of history, under the picture of seven heads and seven kings. All of
these seven heads, or kings of kingdoms, are listed in the word of God.
They have no reference whatsoever to the seven Roman Emperors as you
read in the commentaries. The commentaries always try to put Revelation
in the past to get rid of its future application to the Ecumenical
Council and the Roman Catholic Church. In doing so, they have to make

lived in John's day. This is typical of the dead orthodox, historical
method of perverting the word of God---you make all the Bible prophecy
refer to past historical fulfillment, and you make it a dead word
instead of the living word (Mark 7:1--12).

These seven kings and ten horns are clearly given in Scripture by name.
The first head which Satan uses to rule the world is found in Genesis
10. The first one of Satan's boys (He has a lot of boys, and they run
the world---always have and always will until Jesus Christ comes back.)
is a Hamite, or at least a half‑breed.

Genesis 10:9---"He was a mighty hunter before the Lord: wherefore it is
said, Even as Nimrod the mighty hunter before the Lord."

Nimrod is the son of Cush (vs. 8), and Cush is a full‑blooded African

There has never been any trouble between "Negroes and white people"
since the slaves came over here. If there has been any trouble, it was
between "white people and black people." You understand? If there is any
trouble with Negroes, it is between Negroes and Caucasians, or

woman," then he is a "black man." Any other terminology is "racial

of the Supreme Court and Congress, who passed an "equal rights bill,"
then I have broken the law of the United States if I ever refer to a
"Negro and a white." The newspapers and magazines violate that law every
day. When they speak of "Negroes and whites," it is plainly "racial
discrimination.")

Genesis 10:10---"And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech,
and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar."

Ten is the number of the Gentiles---the Gentiles coming from Noah, the
tenth from Adam. The first head on Satan is Nimrod, the king of Babel
(Gen. 10:10). This is the first type of the antichrist. He is a
half‑breed, Hamitic, black man, who tried to settle in Asia instead of
the land of Ham (where he should have been---see Psa. 105--106). (This
begins the confusion of Babel in Genesis 11---see commentary.)

Exodus 5:2---"And Pharaoh said, Who is the Lord, that I should obey his
voice to let Israel go? I know not the Lord, neither will I let Israel
go."

Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, is the second head with which Satan ruled
the world. If there is any doubt about this, Pharaoh is called "a
dragon" in Ezekiel 29:3. Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, is the second king,
the second head, and the second kingdom---Egypt being a type of the
world.

2 Kings 18:13---"Now in the fourteenth year of king Hezekiah did
Sennacherib king of Assyria come up against all the fenced cities of
Judah, and took them."

The third head found on this "leviathan" who runs and rules the kingdoms
of this world is Sennacherib, the king of Assyria. The Antichrist is
called "the Assyrian," and you'll find at least one Pharaoh was an
Assyrian---not a full‑blooded Egyptian. He was a half‑breed, Hamitic
Assyrian. Here, you find Sennacherib, the king of Assyria (type of the
Antichrist), coming up and attacking Jerusalem to wipe out the Jews
exactly as the Antichrist will do in the Tribulation.

In the Book of Daniel, we find all the other four heads given. The
fourth head is found in Daniel 3---Nebuchadnezzar, the king of Babylon.
If there is any doubt about this, you will notice that Sennacherib, the
king of Assyria, and Nebuchadnezzar, the king of Babylon, are both
called "dragons" (Jer. 51:34, 50:17--18)!

There is no doubt about the remaining three kingdoms. They are all
prophesied in Daniel 2, anywhere from fifty years to four hundred years
before they show up. In the vision of the great image (Dan. 2), Daniel
prophesies on the four coming kingdoms pictured by that image, and they
come to pass "right on the valve‑head" without a single misfire.

The kingdom that follows Babylon is Media‑Persia (Dan. 5:28). Darius,
who befriends the Jews, comes in here. Darius is a friend of Israel, but
the Antichrist will be a friend of Israel for three and a half years
during the Tribulation. So we have Darius, king of Persia, representing
the fifth head of Satan.

There is no doubt at all about the sixth head. Years and years before
Greece ever became a power under Alexander the Great, Daniel was told in
Daniel 10:20, 11:2, 8:20--21 that the next kingdom would be Greece.
Although he is not mentioned in Scripture, this king is Alexander the
Great.

Review:

1\. Nimrod, the king of Babylon.

2\. Pharaoh, the king of Egypt.

3\. Sennacherib, the king of Assyria.

4\. Nebuchadnezzar, the king of Babylon.

5\. Darius, the king of Persia.

6\. Unnamed---"X," the mystery man---the sixth head is a mystery man who
is unnamed. In Daniel he is called "a rough he goat." He's connected
with Greece. (In history he would be Alexander the Great, but we are
dealing with Bible prophecy---as well as history.)

7\. The seventh head, of course, is the kingdom which conquers Alexander
the Great and takes over the world, preparatory to the first advent of
the Lord Jesus Christ: Rome!

Luke 1:68--71; 2:1---"Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath
visited and redeemed his people, And hath raised up an horn of salvation
for us in the house of his servant David; As he spake by the mouth of
his holy prophets, which have been since the world began: That we should
be saved from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us\...And
it came to pass in those days, that there went out a decree from Caesar
Augustus, that all the world should be taxed."

This means that at the Second Coming of Christ, the Jews will be in the
homeland (as they were the first time), Rome is going to be in power (as
it was the first time), and the whole world is going to be in subjection
to the king of Rome (as it was the first time)---the spiritual ruler,
the "holy papa," and he's going to get your tax money---every dime of
it! (You may not believe that, but as I've said before, "The only thing
men learn from history is that men never learn from history.") So, the
seventh and last head of Satan is the head of the Roman Empire, Caesar
Augustus. It is called the "Holy Roman Empire." Did you get that? Satan
claims holiness---the "Holy" Roman Empire! This is now called the "Holy"
Catholic Church. Did you get all that? The basic root meaning of the
word "cat," by the way, is "mother"!!! (Cat‑Holy; Catholic; Holy Cats;
Holy Mother.)

12:3 "And there appeared another wonder in heaven; and behold a great
red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and seven crowns upon his
heads."

The seven-crowned heads represent seven kings or kingdoms by which Satan
controls this earth. The first of these kings is Nimrod, king of Babel,
who ruled about 2400 B.C. The second of these kingdoms (called the
Ancient Kingdom, or Early Kingdom, and studied much by archaeologists)
was the Egyptian Empire. This kingdom was under Pharaoh, king of Egypt,
until approximately 800 B.C. (You don't hear much about Gentile empires
during this time because the crown has gone from Abraham, Isaac, and
Jacob, unconditionally, to the twelve tribes under Saul, David, and the
succession of kings.) With the collapse of the literal, physical,
visible kingdom of heaven promised to Israel (around 710 B.C.), the
third head of Satan appears---Sennacherib, the king of Assyria. He rules
until the succession of Nebuchadnezzar shows up about 606 B.C. The
Babylonian Kingdom, under Nebuchadnezzar, runs from this date until
about 536 B.C. Then in 536 B.C., the kingdom of Media‑Persia takes over
and runs until about 330 B.C. Then in 330 B.C., you have the ascendancy
of Greece under Phillip of Macedonia and Alexander the Great until about
100 B.C.; the Roman situation takes over to A.D. 346. This is the last
head on Satan. Then this Roman kingdom goes into a mystery form called
"Mystery, Babylon the great, the mother of harlots and abominations of
the earth." Notice, very carefully, the first head on Satan is Babel
(Babylonia), the middle (fourth) head is Babylon, and the last head is
"Mystery, Babylon the great." Babel is mentioned at the beginning, in
the middle, and on the end. Did you get that connection? The last
kingdom, under Caesar Augustus, is called "Babylon." (Every Roman
Catholic, pope, priest, cardinal, and bishop who ever lived will tell
you that "Babylon" is another word for "Rome"! Isn't that a riot?)

1 Peter 5:13---"The church that is at Babylon, elected together with
you, saluteth you; and so doth Marcus my son."

This is the proof text used by the Roman Catholic Church to prove that
Simon Peter was not in literal Babylon, but in Rome. In the official
literature of the Knights of Columbus, you will find the reference to 1
Peter 5:13 (Watch out for the 13's!) applied to Rome. Thus the Catholic
Church admits that it is the godless, depraved, seventh head of Satan,
without even believing the Scriptures which point it out! This means
that the seventh head, historically---Caesar Augustus (Rome), had the
Roman kingdom in ascendance until A.D. 346. Then, instead of being cut
off by the Huns, Visigoths, Vandals, etc., after Constantine (A.D. 313),
this seventh head of Satan went into a mystery, religious form to
maintain its political hold over the kingdoms of this world. So, there
never have been more than seven kingdoms, and there never will be. The
seventh and last kingdom on this earth goes into a religious form to
maintain its political hold. That is why today the Roman Catholic Church
is also the Vatican State. That is why Cardinal Spellman (writing in
books as late as the 1900's) says that the pope is head of a political
state as well as a spiritual realm. This also means that I am perfectly
justified in the biting and caustic remarks I have made in this
commentary about the popes and their abortions of the word of God. The
Scriptures clearly indicate that the seventh and final head on Satan is
the seventh, final, and last kingdom, which as a political entity, lasts
