ASV‑RSV crowd (who use the same manuscripts), goes into a mystery
religious form. You still find Rome on the seven hills at the Second
Coming of Jesus Christ (Rev. 17:5--9).

So this seven‑headed dragon is Satan, who controls this world under
seven kingdoms. This seventh kingdom went from a political form to a
religious form in A.D. 345. So any manuscript written around 346
A.D.---like Vaticanus for the RSV and the ASV, like Sinaiticus for the
RSV and the ASV, like "A " in the epistles for the ASV---is a Satanic
manuscript and is not to be accepted as being authoritative on anything.
For that reason, we stick to the AV Bible, 1611, taken from the Textus
Receptus, which was not written between A.D. 346 and 500, when this
seventh head of Satan assumed a religious "holy" form. Bible‑believing
Christians go by the Greek Textus Receptus of the Eastern Greek
Church---not the Western Roman Church. Bible‑believers go back to the
original Christian religion of the Bible‑believing Christianity, which
used a Syrian‑type Text from papyrus of the first, second, and third
centuries, and preserved these in Byzantine manuscripts of the fifth,
sixth, seventh, and eighth centuries, in Asia through Asia Minor, and
then through the Balkans to Germany and England. We do not fool with the
manuscripts produced by the seventh head of Satan, as at that time Rome
went into a "mystery form" and preserved the North African manuscripts
which go to make up Jerome's Vulgate, RV (1885), ASV (1901), RSV (1952),
which manuscripts go from North Africa to Spain and Italy, through the
Latin‑speaking, Hamitic people. Is that clear?

12:4 "And his tail drew the third part of the stars of heaven, and did
cast them to the earth: and the dragon stood before the woman which was
ready to be delivered, for to devour her child as soon as it was born."

Notice that in the passage the dragon is said to be a heavenly being, or
at least "in the heavens." As his sphere of operation on this earth is
religious, his geographical location is up and down through the
waters---the heavens.

The Scriptures interpret themselves, and a "star" in Scripture does not
always refer to a sun. Sometimes it does, but where the first definition
appears in Revelation 1:20, a star is called "an angel." When these
stars are cast to the earth in Revelation 6:13, they are obviously
angels and not suns. If the stars, as such, fell to the earth as suns or
planets, they would burn the earth up immediately. (The Bible uses a
common language which any man can understand, and anybody understands
that the word "star" does not always refer to planet or sun. For
example, people talk about getting knocked in the head and seeing
"stars." People call the whoremongers and prostitutes out in Los Angeles
\[City of the Angels\] "stars." They idolize them. Folks sing "You Are
My Lucky Star." We understand what they are talking about. God writes in
plain language which any fool can understand.) The Scriptures plainly
reveal that these stars are "angels." So in Revelation 12:4 we have the
coming of fallen angels back to this earth again, as they are found in
Genesis 6:1--6. The scene is not the Miltonic scene of the Devil's
angels getting kicked out of Paradise in Genesis 1. The scene is the
future coming of visitors from outer space. (Notice, in Revelation
12:7--9, they do not come down to this earth until the Tribulation.) Of
course, this is another shocker. It means that the long awaited visitors
from outer space are going to show up! When they show up, they will be
mistaken for men from outer space. All the gullible, twentieth-century
educators, scientists, and church leaders will receive them as visitors
from outer space, since they have rejected the literal passages in their
own Bibles given to them by God, to show them that these voyagers who
are coming are demons! This is the judgment which God brings on men for
making a liar out of Him and not believing His word. It is a just
judgment; it is proper and in place. And there is no doubt about these
activities which are going to take place; they are very clear from the
word of God.

This day and age is called the "space age," and everybody is interested
in outer space. People have actually gotten the idea that the AV 1611 is
archaic, that it deals with earthly fables, folklore, and myths, and
that it is no longer fit for an age that is dealing with outer space and
the great scientific endeavors of modern man. All this propaganda has
convinced people that the Bible is no longer reliable. But you must
remember that the AV 1611 always runs several hundred years ahead of the
latest nineteenth and twentieth century scientific advancement. The
Bible covers the subject thoroughly.

In Genesis 1 and 2, you are told that man's sphere of dominion or
activity is to be limited to the earth.

Psalm 115:16---"The heaven, even the heavens, are the Lord's: but the
earth hath he given to the children of men."

Here, you are told that the heavens belong to the Lord, and they do not
belong to man; He has given the earth to man on which to operate. In
Genesis 5:24, there is recorded the translation of a man from earth to
heaven---caught out from earth to heaven "that he should not see death."
In 2 Kings 2, you have a man making an outer space trip. You have
another man caught up into outer space after he is dead and brought back
in the New Testament---Moses. You have another man who goes up to heaven
after he is resurrected from the dead, and returns in less than two
hours, preaches on the earth forty days and forty nights, and goes up
again in Acts 1---Jesus Christ. There have been numerous trips into
outer space long before Christopher Columbus. The "space age" is a
little late getting here. We must remember that the information found in
the Authorized Version is far more authoritative than that by the latest
astronauts. These fellows taking the capsules up, the space ships,
landing platforms, etc., are riding tricycles and they have a long way
to go.

The most effective thing for Satan to do during the Tribulation (which
he is described as doing here---Rev 12:4) is to come down from outer
space. This is always very effective, and as a matter of fact, it is the
way Christ returns in Revelation 19. Also, Satan tempted Christ to do it
in Luke 4:9--12. Therefore, what Satan does is a counterfeit. Men are
now looking for it, and so naturally, it would be the thing to do. It
has been done before. In Genesis 6:1--4 the "sons of God" came down and
cohabited with the daughters of men. It has not only been done before,
but it will be done again (Luke 17:26--27). In Acts 14, Paul and Silas
were accepted as "gods" from outer space, although they were only men.
(As we have found out before, "angels" in the Bible are men.) Therefore,
if fallen angels came to this earth, they would not come floating down,
flapping their wings (as you find them at the dime store, in Roman
Catholic paintings); they would come down and appear as men. They would
be accepted as men; they would be accepted as men from other planets
coming down to this earth from outer space; they would be accepted as
supermen---"men of another race," above and beyond the power of ordinary
man. All they will have to do is show a few scientific feats, and they
will be considered superior to modern man, and they will be accepted as
"gods." They will be held in the utmost reverence by people as
"humanoids" with great and vast knowledge. Anybody who opposes these
beings will be considered to be a crackpot or a fanatic or dangerous to
human progress. When this thing takes place, all the Bible‑believing
people are going to be marked off as nuts!

This thing is going to be so perfect and so slick that there isn't any
way for the world to be able to see through it. That is, the world today
is eager for visitors from outer space; they are expecting it, longing
for it, and looking for it.

Romans 10:6--9---"But the righteousness which is of faith speaketh on
this wise, Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? (that
is, to bring Christ down from above:) Or, Who shall descend into the
deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.) But what saith
it? The word is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart: that is,
the word of faith, which we preach; That if thou shalt confess with thy
mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath
raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved."

Man is violating this commandment by poking his nose around and sticking
his nose up and ignoring the word of God. God's judgment upon man for
doing this---despising and ignoring the word of God---is to bring
visitors down from outer space that are the wrong visitors.

In the situation in Genesis 6, where the fallen angels are said to be
cohabiting with the daughters of men, we suddenly run into a strange
situation in Bible interpretation. At this very point (Gen. 6), Satan
has mustered armored divisions to prevent a man from breaking through
the truth of Scripture. At Genesis 6, we suddenly find Bible-believing
Premillennialists lined up with weapons in their hands, ready to defend
error to the death, so that the doctrines of the Second Coming of Christ
will escape observation. Foremost among these is C. I. Scofield and
editors. The footnote in the Scofield Reference Bible is a shambles. It
reads:

"Some hold that these 'sons of God' were the 'angels which kept not
their first estate' (Jude 6). It is asserted that the title is in the
Old Tesament exclusively used of angels. But this is an error (Isa.
43:6). Angels are spoken of in a sexless way. No female angels are
mentioned in Scripture, and we are expressly told that marriage is
unknown among angels (Matt. 22:30). The uniform Hebrew and Christian
interpretation has been that verse 2 marks the breaking down of the
separation between the godly line of Seth and the godless line of Cain,
and so the failure of the testimony to Jehovah committed to the line of
Seth (Gen. 4:26). For apostasy there is no remedy but judgment (Isa.
1:2--7, 24--25; Heb. 6:4--8, 10:26--31). Noah, 'a preacher of
righteousness,' is given 120 years, but he won no convert, and the
judgment predicted by his great‑grandfather fell (Jude 14--15; Gen.
7:11)."

In this footnote, the pope of Rome suddenly breaks into the Scofield
Board of Editors, and seizes the worthy doctor by the throat, and forces
him back into the Dark Ages to give the "uniform Hebrew and Christian
interpretation." As usual, the Bible interpretation, uniform by the Holy
Spirit, turns out to be something entirely different.

1\. Angels are never sexless. If you get a concordance and run a hundred
references, you will find that every angel in the Bible is male.
"Sexless angels," with hair like women and wings like storks, are in the
gallery of Rome---not in the Bible.

2\. Angels are called "sons of God" and do appear as men. The "godly
line of Seth," to which Scofield refers, is a phantom. There is no such
thing as a "godly line" anywhere in the Bible. There is a Messianic
line; there is not a "godly line." If the word "godly" is made into
"Messianic," then the Messiah has a human line. If He has a human line,
it should be godly. But far from being godly, you will find in the
Messianic line a whoremonger, a prostitute, a half‑breed African, an
adulterer, an adulteress, an Ammonite (which a Jew was forbidden to
marry, Matt. 1:3--10), etc. The so‑called "godly line" like "holy
water," holy crosses, holy Mary, etc., is a hoax; there is no godly line
(Rom. 3:10--23).

3\. "Sons of God" in the Old Testament are never men in the strictly
human sense. The reference that Scofield gives for his argument---Isaiah
43:6---does not refer to "sons of God." The reference is to "sons and
daughters"---human Israelites---not to the godly line of Seth.

4\. Scofield says "marriage is unknown among angels." That is not true.
The Scripture quoted---Matthew 22:30---says no such thing. Matthew 22:30
says, "\...but are as the angels of God in heaven." I realize that the
"in heaven" is left out of the RSV, but Scofield is not supposed to pay
too much attention to the RSV, since it wasn't translated at the time he
wrote his notes. The "angels that sinned" (2 Pet. 2) are not in heaven.
Therefore, to cite the passage is only to becloud the issue, and point
to a verse that has no bearing on the subject.

5\. "Sons of God," in the New Testament (which is why Scofield referred
to them as "godly" in the Old Testament) are sons in whom the fallen
image of God in Adam has been restored by a new birth in Jesus Christ.
Since none of Adam's descendants are "born again," since none of them
receive the lost image back, since none of them have the image, then
none of them could be "sons of God" in the sense of John 1:12. Seth was
not a "son of God"; he did not have God's image. The only image Seth had
was the fallen image which Cain had---the image of a murderer (Gen.
5:1--4).

6\. The "sons of God" are present after the flood, according to the
statement of Genesis 6:4, and so are the infamous daughters of men.
Therefore, if the "daughters of men" were only Cain's line, how is it
that Cain's line is still there after the flood? (I thought they were
all drowned!) Scofield is greatly in error here, and should not be
followed for any distance.

7\. There is not a sound reason on earth why "godly Sethites" marrying
"ungodly Cainites" should produce a race of giants. If they are giants,
they are "men of renown," and they will go down forever in the writing
of Greek mythology as "the gods." They are half‑god and half‑man, and
they are the people whom the twentieth-century Ecumenical Council and
science‑worshipping churchmen are waiting for. These giants were on the
earth before and after the flood. So there is not the slightest chance
whatsoever that they could have come from Sethites and Cainites.
Cainites, according to the standard Premillennial theology, were all
wiped out during the flood, but the "giants" continued. We find them in
the days of Lot (Deut. 2:9, 11, 19--22). We find them in the days of
David (1 Sam. 17:3--4). Never in the history of the world has the
marriage of believers with unbelievers ever produced a race of monsters
which God had to wipe out. Today, as always, if believers marry
unbelievers (1 Cor. 7), they have "trouble in the flesh." Genesis 6 has
no application whatsoever to 2 Corinthians 6:14, and it is a breach of
common sense to apply it to Genesis 6 for the purpose of developing a
good sermon on Christian consecration.

8\. "Sons of God" are angels because they are set in contra-distinction
to the "daughters of men" (Gen. 6:1--2). Notice how clearly the plain
English of the 1611 Text corrects the error of the "uniform Hebrew
interpretation," cited by Scofield, from Rome. It is not "the sons of
Seth saw the daughters of Cain," but "the sons of God saw the daughters
of men." Things different are not equal.

9\. Satan has the power to reproduce, if a man is used as an instrument
(John 8:44; 1 John 3:12). This deep and dark subject cannot be discussed
fully here, but it will be sufficient to note that the "children of
wrath" (Eph. 2:1--4), "children of disobedience" (Eph. 2:1--4),
"children of hell" (Matt. 23:15), and a "generation of vipers" (Matt.
23:33) coming from a man who sold out to Satan and yielded to his
authority, has children whose kinship to Satan is much closer than can
be imagined. Second Corinthians 11:1--4 and John 6:70--71 (speaking of
Judas and Eve) have hidden doctrines in them of such a frightening
nature that it will be better to bypass them and be content to say that
the serpent is able to reproduce, or produce seed and life, by the
permissive will of God (Gen. 3:15; Rev. 13:15). Fundamentalists have
been slandering the Devil so long, on the basis of the passage in Exodus
8:18--19, that it is high time that they woke up to the fact that the
passage deals only with the Devil's inability to create life out of
dust---not to imitate it, produce it, reproduce it, or give it.

10\. The first rule of correct interpretation is that comparative words
match identically. John R. Rice cannot match a "cup" in Gethsemane with
Hebrews 5:7--8 because there is no cup in Hebrews 5:7--8. C. I. Scofield
cannot go scurrying off to Isaiah 43 to find "sons and daughters"
because we are looking for the "sons of God." (Scripture with
Scripture!) The "sons of God" are on this earth before Adam and Eve show
up (Job 38:3--7). These beings are spoken of three times in the Book of
Job---Job 1--2, and 38. "Sons of God," my friend, means sons of God. (I
realize that this is a tremendous sacrifice of terminology to ask of an
intellectual, who might have amplified it in his mind to read "sons
\[offspring, creatures, male created beings, progeny, filiation,
lineage, spiritual extractions\] of God" \[Divine Architect, the
Planner, Divine Mind, the Absolute Knowledge, Jehovah, Allah, Brahma,
the Deity, Vishnu, Jupiter, Jove, etc.\]. But the Bible is not written
primarily to salve the conscience of educated idiots. Genesis 1 ought to
convince you of that.) Scripture with Scripture, "the sons of God" refer
to "the sons of God." If there is a choice between Job 38:7 and John
1:12 for a definition, the nearest context is obviously the correct one.
Thus, a little common sense and simple logic solve the problems that
weigh like Mt. Everest on the minds of Bible‑rejecting or
Bible‑perverting scholars. These angels came once, and they are coming
again (Rev. 12:4--9).

12:5 "And she brought forth a man child, who was to rule all nations
with a rod of iron: and her child was caught up unto God, and to his
throne."

Now, we nearly always refer to the "man child" as the Lord Jesus Christ
for several reasons. He is said to "rule all nations with a rod of
iron," which we know the Lord Jesus Christ is going to do. The woman is
Israel, and she brings Him forth, as Christ came from Israel. The child
is caught up, as Christ was caught up in Acts 1. Yet, there is a
doctrine here that is still shrouded in mystery. Although you can teach
inspirationally that this "man child" is Jesus Christ, there is an
indication that the "man child" is a man sent later to deliver Israel,
who is not the Lord Jesus Christ.

12:6 "And the woman fled into the wilderness, where she hath a place
prepared of God, that they should feed her there a thousand two hundred
and threescore days."

After the catching up of this child, Israel flees to the wilderness to
be fed. Now, this did not take place in Acts 1 when Christ was caught
up. (I realize that in the Old Testament many times there is a gap
between the First and Second Coming of Christ which is only indicated by
a comma, semicolon, or a period. But there would be no need for this in
the Book of Revelation. The "gap" that the Holy Spirit skips in the Old
Testament is the Church Age; and when John writes, he is well into the
Church Age, he has the Pauline epistles before him, and he knows of the
gap between the two Comings.) I'm going to give you the references that
indicate that in the Tribulation the "man child" is not the Lord Jesus
Christ at the first coming, but a literal man child in the Tribulation
sent to deliver Israel. These references are as follows:

1\. Genesis 17:9--14.

2\. 1 Samuel 1:11.

3\. Job 3:3.

4\. Isaiah 66:7.

In these references, there seems to be something else occurring in the
Tribulation (especially in Isa. 66:7). (Notice the cross reference in
Jeremiah 1:6, and Jeremiah is plainly a type of a man in the Tribulation
who is prophesying to Israel.) When you read the gospels, you find out
that many Jews expected the return of Moses and Elijah, but many thought
that Jesus Christ was the second coming of Jeremiah (Matt. 16:14)! There
is no doubt about the time of Jeremiah's prophecy. Jeremiah, at the time
of the king of Babylon (type of Antichrist), prophesies to Israel (about
to be thrown out of the land, as they are in the time of the
Antichrist), concerning the destruction of Jerusalem (as it will be
destroyed in the Tribulation by the Antichrist), and he disappears down
into Egypt to pronounce the final prophecies on the Second Advent, "the
day of the Lord"---Jeremiah 50--52. That isn't all.

Lamentations 4:20---"The breath of our nostrils, the anointed of the
Lord, was taken in their pits, of whom we said, Under his shadow we
shall live among the heathen."

Here Jeremiah says that in the Tribulation the Jews will be trusting a
man who is said to be "the anointed of the Lord," and who is taken by
the Antichrist and his troop "in their pits."

Lamentations 4:21---"Rejoice and be glad, O daughter of Edom, that
dwellest in the land of Uz; the cup also shall pass through unto thee:
thou shalt be drunken, and shalt make thyself naked."

Notice that the Jew is at Uz (where Job was persecuted forty-two
chapters under Satan). He was saved by the literal appearance of God to
restore his captivity and turn him about, as will happen to the Jew in
the Tribulation.

Lamentations 4:19---"Our persecutors are swifter than the eagles of the
heaven: they pursued us upon the mountains, they laid wait for us in the
wilderness."

The Jew will be hunted down by airplanes in the wilderness, where he
will be fed.

Lamentations 5:9---"We gat our bread with the peril of our lives because
of the sword of the wilderness."

Notice, the Jew is getting bread under peril of being killed every time
he goes out to get it. Also, in Lamentations 4:21--22, he is praying for
God to reappear the second time to fulfill the promises God gave to him
in the Old Testament.

So, there is a chance that the "man child" in Revelation 12:3--5 is a
reference to an Israelitish remnant, or a certain individual who is
raised up in the Tribulation. (I do not profess to know enough about
this to carry the thing on through to the end. But, it is very
definitely there in the passage.)

12:6 "And the woman fled into the wilderness, where she hath a place
prepared of God, that they should feed her there a thousand two hundred
and threescore days."

The woman is identified as Israel, and Israel is going to go back into
the wilderness, exactly as she went back in the days that she came up
out of the land of Egypt (Hos. 2:14--19). When she cane out of the land
of Egypt, she went into the wilderness on eagle's wings (Deut. 32:11;
Rev. 12:14).

Revelation 12:13---"And when the dragon saw that he was cast unto the
earth, he persecuted the woman which brought forth the man child."

In the Tribulation, Satan will be personally occupied with the
persecution of Israel, as he always has been.

Revelation 12:14‑17---"And to the woman were given two wings of a great
eagle, that she might fly into the wilderness, into her place, where she
is nourished for a time \[1\], and times \[2\], and half a time
\[½---making 3½\], from the face of the serpent. And the serpent cast
out of his mouth water as a flood after the woman, that he might cause
her to be carried away of the flood. And the earth helped the woman, and
the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed up the flood which the dragon
cast out of his mouth. And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went
to make war with the remnant of her seed, which \[1\] keep the
commandments of God \[Old Testament Ten Commandments\], and \[2\] have
the testimony of Jesus Christ."

These are saved, Hebrew‑Christian Jews in the Tribulation, who still
observe the Old Testament commandments. It is to this group that one
book in the Bible is aimed---"Hebrews" (appropriately called). Because
"Hebrews" was written historically before A.D. 60 or A.D. 70, the
Amillennial and Postmillennial scholarship, and the Premillennial
scholarship, have relegated the book backwards and have tried to limit
its application to Jews in the early part of the Book of Acts.
(Bullinger and Stam and the hyper‑dispensationalists have also done
this.) This overlooks the fact that every verse of Scripture has three
applications---historical, inspirational, and doctrinal. (See Chapter
1.) To limit the Book of Hebrews to the past tense---the early part of
the Book of Acts---is a grievous mistake. All the way through the book,
your attention is called to the Second Coming of Christ, again and
again, in relation to Israel. Notice that all of Hebrews 3, the first
half of Hebrews 6, all of Hebrews 8, and half of Hebrews 10 deal with
this subject. Then, the book is appropriately titled (not to the
Christians, not to the church, not to the Gentiles) to the "Hebrews."
This is the group that is referred to, primarily, in Revelation 12:17.

Now, we have a number of facts about this woman. This woman whom John
sees is a picture that represents Israel. She is defined in Genesis 37
without any shadow of a doubt. This figure is used for Israel in the Old
Testament in Hosea and many other passages. This woman is said to be the
"cast off wife of Jehovah."

A "woman" in the Bible, where she stands for something else, refers to
only two groups. We find a woman in Revelation 17--18, and there is no
doubt about her identity. This woman is built on seven hills; her colors
are purple and scarlet; she has a golden cup; she reigns over the kings
of the earth; she kills the martyrs of Jesus Christ. The woman in
Revelation 12 is a woman who is hated by Satan, persecuted by Satan,
driven into the wilderness by Satan, fed by God, protected by God, while
the seed of this woman keeps the Old Testament Ten Commandments and
believes on Jesus Christ. There can't be much doubt about who the "seed"
is. They are not Seventh-day Adventists (who mess up the passage).
(You'll find that every false doctrine or cult in America today comes
from refusal to believe the Book of Revelation, as it is written. They
get their theologies by trying to pretend that they are "Hebrew
Christians," obligated to keep both sets of commandments. Of course, the
"Church of Christ" elder breaks his neck in the same place, and is even
sillier in his exegesis. He tries to form the "law of liberty"---the
Christian law of the Spirit which operates automatically in the body of
the regenerated believer---into a legal, formal, ritualistic set of
precepts to be followed as a continuation of the Old Testament law! This
is another good way to go to hell and take your church with you. Hell is
going to be so full of members of the Campbellite church that they are
not going to have room to sit down.)

The woman, therefore, is plainly Israel: she goes to the wilderness in
the Tribulation for three and a half years, and she is fed by God as she
was fed when she came out of the land of Egypt. This brings up another
very important subject, hitherto unrevealed and unknown to any
Premillennial scholar anywhere in the world. You understand that when I
say this, I'm never telling you, "I'm about to show you something that
no one else knows!" I'm saying that the Authorized Bible is about to
draw judgment on every man who didn't see it. What we are doing is
seeing how the Authorized Version is thoroughly capable of condemning
every saved or lost man on the face of the earth for wilful ignorance,
and of putting them in their proper place. The "nugget" that I'm about
to show you lies undiscovered by every Premillennial Greek scholar
anywhere in the United States because all were too proud to believe
their Authorized Version. The alibi that lazy scholars have in ignoring
these truths is that they are not "essential," or that these doctrines
are not "practical." In spite of the fact that the Bible says, "Man
shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of
the mouth of God," they say, "These doctrines are 'too far out.' What
good can you do with them?" They mean, "I couldn't use these doctrines
to put money in my pocket." Whether or not you can use certain passages
to build a great organization or to build an income for yourself or to
make a career for yourself or to become famous for being a great
organizer or worker, is immaterial. You are still dealing with the
living word of the living God, and, "Heaven and earth shall pass away,"
but it shall not. The word of God was never intended to just be a tool
whereby you could get a reputation for being a great Christian. This
word of God was written to give you life; it was written to condemn you;
it was written to chasten you; it was written to correct you and nurture
you; it was written to develop you, chastise you, bless you, punish you,
comfort you, whip you, and judge you. There's more in the Bible than
just a few verses which teach preachers how to be recognized as great
Christian leaders!

​Micah 7:14---"Feed thy people . . ." You've read the expression, "thy
people" enough now to know that it is not referring to Christians, but
to Israel. This is another abominable habit that these little "cream‑top
Christians" have. They have some wild idea that every time it says "thy
people" in the Bible, that it is referring to Christians. God's people
are called "servants of Christ"; they're called "sons of God"; they're
called "little children"; they're  called "children"; they're called
"babies"; they're called "brethren"; they're called the "seed of God."
They are never called "thy people." Israel is God's people, and the
expressions "thy people" and "my people" are always a reference in the
Old Testament to Israel and never had any reference to the church at
all. "\...with thy rod, the flock of thine heritage, which dwell
solitarily in the wood, in the midst of Carmel: let them feed in Bashan
and Gilead, as in the days of old."

Micah 7:15---"According to the days of thy coming out of the land of
Egypt will I shew unto him marvellous things."

Notice, God is going to bring Israel out into the wilderness as He did
in Exodus 15--20. He is going to feed them as He did in Exodus; He names
the places where He is going to feed them; He is going to do in the
future what He did in the past when they came out of the land of Egypt.
(History is a circle, and the thing that has been "will be." "There is
no new thing under the sun.") This makes the books of Exodus, Numbers,
and Deuteronomy a picture of things to come. That's why the Devil wants
you to stick to John 3:16 and 1 Corinthians 13 only, and then you won't
know what's coming! Christians who are reading this and who are just
going along with a namby‑pamby, don't‑offend‑anybody, soul‑winning, safe
Christianity, where occasionally you witness, pray, "love the brethren,"

Christian and not believe the AV 1611 is the word of God, preserved

be one of the great fundamental leaders who goes down in history as a
"big boy," but you can't be consistently honest. You think about that!
If God Almighty inspired that Book, and you profess that He did, and yet

twentieth century ever produced. They have been using a Roman Catholic
Bible since 1901! The ASV (1901) is the Roman Catholic Vaticanus text!
So is the "New ASV"!

So we find in Micah 7:14--15 that Israel is going to come out into the
wilderness and be fed as they were before. The references for this are
Jeremiah 50:19--20; Ezekiel 20:30--37; and 1 Samuel 25:2. The Bible is
very clear. They are going to be fed exactly as they were when they came
out of Egypt. This means that they are going to be fed, miraculously,
from heaven with manna. God is going to dump it out of the sky again;
you'll find this remarkable event recorded in Exodus 16. We find later
that the Antichrist prevents people from buying or selling unless they
take his mark. Since the Jew will not take his mark, the Jew has to be
fed supernaturally to stay alive. So he is fed with manna from heaven
exactly as he was when he came out of the land of Egypt!

Job 38:22--23---"Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or
hast thou seen the treasures of the hail, Which I have reserved against
the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?"

Here is a passage on this, subtly tucked away where no scholar would see
it unless he believed what he read, as he read it. Job is a picture of
the Tribulation (forty-two chapters representing forty-two months). Job
is on the ground seven days and seven nights (one for each year in the
Tribulation). Job is in the land of Uz, where Israel will be; Job is
persecuted by the Devil, as Israel will be; Job is rescued by God at the
end, as Israel is in Revelation 19.

"Treasures of the snow." "Treasures of the hail." The manna was small
and round, and when it got hot, it melted. It was small, round, and
white, and the Bible is careful to note that it lay like the "hoar
frost" (the white frost) on the ground. The picture, then, is Israel
during the Tribulation, south of the Dead Sea in Sela (or Petra), the
rock city (back there in the holes where they will hide like they did in
the ghettos of Warsaw), coming out at night to get their bread as God
drops it on the ground, and God providing the provision daily for them,
while the Antichrist tries to wipe them out!

12:6 "And the woman fled into the wilderness, where she hath a place
prepared of God, that they should feed her there a thousand two hundred
and threescore days."

Israel's flight to this wilderness is evidently on the sabbath, by
airplane. This opens up a remarkable verse in Matthew 24, which is
unknown to the people who think the passage refers to the destruction of
Jerusalem (A.D. 70). (The Greek scholars, Alford, Machen, Wuest, Weiss,
Tischendorf, Tregelles, Griesbach, Lachmann, Westcott and Hort, Dr. A.
T. Robertson, etc., were blind to this passage. That's because they were
so busy cutting the bark off individual trees that they forgot there was
a forest there!) The Lord Jesus Christ says to Jews in the Tribulation
in Matthew 24:20, "But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter,
neither on the sabbath day."

The context is the Tribulation (in spite of what the private
interpretations of Rome may be on the subject). When we read "flight,"
anybody in the twentieth century automatically thinks of "Flight 679
loading for departure at Gate 16. All aboard, please." In Israel, the
airlines are grounded on the sabbath---Friday night and Saturday. There
are no commercial flights on the Sabbath in modern, twentieth-century
Israel. (This was a news item, nationally broadcasted by CBS on August
10, 1962.) The indication is, that if you took a flight out of Jerusalem
on the sabbath, you'd have to break the law to do it. In Revelation 12
you are told that this Jewish remnant in the Tribulation "keep the
commandments of God." Let a Seventh-day Adventist work on that one for
awhile!

Revelation 12:14---"And to the woman were given two wings of a great
eagle, that she might fly into the wilderness, into her place, where she
is nourished for a time, and times, and half a time, from the face of
the serpent."

If it says "eagle," why don't we say that she flew away on the wings of
an eagle? (The Bible is always literal except where it is impossible to
take it so.) But in Revelation 12:1 you know the "woman" is figurative,
because Israel is pictured as a woman. The "dragon" is a picture of a
literal beast described literally in Job 41. The "woman" is a picture of
a literal group of twelve tribes described literally in Genesis 37 and
Hosea 2. Then, the two great wings of an eagle is a picture of something
else that is literal. If this woman flees on "the wings of an eagle," it
is plainly not an ordinary eagle because the woman is twelve tribes! You
can't get twelve tribes on the wings of an eagle. But, bless your soul,
you can load them into DC‑3s, 5s, and 7s!

You say, "Brother Ruckman, if it meant airplanes, why didn't it say
airplanes? If you believe the Bible as it says it, like it says it, as
you've been teaching all through this book, why didn't it say
airplanes?" If it had said "airplanes," nobody in the eighteenth century
would have understood it. This Bible is not just written for a bunch of
conceited nuts in the twentieth century, who think that God has to stoop
to using their vocabulary. People in other centuries had to read this
book as well as you. There isn't anybody from Adam to the Second Coming
of Jesus Christ who doesn't know what an eagle is. Suppose He had
written, "And to the woman were given two wings of a great B‑29." Now
who, before 1940, would have known what He was talking about? You see
the Bible is written so you can understand it. I realize that scientists
write so that people can't understand them, so people will think that
they are real bright; but God doesn't do that. God wrote in plain
language so that any fool can understand, if he'll just believe it. (All
this going to the Greek and adding Latin and Greek roots on words to
sell them, like a new kind of soap, may fool some folks, but you are
dealing with God the Holy Spirit here in the Bible.)

In the passage, it says that the "woman" (identified as Israel), goes on
"two wings of a great eagle" (unidentified), but indicated as a flight
on the sabbath in Matthew 24. (I am not going to say positively that it
will be airplanes, but since the whole event takes place in the
twentieth century, the probability is so great that you wouldn't stretch
your imagination to believe it.)

Revelation 12:15---"And the serpent cast out of his mouth water as a
flood after the woman, that he might cause her to be carried away of the
flood."

This is a literal flood that will take place in the land of Palestine,
and it is described in Daniel 9; Job 40:23; Jeremiah 12:5, and 47:2. It
is not a flood of troops. It is a flood as in a flood---water, as a
flood. It is water. There is going to be another miraculous intervention
which is going to save Israel from drowning exactly as there was in
Exodus 15--16. In Exodus 15--16, you read that when they came to the Red
Sea the waters parted, and the children of Israel were not drowned. This
means that the events of Exodus all take place over again. Pharaoh, a
type of Antichrist, will show up again; Moses and Aaron, types of Moses
and Elijah, will show up again; Israel in bondage to Hamitic Negroes
will show up again; the escape from them and their liberation will show
up again; they will be fed by manna again; they will return to Palestine
and possess the land again. The only thing that men ever learn from
history is that men never learn from history.

The reason why the liberals and modernists are so anxious to prove that
the Old Testament is "myth," "legend," and "folklore," is because it is
the only scientific, accurate account of future events. Huntley and
Brinkley have no more idea what they are talking about, when they try to
forecast the news, than if they were blind coal miners buried ninety
feet below the surface for the last 600 years. The only scientifically
accurate account of "things to come" is found in the Old Testament.
(That's why all the modern Christian works are built on the "New
Testament only." The Devil is taking over, and the entire body of
"Christians" \[including those born‑again\] are rapidly turning to
rejection of the word of God. You're on the end of the rope, brother!)
This means that any real New Testament Christian in the last days is
going to occupy the same place that New Testament Christians occupied in
the first three centuries of church history. Now's your time to shine;
it's getting dark.

12:7 "And there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought against
the dragon; and the dragon fought and his angels."

Notice, this war takes place in the future. In 1990, wars aren't over
even in heaven! God declares war in Genesis 3:15, and there is still
fighting in Revelation 12---66 books and 6,000 years later. "The Lord
God is a Man of War" (Exod. 15:3), and if you think that there is going
to be "peace on this earth" in thirty years, you are sadly mistaken.
There is no end of war until Revelation 20. In the Holy Bible (which is
able to criticize you and correct you and straighten out your mistakes),
there are only four chapters in which somebody is not fighting---Genesis
1--2, and Revelation 21--22. The rest of the time it is a shooting war!

Michael is peculiarly Jewish in his operations and work (and has nothing
to do with the church), proving again that the "woman" could not be the

being who stands up and fights for Israel, and represents Israel, in
heavenly places. As each nation on this earth has an angelic
representative in heaven (as recognized by some demoniac workers and
spiritists), Michael stands for the children of Daniel's people (Dan.
10:21). Michael is peculiarly Jewish in his operations.

12:8 "And prevailed not; neither was their place found any more in
heaven.

9 And the great dragon was cast out, that old serpent, called the Devil,
and Satan, which deceiveth the whole world: he was cast out into the

Satan does not prevail against Michael, and he is cast out into the

on the AV 1611, King James Bible and watch television, as sure as you

damnation that ever fell on a man or a nation.) The "visitors from outer
space" are going to show up on schedule, and they will come as men from
other planets, and will be accepted as such. Their purpose on this earth
will be to gather the nations into one Ecumenical church at Rome, so
that God can clean out the whole thing and burn it and stomp it out in
one stroke at Armageddon (Rev. 19).

12:10 "And I heard a loud voice saying in heaven, Now is come salvation,
and strength, and the kingdom of our God, and the power of his Christ:
for the accuser of our brethren is cast down, which accused them before
our God day and night."

Here Satan is pointed out as an "accuser," or an adversary, a kind of a
"prosecuting attorney." We are told in the passage that he has been
accusing Christians and saints until the middle of the Tribulation. As
the middle of the Tribulation has not showed up yet, the Devil is still
in full power, going up and down, carrying on the accusation, as he did
in Job 1--2.

12:11 "And they overcame him by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word
of their testimony; and they loved not their lives unto the death."

"The death" means a particular kind of death, as that found in the
Tribulation.

12:12 "Therefore rejoice, ye heavens, and ye that dwell in them. Woe to
the inhabiters of the earth and of the sea! for the devil is come down
unto you, having great wrath, because he knoweth that he hath but a
short time."

The time element is given---3½ years. This passage explodes all kinds of
theories and myths in modern Christianity and ancient Christianity. The
picture is not the picture of a winged angel falling out of heaven with
a spear in his hand (as Dore has drawn in Milton's "Paradise Lost"). The
picture is a 33-year‑old male "angel of light" (professing to be Jesus
Christ), "the god of this world" (the anointed "Xristos" of Ezek. 28), a
magnetic, charming personality, coming down to this earth professing to
be God in the flesh. He doesn't show up as a monster. (The monsters come
up from below! See Rev. 9.) He comes down as a magnetic, attractive
personality to take over the earth under one ecumenical, world movement
(Rev. 13:3--8). When he shows up from outer space (the "superman from
outer space," the "man from Mars"), all science, education, philosophy,
art, music, Life, Look, Newsweek, radio, television, and press will hail
him as the greatest superhuman being which evolution ever produced! The
judgment of heaven on this scene is "Woe to the inhabiters of the earth
and of the sea!" For this is the one who took the kingdoms from Adam,
who caused God's Son to die for sin, and who damned so many souls into
hell that you couldn't count them if you stayed up all night. (He'll
settle your hash too if you'll let him do it!) (See book on The Mark of
the Beast, 1960; reprint 1994.)

In Revelation 12:11 there are three classifications of believers listed,
and this threefold classification occurs a number of times in the word
of God. Of course, the direct context has reference to Tribulation
saints. "And they overcame him (the direct reference is to the "dragon"
or "serpent" in vs. 9) "by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word of
their testimony" (as John says he is there for the testimony of God);
and they loved not their lives unto the death (a particular kind of
death---decapitation)." Although there is a direct reference to
Tribulation saints, there is a definite spiritual application here to
three types of Christians. This three‑fold classification is found so
many times in the word of God that there can be no doubt about the
connection.

Of the twelve apostles (class one), there were three (class two) who
were in the inner circle---Peter, James, and John. Then, of these three,
there was one---John (class three), the beloved disciple, who was closer
than any of the rest of them. He lay on the Lord's breast at the Last
Supper; he went with Him all the way to the cross. John is in a class by
himself. We read of the betrayal at the Last Supper, when they were all
sitting around the table, that the Lord said, "One of you shall betray
me." We read that each disciple said, "Lord, is it I?" But John said,
"Lord, who is it?" John had a clear conscience about the matter (John
13:13--26).

So we have three classes of Christians. We have the twelve---the general
called group; we have the three---the intimate circle; we have the
one---the close one who is really faithful. Note further:

Matthew 13:8---"But other fell into good ground, and brought forth
fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold." The best,
the next best, the least. Thirty, sixty, hundred---different degrees of
fruit bearing for the Lord. In John 15, the Lord Jesus Christ says that
a Christian can bear "fruit" (vs. 4), class one; "more fruit" (vs. 2),
class two; or "much fruit" (vs. 8), class three. Christians serve the
Lord through three motives:

1\. Those who serve because they are afraid of Him, serve as slaves.

2\. Those who serve just to get rewards, or wages, serve as hirelings.

3\. Those who serve because they love Him, serve as sons.

This threefold classification occurs in Revelation 12:11. Every
Christian overcomes the Devil by the blood of the Lamb because every
Christian is saved by the blood of the Lamb (class one). But not every
Christian testifies for the Lord, and those who do make up the next
classification---those who overcome the Devil "by the word of their
testimony." Yet, those who testify of Christ do not all love the Lord
Jesus Christ more than they love their lives, their families, or the
world. This bunch "loved not their lives unto the death," making the
third distinct classification. The same classifications are found in
Revelation 17:14.

Revelation 17:14---"These shall make war with the Lamb, and the Lamb
shall overcome them: for he is Lord of lords, and King of kings: and
they that are with him are \[1\] called, and \[2\] chosen, and \[3\]
faithful."

Every Christian is "called" (Rom. 8:29--30). Not every Christian is
"chosen" for a job, out of those who are "called." Of those that are
"chosen," not every one is "faithful." So we have the three
classifications again in Revelation 17---"called," "chosen," and
"faithful."

Then, this threefold classification is of a practical use. The twelve
disciples (exclusive of Judas Iscariot) represent those thirty‑fold
Christians, who have faith, who are called, who overcome the Devil by
the blood of the Lamb, and who "bring forth fruit." The three
disciples---Peter, James, and John---are representative of those
sixtyfold bearing Christians, who are chosen, that "bring forth more
fruit," and that have "the word of their testimony." The last group is
representative of John, the beloved disciple, who was called, chosen,
and faithful, who was saved by the blood, who had a word of testimony,
who loved his life not unto the death, and who brought forth much
fruit---one hundredfold.

12:13 "And when the dragon saw that he was cast unto the earth, he
persecuted the woman which brought forth the man child.

14 And to the woman were given two wings of a great eagle, that she
might fly into the wilderness, into her place, where she is nourished
for a time, and times, and half a time, from the face of the serpent.

15 And the serpent cast out of his mouth water as a flood after the
woman, that he might cause her to be carried away of the flood.

16 And the earth helped the woman, and the earth opened her mouth, and
swallowed up the flood which the dragon cast out of his mouth.

17 And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went to make war with
the remnant of her seed, which keep the commandments of God, and have
the testimony of Jesus Christ."

Now, getting the whole chapter together and coming down to the last
verse, there are two things we need to study.

The first thing is the peculiar, vehement hatred Satan holds against the
nation of Israel---which is manifest in history. Satan's object of
hatred is Israel because the Saviour came from Israel and every book in
the Bible was written by somebody from Israel. Also, this hatred stems
from the fact that  Israel is promised this world, and since Satan is
the "god of this world" (2 Cor. 4:4--5), he does not particularly relish
seeing Israel take it from him (which is going to happen at the Second
Advent of Jesus Christ).

Of course, these great Bible truths are entirely ignored by Amillennial
and Postmillennial theologians. (When I say Amillennial and
Postmillennial theologians, I am referring to people who do not believe
that Christ is coming back to reign a thousand years, or to people who
believe the "modern man" is going to bring in the thousand‑year reign of
Christ first before Jesus Christ comes back. These theologians ignore
the fact that in this age Israel is cast off in rejection, but is later
to be restored. These backslidden, carnal, and ignorant leaders all
assume that Israel's persecution is due to the fact that God is all
through with them forever. If you want to see this brought out clearly,
buy that great, non‑Christian, vulgar caricature called the Amplified
New Testament, recommended by Billy Graham, Theodore Epp, and dozens of
other fundamentalists! In 1 Thessalonians 2:14--16, the Amplified
version says that God's wrath is come upon the Jew completely and
forever! Exactly why Fundamental and Premillennial men recommend this
preposterous translation is something beyond me. The Amplified version
is written by an Amillennial group of reformed theologians, who do not
believe in the Millennial reign of Christ on this earth.)

John 18:36---"Jesus answered, My kingdom is not of this world: if my
kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should
not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence."

The writers of the Amplified version take out the word "now" so you
would think that His kingdom never was "from hence." These conceited,
ignorant people are called "conceited" and "ignorant" in Romans 11:25.
Notice how careful the Holy Spirit is to use the words that I have
used---conceited and ignorant. This means that regardless of their
degrees or their proficiency in Greek or Hebrew, they are conceited,
ignorant people, according to the Holy Spirit.

Romans 11:25--28---"For I would not, brethren, that ye should be
ignorant of this mystery, lest ye should be wise in your own conceits;
that blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the fulness of the
Gentiles be come in. And so all Israel shall be saved: as it is written,
There shall come out of Sion the Deliverer, and shall turn away
ungodliness from Jacob: For this is my covenant unto them, when I shall
take away their sins. As concerning the gospel, they are enemies for
your sakes: but as touching the election, they are beloved for the
fathers' sakes."

Compare Jeremiah 33:8, 1 Thessalonians 2:14, and Romans 11:5--7. Then
the Amillennial teachers---Gresham Machen, Berkhof, Dabney, Hodge,
Kuyper, the entire body of Reformed theologians of Presbyterians,
Congregationalists, etc. (coming from John Calvin), A. T. Robertson,
Louisville Seminary, Dallas, Fort Worth, etc., without exception, every
leading major denominational seminary anywhere in the United
States---are ignorant and conceited, and have perverted the word of God,
and are lying about the position which Israel occupies in this present
age. In this present age, Israel is the cast‑off wife of Jehovah, who is
going to be restored at the Second Advent of Jesus Christ. (The man who
doesn't believe this is ignorant and conceited, according to the Holy
Spirit (Rom. 11:21--25).

In plainer words, God is going to take up His dealing with the Jews and
drop His dealing with the Gentiles. As the Holy Spirit moves on (East to
West, as He always moves), and finishes dealing with the Gentiles, there
is going to be nothing left behind but a huge, apostate, God‑forsaken,
ecclesiastical morgue, headed by the pope of Rome; it will claim to be
the spiritual, "true Israel," because it has spiritualized the promises
of the Old Testament and apprehended them for itself. This godless,
apostate, ecclesiastical abomination is all that is going to be left of
the Gentile church. God is not through with Israel; God is going to
restore Israel; so in this age, the Devil's peculiar wrath is aimed
against Israel. There is no doubt about the accuracy of Scripture on
this point. When the Lord Jesus Christ is crucified, the Jewish people
reject the crown that God gave them (to the kingdom of heaven), and give
it to Rome. The Jewish people, led by their rulers and chief priests,
said, "His blood be on us, and on our children" (Matt. 27:25). They
forsook the Lord God Jehovah, their Old Testament leader, and said that
the only king they wanted was the Roman pope, the "holy father," Caesar
(John 19:15).

So, the course for this age is clearly defined. In this age, Israel is
in temporary rejection, and will only be restored at the Second Advent
of the Lord Jesus Christ. Satan's particular object of hatred, in this
age and dispensation, is Israel, whom he has power over by the
permissive will of God because they rejected their Messiah. At a future
date, when Israel is restored under the Messiah, then Israel is going to
be converted and saved. As this time approaches, the Devil is going to
launch a vehement, concentrated attack on Israel, which is what we are
now studying in Revelation 12:16--17.

If there is any doubt about this supernatural hatred of Israel as a
nation, then let history speak for itself. In A.D. 135, Hadrian, the
Roman Emperor, had 580,000 Jews killed. (You understand that this was
after the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70. Titus wiped out Jerusalem

People got so hungry, at this time of the seige of Jerusalem, that they

banished all Jews from England. In 1096, a "Holy War" was started in

Under Louis XV (Catholic), in 1723, the Jews were given permission to

In 1345--1350, the Jews were burned in Germany, and were accused of

Dominican Catholics ran them out of Spain in "The Inquisition" in 1492.
In 1938 Hitler (a Roman Catholic) and Joseph Paul Goebbels (a Roman

Never let the newspapers fool you about the Roman Catholic Church

Two ships (with no port to land at) left England around 1918 (after the

Nicholas I of Russia (in 1825) ordered 5,000,000 Jews to move into a

1943--45, or they disappeared in the area around Hungary, Yugoslavia,
and Austria. (These statistics are according to the President of the
Hungarian independent Party. He also said that 5,000,000 were cremated
in murder factories at Auschwitz.)

The Jews said, "His blood be on us, and on our children," and that's
just exactly where it went. (When the Bible says something, you can live
and die on the verse. It won't miss it by half a minute.) This
persecution of the Jews has gone on years and years and years and years

out and killed them. The Jews have been driven, killed, and kicked
across this earth for 1900 years, and will continue to be driven,

is going to "come on the scene."

There is no doubt about this thing at all, as far as persecution goes.
They have been persecuted continually, and will continue to be
persecuted. The Roman Catholic information which Adolph Hitler used in
Mein Kampf was literature written by the Dominican monks in Spain at the
time of the "Inquisition." This collection of papers is called The
Protocols of the Elders of Zion, which supposedly proves that the
"Seventy International Jewish Bankers" are planning to take over the
world with its gold, and then wipe out the Gentile race by making them a
mongrel race, in subservience to Israel. This is what Roman Catholic
Hitler used, and this is what is being used in the United States today
to promote anti‑Semitism. (You'll see it come on more in the future. It
extends through history.)

In the Russian Revolution (1916--1918), the Jews of Eastern Europe
became the victims of pogroms in Poland, and large scale massacres
perpetrated by the White Russians, or counter‑revolutionary forces. The
bloody events of 1918 were repeated in 1945, and the Jewish self‑defense
groups could do little against the organized armies. They were killed;
they were persecuted; they were tortured. (I don't care how tolerant the
Roman Catholic Church seems to be getting in order to swallow up you
bunch of apostate Protestants; don't you believe them for five minutes.
The official teaching in the Roman Catholic Church is that every
orthodox Jew on the face of the earth is cursed by Almighty God. A good
Roman Catholic believes that every orthodox Jew in the world is cursed
\[Anathema\] by God, because every one of his popes, bishops, and
priests has to accept as infallible doctrine, the teaching that
everybody who doesn't subscribe to the articles of the Roman Catholic
Church \[in the Council of Trent, 1546\] is cursed. God has no sympathy
with you stupid sinners \[who have college educations and high school
educations!\], who stick out your chest and brag about your religion and
say, "I was born a Catholic and I'll die a Catholic." Every Jew on this
earth was cursed by the Roman Catholic Church, officially, at the
Council of Trent, 1546. If you don't know that and believe it, you are a
blank idiot!)

In Otto von Bismarck's reign, the Jews were persecuted. He made use of
anti‑Semitism in his war against the liberals, radicals, Karl Marx,
Ferdinand Lasalle, and the leaders of the socialist movement in Germany,
who were of Jewish origin. There were "blood accusations" against the
Jews in countries like Germany, Austria, and Hungary in 1930. The Jew
said, "His blood be on us, and on our children," and it has been
required ever since the resurrection of Christ, and it will be required
until the Second Coming of Christ. So we are not surprised to read in
Revelation 12:17, "And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went to
make war with the remnant of her seed." The Devil is not mad at the
Roman Catholic Church. The priests say that "the gates of hell shall not
prevail against us," and brag about how the Devil has tried to overthrow
their church, but how it is still here. But the Devil has never tried to
"overthrow the Roman Church." He wouldn't think of it! The Roman
Catholic Church is the best gimmick he's got on this earth. If I were
the Devil, I'd bless your church, and prosper it until it was the
biggest, richest, wealthiest, most influential body on the face of the
earth. If I were the Devil, I would get "all power on heaven and earth"
directly from Jesus Christ, to let you have everything you wanted, as a
church, and make sure that you came out on top. I could flat use you, if
I were the Devil! No, the Devil is not angry at the Roman Catholic
Church. He'll protect it and take care of it, and the gates of heaven
won't come anywhere near it! The church that Christ built, founded upon
the Rock, Jesus Christ (1 Cor. 10), might make the Devil angry. But he
is not even as "put out" with that true Church as he is with the nation
that brought forth the Saviour and the Bible. In the final analysis, his
wrath is turned against the nation which God called out, the nation of
whom God said (through Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob), in Genesis 12:3, "And
I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and
in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed."

"In Thee." Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and not a Gentile, political,
religious, Roman Catholic, North African, pseudo‑religious, United
Nations monstrosity that isn't worth the powder and shot it would take
to blow it to purgatory.

Now we come again to that startling doctrine in the Bible---the doctrine
that a man can lose his salvation. (Of course, this is shocking news to
a Baptist and a Presbyterian!) Before we begin with the passage, let it
be stated very clearly, and thoroughly understood by all involved, that
there are passages in the Bible which definitely indicate that somebody
can lose salvation after they have it. Let me qualify that statement by
saying that some of these passages are distorted to prove that a
Christian can lose it. Then again, many of them are distorted to prove
that a Christian can't lose it. Before we study these passages, let's
understand very clearly that the period in which these verses take place
is the period that follows the Church Age---the Tribulation. This is
after the Body of Christ is "caught out." To say that the people in the
Tribulation have the same thing happen to them when they get saved as we
do in this dispensation, is the grossest absurdity. For example, in this
age you are "born again" (no man in the Old Testament ever was, or ever
will be, "born again"). In this age you become part of Jesus Christ
(nobody in the Old Testament was ever part of Jesus Christ, nor is there
any indication that they were directly, or indirectly, familiar with
such a doctrine, nor are they in the Tribulation). In this age when you
are saved, the Holy Spirit comes into you and spiritually circumcizes
you from the body of your flesh, and baptizes you into the Body of
Christ. (There is no indication that this operation happens anytime in
the Old Testament, or anytime in the Tribulation, nor is there any
reason why anybody would think that it would.) Why some people, who
believe in eternal security, think for some reason that everybody who
was ever saved was saved "by grace through faith" plus nothing is a
great mystery. Noah was saved by building an ark. Some say, "But Noah
found grace in the eyes of the Lord." He found grace, but he was not
saved by grace. Noah was saved by water (1 Pet. 3:20--21).

We suddenly come upon another doctrine that is unknown to fundamental,
Premillennial scholars. There is no indication that Pember, Harry
Ironside, Larkin, Dr. DeHaan, Wilbur Smith, Theodore Epp, Bullinger,
Dwight Moody, Percy Crawford, Harold Ockenga, Billy Graham, or any of
the great evangelistic teachers and preachers of the twentieth century
had any idea what the following verses referred to.

Revelation 12:17---"And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went to
make war with the remnant of her seed, which keep the commandments of
God, and have the testimony of Jesus Christ."

Revelation 14:12---"Here is the patience of the saints: here are they
that keep the commandments of God, and the faith of Jesus."

Revelation 22:14---"Blessed are they that do his commandments, that they
may have right to the tree of life, and may enter in through the gates
into the city."

Now, these verses are used by the Seventh-day Adventists and the Church
of Christ to prove that you have to keep the commandments to get to
heaven. (Of course, both of these groups ignore the fact that the
passages, as given, refer to the Old Testament Ten Commandments given to
Israel, and apply in the Tribulation and the Old Testament. They were
never given to the New Testament believer in the Church Age.) Watch how
the entire Bible begins to bear witness to a period of time in which a
man can lose salvation! Keep in mind that in the Old Testament the Holy
Spirit came into men and left them (as in the case of Saul). He came in
them and did not leave them (as in the case of David). He came in them,
left them, and came back on them (as in the case of Samson). That is,
there is no fixed doctrine on the indwelling of the Holy Spirit in the
Old Testament. He comes and goes as the situation demands. Since the Old
Testament saint is not circumcized, since his soul is stuck to the body
of his flesh, the Holy Spirit can only remain in a man as long as he
obeys the law. David realized this, and when he committed his terrible
sin, he prayed, "Cast me not away from thy presence; and take not thy
holy spirit from me" (Psa. 51:11). The Spirit of the Lord departs from
Samson, but He returns. The Spirit of the Lord departs from Saul and
does not return. (Hence, Saul is the favorite man, in the Old Testament,
for the Holiness people, Church of Christ, Roman Catholics, Lutherans,
Episcopalians, Seventh-day Adventists, Mormons, Jehovah Witnesses, to
point at to prove that a born‑again Christian in the New Testament can
lose his salvation! Of course, this kind of private interpretation
indicates what kind of people these are. They are people under the curse
of the law trying to be saved by their self‑righteous works. They
voluntarily reject the shed blood of Christ as the complete solution for
the problem of justification, and count it as only part in a series of
self‑righteous works, where they are going to earn heaven by their own
goodness. That is the teaching of these groups.)

Matthew 24:13---"But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall
be saved."

Notice, the indication is that if he doesn't "endure until the end" that
he will not be saved. Notice that in the context that the "gospel of the
grace of God" is not being preached, but the "gospel of the kingdom" is
(vs. 14). The "end" is not the end of your life, but the end of a period
of time called the "Tribulation" (vs. 14, 21--22, 29).

Matthew 24:50--51---"The lord of that servant shall come in a day when
he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of, And
shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites:
there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth."

Here we have a servant serving the Lord, and he is cut asunder and goes
to hell with the hypocrites. In Matthew 25 we have a bunch of people who
lose oil (a type of the Holy Spirit), and cannot see the Lord, or greet
Him when He comes back.

Matthew 25:30---"And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer
darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth."

Here we have a servant of Christ being cast "into outer darkness"! (If
these people who believe in eternal security keep trying to twist these
verses around to prove that this fellow was just a "professor" instead
of a "possessor," let them face the horrible, terrible fact that in the
next series of verses---Matthew 25:31--46---you are told that every
sheep who was ever saved was saved by works!) This is why you find
Matthew 25 used by the Roman Catholic Church, Seventh-day Adventists,
Jehovah's Witnesses, Lutheran, Episcopalian, Methodist, Mormon, and all
the heretics (!) to try to prove that a man can get righteous enough to
gain heaven by his works. They use Matthew 25:31--46. Notice that in
this passage nobody is saved by grace, and nobody in the passage is
saved by faith. Notice, too, that nobody in the passage is a "born‑again
Christian." Nobody in the passage is called a "Christian." Nobody in the
passage is in the "church," and the church is not in the passage! When
God told you to "Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that
needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth," He
wasn't trying to browbeat you or kid you; He was telling you a dying
truth. Matthew 25 plainly teaches salvation by good works. Let's face
it; it's there! Exactly as you find it in James 2---"Ye see then how
that by works a man is justified, and not by faith only." But what
everybody fails to believe (and the problem is always belief) is that
the passage (Matt. 25) said, "When the Son of man shall come in his
glory" (vs. 31) "and before him shall be gathered all nations" (vs. 32).
The King James English always solves the theological problems which the
religious leaders invent by their irreverent, blasphemous, ridiculous,
mis‑handling of the word. What the greatest cardinals, bishops, and
popes cannot find, any simpleton can get off a dime store counter if he
will believe what he reads! The problem is unbelief.

In Matthew 25:31--32, the Son of God is not referred to, but the Son of
man. (Paul never refers to Jesus Christ as the Son of man.) The Son of
man is always Jesus' earthly, literal, designation to Israel, as the
Messiah over the literal, physical, visible kingdom. The passage states
when He comes "in His glory," not when He comes at the Rapture to catch
out the Christians! In Matthew 25:32, He is judging nations---not
Christians! There are three classes present---"His brethren" (vss. 40,
45), "the sheep," and "the goats." There's no discussion of "saved" and
"lost." The text has to do with the sheep treating brethren right, and
the goats not treating them right. To add insult to injury (in view of
the fact that the official teaching of the Ecumenical Council, the
National Council of Churches  
---and every major denominational seminary in the world---that Matthew
25 is a reference to the White Throne Judgment of Rev. 20), in Matthew
25:34 the reading is not, "Come get to heaven or New Jerusalem," but
"Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom." The "inheritance"
is always gotten by works every time you find it mentioned in the New
Testament (Col. 3:24). You are saved "by grace through faith," but
brother, that inheritance is not "by grace through faith"---it's earned!
Notice, it is earned by clean living in 1 Corinthians 6:9--12; it is
earned by serving the Lord and working for Christ in Colossians
3:24--25; it is earned by works in resisting temptation and loving the
Lord in James 2:5; it is earned by works after you are saved by walking
after the Spirit and not after the flesh in Galatians 5:16--21. The
expression "inherit the kingdom" in Matthew 25 never refers to getting
to heaven, and there isn't any place in any Bible where it ever referred
to that! Only the private interpretation of a God‑defying,
Bible‑perverting materialist could make it refer to anything else. (I
say this realizing, of course, that this interpretation runs contrary to
every denominational seminary in the world today, and I say it as a
Bible‑believer, who believes what he reads, where he finds it, as it is
written, and never goes to the trouble of trying to interpret anything.
Thank God we don't have to interpret! The Bible interprets itself \[1
Cor. 1:10--13\].)

The Bible tells you all too plainly in this passage that any man who
teaches that Matthew 25 is a picture of the Church Age or the
Christians,or the White Throne Judgment or the plan of salvation or the
Christian "enduring to the end" by good works, is a false prophet---that
is, if you believe what you read. (If you don't, then my interpretation
is just as good as yours! Right?) In Matthew 25, you might as well face
it, you have some people who are saved by works. You have some people
who are lost because they don't do the works. Some of us born‑again
people know that all the Bible is not doctrinally applicable to the way
of salvation in this dispensation. We know that Noah was saved by
building an ark, and saved by water. We know that you can build an ark
until you are blue in the face, and go under that water 50,000 times, or
float on top of it like Noah did, shut yourself out from it like Noah
did, or get sprinkled like Judas, and you still will wind up in the
Lake---and the "Lake" is not water! The point is, that when a man is
saved in this age, there are things which happen to him that did not
happen to Noah or Abraham or Moses or David or Isaac or Jacob or Enoch,
or any of the heroes of faith in the Old Testament. "By faith" they may
have done a number of things, but that doesn't mean that when they got
saved that anything happened to them other than that they just believed
in God. Those of us who believe on Jesus Christ as our Saviour in this
dispensation are:

1\. Spiritually circumcised (Col. 2).

2\. Born again (John 3).

3\. Indwelt by the Holy Spirit (John 14).

4\. Sealed by the Holy Spirit (Eph. 4).

5\. Put into Jesus Christ (1 Cor. 12).

6\. Baptized by the Holy Spirit (Eph. 4).

7\. Baptized into the Body of Christ by the Holy Spirit (1 Cor. 12).

You couldn't find these things mentioned, or said, in connection with
Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Moses, David, Samson, or Jephthah if you read
that Bible through until you were blind as a bat.

Let us look further in Hebrews 3. (These, too, are verses that deal with
the Jews in the Tribulation.)

Hebrews 3:6---"But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are
we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm
unto the end."

There is the verse a Catholic priest uses to prove that you can lose
salvation! Notice in the context that the "end" is not the end of your
life, but the end of a period of time. Look at the context (vss. 7--15),
and remember that "a text without a context is a pretext!"

Hebrews 3:14---"For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the
beginning of our confidence steadfast unto the end."

The end of what? Verses 16--19---a period of time, brother! Look at the
context---Hebrews 4:1--9. You are not made a partaker of Christ if you
"hold the beginning of your confidence stedfast unto the end." You were
made partaker of Christ the moment you trusted Him as your personal
Saviour (John 1:12; Eph. 1; Rom. 10:9--10, etc.). I don't have to worry
about becoming part of His house if I "hold." I am in His body which is
apart of "His household" (Eph. 2--3).

In Hebrews 6:4--6 you might as well face it, brother, they lost it.

Hebrews 6:11--12---"And we desire that every one of you do shew the same
diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end: That ye be not
slothful, but followers of them who through faith and patience inherit
the promises."

"Inherit the promises." Not get eternal life! Don't read something into
it that isn't there.

Look at Hebrews 8:8--12, and observe that the new covenant used in the
Book of Hebrews is aimed at "the house of Israel" and "the house of
Judah." It is not aimed at "spiritual Jews." It is not aimed at
"spiritual Hebrews." It is not aimed at "heirs of Abraham." It means
what it says. When God says, "I will make a new covenant with the house
of Israel and with the house of Judah," in Hebrews 8:8, do you know what
He means? He means the "house of Israel" and the "house of Judah." (If
55,000 popes or the greatest scholars in this world said that the
passage was addressed to the "spiritual household" of the church which
fulfills the promises to Judah and Israel, all I would say is that the
Bible doesn't say that. So you can forget it; you can just pitch it
out.)

Notice the same thing in Hebrews 10:26--31. Here is one of the most
famous passages in the Bible used to prove that you can lose salvation.
Yet, the AV 1611 told you that the passage applied to a Jew in the
Tribulation. Look at Hebrews 10:26--31. The Bible‑rejecting "Bible
teachers" and preachers are trying to make "the Lord shall judge his
people" refer to Christians! If you've learned anything, you've learned
that the expression "His people," "My people," and "thy people" never
refer to anybody but Israel. Did you ever notice that Hebrews 10:30 is a
quotation from Deuteronomy 32:35--36? What a jolt! (Dr. DeHaan is
teaching that the passage refers to the Judgment Seat of Christ; the
Baptists are trying to prove it's a fellow who didn't quite make it; the
Holiness people are trying to prove it's a fellow who did make it and
lost it! The whole bunch of them have gone to private interpretation to
pervert the word of God.) The passage quoted is Deuteronomy 32:35--36,
and if you read Deuteronomy 32:35--40, there will be no doubt in your
mind at all to whom the passage is addressed, or to the period of time
to which it applies. It is a passage addressed to Hebrews in the
Tribulation, immediately preceding the restoration of Israel and the
setting up of the Davidic kingdom in the land of Palestine. You may as
well face it. There are scores of verses in the New Testament that teach
that someone can lose salvation, but not a single one of them is aimed
directly at a New Testament, born‑again, Gentile, Christian believer who
is in the Body of Jesus Christ.

Look at your chart on the Tribulation. You have a line going up at the
one‑inch mark, representing the Rapture of the church, and not returning
again until the eight‑inch mark. This period between the one‑inch mark
and the eight‑inch mark is the Tribulation or Daniel's Seventieth Week
or the Time of Jacob's Troubles. Notice before the one‑inch mark that
you had "the Church Age," where a man was saved "by grace through faith"
plus nothing. He doesn't even have to show any works as an evidence of
his salvation. Any poor, lost sinner who comes to Jesus Christ and
receives Him by faith as his personal Saviour is born again, sealed with
the Holy Spirit until the day of redemption, and baptized into the Body
of Christ. When the Lord Jesus Christ comes (as you have the arrow
coming down at the one‑inch mark), and the Body of Christ is caught up
(as you have the arrow going up at the one‑inch mark), you are then
entering back into an Old Testament situation, like you had before the
Church Age. You are going back into a system which is under law. I
realize that this is hard for these people who think that the law was
done away with for a Christian (Gal. 3, Cor. 3,  
1 Tim. 1), and therefore, the world is going to be all through with the
law forever. This is another error. The Seventh-day Adventists
capitalize on this error in fundamental theology, just like the Church
of Christ capitalizes on the refusal of Baptists to believe in the
baptism of the Holy Spirit. That is, God allows the Devil to raise up
heretics to make His people study. The Church of Christ is a group of
apostates whom the Lord has let the Devil raise up to make the Baptists
admit that the one true baptism is not immersion in water. As long as a
Baptist maintains that position, the Campbellite can hang him. By the
same token, the Lord has allowed the Seventh-day Adventist group to come
up to show the Christian that although the Christian is not under law
but under grace, that the law will be reinstituted in the Tribulation
and in the Millennium when Israel comes back into prominence, and the
sabbath day will be observed again (Col. 2:16--17, Isa. 66:21--24). The
"sabbath" and the "new moon" will be observed in eternity, and the
sabbath is observed in the Tribulation (Matt. 24) where the Tribulation
saints keep the commandments of God.

From the one‑inch mark to the eight‑inch mark on your chart, although a
man is saved by grace through faith in the shed blood of Jesus Christ, a
number of things do not happen to him when he gets saved that happen to
you when you get saved in this age. When that man is saved, he is not
put into Christ's Body because the Body of Christ is gone. When that man
is saved, the Holy Spirit does not circumcise his soul from the "body of
his flesh." Although a man is saved in the Tribulation "by grace through
faith," he has to "endure to the end" of that period to be a "partaker
of Christ." If a man in the Tribulation does not "endure until the end,"
and have "faith plus works," he can lose his salvation, because his
salvation is not based on a permanent, indwelling Christ, but upon the
continued act of his personal obedience to the commandments of, God. Is
that clear?

That system will straighten more Bible out for you, in fifteen minutes,
