than you can get straightened out in four years at any five fundamental
schools on the face of this continent. When you find a verse in your
Bible that teaches you can lose salvation, you watch that verse, and it
will apply every time, DOCTRINALLY, to the Tribulation. And don't you
misquote me! Don't you try to make a liar out of me! Don't you go around
saying, "Dr. Ruckman says that this passage applies only to this or that
person, and has no application to the Church Age!"

2 Timothy 3:16---"All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is
profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in
righteousness."

A Christian can learn something from every verse in the Bible. Every
verse in the Bible can be a blessing to a Christian; every verse in the
Bible can be used by a Christian. But, if you are going to get your
doctrine straight---the truth of God, as God wrote it, where He wrote
it, as He said it---you've got to believe what He said, as He said it,
where it's written, the way you find it. I'm talking about a 1611,
Authorized Version, Reformation Text, on a dime store counter, $1.50 per
copy.

This brings us to the end of Revelation 12. There's been a lot of
material in Revelation 11--12, and there's a lot more new material
coming in Revelation 13. This middle portion of the Book of Revelation
(Chapters 11--13) is the most loaded portion of Scripture anywhere in
the word of God. The only three chapters that can compete with
Revelation 11--13 are Genesis 1--3. Outside of Genesis 1--3, Revelation
11--13 has more new material in it than any other three chapters in the
word of God---exclusive of none. (There is a great deal of spiritual
material in John 1--3, but oh, the light that Revelation 11--13 gives on
the entire body of Scripture!) Do you realize that in these last two
chapters, you have learned a dozen things that were unknown to the
greatest Bible teachers in the world? In 2,000 years of church history,
they haven't even been able to find the passages which dealt with these
things we have been talking about. And the reason for this is shockingly
simple: the greatest teachers and preachers have eventually gone to the
Greek and Hebrew to get their information, and they "dead‑ended" in
Greek and Hebrew lexicons instead of the authority of the Holy Spirit
Himself who alone is able to "open the Bible" (John 16:13).

In these last two chapters, you've learned Moses and Elijah are going to
come back; you've learned that there is going to be temple worship again
with human sacrifices at an altar, on this earth; you've learned that
when Moses and Elijah appear again the events of Exodus 1--20 will be
repeated; you've learned the Antichrist is Judas Iscariot, reincarnated
by Satan coming down from heaven; you've learned that there is going to
be a post‑Tribulation Rapture; you've learned that there are going to be
saved people at the Great White Throne Judgment who came out of the
Millennium and the Tribulation; you've learned that Israel is going to
be persecuted as she never was before; you've learned the length of time
of persecution, where she is going to be when she is persecuted, and who
is going to persecute her; you've learned that nearly all the events in
Exodus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy are going to take place again; you've
learned that there is an element of works connected with faith in the
Tribulation; you've learned that Israel is going to be fed with manna
again; and above all, you've found the key to Matthew, Acts, and
Hebrews, which the Holiness people and the Roman Catholic Church could
never unlock. That is, the passages which deal doctrinally with the
Tribulation period, which precede the Second Advent, are given in
Matthew and Acts 1--7, because for all practical purposes, the Second
Advent is about to be fulfilled.

In the next chapter, we run head on into the man about whom there is
more information in the Bible than any two other men put together,
outside of Jesus Christ; for there is more information in the Bible
about the "Antichrist" than there is on Moses and Paul combined.

CHAPTER 13

13:1 "And I stood upon the sand of the sea, and saw a beast rise up out
of the sea, having seven heads and ten horns, and upon his horns ten
crowns, and upon his heads the name of blasphemy.

2 And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard, and his feet were
as the feet of a bear, and his mouth as the mouth of a lion: and the
dragon gave him his power, and his seat, and great authority."

Remind yourself that "the beast" is primarily a king. Although a beast
also represents a kingdom, a beast is never a kingdom only, and it is
always a king before it is a kingdom (see Dan. 7). This is very
important because many of the Premillennial scholars have limited the
beast in Revelation 13:1--2 to the revived Roman Empire, and have gotten
Bible students all fouled up by making them think that the Antichrist is
the next beast who shows up in 13:11. This brings in all kinds of
complications, and it makes "the number of the beast" (vs. 18) apply not
to "a man," but to "mankind," and yet 13:18 says, "For it is the number
of a man," not the number of a kingdom, or "mankind."

Since the Scripture is always the correct interpreter, let us turn to
Daniel 7. In Daniel 7 (the vision of the four beasts), we see that
Daniel's four beasts match the ones in Revelation. The three beasts who
are described first in Daniel 7 (and they come up out of the great sea
exactly as in Rev. 13:1) are said to be a lion, a bear, and a leopard ‑
exactly as you find a leopard, bear, and lion in Revelation 13:2. That
isn't all. In Revelation 13:2, the beast is a leopard, bear, lion, and
dragon. In Daniel 7, he is a lion, bear, leopard, and then a nondescript
beast who has ten horns (Dan. 7:7--8). The beast in Daniel 7 has the
same number of horns found in Revelation 13:1--2. That is, these beasts
are identical. The fourth beast of Daniel's vision (Dan. 7:7) is the
beast of Revelation 13:1--2. He is a nondescript beast and is not named
in Daniel 7:7--8. He has more than one head like "leviathan," and he has
ten horns.

Daniel 7:17---"These great beasts, which are four, are four kings, which
shall arise out of the earth."

Yet, we read in Daniel 7:3 that the "four great beasts came up from the
sea." Then, the reference to the "sea" is to a body of water on this
earth, and not a "multitude of people" as the "waters" in Revelation 17.
The word in Daniel is "sea," and the word in Revelation is "waters."
These are two different words.

Daniel 7:17---"These great beasts, which are four, are four kings, which
shall arise out of the earth."

Notice, the Scripture says "kings" first, and then "kingdoms."

Daniel 7:23---"Thus he said, The fourth beast shall be the fourth
kingdom upon earth, which shall be diverse from all kingdoms, and shall
devour the whole earth, and shall tread it down, and break it in
pieces."

This is the "fourth kingdom." Therefore, the four beasts in this passage
begin with Persia and not Babylon, if you're going to match them with
Daniel's image. (This puts the Scofield notes, and the ones by Ironside,
Gaebelein, Pettingill, and Larkin, off again.)

Notice the context of Daniel 7.

Daniel 7:22---"Until the Ancient of days came, and judgment was given to
the saints of the most High; and the time came that the saints possessed
the kingdom."

This is as you find a post‑Tribulation Rapture of saints in the
Tribulation, and as the passage in Revelation 14:12 says, "Here is the
patience of the saints: here are they that keep the commandments of God,
and the faith of Jesus."

In this passage in Daniel 7 notice the Antichrist in 7:25, and notice
the peculiar expression "the end" in 7:26 and 7:28, exactly as you find
it in Matthew 24:13.

Matthew 24:13---"But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall
be saved."

Hebrews 3:14---"For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the
beginning of our confidence stedfast unto the end."

(Notice how the King James Bible is put together much more accurately
than the human body. The context always matches the context of any verse
which deals with the verse in the context, even if the next context is
2,000 years removed from the one before it. The passages always fit
perfectly without any slip or mistake or hitch or error in any way,
shape or form, as they show up in the Authorized Version, 1611. It is so
well put together that it avoids pitfalls that liberals have not even
yet suspected are there. It not only covers the mistakes that they think
are there, and not only straightens out the problems they have found,
but this AV Bible is so well put together that it straightens out
problems that they aren't even aware of yet! That has always been one of
God's great jokes, to hear these little apostate, backslidden,
Premillennial, funda­mental Greek teachers correcting the King James
Bible, which is in the English, when there are places in the English
that straighten out problems they haven't even run into yet. It has
always amused me to read these "nuggets" by Kenneth Wuest, a Greek
scholar at Moody Bible Institute; he shows how the Greek kind of "helps
the King James" get around some problems which are knotty unless one
goes to "the Greek." You can find the answer in the English AV 1611 to
every problem that Wuest had, and you can get answers, found in the
English, to problems that he hasn't discovered yet. The Bible is very
sound.)

So in Revelation 13:1--2, we have a corollary in Daniel 7, and in Daniel
7, you are plainly told that it is a "king," as well as a "kingdom."
This must be remembered because "the beast" is "a man" and "a king," and
he is the main subject of Revelation 13.

Notice: Revelation 13:1---"a beast," 13:2---"the beast," 13:3---"the
beast," 13:4---"the beast," "the beast," "the beast," 13:14---"the
beast," "the beast," 13:15---"the beast, "the beast," "the beast,"
13:17---"the beast," 13:18---"the beast," 14:9---"the beast,"
14:11---"the beast," 16:2---"the beast," 16:10---"the beast,"
16:13---"the beast," 17:7---"the beast," 17:8---"the beast," "the
beast," 17:11---"the beast," 17:12---"the beast," 17:13---"the beast,"
17:16---"the beast," 17:17---"the beast."

You might as well face it. There are a great deal more references to
"THE BEAST" in four chapters of the New Testament than you have to water
baptism in the entire body of the Pauline Epistles. This "beast" is very
important. (See comments on Revelation 11 on this "beast.")

Revelation 13:1---"And I \[John\] stood upon the sand of the sea
\[Mediterranean\], and saw a beast rise up out of the sea \[This beast
is like a sea monster---Job 41.\], having seven heads and ten horns \[We
know that this beast is closely connected with Satan because in Rev.
12:3 it says, "a great red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns."\],
and upon his horns ten crowns, and upon his heads the name of blasphemy
\[not crowns as in Rev. 12:3\]."

Notice a slight difference. The "beast" in 13:1 has "upon his horns ten
crowns, and upon his heads the name of blasphemy," and the "beast" in
12:3 has "seven crowns upon his heads" and no crowns on the ten horns.
In Revelation 12:3, it is a "red dragon," in Revelation 13:1--2 it is a
"leopard" with the "feet of a bear" and the "mouth of a lion." In
Revelation 12:3 it is "seven heads" with "seven crowns," in Revelation
13:1 it is "seven heads" with "ten crowns." In Revelation 12:3 it is
"ten horns" that are crownless. In Revelation 13:1 each horn has a
crown. So there has been a change! This "great red dragon" has come down
to the earth (He's no longer in heaven.), and he comes up out of the sea
in the Mediterranean area! He comes up representing a ten‑king kingdom
instead of a seven‑king kingdom.

One of the greatest lessons I learned in the early days of my ministry
took place on a cold winter night in a town called Piney Grove, S.C.
(You go east from Augusta, Georgia, toward the South Carolina line, and
around Aiken, almost on the state line between South Carolina and
Georgia, and you'll find "Piney Grove.") About three years after I was
saved (around 1951), I had a revival meeting at this place, in a very
small church. At the time we had the revival, they were meeting in a
tent, and at this particular time, I was staying in different homes each
night. (There was no permanent place for an evangelist as it was a very
small work.) I spent the night at about three different houses in the
period of one week.

One night an old man, who had been giving the congregation a good bit of
trouble (and later left), asked me to stay overnight at his farm house.
I consented. We left the church about 9 o'clock and got to his house
about 9:30. He was a bachelor about 60 or 65 years old. (This was one of
the most unique experiences I've ever had in the ministry. It is an
experience that has stayed with me through the years that I've never
forgotten. It probably impressed me more than anything else I've come in
contact with to ignore what any man said the Bible taught, and, instead,
to read the Bible to see what it said.) I walked into the house with
this man, we sat down at the table, he reached up to the mantle over the
fireplace, and took down a huge Bible (one of these "Hertel Blue Ribbon"
editions) about six inches thick.

He said, "Young man, you're doing a fine job."

I said, "Thank you."

He said, "I appreciate it."

I said, "Thank you."

"But there are just a few things that you need to get straightened out,"
he said.

"Fine! Fine!" I replied. "Let's find out what they are." I was sincere.
I have never in my life refused to sit down with a man, open the Bible
with him, and compare Scripture with Scripture. I have refused to debate
with some of these jugheaded Campbellites who can't read Scripture and
are just trying to get attention because nobody pays any attention to
their ministry unless they get connected with somebody who is getting
some attention. I never knew a lion in my life who took time out to
debate with a skunk, because even if he whipped the skunk he would still
stink for six months. But I would never refuse to sit down with a
Campbellite, Catholic, Jew, or anyone else, at anytime, with an open
Bible, and go through the Scriptures.

So, we opened our Bibles on this table at 10 o'clock at night. We sat at
that table from 10 until 2 in the morning. We went to bed at 2:30 and
got up at 8. Then, we sat at that table from 8 until 12 noon. The two of
us sat at that table with open Bibles from 10 until 2 (four hours), and
from 8 until 12 (four hours)---a total of eight hours. For eight hours I
went through the Scriptures with him, and let me tell you what he
believed. That man has assimilated, completely, by memory, the entire
teaching of Seventh-day Adventism, Latter‑day Saints, Jehovah's
Witnesses, Church of Christ, Christian Science, Unity, the Roman
Catholic Church, British Israelism, and the Holiness groups!

That man turned to more than 400 verses in those eight hours. We "went
at it" about fifty verses an hour---nearly one verse per minute. He knew
where every verse was that taught that you could lose salvation, that
you were saved by being baptized in water, that you could become
eradicated and become sinless after you were saved, that you had to
observe the sabbath and abstain from pork to be saved, that you were
unconscious at death and the soul slept, that there was no literal hell,
that the soul was annihilated in eternity, that the Devil was eventually
restored, that there was no Tribulation or Millennium, that the Second
Coming of Christ was spiritual, that you became consecrated by
meditation, that you could be baptized for people who had just died,
that Jesus Christ was a created God and not "Jehovah" God of the Old
Testament, that you should pray to the Virgin Mary and to the saints,
that the one true church was the "Church of Christ" which you entered by
water baptism, that the first church was the Roman Catholic Church, that
the church was built upon Peter, and that the true Israel was the
English‑speaking people!

That man had assimilated every heresy I had ever heard of, and knew
where the verses were to prove it. I sat down with him for eight hours,
and for eight hours I'd say, "It is written," and he would say, "It is
written again," and I would say, "It is written again," and he would
say, "It is written again." We sat there and turned the pages of the
Bible through 400 verses for eight hours! When I got through with him,
he was shaking and so mad that he was trembling from head to foot. He
was never convinced of a single doctrine, and he never shook me in a
single doctrine. After eight hours in that Book, and then an afternoon
nap, I had time to get my wits together and assimilate things, and then
a terrific thought struck me, and I've never forgotten it. The thought
which struck me was that in eight hours of dealing with that man, I
could not remember a single verse which he quoted that he had not
changed at least one letter in the verse from the King James text! When
I reviewed in my mind what I had been through, it suddenly dawned on me
that every verse the man had quoted he had changed at least a word or a
letter or a phrase from the Authorized Version, even though that was the
text he was using.

I remember one instance in particular, when we came to John 8 and were
talking about the Deity of Christ.

I said, "Jesus Christ is Jehovah. He said, 'Before Abraham was, I am.'"

I remember that old reprobate said, "No, Jesus meant, before Abraham was
that He was."

I said, "But the text says, 'Before Abraham was, I am.'"

"Well, He means I was," he said.

I said, "Well, if He means 'I was,' why didn't He say 'I was'? He said
'I am.'"

He said, "Well, He means 'I was.'"

I said, "Wait a minute! The text said 'Before Abraham was, I am.'"

He said, "But what does that mean?"

I said, "Never mind what it means! What does it say?"

(Now, I know the Lord put that in my mouth, I wasn't smart enough to
think it up!) But from that day to this (I'll be right frank with you),
I have never given two flips about what the Bible "teaches." I have no
more respect for what the Bible means or what it teaches than I do for
anything that Buddha ever dreamed up in an opium nightmare. But, I am
always greatly concerned about what the Book says. I've observed through
the years that a man can make it teach anything that he wants it to
teach. (Rome is the perfect example.) I've also observed that a man can
make it mean anything that he wants it to mean. But brother, you can't
make it say anything but what it says.

So in these passages, we are sticking to what it says. If it says "seven
crowns upon his heads" in one place, with no crowns on the horns, and
says "upon his horns ten crowns" in another place, with no crowns on the
heads, then that's it. It doesn't make any difference what you think it
means, what I think it means, what it teaches, or anything else, because
we are just going by what it says.

The "beast" of Revelation 13:1--2 is not identical with the beast of
Revelation 12, but they are connected. The beast in Revelation 12 is
Satan, and the one in Revelation 13 is Antichrist, who gets his power
from Satan. Notice in Revelation 13:2, "and the dragon gave him his
power, and his seat, and great authority." The "dragon" has a S‑E‑A‑T!
Did you get that? This "dragon" was identified as Satan in Revelation
12, and is said to have a "seat" in Revelation 13:2. We have a reference
to this in Revelation 2:13.

Revelation 2:13---"I know thy works, and where thou dwellest, even where
Satan's seat is: and thou holdest fast my name, and hast not denied my
faith, even in those days wherein Antipas was my faithful martyr, who
was slain among you, where Satan dwelleth."

"Where Satan's seat is." Where he sits. He'll sit in the holy of holies
on the mercy seat in the temple in the Tribulation. But, he has a seat
right now. If the Devil sat in his seat and spoke from his seat---"out
of the chair"---to give infallible doctrine, do you know where he would
be speaking from? He would be speaking "ex‑cathedra," out of the chair,
"out from the seat." Where did you ever hear that before?

Then this "beast" who comes "up out of the sea" is a "man" (Rev. 13:18).
See comments on Revelation 11:7.

The "beast" is represented as a "leopard" who has the "feet of a bear"
and the "mouth of a lion." He is a man; he is connected with ten kings
when he appears; and this ten‑king‑kingdom that he reigns over will
appear in the Mediterranean area. This beast is connected with the
"dragon," and gets all the power that the "dragon" has, because when he
comes up this time, he is the Devil incarnate. When Judas appeared the
first time, Satan merely entered into him to perform a work, but Judas
was "a devil," not a human being. He becomes "the Devil" in the
Tribulation. (He was not the Devil, but he will be the Devil.) Satan
will incarnate himself in this man, and this man will become an unholy
trinity, exactly as the Lord Jesus Christ was an incarnation of the Holy
Trinity. This unholy trinity has three parts in it, exactly as the Holy
Trinity.

Revelation 16:13---"And I saw three unclean spirits like frogs come out
of the mouth of the dragon, and out of the mouth of the beast, and out
of the mouth of the false prophet." (1) Dragon; Satan the father; (2)
Beast; Satan the son; (3) False prophet; Satan the unclean spirit.

Then, this man is a Satanic trinity, incarnated in the flesh, to
represent the Lord Jesus Christ. In the Old Testament, you'll find all
three members of this unholy trinity pictured, or typified, in one
verse---Numbers 22:41. All three of the names in this verse begin with a
"B," the second letter in the alphabet. (Haven't you noticed that no
book in the Bible begins with a "B"? Haven't you noticed that twelve of
them begin with "J"? Haven't you noticed that 85 percent of the words in
the Bible that begin with "B" are bad words, and 85 percent of the words
in the Bible that begin with "J" are good words? What have you been
studying? You'll find that "Judah," "Jesus," "Jerusalem," and "Jehovah"
pretty well fit, and you'll find that "Babylon," "Benjamin," "Balak,"
"Belial," and "Beelzebub" don't.)

Numbers 22:41---"And it came to pass on the morrow, that Balak took
Balaam, and brought him up into the high places of Baal, that thence he
might see the utmost part of the people." Balak, the false king, type of
Antichrist, took Balaam, the false prophet, type of the false prophet in
Revelation 13, to the places of Baal, the dragon, Satan, type of the
false god!

Revelation 13:2---"And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard,
and his feet were as the feet of a bear, and his mouth as the mouth of a
lion: and the dragon gave him his power, and his seat, and great
authority."

Here we have the three beasts of Daniel 7 given in reverse order. Notice
the passage carefully. "The beast which I saw was like unto a leopard;"
that's the main thing to notice. The beast is not a "dragon" or "bear"
or "lion," but he is like a leopard. As a matter of fact, the only thing
different about this beast that is any different from a leopard is that
he has "seven heads (13:1) and ten horns (13:1)." He has a mouth like a
lion and feet like a bear, which a leopard does not have. Aside from
these things, his neck, ears, nose, eyes, tail, chest, back, stomach,
sides, and legs are those of a leopard. This is a monster; this is a
composite monster---three beasts in one. The Hebrew word for "monsters"
or "beasts" (in the plural) is "behemoth." You will find this Hebrew
word in your English text in Job 40:15, where there is another picture
of a composite, multiple animal who is leopard, bear, and lion combined.

Then this monstrosity which shows up is primarily a leopard---a spotted
animal. Here we learn a great truth about Bible types. The Lord Jesus
Christ, when He comes on this earth, is said to be "the Lamb of God,
which taketh away the sin of the world." He is typified by a Passover
Lamb in Exodus 12, and is a "Lamb to the slaughter" in Isaiah 53. So
God's Christ, the Lord Jesus Christ, God's anointed, is like an animal
that is white---not a spot on him (Exod. 12:2--6). But when God wants to
reveal the Antichrist (the Devil's christ, the Devil's anointed, the
Devil's "savior"), then the beast that is picked is a beast that has
spots all over him. "Can the Ethiopian (Ham, Nimrod) change his skin, or
the leopard his spots?" This beast is spotted all over. That isn't all.
He's integrated! A leopard is yellow‑brown, like an Asiatic; he has a
white stomach, like a European; he has black spots all over him, like an
African! This is one composite man, put together to represent all three
races. He is a half‑breed Roman Catholic Syrian Jew. He is a Hamitic Jew
who is over the Gentile church. He is a perfect composite, one‑world,
united, inter-related, integrated, synthesized, completely
representative, total abomination who will have a universal appeal. A
black man can keep up with him because he's got black spots on him; a
white man will take him up because he has a white stomach; Asia will
follow him because he is an Asiatic animal---yellow‑brown.

"Spots," wherever they are found in the Bible, always indicate something
bad; they never indicate anything good. As people are anxious to get rid
of pimples, blackheads, and blemishes, so is the Bible.

Ephesians 5:27---"That he might present it \[the Church\] to himself a
glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but
that it should be holy and without blemish."

Jude 12---"These \[unsaved people\] are spots in your feasts of charity,
when they feast with you, feeding themselves without fear: clouds they
are without water, carried about of winds; trees whose fruit withereth,
without fruit, twice dead, plucked up by the roots."

Jude 23---"And others save with fear, pulling them \[people who have
leprosy from taking the mark of the beast\] out of the fire, hating even
the garment spotted by the flesh."

We find that a spot falls on those who have the mark of the beast, and
this spot is leprosy, connected with a "leopard." If you think this is
far‑fetched, don't forget Leviticus 13 (watch the 13's!), where you'll
find that spot in verse 13, which is leprosy, and it is called a "bright
spot" in verse 26 (2 x 13), and verse 39 (3 x 13). This spot is a plague
that falls on a man and eats into his clothes and his house, as well as
his body. "Can the Ethiopian change his skin (from Ham), or the leopard
his spots?" A leopard is an African animal. The leopard's spots are
black like the Ethiopian's skin. Back in 1600--1800 if they were going
to kill a pirate, they gave him the "black spot." When they mess a man
up, they put him behind the "8 ball," and they "black‑ball" him. This
leopard is marked with a black spot. Check the references; Jeremiah
13:23; 2 Peter 2:13; Deuteronomy 32:5.

Now, the beast is a leopard, lion and bear, and yet, the main part is a
leopard. According to big game hunters, these three animals have a
rather peculiar habit. All three of these animals mark trees by slashing
off a strip of the bark as high as they can reach. This is not just to
sharpen the claws. All three of these animals will go through the jungle
and reach up full length and put their one paw out and slash off a strip
of bark. They will mark a tree. (Trees, of course, in the Bible, are
likened to people. They have families; they have roots---Christ is the
"root of Jesse.")

Psalm 92:12--14---"The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree: he
shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon. Those that be planted in the house
of the Lord shall flourish in the courts of our God. They shall still
bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be fat and flourishing."

Psalm 37:35---"I have seen the wicked in great power, and spreading
himself like a green bay tree."

Mark 8:23--24---"And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out
of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon
him, he asked him if he saw ought. And he looked up, and said, I see men
as trees, walking."

Bears, leopards, and lions mark trees. Hence, we're not surprised to
find the "mark of the beast" in Revelation 13:16. Those who receive "the
mark of the beast" (16:2), receive a plague or a pox or a disease. (They
also go to hell and burn forever, Rev. 14:9--11.)

The "mark of the beast," then, is a black spot. (Somebody said "the mark
of the beast" was not keeping the sabbath, or observing Sunday. But a
Seventh-day Adventist is no more qualified to comment on what "the mark
of the beast" is than a three year old fuzzy wuzzy is able to comment on
Grand Opera.) The passage is "the beast"; "the beast" is a leopard.
Leopards are marked; the mark of a leopard is a black spot. All leopards
are marked with black spots, unless they are solid black, in which case
they are panthers. "Beware of a black cat!" Did you ever notice the
"cat‑holics" and their black clothes! "The mark of the beast," in the
passage, is a black spot. Hence, "styx," the Latin word for black, ends
in "x." The word for "cat" in Latin is "felix," which ends in "x." A
"styx‑felix" is a "black cat." Those who receive his mark write "x," and
they are cursed with a "pox." The rider of the white horse in Revelation
6, the bowman, made the sign of the "crux," and he came peaceably, the
Latin word for peace being "pax." Here in Revelation 13, he speaks with
the voice of God, "vox." You should study words that end in "x." This
white horse rider is a king, "rex," who is a "hoax," and his number is
"six" (Rev. 13:18), the Greek for "six" being "hex" (see The Mark of the
Beast).

Like Baibars, the one‑eyed panther (Zech. 11:17) who polished off St.
Louis in the last dismal crusades in 1257, "the beast" suddenly appeared
in the lines of the verse. "Beware of a black cat!" "They black‑balled
him." "He's behind the eight ball." "They slipped him the black spot."
"X marks the spot." "If you can't sign your name, write 'X'." The word
for leopard in Hebrew in "nimur," the one for Nimrod in Genesis 10:9.
Nimrod comes from Ham; he is an Ethiopian, the son of Cush. "Can the
Ethiopian change his skin, or the leopard his spots?" The Ethiopian has
"black" skin, and the leopard has "black" spots. The beast is a leopard.
Well, how is a leopard marked? If it is the "mark of the beast" that you
are looking for, and the beast is a leopard, then how is he marked? A
leopard is marked with black spots. The black spot on a leopard is
shaped like a Rosetta; that is, like two half‑moons. If the spots are
examined carefully, it will be seen that most of them resemble two huge,
black lips, pressed against the skin like a kiss. Since you know that
Antichrist is Judas Iscariot, did you ever wonder why he betrays the Son
of Man with a kiss? Why do people sign "love and kisses" with "XXX"? The
"X" and the "cat" are inseparable, and so is the color "black."

Study words that end in "X." A "paradox" is a mystery, and the
Antichrist is called "the mystery of iniquity" in 2 Thessalonians 2:7;
he takes over the world peaceably, and the Latin word is "pax"; he is a
king, "rex"; he pretends to have the voice of God, "vox," when he speaks
"ex‑cathedra," out of the seat; he majors on kisses and "sex" (Rev,
2:20--21); he will "tax" the whole world; he will "vex" people, and they
will be beheaded with an "ax" (Rev. 20:4), and buried in a "box." Did
you ever study "Jax" beer? Those who worship the Devil will receive a
"pox," and it is a black "pox," "styx." It comes from a cat, "felix,"
for the leopard is a member of the cat family. A North American cat is a
"lynx," but a Roman cat is a "felix." A man who is half‑man and
half‑cat, as this man is, is a "sphinx." He is typified one time by
Herod, who is called "fox." When you "hex" somebody, you cast an evil
spell on them, as this one does with the number "six." He is actually a
"jinx" or a "hoax." He is connected with "black," and black everywhere
in the Bible is a symbol of night or death. Black never stands for
purity, truth, honor, or bravery in any language in any nation. Black
robes are worn by Catholic priests!' When you examine the word
"Catholic," you find that it is a compound word "cat‑holic," meaning one
who is wholly given to a cat. An "alcoholic" is wholly given to alcohol.
The word "Catholic" doesn't mean universal. That was Plato's use of the
word (I don't care what the priest says), but the dictionary doesn't say
that. "Catholic" means wholly given to a cat, and the basic root meaning
of cat is "mother"! No wonder the Catholic priests and nuns wear black
clothing! No wonder you are warned about black cats. No wonder people
don't like blackheads and black balls. No wonder widows wear black at
funerals. No wonder that the black horse in Revelation 5 is for famine.
No wonder that when there is a "blackout," there is no light visible. No
wonder black and white are opposites, and black tries to become grey
through integration! No wonder they use black smoke when electing popes!
No wonder the black people were given the homeland title of "The Dark
Continent." No wonder the word "Ham" means "burnt black." No wonder that
all of Ham's descendants gravitate to the Equator to be near the sun.
Africa is said to be "the land of Ham." Africa contains Egypt from which
Israel was delivered. Africa, containing Egypt, is a type of the world
in Scripture, and is called "the iron furnace." North Africa is the
location of the Pharaohs! North Africa is the location of the Sphinx!
North Africa is the location of the Latin Church that blossomed later in
Italy---Augustine, Tertullian, Jerome, Eusebius, and Constantine. North
Africa is the location of the type of text found in Vaticanus and
Sinaiticus, the basis for the Revised Standard Version and the American
Standard Version! Both of these manuscripts were found in the
monasteries and dwellings of "black‑robed" Catholics. North Africa was
never chosen by God to be the territory in which a single book of the
Bible was written. North African, Egyptian, or Hesychian type text,
therefore, is Satanic and "Catholic" in origin. You may sum it up by
saying that no Bible or religion which bears these marks of
identification---the "X's," the "blacks," and the Afri­can---is in any
way, shape, or form, directly or indirectly, connected with Bible
Christianity. No matter what the profession of "faith" is, or no matter
how great historical evidence is produced, the Bible has already, by its
own, clear, plain English, defined the group that bears these marks. It
is Satan's faith, Satan's christ, and Satan's church. You have found the
"mark of the beast." It is black; for the beast, my friend, is a
leopard.

Revelation 13:2---"And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard,
and his feet were as the feet of a bear, and his mouth as the mouth of a
lion: and the dragon gave him his power, and his seat, and great
authority."

Let's go back to Daniel 7 and locate this man exactly. In Daniel 7 we
find all three of these beasts mentioned in the reverse order. In Daniel
7:3--7 it is a lion, a bear, a leopard, and a beast. In Revelation 13 it
is a leopard, bear, lion, and beast. In Daniel 7:17, you are told that
the beasts represent "kings" instead of kingdoms. It is true the fourth
beast is called a "kingdom" in Daniel 7:23, but they are said to be
"kings" in verse 17. So if you knew the location of these beasts, you
would have some idea about the Second Coming of Christ and the rise of
Antichrist.

Here we find another serious mistake made in the Scofield Reference
Bible, which, of course, is followed by Dr. DeHaan, E. S. English,
Pember, Larkin, Bullinger, etc. (I'm not saying this to be critical of
good men. I'm saying this to show you how any dumbbell like me, or you,
can get something from the word of God that a scholar can't get, if we
will simply believe the English---which we are about to do. We are going
to read very carefully, and we are going to believe what we read.)

Daniel 7:1--3---"In the first year of Belshazzar king of Babylon Daniel
had a dream and visions of his head upon his bed: then he wrote the
dream, and told the sum of the matters. Daniel spake and said, I saw in
my vision by night, and, behold, the four winds of the heaven strove
upon the great sea. And four great beasts came up from the sea, diverse
one from another."

Daniel 7:17--18---"These great beasts, which are four, are four kings,
which shall arise out of the earth. But the saints of the most High
shall take the kingdom, and possess the kingdom for ever, even for ever
and ever."

Notice that the beasts are terminated by the Second Coming of Christ.
Notice that when the fourth beast is mentioned (vs. 23), the Second
Coming follows imme­diately. In verse 24 and verse 25 the fourth kingdom
is the one mentioned in Revelation 13.

Daniel 7:24---"And the ten horns out of this kingdom are ten kings that
shall arise: and another shall rise after them; and he shall be diverse
from the first, and he shall subdue three kings."

The one that comes up later (vs. 25) is the Antichrist. The uniform
interpretation of the passage is that the four beasts of Daniel 7 match
the four kingdoms of Daniel 2. Notice how these two dreams (Dan. 2, 7)
are made to match by all the fundamental expositors, including the best.
We will see shortly that they don't match.

In Daniel 2 is the vision of Daniel's image, and this image is explained
by Daniel as referring to the kingdoms which shall follow the King of
Babylon. You'll notice in the interpretation, that the "head of gold" is
Nebuchad­nezzar, the king of Babylon (vs. 37­--38). The next kingdom (vs.
39) is the Kingdom of Media‑Persia, followed by the Kingdom of Greece.
The next kingdom (vs. 40) is the Roman Empire, followed by the
Antichrist's ten‑toed, ten‑confederated kingdom (vs. 42--44). Notice
that whenever the number "ten" shows up that the Second Coming of Christ
follows it immediately. You no sooner have ten Gentile toes (as "ten" is
the Gentile number; Noah, being the tenth from Adam, is the father of
the Gentiles, having his Gentile nations separated in Gen. 10, see
commentary on Genesis), than here comes Jesus Christ in Daniel 2:44--45,
at the Second Advent. You no sooner have a ten‑horned beast in Daniel
7:17 than the Lord comes back in 7:18. You no sooner have a ten‑horned
kingdom in 7:24--25, than you have the Second Coming in 7:26--27.
Because of this similarity, these kingdoms have been matched by
Scofield. Scofield says that the lion (of Dan. 7:4) is the King of
Babylon in Daniel 2:37. Then he says that the bear (of Dan. 7:5) is
Media‑Persia, in Daniel 2:39. He then says that the leopard (of Daniel
7:6) is Greece, in Daniel 2:39. He then says that the fourth beast (of
Dan. 7:7) is Rome, in Daniel 2:40, and that the little horn that comes
up in Daniel 7:8, 20, and 25 matches the toes in Daniel 2:41--42. Now,
this looks really good; it looks really logical. There is just one thing
that throws the whole system of interpretation clean out the window.

Daniel 7:1, 3, 17---"In the first year of Belshazzar \[who came after
Nebuchadnezzar\] king of Babylon Daniel had a dream and visions of his
head upon his bed: then he wrote the dream, and told the sum of the
matters." "And four great beasts came up from the sea, diverse one from
another." "These great beasts, which are four, are four kings, which
shall arise out of the earth."

Then, the first one, the lion, has to come after Belshazzar is through,
and Belshazzar is the last King of Babylon. Therefore, the lion of
Daniel 7:4 cannot match the head on Daniel's image. The four beasts that
come up in Daniel 7 are said to be four kingdoms or kings that come up
after Babylon is all through. When Daniel has the dream, it is in "the
first year of Belshazzar, king of Babylon," but Belshazzar is murdered
in Daniel 5:29--30, and the Media‑Persians take over the kingdom.
Therefore, the lion of Daniel 7:4 has to match Media‑Persia; the bear of
Daniel 7:5 has to match Greece; the leopard of Daniel 7:6 has to match
Rome; the fourth beast of Daniel 7:7 has to match Antichrist's kingdom;
and here comes the Antichrist in Daniel 7:8. Now, this will match! Here,
the Scofield notes are definitely off by the plain teaching of the first
verse in the chapter. Compare Daniel 7:1 with Daniel 7:17, and notice
that those four beasts cannot come up until after Belshazzar, the King
of Babylon, who is the last king that Babylon has.

Now, there is something else that has escaped the notice of all these
gentlemen, evidently. It has been presumed throughout, that any
information dealing with events in the Church Age is unknown. It has
been presumed that all that Daniel gives us is a reference to the
kingdoms from Nebuchadnezzar to the first coming of Christ. So Daniel
takes the revelation up to Rome (which is the legs on the image and the
fourth beast in Dan. 7:7), and then there is this gap which is unknown
between the fourth beast of Daniel 7 and the legs of Daniel 2, and the
little horn of Daniel 7:8 (which would match the ten toes of Dan. 2).
But this gap could be fulfilled by Daniel 7.

Daniel 7:2---"Daniel spake and said, I saw in my vision by night, and,
behold, the four winds of the heaven strove upon the great sea."

Notice the expression "by night."

Daniel 7:13---"I saw in the night visions, and, behold, one like the Son
of man came with the clouds of heaven, and came to the Ancient of days,
and they brought him near before him."

Notice the expression "night visions." (What follows in Dan. 7:14 is the
Millennium.) What makes this passage so interesting, is that the Holy
Spirit may have dumped the United States, Russia, and Great Britain
right into your lap. (I'm not saying that He did, but any man who knows
the Bible knows perfectly well that the dispensation which is called
"night time" is not the dispensation from Nebuchadnezzar to the first
coming of Christ; it is the dispensation from Pentecost to the Second
Advent. It is the Church Age!) Now watch the Scriptures, and see how
true this thing is!

1 Thessalonians 5:1--7---"But of the times and the seasons, brethren, ye
have no need that I write unto you. For yourselves know perfectly that
the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief IN THE NIGHT. For when they
shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them,
as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape. But ye,
brethren, are not in DARKNESS, that that day should overtake you as a
thief. Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day: we
are not OF THE NIGHT, nor of darkness. Therefore let us not sleep, as do
others; but let us watch and be sober. For they that sleep sleep IN THE
NIGHT; and they that be drunken are drunken IN THE NIGHT."

Romans 13:12---"THE NIGHT IS FAR SPENT, the day is at hand: let us
therefore cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armour
of light."

Notice that this dispensation is called "night that is far spent."

John 9:5---"As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world."

John 12:35--36---"Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the
light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon
you: for he that walketh in DARKNESS knoweth not whither he goeth. While
ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of
light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from
them."

When Jesus Christ was on this earth, He said, "I am the light of the
world." When He went out of the world, He said to the Christian, "Ye are
the light of the world." When Jesus Christ comes back, "then shall the
righteous shine forth as the s‑u‑n in the kingdom of their Father. Who
hath ears to hear, let him hear" (Matt. 13:43). When Jesus Christ comes
back, He is called the "S‑U‑N" in Malachi 4:2. This dispensation, the
Church Age, is called night time any­where in the word of God.

Isaiah 21:11--12---"Watchman, what of THE NIGHT? Watchman, what of THE
NIGHT? The watchman said, The morning cometh, and also THE NIGHT: if ye
will enquire, enquire ye: return, come."

Jesus Christ is called the "bright and morning star."

The Christian is told in this present night time to "take heed, as unto
a light that shineth in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day
star arise in your hearts" (2 Pet. 1:19).

Mark 13:35--37---"Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of
the house cometh, at even \[6 P.M.‑9 P.M.\], or at midnight \[9
P.M.--12\], or at the cockcrowing \[12--3 A.M.\], or in the morning \[3
A.M.--6 A.M.\]: Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping. And what I
say unto you I say unto all, Watch."

So when Daniel says, "I saw in my vision by night," he might be
referring to a dispensation beyond the first coming. If he is, then
Daniel 7, with these Gentile kingdoms in it, gives the course of the gap
that is between Daniel 2 and the Book of Revelation. By that I mean that
the empires of this world are given as follows:

1. Nebuchadnezzar, King of Babylon.

2. Darius, King of Media‑Persia.

3. Alexander the Great, Greece.

4\. Caesar, Rome.

5. Great Britain (Dan. 7:4).

6\. Russia (Dan. 7:5).

7. United States (Dan. 7:6).

8. Mystery Babylon---The Roman Catholic Church with Satan at the head of
it, the Devil incarnate.

Revelation 17:11---"And the beast that was, and is not, even he is the
eighth, and is of the seven \[No. 4\], and goeth into perdition."

Now, that's quite a mouthful to chew! But don't forget that we are now
dealing with a ten‑horned, ten‑crown situation. In Revelation 13, there
are ten kings---not just seven.

The seven heads on the dragon were:

1. Nimrod, King of Babel.

2. Pharaoh, King of Egypt.

3. Sennacherib, King of Assyria.

4. Nebuchadnezzar, King of Babylon.

5. Cyrus, King of Persia.

6. Alexander, King of Greece.

7. Caesar, King of Rome.

We learned in Revelation 12 that these empires entered into a mystery
form, a religious form---the Holy Roman Empire. Out of this comes the
other three kingdoms to make ten horns and ten kings. They are:

1. Great Britain, a lion.

2\. Russia, a bear.

3. United States, a leopard, an integrated melting‑pot of all nations.

Going back now to the Devil in Revelation 12, he only has seven heads
with seven kings, and he is the eighth. Yet, as the eighth king in
Revelation 17, he rules with ten kings who have ten crowns.

Revelation 17:12---"And the ten horns which thou sawest are ten kings,
which have received no kingdom as yet; but receive power as kings one
hour with the beast."

The first beast in Daniel 7 is a lion. On Daniel's image (Dan. 2), he
does not match the head, he matches the shoulders and
arms---Media‑Persia. As Media‑Persia, he is a type of England (Ezra
1:1--2). Since the Jews were commanded by the king of Media‑Persia to go
back to Palestine and re-inhabit it the first time under Persian reign,
so they were ordered by the Balfour Declaration, under England, in 1918,
to go back and re-inhabit it the second time. Persia is a type of
England, the lion.

The second beast in Daniel 7, the bear, will match the "belly and loins"
on the image in Daniel 2. This beast would represent Greece. Greece, a
bear, is a type of Russia. Since Greece was Greek‑Orthodox, so was
Russia until the revolution in 1918; Russia comes from Greece, and
Russia's alphabet and language come from Greece.

The third beast in Daniel 7, a leopard, will match the legs made out of
iron on the image in Daniel 2. This beast would represent Rome. This
Roman beast (leopard) would be a type of the United States. Notice that
this leopard has four wings on it. It only has two wings now---Alaska
and Hawaii. It will get the other two later! (Notice the "13's" you
studied in connection with the leopard and the "black" and the
integration, etc. The third beast, the leopard, is a type of the United
States.)

The fourth beast, in Daniel 7, would represent the Antichrist's ten‑king
kingdom. Notice the ten horns at the end of Daniel 7:7. The Antichrist
would come from this group, and take it over and run the earth with the
ten kings under him, exactly as you find him in Revelation 13:2--3.

Daniel 7:24--25---"And the ten horns out of this kingdom are ten kings
that shall arise: and another shall rise after them; and he shall be
diverse from the first, and he shall subdue three kings. And he shall
speak great words against the most High, and shall wear out the saints
of the most High, and think to change times and laws: and they shall be
given into his hand until a time \[1\] and times \[2\] and the dividing
of time \[31/2\]."

Where did you ever read that before? In Revelation 12 you read where "a
time, and times, and half a time" was the length of time Israel was fed
in the wilderness "from the face of the serpent." Three and a half
years!

Daniel 7:26--28---"But the judgment shall sit, and they shall take away
his dominion, to consume and to destroy it unto the end. And the kingdom
and dominion, and the greatness of the kingdom under the whole heaven,
shall be given to the people of the saints of the most High, whose
kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and all domin­ions shall serve and
obey him. Hitherto is the end \[as in Matt. 24; Heb. 3, 6, etc.\] of the
matter. As for me Daniel, my cogitations much troubled me, and my
countenance changed in me: but I kept the matter in my heart."

Keeping in mind that the lion represents England, the bear represents
Russia, and the leopard represents the United States, go back to
Revelation 13:2, and you can read future history the way Huntley and
Brinkley, etc. will never be able to read it.

Revelation 13:2---"And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard
\[The final world‑kingdom is like the United States. It's spotted; it's
integrated. There's no pure blood left in it. It's mixed. It's an
Asiatic‑African‑Euro­pean mongrel melting pot, with the white man on the
bottom, as the leopard has a white belly. It is Asiatic all over,
spotted with African blood.\], and his feet were as the feet of a bear
\[This thing moves like a bear, and Russia has the greatest infantry in
the world. The only part of it that is like Russia is its feet, so it
moves like Russia. It's like the United States, but it operates like
Communist Russia.\], and his mouth as the mouth of a lion \[He speaks,
Rev. 13:5--6. He is an English‑speaking beast. His mouth is the only
part of him that is like England. This means that the universal language
at the Second Coming of Christ is going to be English. To get the
Protestants into the Roman Catholic Church, Papa Paul will put the mass
into English. That is, 'the gates of hell' are going to use his
church!\]: and the dragon gave him his power, and his seat, and great
authority." (See commentary on Matthew, Matt. 16:16--18.)

This last form of the ten‑federated kingdom under the Antichrist is
going to take over the whole world (Dan. 7:21; Rev. 11:7; Dan.
11:31--34; Rev. 13:7­--8).

The man of Revelation 13:18 is a beast; he's "like unto a leopard." He
takes over the earth with a ten‑feder­ated kingdom under the Roman
Catholic Church; he takes it over under a Roman legal system like the
old Latin system; he takes it over with a social system like the United
States, which operates politically like Communism and it has English as
the universal language. He takes over the earth for three and one-half
years with ten other kings, and they make war against Jesus Christ; they
persecute Israel and kill them down to 144,000; they cut off their
heads; they immolate them as human sacrifices to the Devil in the
temple.

At this time, Satan will come down to this earth as a man, go into the
temple at Jerusalem, sit on the "mercy seat" with one hand on one cherub
and the other hand on the other cherub, and "as God sitteth in the
temple of God, shewing himself that he is God" (2 Thess. 2).

13:3 "And I saw one of his heads as it were wounded to death; and his
deadly wound was healed: and all the world wondered after the beast."

One of the heads of the beast is cut off and wounded. It is either
Nimrod (King of Babel), Pharaoh (King of Egypt), Sennacherib (King of
Assyria), Nebuchadnezzar (King of Babylon), Cyrus (King of Persia),
Alexander (King of Greece), or Caesar (King of Rome). One of these heads
is "wounded to death" and the "deadly wound was healed." One of those
nations that was cut off is going to revive and take over.

13:4 "And they worshipped the dragon which gave power unto the beast:
and they worshipped the beast, saying, Who is like unto the beast? who
is able to make war with him?"

The Greek word for worshipped is "pros­kuneo," and it is the word for
"kiss." They "kissed" him---exactly as it was called to your attention
in Revelation 9:20. They "kiss" the Devil. (How wild can you get? You
can get as wild as an AV 1611, and that's pretty wild!) In this worship
of the Devil, they "kiss" him. XXX!!

Romans 11:4---"But what saith the answer of God unto him? I have
reserved to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to
the image of Baal."

Back in the Old Testament, the men who did not worship Baal, didn't kiss
him.

1 Kings 19:18 (6+6+6)---"Yet I have left me seven thousand in Israel,
all the knees which have not bowed unto Baal, and every mouth which hath
not kissed him."

Luke 22:48---"But Jesus said unto him, Judas, betrayest thou the Son of
man with a kiss?"

"XXX under the mistletoe at X-mas."

13:5 "And there was given unto him a mouth speaking great things and
blasphemies; and power was given unto him to continue forty and two
months.

6 And he opened his mouth in blasphemy against God, to blaspheme his
name, and his tabernacle, and them that dwell in heaven.

7 And it was given unto him to make war with the saints, and to overcome
them: and power was given him over all kindreds, and tongues, and
nations.

8 And all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him, whose names are
not written in the book of life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of
the world.

9 If any man have an ear, let him hear.

10 He that leadeth into captivity shall go into captivity: he that
killeth with the sword must be killed with the sword. Here is the
patience and the faith of the saints.

11 And I beheld another beast coming up out of the earth; and he had two
horns like a lamb, and he spake as a dragon."

This man is a false prophet. As Judas is resurrected as the Antichrist,
this man is a resurrected false prophet---perhaps Baalam. He's a "wolf"
in John 10, typified by Deuteronomy 18:21--22.

13:12 "And he exerciseth all the power of the first beast before him,
and causeth the earth and them which dwell therein to worship the first
beast, whose deadly wound was healed.

13 And he doeth great wonders, so that he maketh fire come down from
heaven on the earth in the sight of men,

14 And deceiveth them that dwell on the earth by the means of those
miracles which he had power to do in the sight of the beast; saying to
them that dwell on the earth, that they should make an image to the
beast, which had the wound by a sword, and did live."

The Devil has power to do miracles (Rev. 16:13--14). The word in Greek
for "sword" is a small sword like the one with which Ehud killed Eglon,
in Judges 3:21 (see Rev. 2:16).

This Antichrist is an idolater who uses images. So as an imitation of
Jesus Christ (a false messiah or prophet), he's called the "idol
shepherd" in Zechariah 11:17. Notice that the context is Judas Iscariot,
Zechariah 11:13. Look out for the "13's"!

13:15 "And he had power to give life unto the image of the beast, that
the image of the beast should both speak, and cause that as many as
would not worship the image of the beast should be killed."

Here the Devil gets permission from God to "give life unto the image of
the beast," proving that people who say that the Devil can't reproduce
are wrong. He can get permission from God to counterfeit life, and he
will.

13:16 "And he causeth all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and
bond \[Free and bond? Man is not going to solve the integration problem.
They are "free" and "bond" in the Tribulation!\], to receive a mark in
their right hand, or in their foreheads:

17 And that no man might buy or sell, save he that had the mark, or the
name of the beast, or the number of his name."

Then this man sets up an Ecumenical Council over the United Nations,
takes over the world at Rome, and fixes you so that you can't get milk,
bread, and butter for your babies unless you take a black mark right
smack between the eyes or in the palm of your hand, as when you kiss
somebody on the forehead or blow a kiss from the palm of your hand.
You'll take it, or you can't buy! The government will require you to
take a Social Security number now to prepare you for what they are going
to do. It is going to be a Federal offence not to take the mark of the
beast. You unsaved religious leaders who are messing around and putting
off Jesus Christ, God has got a monkey wrench that is going to fit your
nut! God Almighty is going to put you in a place where it is going to be
a Federal offence for you not to worship the Devil! You'll worship him!
You won't go to jail! If you are such a sorry, God‑forsaken rascal right
now that you are ashamed to confess Jesus Christ, don't kid yourself
into thinking that you've got the guts to resist the Federal Government
when they tell you to worship the Devil. You'll go along. You're
"chicken" right now, or you would have been saved already.

13:18 "Here is wisdom. \[Not hidden, Apocalyptic, figurative,
symbolical, etc.\] Let him that hath understand­ing count the number of
the beast: for it is the number of a man \[not "man," but the number of
"a" man\]; and his number is Six hundred three score and six."

SIX! SIX! SIX! (As in John 6:66) HEX! HEX! HEX! We have his name---it's
Judas Iscariot; we have his mark---it's a "black" spot; we have his
sign---it's a kiss; we have his religion---he's a Roman Catholic (a
Cat‑holic, a Sphinx, a "Cat‑man," wholly given to a "black Cat"); we
have his nationality---he's Hamitic Syrian‑Jew; we have his power---he
gets it from the Devil; we have his number---666. You'll find the number
given two other places in the Scripture.

Ezra 2:13---"The children of Adonikam, Six hun­dred sixty and six."

The word "Adonikam" means "lord of the rebel­lion."

2 Chronicles 9:13---"Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one
year was six hundred and threescore and six talents of gold."

We are told to "count the number." The "christ" of Revelation 13:18 is
clearly connected with two numbers in Scripture; the number of man---6,
and the number of Satan---13.

For a moment, the Greek and Hebrew can bear some study. Every Hebrew or
Greek letter has a numerical value. The man we are looking for is "666,"
be he in English, Greek, or Hebrew.

An endless excursion into the possible numerical combinations of these
numbers is unnecessary. Commenta­tors from Alpha to Omega have tried to
prove that since an innumerable number of combinations are possible,
which can be made to prove anything, therefore, there is no real meaning
to Revelation 13:18, that it is only a figurative statement. But this
will not do at all, for in the previous study this false "christ" has
been narrowed down and fenced up and hemmed in and boxed out, until we
no longer have to go into a maze of numerology to find our man. We
already know so much about this man, from the Scripture given us, that
it is frightening, and we know in which direction to look to locate the
man. We shall look where the people looked, when they looked at Jesus
Christ and then at Pilate and said, "We have no king but Caesar." Caesar
shouldn't be too hard to find.

Latin, in Greek, is Lateinos: 30 + 1 + 300 + 5 + 10 + 50 + 70 + 200 =
666.

Teitan (the English word is "Titan," one of the old poetic names for the
Sun God, reverenced on Sun‑day, and whose birthday is December 25th),
is: 300 + 5 + 10 + 300 + 1 + 50 = 666.

To a Jew, the great paranoid Nero was known only as "Neron Kesar. " His
numerology is: 50 + 200 + 6 + 50 + 100 + 60 + 200 = 666.

Romith, the word for Rome in ancient Hebrew will come out: 200 + 6 +
40 + 10 + 10 + 400 = 666.

Nimrod, the son of Kish, in Hebrew will add to 666.

Satan, in Chaldean, will add to 666.

VV IL DUCE (Mussolini) will add to 666.

But the best is for the last. In VV IL DUCE we observe not only Greek
and Hebrew numerology, but Latin numerology. In Latin the V stands for
5; the L stands for 50; the C stands for 100; the D for 500. The "U" in
DUCE, of course, is written as another "V," for Roman letters use the
"V" for "U" as in PVBLIC LIBRARY. Thus, VV IL DUCE, "Long live the
Duce," is a perfect 666.

Notice the final one, "VICARIVS FILII DEI." This inscription was borne
faithfully on the forehead of many popes down through the centuries.
Then, late in the nineteenth century, the College of Cardinals decided
that they should get it off the popes' heads as quickly as possible
because there was an ominous rumbling of Premillennialism in the
background. Some fanatical men named Moody, Spurgeon, and Cronin had
gotten together with the "brethren," and some more things. The Cardinals
said, "Get that inscrip­tion off his head." But the popes always used to
faithfully wear (before they turned into a bunch of hypocrites) the
inscription "VICARIVS FILII DEI." It is Latin. It has a 500, and a 100,
and two 5's, and a 50, and six 1's in it---V=5, D=500, C=100, L=50, I=1.
The E, A, R, S, and F are not Roman numerals (FEARS!). Every letter that
is a numeral adds up to exactly 666. Strike out the non­-numerals, and
all that is left is 666. Now, how long are you going to face these
multiplied coincidences and go on shutting your eyes and saying, "Well,
I don't believe in criticizing folks' religion"? VICARIVS FILII DEI
means "Faithful Vicar of God," or "Vicarious (Substitute) Son of God,"
or "Shepherd Son of God." (See details in The Mark of the Beast.)

The man in Revelation 13 is a Satanic trinity. As Satan the father, he
is Lucifer (the "anointed cherub" who covered the throne), who is fallen
and will become incarnate in a "pope" at Rome. When the dragon comes
down to this earth, this man has been the "pope" at Rome for three and
one-half years. Upon his incarnation at Rome, he becomes the Devil
incarnate, and the spirit of Judas Iscariot comes up from the bottomless
pit and enters into this man. His soul, as such (in the man), is Satan;
his spirit, as such, is Judas Iscariot; his body is the body of a Roman
Catholic "Holy Father," a black‑robed Baal worshipper from North Africa
in the Phoenician line of demoniac religions.

(If that seems "prejudiced," bigoted, or "hate litera­ture" to you, I
know one thing about you without ever meeting you. You are one of the
most Bible‑rejecting sinners who ever walked on the face of this earth.
And this earth has had a lot of footsteps go across it, friend. You may
have an I.Q. of 150, be president of the First National Bank, and be a
vestryman in your church; but if you don't understand and see that last
statement, you are undoubt­edly a Bible rejector of long lineage. "If any
man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.")

If there should be any doubt in your mind about what I've said or why I
have said it, at this point we will skip from Revelation 13 to
Revelation 17, so that you will have fixed in your mind, once and for
all, the connection that the Kennedy family, the popes, Claire Booth
Luce, Life, Look, Time, Newsweek, TV, etc. have with your soul in the
U.S.A. for the next ten years. Revelation 17 shows you how the coming
years all revolve around one thing---the Roman Catholic Church. This is
going to affect your life, your family, and your job in the next twenty
years.

I realize that if you are a Roman Catholic, or your friends are, that
you would never go along with this. Yet, it is a very strange thing.
This great religious pervert, who professes to believe the word of God
and professes to be the guardian of the Scripture, appeals to Scripture
constant­ly for its points and then refuses to believe the Scriptures
mean what they say in regards to Rome herself. For example: a pamphlet
called Revelation, A Divine Message of Hope, No. 51, by the Knights of
Columbus, which is published by the Supreme Council of the Knights of
Columbus, Religious Information Bureau for the Roman Catholic Church,
St. Louis 8, Missouri, says on page 13:

"One aspect of the Apocalypse that deserves special mention because it
figures so prominently, is the image of Babylon (Rev. 17). Babylon, in
the Book of Revelation, stands for the pagan Roman Empire, just as
Babylon in 1 Peter 5:13 doubtless means Rome."

Now, notice that the Knights of Columbus identify Babylon as Rome. But,
they are very careful to tell you that it applies only to "pagan Rome!"
This gives you a clue as to what the popes are going to do to the word
of God. They are going to say that even though the word of God has
called Rome a spiritual, religious, pagan whore, that since the Bible
was written, things have changed! Therefore, the Bible is no longer able
to accurately tell you anything about Rome. That is, they are going to
tell you that Revelation 17--18 applies to a Rome that is now gone. The
proof for this is not derived from the Bible; the proof of this private
interpretation is gotten from the traditional legends of Eusebius,
Papias, and Constantine; they do not get it from the word of God. The
Knights of Columbus continue with:

"First is the picture of the great harlot, Babylon seated upon the beast
(Rev. 17:1--6). The harlot is the Goddess of Rome (p. 38)."

Now, notice how very carefully the Holy Spirit contradicts this private
interpretation by the Knights of Columbus, and notice how accurately the
Holy Spirit always corrects these Bible perverts by simply over‑riding
their interpretation. In Revelation 17:18, the Holy Spirit says (in any
Bible) that "the woman which thou sawest is that great city, which
reigneth over the kings of the earth." The Knights of Columbus said that
the woman was "the Goddess of Rome!"

Tyndale's Bible in 1525 says:

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city which reigneth over
the kings of the earth."

The Great Bible of 1539 says:

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city which reigns over
the kings of the earth."

The Geneva Bible of 1560 says:

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city which reigneth over
the kings of the earth."

The Bishop's Bible of 1602 says:

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city which reigneth over
the kings of the earth."

The ASV and the RV say:

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city which reigneth over
the kings of the earth."

The RSV, 1946 and 1952, says:

"And the woman that you saw is the great city which has dominion over
the kings of the earth."

The King James, 1611, AV says:

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city, which reigneth over
the kings of the earth."

The Douay‑Rheims of 1582, which is the Roman Catholic bible that they
profess to believe (!), as they pervert it, says:

"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city which hath kingdom
over the kings of the earth."

This means that the Knights of Columbus have deliberately attempted to
deceive their readers; they have rejected the word of God, and inserted
a private interpreta­tion that has nothing to do with Scripture. (If you
want private interpretation, the greatest private interpreter on the
face of this earth is Rome!) Now, quoting from pamphlet 51, page 38, of
the Knights of Columbus, and not taking anything out of context, here is
the entire passage.

"First is the picture of the great harlot Babylon seated upon the beast.
The harlot is the Goddess of Rome. She is seated upon waters, the symbol
of evil."

Revelation 17:15---"And he saith unto me, The waters which thou sawest,
where the whore sitteth, are peoples, and multitudes, and nations, and
tongues."

Now, do you see why there are so many references to Catholicism in this
commentary? (The material I'm reading from was published from 1955--60.
I'm not reading anything "archaic.") The first rule of true Bible
learning is that if the Roman Catholic Church ever says anything
contrary to the AV 1611, you can put it in the garbage can without a
second thought.

Revelation 17:15---"And he saith unto me, The waters which thou sawest,
where the whore sitteth, are peoples, and multitudes, and nations, and
tongues."

This is the reading from the official Catholic bible, adopted at the
Council of Trent (1546). Here we have a dandy picture of the kind of
"Bible teaching" that you get from the Roman Catholic Church. They use
the Bible, and quote it to prove points, but they don't even believe
their own Bible that they are using! There isn't a Catholic pope who
ever lived that believed one book of the Bible, as it was actually
written. If he happened to believe some of it, as it was found, it was
because the traditional predecessors in his line told him to believe it!

Now "the waters" are not evil; they are "multitudes," "nations," and
"peoples." "The woman" is not the Goddess of Rome; she is "a city." That
is, if a Roman Catholic Bible is reliable!

Continuing on page 38 in the Knights of Columbus pamphlet, we read:

"The seven heads of the beast (Rev. 17:9) are seven hills on which the
woman is seated, a certain reference to Rome, the City of Seven Hills.
Here we see why John characterized the power of evil as Babylon, just as
Babylon represented to the Old Testament Jew all that was wicked, and
symbolized persecution, so does Rome in John's time."

Do you see what they did that time? They made the Book of Revelation
apply only to pagan Rome in the time of John the Apostle. The Roman
Catholic Church has pronounced the entire Book of Revelation as being in
the past, and applying to nothing except "pagan Rome" in the time of the
Apostles! Yet all of Revelation 21--22 is dealing with eternity;
Revelation 20 is dealing with the future one thousand year reign of
Christ and the Great White Throne Judgment, and every orthodox Catholic
who ever lived believes in the "Last Judgment" of Revelation 20.
Revelation 13 deals with the Antichrist, who is yet to show up. They've
done just what I told you they would do in Chapter 1 of this book. They
made the date of the writing of the Book of Revelation under the reign
of Nero, A.D. 68, in order to take the whole book and put it into the
past so that it will have no application to Papal Rome! To stop this
criminal excursion, the Holy Spirit pulls a trick in Revelation 17 to
upset the apple cart---as He usually does.

Revelation 17:6---"And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the
saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus: and when I saw her,
I wondered with great admiration."

Notice that when John sees this "woman" he is shocked and upset, and
wonders what she has been doing. If this "woman" were "pagan Rome," John
wouldn't have been the least bit upset, because John himself was a
prisoner on the Isle of Patmos in A.D. 90, due to Roman persecution! Why
would John "wonder" about pagan Rome killing Christians? They were
trying to kill him. The Holy Spirit has injected something here that
messes up the whole system of private interpretation, taught by the
popes to get rid of their identity. In the Roman Catholic bible, you
find the following:

Revelation 17:6---"And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the
saints, and of the blood of the martyrs of Jesus: and I marvelled when I
had seen her with great admiration."

The RSV says:

Revelation 17:6---"And I saw the woman, drunk with the blood of the
saints, and the blood of the martyrs of Jesus. When I saw her I
marvelled greatly."

If this is "pagan Rome," what is John marvelling about? Is he just
stupid? Rome had him on the Isle of Patmos, and he was being persecuted
"for the word of God, and for the testimony of Jesus Christ." If John
was a martyr and a saint of Jesus Christ, and Rome was after his blood,
why would he marvel at this "woman," pagan Rome, being after his blood?
In addition to that, the Lord Jesus Christ plainly told John about all
these things before they ever took place.

John 16:1--3---"These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not
be offended. They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time
cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.
And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the
Father, nor me."

Therefore, the whole subjective, private interpreta­tion of Rome,
regarding Revelation 17, turns out to be nothing but a wild,
cock‑and‑bull story that only a depraved idiot could invent. In the
place where this "woman" appears, you are beyond the Church Age, and the
Church is not even present. Yet this "woman" is said to be "drunken with
the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus."
But when this happens, the Church has left (Rev. 4:1--3). This couldn't
be pagan Rome, because the Church was on the earth at the time of John
the Apostle. There hasn't been one reference to the Church since it
disappeared in Revelation 4!

Again, we have come upon a basic problem involved in Bible
interpretation which we've talked about before. Whenever you try to
figure out what a passage means or teaches and ignore what it says, you
will make it teach or mean what you want it to teach or mean, and you
will reject what God told you. This has been the standard practice of
the popes for nearly 1,600 years.

Now, going back to Revelation 17:1. You know what the Catholic Church
does with the passage; now watch what the passage does with the Catholic
Church.

Revelation 17:1---"And there came one of the seven angels which had the
seven vials, and talked with me, saying unto me, Come hither; I will
shew unto thee the judgment of the GREAT WHORE that sitteth upon many
waters."

Compare Scripture with Scripture, and watch it step all over the Knights
of Columbus.

Revelation 17:15---"And he saith unto me, The waters which thou sawest,
where the whore sitteth, are peoples, and multitudes, and nations, and
tongues."

Isn't it wonderful how the Bible interprets itself? In Revelation 17:1
the "waters" are "peoples," "multitudes," "nations," and "tongues." Do
you know why they are? Because the Bible says they are. It doesn't teach
anything. It says it! Do you know what it means? It means just what it
says!

Revelation 17:2---"With whom the kings of the earth have committed
fornication, and the inhabitants of the earth have been made drunk with
the wine of her fornication."

You can run the passage right back to Revelation 2, and there you find:

Revelation 2:20, 22---"Notwithstanding I have a few things against thee,
because thou sufferest that woman Jezebel, which calleth herself a
prophetess, to teach and to seduce my servants to commit fornication,
and to eat things sacrificed unto idols." "Behold, I will cast her into
a bed, and them that commit adultery with her into great tribulation,
except they repent of their deeds."

Jezebel was the pagan queen who married Ahab in the Old Testament. See
comments on Chapter 11. It was found to be Baal‑worship, which called
the black‑robed priests "father," which worshipped Mary as the "queen of
heaven" (called Isis, Osiris, etc.), and observed December 25th, the
birthday of Baal, the Sun god, as the official birthday of the Lord
Jesus Christ. Jezebel is pictured as a religious prophetess who
incorporates fornication into the church as a part of the worship
service.

Revelation 18:3---"For all nations have drunk of the wine of the wrath
of her fornication, and the kings of the earth have committed
fornication with her, and the merchants of the earth are waxed rich
through the abundance of her delicacies."

Notice, there is no reference whatsoever to "pagan Rome." The earth did
not "wax rich" by the abundance of pagan Rome's "delicacies."

Revelation 17:3---"So he carried me away in the spirit into the
wilderness: and I saw a woman sit upon a scarlet coloured beast, full of
names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns."

There is no doubt about the "woman." Look at Revelation 17:18.

Revelation 17:18---"And the woman which thou sawest is that great city,
which reigneth over the kings of the earth."

Now, that's very clear. It is not the "Goddess" found in the ridiculous
pamphlet published by the Knights of Columbus. It is "that great city,
which reigneth over the kings of the earth." It is not the great city
that had an army of occupation in the city of Jerusalem at the time of
John the Apostle (A.D. 90), and reigned over the occupied countries.
This city, here, reigns "over the kings of the earth."

"Having seven heads and ten horns." We are not going to interpret, we
are just going to read.

Revelation 17:12---"And the ten horns which thou sawest are ten kings,
which have received no kingdom as yet; but receive power as kings one
hour with the beast."

It couldn't be pagan Rome because pagan Rome didn't have "ten kings,
which have received no kingdom as yet; but received power as kings one
hour with the beast." That isn't all!

Revelation 17:13--14---"These have one mind, and shall give their power
and strength unto the beast. These shall make war with the Lamb, and the
Lamb shall overcome them: for he is Lord of lords, and King of kings:
and they that are with him are called, and chosen, and faithful."
(Notice the Lamb shall overcome them, not the Church or the Christians;
He is "Lord of lords, and King of kings," Rev. 19:16, 11:15.)

The contest in Revelation 17 is between "ten horns" which are "ten
kings" who fight against the "Lamb," the "King of kings," who comes back
at the Second Advent and takes the earthly, literal, visible, physical
kingdoms from these kings. Now watch the "Knights of Columbus" get
around this thing with the most preposterous double­talk you ever heard
in all your life. From the Knights of Columbus' pamphlet Revelation, A
Divine Message of Hope, page 39, we read:

"The ten kings in Revelation 17:12, given as an explanation of the ten
horns of the beast, probably refer to the other kings of the earth, who
in John's time shared in the power of Rome, though only partly for one
hour. They too make up the world that is opposed to Christ, and assist
in the persecution of the church. But, eventually, they and the beast
will turn against Rome and destroy her. John not only predicts the
destruction of the Roman Empire, by picturing the beast which has been
previously described, he shows that he does not see the power of
Antichrist simply identified with Rome. It is a power that continues,
and will outlive Rome."

Now, notice how carefully the "Knights of Colum­bus" have even garbled
their own interpretation of the passage. They have been telling you for
thirty-eight pages that only pagan Rome is referred to in Revelation 17.
Then, they suddenly decide that it is not really pagan Rome, but just
Rome. But when they say "just Rome," they realize that this is they! So
they take it and twist it and claim that it wasn't really a reference to
Rome at all, but a "power" that continues which will outlive Rome! Then
they are really in trouble, because Rome outlives pagan Rome by becoming
papal Rome! The "pagan Roman Empire" becomes the "Holy Roman Empire,"
and all the disastrous implications which the "Knights" were trying to
avoid come through with tremendous force! They are right! It is a
"power" that outlives pagan Rome; it is a power "given to me in heaven
and earth." (That's the power Jesus gives to Satan! He's the "prince of
the power of the air," and all the kingdoms of the world are his.) It is
the power that the sixty-four gates of hell shall not prevail against,
because Satan owns the gates of hell.
