The statement is not one class is a federation of kings who gang up on
Rome, and then another class is a "power that used Rome," outlives Rome,
and persecutes the Church under another form that is not Roman. The
statement is that it is Rome, every shot. You can make it pagan Rome,
pizza Rome, papal Rome, or pepperoni Rome; you'll come out at the same
place every time you go in. The "ten kings" in the context of Revelation
17:13--14, are not in an historical "group of kings" (who were not
kings, but became kings and then destroyed Rome, and then kept on
persecuting the Church in another form)! That's the most outlandish,
ridiculous, extravagant piece of nonsense a religious comedian ever
concocted.

The statement is that the "ten kings" (vs. 12) are with the "beast" (vs.
13), and "These shall make war with the Lamb." They're not going to
persecute the Church; they make W‑A‑R with the Lamb.

Revelation 17:4---"And the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet
colour, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having a
golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her
fornication."

Do you know of a city that "reigneth over the kings of the earth," whose
colors are "purple and scarlet," whose symbol is a "golden cup"?

Revelation 17:6---"And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the
saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus: and when I saw her,
I wondered with great admiration."

Do you know of a city that "reigns over the kings of the earth," whose
colors are "purple and scarlet," whose symbol is a "golden cup" (That
wasn't the symbol of pagan Rome. It was a fasces, the blade and the rods
bound together!), who is guilty of killing "the martyrs of Jesus
Christ"? Did you ever read Fox's Book of Martyrs? Did you know that
mystery Babylon has killed more born­again, Bible‑believing Christians in
one night than Ameri­can Puritans, Protestants, Quakers, and Church of
England did in 200 years? (Don't try to go to the "Salem witch trials"
to try to prove your point which your priest passed on to you. If you
think there is any comparison, you just betray your ignorance.) There
have been more than two million Bible‑believing Christians killed in
Germany, France, Austria, Spain, Hungary, and Switzerland alone between
A.D. 1,000 and 1,700. That doesn't even include the persecutions in
Italy and South America.

Revelation 17:9---"And here is the mind which hath wisdom. The seven
heads are seven mountains, on which the woman sitteth."

To repeat, in Revelation 17 we find a city (vs. 18), which is built on
"seven mountains" (vs. 9). The city has "purple and scarlet" colors (vs.
4), and the symbol of the city is a "golden cup" (vs. 4). The city has
killed "martyrs" and "saints" (vs. 6). This city is a political power
(vs. 18). This city loves gold first (Rev. 18:12) and souls of men last
(Rev. 18:13). The city is drunken (18:3), commercial (18:15), rich
(18:12), proud (18:7), mystical (17:5), and damned by God (18:18).

Revelation 17:5---"And upon her forehead was a name written, MYSTERY,
BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH."

Revelation 18:24---"And in her was found the blood of prophets, and of
saints, and of all that were slain upon the earth."

Revelation 18:5, 8---"For her sins have reached unto heaven, and God
hath remembered her iniquities." "There­fore shall her plagues come in
one day, death, and mourning, and famine; and she shall be utterly
burned with fire: for strong is the Lord God who judgeth her."

Is that clear? Pagan Rome did not sink like a millstone into the sea
(Rev. 18:21). The Christians were not told to get out of pagan Rome;
they died in it (Rev. 18:4). Not one verse in Revelation 17 will match
pagan Rome completely.

Revelation 18:4---"And I heard another voice from heaven, saying, Come
out of her, my people \[Remember "my people" and "thy people" as you
have learned in these studies, are not Christians but Jews in the
Tribula­tion.\], that ye be not partakers of her sins, and that ye
receive not of her plagues."

This was not written to Christians in A.D. 70. (The Bible is so clear on
this that you really have only one problem, if you read the passage.)
You know that the woman is a city; you know she's built on seven
mountains; you know her colors are purple and scarlet; you know her
symbol is a golden cup; you know she kills martyrs and saints; you know
she is a political power; you know she loves gold first and souls of men
last; you know she's a drunken, commercial, rich, proud, mystical city
that God has damned. Now, you only have one problem, and it has nothing
to do with the Greek or the Hebrew or "interpretation." The only problem
you have is if you are loyal to that city, what on earth can you do?
Don't you see the trouble? Where the average investigator or scientist
is only looking for amoral objective truths in the physical realm, the
Bible is dealing with moral absolute truths in the religious realm. The
question is not, "Do we have the right interpretation?" The question is,
"Having read the passage and understanding exactly what He is talking
about, how would you feel if it were aimed at you?" Scientists, as
Nicodemus, are interested only in the abstraction that involves someone
else. Note: when Nico­demus asked Jesus, "How can a man be born again?",
he doesn't ask, "How can I be born again?"

Now, before we join the crowd and attempt to eliminate the passage in
Revelation 17--18 by changing the date, "interpreting figuratively," and
all the hoaxes which Rome has undertaken in order to cover up their
dirty, godless, religious perversion, because the passage is squarely on
them---let us consider some facts.

1. If you read the passage, you did identify the city. If you say you
didn't, you're just lying.

2. You identified the city with no help from the original Greek or your
official church's interpreta­tion. In plainer words, you don't have to go
to high school to identify the city.

3. You could not possibly have identified this city as New York,
Washington, London, Manila, Shang­hai, Hollywood, New Orleans, Memphis,
Los Angeles, San Francisco, Miami, Lisbon, Tokyo, Cairo, New Delhi,
Suez, or anything else. You know what that city is!

4. You know what the passage refers to, you have full knowledge of it,
and any man who ever read it had full knowledge about it.

The whole problem is---how are you going to get rid of the passage if
you don't like it? That's how a "pope" handles the passage. There isn't
a pope who ever lived that didn't know what the passage said when he
read it---in his own bible! He has full knowledge of it. Every "pope"
who every lived only has one problem---how to get rid of Revelation
17--18.

This question is the first and foremost consideration of every
translator and revisor who revised the Bible after 1611, unless he
believed what he was handling was the complete, absolute, authoritative,
infallible word of God---as the King James translators did.

Revelation 17:5---"And upon her forehead was a name written, MYSTERY,
BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH."

There is no doubt about what happened at all. Pagan Rome became papal
Rome in A.D. 500, during the transition period of Eusebius, Papias,
Constantine, Cypri­an, and Jerome's Latin Vulgate---A.D. 325 to A.D. 500.
When John next sees Rome, he no longer sees Rome as a pagan, godless
city that is opposed to New Testament, Bible‑believing Christians in
A.D. 90. He sees her in a mystery form---"MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT."
He sees her as a woman on seven hills who professes to be the one true
body, the "infallible pillar and ground of truth," and who professes to
believe every Fundamental Doctrine in the word of God! She professes to
believe the Virgin Birth, the Crucifixion, the Resurrection, and the
Second Coming of Jesus Christ, and yet, she is occupied with murdering
born‑again, Bible‑believing people or Tribulation saints or Jews in the
Tribulation. That's why John "wondered with great admiration."

This woman is found in Matthew 13. Notice that every time she is found,
she is pictured not only as a city, but as a religious prophetess
handling doctrine, like the woman you found in Revelation 2:20.

Matthew 13:33---"Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of
heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three
measures of meal, till the whole was leavened."

This woman is never the church, as the church is only referred to as a
"wife," " virgin," or "bride." This woman takes leaven, which is defined
in Scripture as false doctrine (Matt. 16:6--12), and hides it in three
measures of meal---which is defined as "the children of the kingdom"
(vs. 38)---until the whole loaf is leavened and filled with corruption.
This is Rome "shepherding" the Protestant, Catholic, and Greek Orthodox
branches of the church, and filling them full of rot. The woman is a
prophetess, typified by Jezebel, and identified as a city built on seven
hills, which is the mystery form of ancient Babylon. (See commentary on
Matthew.)

Notice that the beast she is connected with (Rev. 17:3) is identical
with the one found in Revelation 12:3. The beast she's on is not a
leopard, which is yellow‑brown, black, and white, but a "scarlet
coloured beast," red! This means that the woman rides on Satan; she is
connected with the ten‑horned, ten‑crowned kings of Daniel 2 (Rev.
17:12). (See commentary on Genesis, Gen. 11:4‑8.) This woman is cursed
by God, and she is responsible for the death of the Lord Jesus Christ.

Revelation 18:24---"And in her was found the blood of prophets, and of
saints, and of all that were slain upon the earth."

The saints were "chosen in him before the foundation of the world" (Eph.
1:4). So whatever city crucified the Lord and Saviour is indirectly
responsible for the death of every Christian that ever died. There is
only one city that meets this requirement; there is only one city in
hell, heaven, or on earth that put nails through His hands, a crown of
thorns on His head, a spear in His side, and then professes to believe
in Him; there is only one city that had Christians eaten in the arena
and burned at the stake, and went on professing to be the "Holy Mother
Church" of all Christians; there is only one city whose colors are
purple and scarlet, whose symbol is a golden cup, whose political
shenanigans involve the "kings of the earth," while professing to be a
church. And if you don't know where that city is, all I can say is that
Christians are commanded to "comfort the feebleminded" (1 Thess. 5). If
you don't know who this woman is, there is one of three things wrong
with you: (1) you are either as blind as a bat and can't read Braille,
or (2) you are lazy and insincere, and don't want the truth because it's
going to cut you up, or (3) you are so full of the Devil that your
hatred for the word of God can only be paralleled by that of Satan
himself.

CHAPTER 14

In our study of the Revelation, we have come through the Tribulation two
times---once under the figure of "seals," and the next time under the
figure of "trumpets." The third time through, the Bible is discussing
the Antichrist and his activities. He appears in Revelation 11:7,
Revelation 12, Revelation 13, and again in Revelation 14, as the Holy
Spirit takes you through the Tribulation once more to the end. Before
starting in Revelation 14, please notice that the context is the last
three and one-half years. This is very important because we have run
into these people---the 144,000---before in the Book of Revelation. (We
ran into them in Rev. 7 before we got to the end of the "seals.")

14:1 "And I looked, and lo, a Lamb stood on the mount Sion, and with him
an hundred forty and four thousand, having his Father's name written in
their foreheads."

Now, you have encountered these people before in Revelation 7:4--8, and
it was very clear from the Scriptures that they were from the twelve
tribes of Israel. That is, they were not "spiritual Jews." They were not
the "circumcised in heart" (as in Rom. 2), they were not the "Israel of
God" as in Galatians 6; they were not anything which heretics say they
are in order to twist the word of God. They are what they are---144,000
literal, physical Jews of the literal, physical twelve tribes of Israel!

Notice that when this same group shows up in Revelation 14:1, they are
up in heaven.

Revelation 14:3---"And they sung as it were a new song before the
throne, and before the four beasts, and the elders: and no man could
learn that song but the hundred and forty and four thousand, which were
redeemed from the earth."

Revelation 7:11---"And all the angels stood round about the throne, and
about the elders and the four beasts, and fell before the throne on
their faces, and worshipped God."

This scene is not on earth but in the third heaven. The great multitude
who appear here have been raptured.

Revelation 4:10---"The four and twenty elders fall down before him that
sat on the throne, and worship him that liveth for ever and ever, and
cast their crowns before the throne\...."

Now, the scene is laid in heaven, not on earth, for remember that John
was caught up to the third heaven (Rev. 4:1--2). Yet, in Revelation 7:4,
when the 144,000 were first mentioned, they were on the earth being
"sealed" to go through the Tribulation. The next time they show up, in
Revelation 14:1--3, they have been raptured and are up in heaven.

This brings up the problem of the expression "the mount Sion" in
Revelation 14:1. (Again, the expositors, Protestant and Catholic, begin
to alter the Scriptures to suit themselves. Most of them say that "mount
Sion" is supposed to represent "the church." The "mount Sion" of
Revelation 14:1 is no more connected with the Church than Mt. Everest is
connected with Cuba.) The "mount Sion" in Revelation 14:1 is said to be
"the mount Sion." If you remember earlier references on the "empty
places" over the North, you will have no trouble in identifying where
this place is; it is a literal, physical place that has nothing to do
with the "church." It is not even a thing, but it is a place.

Hebrews 12:22---"But ye are come unto mount Sion, and unto the city of
the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of
angels."

This is talking about a "heavenly" city in a "heavenly" place, over your
head straight North, up in the air; and it has nothing to do with an
earthly place. This is the universal mountain that points North, and the
Devil was on top of this mountain in the beginning!

Ezekiel 28:14---"Thou art the anointed cherub that covereth; and I have
set thee so: thou wast upon the holy mountain of God; thou hast walked
up and down in he midst of the stones of fire."

If you remember our studies in Revelation 4--5, you know that the "sides
of the north" always refer to a height above the universe.

Psalm 48:2---"Beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth, is
mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great King."

Then this is not referring to a literal, physical, earthly Jerusalem.
You were told that it was the "heavenly Jerusalem" in Hebrews. You were
told it was "the mount Sion" in Revelation. You were told that it was
"on the sides of the north" in Psalm 48. In Isaiah 14:13, you were told
that the "sides of the north" are far above the planetary systems and
constellations. You don't have to guess what this "Mt. Zion" is. You
don't have to plead ignorance; you don't have to sit there and say, "Oh,
my! Somebody has to be so smart to get that. Why did God write such an
obscure and confused book? Why didn't He make it clear?" It is clear if
you will just read it! (The point is that God knows how much you can
read, and how much you can study, and He will certainly hold you
accountable for your ability. Man thinks that if he does not study God's
word that God will not hold him accountable. This is typical of the
Roman Catholics. They deposit their personal accountability in the hands
of their church or their priest, and have some wild idea that he is
going to handle it for them. My friend, God is going to hold you
accountable for every word in that book. "Man shall not live by bread
alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God." The
only search warrant for research for a Ph.D.'s thesis was given by the
Lord Jesus Christ when He said, "Search the scriptures." "Study to shew
thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed,
rightly dividing the word of truth" \[John 5:39; 2 Tim. 2:15\].)

You don't have to be a genius to see that these two "Mount Zions" are
different. One of them is on earth, and one of them is in heaven. One of
them is connected with the New Jerusalem from above, "which is the
mother of us all," the heavenly Jerusalem (Gal. 4; Rev. 14). The other
one is the earthly Jerusalem, on the earthly Mount Zion, which is now
"in bondage with her children" (Gal. 4). The place where the 144,000
appear in Revelation 14:1--3 is straight up over your head about 150
million light years---the "sides of the north," where these 144,000 have
been raptured.

Revelation 14:1---"And I looked, and lo, a Lamb stood on the mount Sion,
and with him an hundred forty and four thousand, having his Father's
name written in their foreheads."

Again, we find Christ pictured as a Lamb. (It is plainly figurative for
a lamb's "father" has no name; but this Lamb's Father has a name, for He
is a capital "L" and His Father is a capital "F." This Lamb is the Lord
Jesus Christ.)

14:2 "And I heard a voice from heaven, as the voice of many waters, and
as the voice of a great thunder: and I heard the voice of harpers
harping with their harps."

In heaven they have musical instruments to praise the Lord, and they are
given as "harps" (as we find it again in Rev. 15:2--3).

14:3 "And they sung as it were a new song before the throne, and before
the four beasts, and the elders: and no man could learn that song but
the hundred and forty and four thousand, which were redeemed from the
earth."

This group is a very special group, because no man could learn this song
except 144,000. These are not the "Jehovah's Witnesses," but a very
special group. (Some people who like to pretend they are very special,
like to identify themselves with this group.) These 144,000 are very
unique. The song they sing is found in Revelation 15:3---the "song of
Moses." That is, they are related to the Old Testament Mosaic Law. They
also sing the "song of the Lamb." Thus, they are related to salvation by
grace through faith plus the Mosaic Law. Their salvation, in the
Tribulation, has come by "enduring to the end" over the "mark of the
beast" (see Rev. 12:17).

Revelation 15:2--3---"And I saw as it were a sea of glass mingled with
fire: and them that had gotten the victory over the beast, and over his
image, and over his mark, and over the number of his name, stand on the
sea of glass, having the harps of God. And they sing the song of Moses
the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and
marvellous are thy works, Lord God Almighty; just and true are thy ways,
thou King of saints."

You will find the "song of Moses" in Deuteronomy 32.

Revelation 14:12---"Here is the patience of the saints: here are they
that keep the commandments of God, and the faith of Jesus."

"The commandments of God," like "the song of Moses;" and "the faith of
Jesus," which is "the song of the Lamb."

Revelation 12:17---"And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went to
make war with the remnant of her seed, which keep the commandments of
God \[the Mosaic Law or "the song of Moses"\], and have the testimony of
Jesus Christ \[New Testament salvation by grace and the blood of the
Lamb\]." (See comments on the verse.)

So, it is emphasized very clearly that Tribulation salvation is a
combination of faith and works, and not one of faith only. You'll find
this thought repeated in James, which incidentally, is written to "the
twelve tribes," exactly as you find it in Revelation 7.

James 1:1---"James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to
the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad, greeting."

This is not written to the "Israel of God," nor to the spiritual Jew,
nor to the "seed of Abraham." The passage, which speaks of justification
by works (James 2), is aimed at "the twelve tribes" (James 1:1), as the
twelve tribes in Revelation 7, and those that are saved by grace and
faith and works, and the Mosaic Law (Rev. 11--12, 14; Matt. 14, etc.).

Revelation 14:3---"And they sung as it were a new song before the
throne, and before the four beasts, and the elders: and no man could
learn that song but the hundred and forty and four thousand, which were
redeemed from the earth."

"And they sung as it were a new song." It's new! It's not sung in the
Church Age!

14:4 "These are they which were not defiled with women; for they are
virgins. These are they which follow the Lamb whithersoever he goeth.
These were redeemed from among men, being the firstfruits unto God and
to the Lamb."

Notice this select gathering are all single men, and they are "not
defiled with women." They are not Catholic priests or Trappist monks who
have taken the vows of chastity (and then taken the chance when the
opportunity occurred!). They are not married men, and they are not part
of the "bride of Christ." No Christian anywhere in the word of God is
ever referred to as "a virgin." The Body of Christ, made up of
Christians, is called "a chaste virgin" in 2 Corinthians 11:2, but
notice very carefully the singular. Shall we make a difference about an
"s" in a word? You bet your booties we will! An "s" in the AV 1611 is
the difference between correct doctrine and false doctrine (see Rev.
11:15). The bunch in Revelation 14 are "virgins." You say, "What's the
difference in 'virgin' and 'virgins'?" Well, it's the difference between
being in the Church Age or the Tribulation. It's the difference in being
saved by grace or saved by works.

Matthew 25:1---"Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten
virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom."

Look at the plural---V‑I‑R‑G‑I‑N‑S! Notice that the statement is not,
"Then shall the body of Christ, the Church, be likened to virgin single
men, who took a vow of chastity in the Roman Catholic Church." The
statement is, "Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten
virgins." When the Bridegroom comes, they don't go out to marry Him (as
the "chaste virgin," the "bride of Christ," will), they go out to meet
Him!

Matthew 25:6---"And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the
bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him." Not marry Him!

Then the 144,000 of Revelation 14 are the same ones mentioned in Matthew
25; they are Tribulation saints. They are v‑i‑r‑g‑i‑n‑s who "endure to
the end" of the Tribulation and are caught up to "meet the Bridegroom"
as He comes. They are not members of the one true Body of Christ which
is caught out to meet Christ when He comes for the Church---"the chaste
virgin" (2 Cor. 11:1--3). They are virgins who "endure to the end" of
the Tribulation, and keep the Mosaic Law, and sing the "song of Moses."
They go up to meet the Bridegroom as He returns to land on the earth.
(This is not my interpretation,or Baptist interpretation; this is the
infallible doctrine of the Holy Spirit, direct from the word of God in
plain English, for English‑speaking people. You see how people get all
screwed up? They get one idea fixed in their head, and if the Bible
doesn't bow down to that one idea and match it, then you've got to go to
the Greek and change the English text to match the idea they have! The
Bible isn't written just for a mass of Gentiles in The Church Age. The
passage we are studying right now is no more connected with "the Church"
than a caboose on a train is connected to a jackrabbit.) Revelation 14:4
is a select group of saints in the Tribulation, who are keeping the
Mosaic Law and are called "virgins," who number 144,000, and they are of
the twelve tribes of Israel. This is plainer than John 3:16. They are
present at the marriage of the Lamb, but they don't marry the
Bridegroom. (Notice how clearly this is brought out in Psa. 45, the
entire chapter being on the marriage of the Lamb.)

Psalm 45:13--14---"The king's daughter is all glorious within: her
clothing is of wrought gold. She shall be brought unto the king in
raiment of needlework: the virgins her companions that follow her shall
be brought unto thee."

The virgins are "companions" of the chaste virgin, the bride of Christ.
They are not in the "bride of Christ." Therefore, if any man tells you
that the "virgins" in Revelation 14:1--4 are Catholic "priests" who have
never been defiled with women, and that they are the firstfruits of God,
you retort with, "What are you, a Jehovah's Witness?" Everybody can't
get in on the act! You Seventh-day Adventists can't jam yourselves in
there because you think you keep the Old Testament Mosaic Law, because
you don't keep it. You just think you keep one or two of the Ten
Commandments. For those of us who watch you, there is considerable doubt
in our minds whether you even keep those. You Jehovah's Witnesses can't
jam yourselves in there just because you think you are 144,000 Jehovah's
Witnesses, for some of you are married, and some of you that aren't
married have fooled with women! You don't keep the Mosaic Law either,
for you shave and cut your hair! You don't know the "song of Moses." You
can't get in on the act. The act is reserved for v‑i‑r‑g‑i‑n‑s, who are
Tribulation saints, who "endure to the end" by not taking the "mark of
the beast," and the Mosaic Law and works figure in their salvation. They
are v‑i‑r‑g‑i‑n‑s who are raptured at the end of the Tribulation to be
present at the marriage of t‑h‑e "chaste virgin," the Body of Christ, to
the Bridegroom, the Lord Jesus Christ.

You find that the Holy Spirit reiterates the same thing in Song of
Solomon.

Song of Solomon 6:8--9---"There are threescore queens, and fourscore
concubines, and virgins without number. \[Too many to count.\] My dove,
my undefiled is but one; she is the only one of her mother, she is the
choice one of her that bare her. The daughters saw her, and blessed her;
yea, the queens and the concubines, and they praised her."

O‑N‑E, is that clear? This "one" is identified as the Bride of Christ.
Nothing could be clearer; nothing could be plainer. Therefore, the
virgins in Revelation 14 are clearly identified in the AV 1611 English.
(What Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Amplified, Centennial, RSV, ASV, New
English, etc. cannot get from their obscure and sterile "classical
Greek," the Holy Spirit can give you from the plain English, if you
believe what He says.)

Revelation 14:4---"These are they which were not defiled with women; for
they are virgins."

Right here, the RSV steps in to give you some "help" in making it clear
from "the original Greek!" They have written:

Revelation 14:4---"It is these who have not defiled themselves with
women, for they are chaste."

Every manuscript ever found, including the Devil's manuscripts, says
"virgins" and not "chaste." Do you know why the RSV committee said
"chaste"? They said "chaste" because translators of the RSV do not
really believe that the verse has reference to literal, physical women.
They think the text has to do with being chaste in doctrine, or being
pure in spiritual worship! You will find Fundamentalists picking up the
same stupid teaching and saying, "Well, it's a reference to the fact
that they weren't involved in false worship." (Brother, you'd better
read Revelation 2--3 again!) False worship in the last days has
fornication as part of the worship service! (The footnote in the RSV on
the word "chaste" reads, "Greek: virgins." In plainer words, the RSV
admits that the word "chaste" is not from the Greek anyway! It's a very
weird situation where scholars claim to have a better translation from
better Greek, and then admit in their own translation that they don't
even use it!)

14:5 "And in their mouth was found no guile: for they are without fault
before the throne of God."

Then this brings us a picture, right before the Second Advent, of the
Tribulation saints who "endured to the end" (Matt. 24:13), who overcame
the Devil by not taking the "mark of the beast," and they did this by
observing the Mosaic Law (Matt. 24; Rev. 11--12, 14, 22), and by
believing on Jesus Christ as their Saviour. Since they were not
circumcised by the Holy Spirit, they have to "endure to the end" by
works. So, their salvation is a combination of grace and works. They are
caught up at the end of the Tribulation to meet the Bridegroom. Some are
pictured as losing salvation in Matthew 25 because they do not "endure
until the end." You will find this in Matthew 22, 24--25, which is why
the gospel of Matthew is the favorite gospel of all Catholics, Liberals,
Seventh-day Adventists, Jehovah's Witnesses, and Campbellites. They all
like these verses of Scripture which have nothing to do with them or
anybody else, but the verses give them a Scriptural alibi to lie, swear
falsely, and pervert the word of God for their own ends. This is typical
of all unsaved religious people. If you want to get screwed up in the
Scripture, you just get wound up in Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews, and
you'll wind up thinking you're in a hurricane off the coast of Florida.
The clear straight Christian doctrines for this age are found in Romans
through Philemon. Although "all Scripture is written for our learning,"
unsaved preachers have the wild notion that all Scripture is written
doctrinally for the Christian, which it is not. Revelation 14 is a
perfect example of this.

These are Tribulation saints; they are called "virgins." Their chastity
involves not participating in the heathen worship of the Tribulation
(which involves the act of immorality); these 144,000 Tribulation saints
are all Israelites (literal, physical, visible Jews) from the twelve
tribes of Israel; they are sealed through the Tribulation and caught up
at the end of the Tribulation, and appear in the passage (Rev. 14:1--4)
in heaven on "the mount Sion," the heavenly Jerusalem.

This last intermittent period, a month or six months before the end of
the Tribulation (the period between the catching up of the Tribulation
saints and the actual Advent), is unknown. This is where Christ keeps
saying, "But the end is not yet." The time is unknown. (Whoever finds
it, will find it eventually in the Book of Daniel.) It is a secret that
is as yet unrevealed to fundamental scholars, and will be kept
unrevealed, undoubtedly, as the fundamental scholars have gone to the
Hebrew in Daniel to get their information; consequently, they have lost
the thread of revelation. God doesn't reveal great truths by going
vertically down into some language until you hit dead bottom in a
lexicon. God the Holy Spirit reveals truth by "Scripture with
Scripture," and comparing everything which God wrote until you find His
final, complete statement on what He intended you to get. This means, of
course, that if anybody ever does discover the end period of time---the
actual date between the post‑Tribulation Rapture and the Second
Advent---they are going to get it by consulting the plain English
prayerfully. They'll never get it from the Greek!

14:6 "And I saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven, having the
everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on the earth, and to
every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people,

7 Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the
hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and
earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters."

This angel who flies "in the midst of heaven" preaches another gospel,
which is not "Paul's gospel."

Galatians 1:8---"But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any
other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him
be accursed."

Here we find an angel preaching "another gospel." This gospel is called
the "everlasting gospel." This brings up a very, very interesting
problem for a Campbellite. If Galatians 1 refers to everything from
Matthew 1 to Revelation 22, then the angel in Revelation 14 is cursed of
God, and is not preaching the truth; and Simon Peter did not preach the
truth at Pentecost! That isn't all! If there is only one gospel from
Matthew 1 to Revelation 22, Jesus Christ was very careless in His
earthly ministry to Israel.

Jesus Christ never told anybody in Israel, "For by grace are ye saved
through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God." And
He never told any of them that they would be part of a single body of
believers made up of Jew and Gentile (1 Cor. 12:10--20).

This points out a major mental problem which all Campbellite (Church of
Christ) people have. Even though they plainly see that their system
makes the Bible contradict, they would rather have the Bible contradict
(while maintaining it doesn't) in order to keep their own position, than
make their position fit the word of God so that neither contradicts.
When Jesus was on this earth,in Matthew 10, He preached only to Israel.
He told the apostles, "Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the
dead, cast out devils" (Matt. 10:5--8), before and after the
resurrection (Mark 16). Paul, the Apostle to the Gentiles, never wrote
one word about the Christians being commissioned to "cleanse the lepers"
or to preach only to the "lost sheep of the house of Israel." Christians
are to preach the gospel "to every creature," and yet the complete
commission given to the eleven apostles, in Mark 16, was never carried
out by Christians in this age!

Mark 16:15---"And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and
preach the gospel to every creature."

They are told to "preach the gospel to every creature," but they quit at
Jerusalem and stay there (Acts 8, 15). They become apostles to the
circumcision, not to the Gentiles (Gal. 2).

Mark 16:16---"He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he
that believeth not shall be damned."

When the poor Campbellite elder gets to this verse, he quotes it as
though he was getting down to the rock‑bed root of "apostolic faith and
practice," and doesn't realize that the next two verses knock his whole
system in the head.

Mark 16:17--18---"And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my
name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues; They
shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not
hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover."

These verses are the apostolic "signs and wonders" given only during the
ministry of the apostles. After the ministry of the apostles is over,
the signs and wonders of the apostles are gone. This puts the
Campbellite in a very peculiar position, because his favorite passage
(Acts 2:38) is only a very brief, intermittent, historical account of
the Apostolic ministry to a particular group of Jews. The great Age of
Grace, which continues for 1,900 years after this book is all over, is a
period that is unknown to a Campbellite minister. He doesn't even know
what it's all about. If what Paul said in Galatians 1:8--9 is true, how
do you account for the fact that Peter didn't preach this gospel at all?
What Peter preached in Acts 2 is not what you find in Galatians 2--4,
and it isn't what you find in Romans 10:9--10, and it isn't what you
find in John 3! (The gospel of John had not been written when Peter
preached in Acts 2; Galatians is not written when Peter preached in Acts
2; Revelation is not written when Peter preached in Acts 2.)
Campbellites and Catholics go around preaching Galatians 1:8--9 as
though it applied to everything from Acts 2 to Revelation 22. Yet in
Acts 2, Peter knows nothing about the grace of God, as he is still a
pork‑abstaining, circumcised, sabbath‑observing, temple-worshipping Jew,
who is afraid to eat pork in Acts 10! He still teaches water baptism as
part of salvation (as John the Baptist and the Old Testament), and he
mentions nothing at all about Christ dying for anybody's sins. (Compare
1 Cor. 15:1--6.) There isn't any translation of any Bible on the face of
the earth which mentions Christ dying for anybody's sins, in Acts 2. The
reason why a Campbellite preacher can't face these problems is that
basically, he has a mental problem. It's not that he's "full of the
Devil" or a "heretic;" it's just that he is a simpleton. When you get to
Revelation 14, you have an angel preaching another gospel, and there
isn't one mention of the "new birth," the "resurrection," or the
"crucifixion" in the everlasting gospel! There is also no mention of
baptism!

Revelation 14:7---"Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to
him; for the hour of his judgment is come\...."

That's not the gospel of the grace of God! Does Paul mean in Galatians 1
that this angel in Revelation 14:7 is cursed? Do you see the mess that a
man can get into if he refuses to believe what he reads, as he reads it,
where he finds it? For example:

Acts 2:38--39---"Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized
every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins,
and ye \[the house of Israel! Look at the context.\] shall receive the
gift of the Holy Ghost. For the promise is unto you \[the house of
Israel\], and to your children \[the house of Israel\], and to all that
are afar off, even as many as the Lord our God \[the God of
Israel---Look at the context.\] shall call."

Alexander Campbell went around quoting Acts 2:38 as though it was the
"plan of salvation" for a Gentile! What an insane thing! The context of
the entire chapter is given to the house of Israel. "Ye men of Judaea,
and all ye that dwell at Jerusalem" (Acts 2:14). "Ye men of Israel, hear
these words" (Acts 2:22). "Therefore let all the house of Israel know
assuredly" (Acts 2:36).

The gospel in Acts 2 is not the gospel Paul is talking about in
Galatians 1. Paul, in Galatians 1, is talking about the gospel of the
grace of God. (Galatians 1 was written many years after Pentecost. When
Galatians 1 was written, the gospel of the grace of God was so
thoroughly established that even Peter was preaching it.)

Acts 15:11---"But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus
Christ we shall be saved, even as they."

Paul has taught Peter this gospel, and he is preaching it. There isn't
one cotton‑picking word about "BUP‑tism" in Acts 15. You see, a
Campbellite is unable to read the simplest passages. In Galatians 1 Paul
said that an angel would be "accursed" if he preached "any other
gospel," but an angel did preach one, and so did Peter! They just
preached them at different times. The poor Campbellite cannot "rightly
divide the word." He doesn't know the times; he doesn't know the
seasons; he doesn't know the dispensations; he doesn't study. All a
Campbellite does is read debates and sermon outlines by Campbellite
preachers to try to prove that if you don't join his church, he's a
little better than you are because he's got enough sense to join "the
real thing." All a Campbellite is, in the final analysis, is just a
"Southern Catholic." There isn't a Catholic who ever lived that doesn't
teach baptismal regeneration; there isn't a Catholic who ever lived that
doesn't teach that his church is the one true church of Christ; there
isn't a Catholic who ever lived that couldn't prove it just as quick as
a Campbellite, if it came to a showdown and a test of strength. The only
difference between a Catholic and a Campbellite is that a Catholic likes
to be called a "Catholic," and a Campbellite likes for folks to think
that he is a "Christian."

In Acts 2, Peter preaches one gospel; in Galatians 1, Paul preaches
another gospel; in Revelation 14, an angel preaches another gospel. This
brings up a problem---is the Bible a contradiction? NO! When Simon Peter
preaches in Acts 2, he's preaching in A.D. 33. with no revelation but
the Old Testament. When Paul preaches in 1 Corinthians 15 and Galatians
1, he has already received the revelation of the grace of God, which was
ushered into this dispensation during the transition period in the Book
of Acts. When Paul writes his epistles, late in the Book of Acts, he has
been shown things that Peter, James, and John knew absolutely nothing
about. The Catholic and the Campbellite are both, in their ignorance,
forever going back to a Jewish, circumcised minister, Peter, ministering
to the Jews, in order to prove doctrines which are not even connected
with the born‑again Christian Gentile. This is typical---going to Peter
instead of Paul!

Notice the Roman Catholic teaching on this. This is from the Papal
Encyclicals, and this is "nihil obstat," by John A. Goodwine, J.C.D.,
censor librarian, bearing the imprimatur of Francis Cardinal Spellman,
Archbishop of New York. "Nihil obstat" and "imprimatur" are official
declarations that a book or pamphlet is free of doctrinal or moral
error. This is the doctrinal statement from page 15:

"Catholicism insists that it teaches the preaching of Christ, and only
that. The Catholic believes that his beliefs are identical with those of
the Apostolic Church without a jot or tittle changed."

This is typical of the Roman Catholic Church which rejected the entire
doctrinal content of the Pauline Epistles. Jesus preaches to Israel, and
Paul preaches to Gentiles; Jesus preaches salvation by works in Matthew
24--25, and Paul preaches salvation by grace in Ephesians 2. You cannot
get them together unless you slight one of them, and Rome and the
Campbellite church just kick Paul clean out, and take Simon Peter, the
Book of Acts, and the Book of Matthew---the earthly life and preaching
of Christ. Of course, this is a distorted way of handling the Scripture.
If all you preach and teach is the preaching and teaching of Jesus
Christ and the Apostles, you're nothing but a God‑forsaken,
ecclesiastical wreck. Over three‑quarters of their preaching and
teaching applies to Israel before the crucifixion or in the Tribulation!

There's no truer statement ever written than the one from the Catholic
book mentioned before, on page 11:

"Hence it is that the Catholic does not say in the first instance 'What
does the book say?' Rather he asks, 'What does the teaching church
say?'"

That is, an honest Catholic doesn't give a flip what the Book says. If
the Book ever says one thing and the "teaching church" says something
else, he'll burn that Bible as quick as look at it. Notice the wording:
it doesn't say, "What does the church say?", but, "What does the
teaching church say?" Again, quoting from the book:

"This fundamental vision of the church causes the Catholic to look to
the episcopate for doctrine because the Roman Catholic bishops are the
authentic exponents of God's message. This is no idolatry of the bishops
or their primate, the Bishop of Rome, but it is only a consequence of
the Catholic theory of the church."

There you have the Catholic admitting that his entire authority is based
on theory! (You'll find that statement printed "without any objection"
by Francis Cardinal Spellman, the Archbishop of New York.) That's
typical of Campbellite and Catholic interpretation. It is based on
nothing but a subjective, hypothetical supposition that "The Church" is
their particular organization. Using that subjective bias as a framework
for perverted exposition, these poor, depraved fanatics come to the word
of God and force every verse to teach that their church is "it." But
every once in a while the Holy Spirit dumps a problem in their laps that
they can't get out of, and this is one of them---the angel in Revelation
14, and Peter in Acts 2, were not accursed, even though Paul said they
would be in Galatians 1. Let's see you run to the Greek and get out of
that one!

The English will yield the true exposition. The gospel has four forms:

1\. We have the "gospel of the grace of God." This is the good news in
this age that God will save any sinner by grace who will simply trust
him by faith; this is the good news of Romans 10 and Romans 4:5, "But to
him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly,
his faith is counted for righteousness."

2\. The gospel of the grace of God is also called "my gospel" by Paul in
Romans 2:16. But "my gospel" includes, of course, the greater revelation
of the one, true Body of Christ, the called‑out Church, the body of
born‑again believers (who have no more use for the Catholic or
Campbellite organizations than they do for a VFW Hall or a USO). The
calling out of this group of born‑again believers (who are born again of
the Holy Spirit), makes up one body, which is composed of Jews and
Gentiles.

3\. We have the gospel of "the Kingdom." This is the earthly gospel that
Jesus Christ preached to Israel, which the Roman Catholic Church
professes is the only true teaching.

4\. The last gospel is "the everlasting gospel." This is not the good
news of the kingdom of Heaven given to Israel; this is not the good news
of the grace of God given to a sinner, that God will give him a new
birth and put him in the Body of Christ---this is a message of
repentance to Gentiles in the future.

So we have four forms of the gospel. We have the gospel of the kingdom
of Heaven, given to Israel; we have the gospel of the grace of God,
given to the unsaved sinner; we have Paul's gospel given to the church,
the Body of Christ; and we have the everlasting gospel given to
Tribulation Gentiles.

Revelation 14:7---"Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to
him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made
heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters."

Notice, the message to the Gentiles is not, "Fear God, and keep His
commandments," as it is given to the Old Testament Jew under Solomon in
Ecclesiastes 12 (where Campbellites and Jehovah Witnesses like to hang
out); it is, "Fear God, and give glory to him."

14:8 "And there followed another angel, saying, Babylon is fallen, is
fallen, that great city, because she made all nations drink of the wine
of the wrath of her fornication."

This verse shows why we studied Revelation 17 immediately after
Revelation 13. It was so that you would know about the fall of Babylon
before you got to Revelation 14:8, where Babylon has already fallen.
(This shows you that the accounts in Revelation are not consecutive
history, but are simultaneous accounts of the same event. The Book of
Revelation is not a continual, progressive, historical, blow‑by‑blow
description of the Tribulation from Chapter 4 to Chapter 19, but it
contains four accounts of the Tribulation and the Second Coming of
Christ exactly as Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John contain four accounts of
the first coming of Christ.)

14:9 "And the third angel followed them, saying with a loud voice, If
any man worship the beast and his image, and receive his mark in his
forehead, or in his hand,

10 The same shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is poured
out without mixture into the cup of his indignation; and he shall be
tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels,
and in the presence of the Lamb."

We have covered "the beast" thoroughly in the previous chapter. We
identified him, his image, his mark, and everything about him. A person
will have to receive his mark to make a living in the Tribulation, and
he has been identified so thoroughly that you could not possibly miss
him. If a man worships him, or receives his mark, that man is going to
hell for certain, and there is no hope for him. Therefore, in the
Tribulation, the gospel is "endure to the end." If people do not "endure
to the end," if they take the "mark of the beast" and do not give glory
to God, they lose their salvation and go to hell.

14:11 "And the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever:
and they have no rest day nor night, who worship (Again, the word
"worship" means to kiss.) the beast and his image, and whosoever
receiveth the mark of his name.

12 Here is the patience of the saints: here are they that keep the
commandments of God \[Old Testament Ten Mosaic Law Commandments like a
Seventh-day Adventist, who got the wrong verse at the wrong time and
goes to hell with it!\], and the faith of Jesus."

They must believe that the Messiah has died, been buried, and is risen
from the dead, and is coming again. Thus, the plan is made clear. After
the Body of Christ is caught out, the Holy Spirit still convicts of sin
and saves people, but although they are saved "by grace through faith,"
they are not kept "by grace through faith," because they are no longer
put into the Body of Christ and indwelt by the Holy Spirit, as the
Christian is in this dispensation. After the Body of Christ is caught
out, a dispensation comes in where a man must believe on Jesus Christ,
and then, in order to "endure to the end," that man must observe the
commandments of God, or "Fear God, and give glory to him." If the man is
a Jew, he is under the Mosaic Law; if he is a Gentile he must "endure to
the end." without taking the "mark of the beast." If he takes the "mark
of the beast," which will be the mark of a Roman Catholic pope (who
later goes to Jerusalem and deifies himself as God---and he's getting a
good start now), he is damned. If a man gets through and endures to the
end, he will come in as a "sheep" (Matt. 25:31--46), and "inherit the
kingdom prepared for him from the foundation of the world." But if he
takes the "mark of the beast," he dies and goes to hell, and he is put
in the lake of fire at the Second Coming of Christ (Matt. 25:31--46).
You see how clearly this interpretation places all the Scripture in its
proper place. It means that such passages as James 1--2; Hebrews 6, 10;
Matthew 24--25; Mark 13; Luke 17; Hebrews 9, etc., all fall into a
Tribulation context, where there is an element of faith and works
mingled, and all are connected with resisting the "mark of the beast"
until the end of the Tribulation. This means that in the Tribulation,
you can lose it! Now, this is terrible doctrine for a Baptist or a
Presbyterian, but we must remember that long ago Baptists and
Presbyterians quit believing that the Authorized Version was the word of
God. There is not a fundamental seminary in the United States which
teaches or believes that the AV 1611 is the word of God, or even the
best translation of the word of God. Consequently, the truth that I'm
talking about right now---taught first in 1954---is unknown to
Premillennial scholars.

A man in the Tribulation can lose his salvation for the following
reasons:

1\. He is not spiritually circumcised.

2\. He is not in the Body of Jesus Christ.

3\. He has not been "born again."

4\. His soul is "stuck to his body," as it was in the Old Testament, and
can be spoken of synonymous with the body (See commentary on Gen. 2:7.).

5\. He is in a situation where his salvation is conditional upon his
enduring to the end of a period of time, instead of resting in the
finished work of Someone.

6\. There is an element of the commandments connected with his
salvation, which is not given in this dispensation, and which he has to
keep to prove that he has "the faith."

7\. He is in a period, and an age, in which Israel is coming back into
ascendancy, and consequently, the Mosaic Law is coming back into
operation.

(I know that Dr. DeHaan, Theodore Epp, John R. Rice, and others cannot
go along with this; but you must remember that these men have spent so
much time building works and winning souls, which is commendable, and
writing books, which is commendable, and trying to edify the saints in
the simple fundamentals in the word of God, which is commendable, and
giving people "the sincere milk of the word," which is commendable, that
they have had very little time to prayerfully study and dig in the word
of God itself. They are men of God, but when it comes to correctly
expositing the word of God as it is written, in passages that deal more
than with just the milk in the word---you're not going to get any
information from them at all. If you ever get any information, you're
going to get it out of a King James, AV 1611.)

In our study of Revelation 14, we have come upon a passage that deals
with hell. Having discussed the post-Tribulation Rapture of Tribulation
saints, who are called "virgins," we now have found a passage in
Revelation 14:9--11 that describes the condition of those who did not
"endure to the end."

Revelation 14:9--10---"And the third angel followed them, saying with a
loud voice, If any man worship the beast and his image, and receive his
mark in his forehead, or in his hand, The same shall drink of the wine
of the wrath of God, which is poured out without mixture into the cup of
his indignation; and he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in
the presence of the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb."

"Cup of his indignation." This is the same cup that the Lord Jesus
Christ did not want to drink in the Garden of Gethsemane. The Scripture
identifies the "cup" as the "wine of the wrath" of God's indignation,
poured out (Isa. 51:17). This is God's wrath and judgment against sin.
It was the cup that Jesus Christ prayed to be delivered from, but had to
drink in order to take the sinner's place. It has nothing to do with
dying; it has nothing to do with dying on the cross. It has to do in
full measure with the wages due to sin as a full sinner. There is a
reference to this cup in Jeremiah 25:15 and Isaiah 51:17.

"And he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of
the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb."

Revelation 14:11---"And the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever
and ever: and they have no rest day nor night, who worship the beast and
his image, and whosoever receiveth the mark of his name."

This is one of the clearest passages in the New Testament on the eternal
punishment of the lost in hell. It goes without saying that the majority
of people in America do not believe in a literal hell for a very good
reason---they are going there! The fact that preachers have quit
preaching about hell is no sign that hell has become air‑conditioned or
renovated or redecorated. One of the tragedies of this age is the fact
that the preaching of "hell‑fire" has gone out of the pulpit. The time
has come in America when even Bible‑believing preachers are ashamed to
preach about a literal, everlasting, burning hell. They are so afraid
that this truth is unreasonable, illogical, or non‑intellectual, and
that they will be branded as hillbillies, fanatics, and "hate mongers"
if they speak of it, that they remain muzzled. This feeling in America
is so common today that the statement on hell has been taken out of the
"Apostles' Creed," where it says Christ "descended into hell." The
Seventh-day Adventists and Jehovah's Witnesses have decided that you are
annihilated. The liberals, Communists, Jehovah's Witnesses, and British
Israelites have all decided that, "All is well, there ain't no hell."
Yet the grounds for rejecting a literal hell are not found in the New
Testament. As a famous Greek scholar once said---who was as misguided as
any Greek scholar, but still knew his Greek---"If anybody has quit
believing in a literal, burning, everlasting hell, they have not ceased
to believe it on the grounds of the Greek New Testament." I may add that
they have not ceased to believe it on the grounds of the English New
Testament. The English New Testament and the Greek New Testament teach a
literal, burning, everlasting hell!

This is such an unpopular doctrine, and such a hated doctrine, and such
an "uncouth" doctrine, that the majority of preachers in America have
quit believing that it is a Bible doctrine. You will notice that the
best "bibles" translated by the conservatives, like the Amplified and
the ASV, refuse to translate the word "hell." Where they find the word
"hades" in the Greek, they leave it untranslated, and transliterate into
"hades," which is not even a translation. ("Hades" is a transliteration
of 'ades. The new "bibles" are afraid to translate.) They will translate
the Greek "ouranos" into "heaven," yet they will not translate the Greek
"hades" into "hell." In plainer words, you have to be a Greek to go to
hell (Luke 16:19--21), but if you are an Englishman, you go to heaven (2
Cor. 12:1--4). This shows the true attitude of the best modern
translators toward God's word. I may say (without any fear of
contradiction at the Judgment Seat of Christ) that the best translations
on the market today are translated by men who do not have enough
reverence for the Bible to put in the left eye of a blind mosquito.

In a poll by a professor at Northwestern University, cited by W. O. H.
Garman in The Present Day Trends in World Wide Apostasy, we find the
following:

Ninety-six percent of Congregational ministers do not believe in literal
hell.

Thirty percent of Lutheran ministers do not believe in literal hell.

Ninety-six percent of Episcopalian ministers do not believe in literal
hell.

Eighty-five precent of Presbyterian ministers do not believe in literal
hell.

Ninety-two percent of Methodist ministers do not believe in literal
hell.

Fifty percent of Baptist ministers do not believe in literal hell.

The majority of preachers no longer preach about hell because they don't
believe in it, and they don't believe in it because they are headed
there. When you find a man busy renovating and air‑conditioning hell,
you can be assured he's about to move in.

But we should examine the passages in the New Testament which say that
hell is a literal place burning with a literal fire that never goes out,
and that the sinners who burn in it never burn up, for they have soulish
bodies instead of physical bodies. (See comments on Rev. 6:9--10.)

Revelation 14:10--11---"The same shall drink of the wine of the wrath of
God, which is poured out without mixture into the cup of his
indignation; and he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the
presence of the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb: And the
smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever: and they have no
rest day nor night, who worship the beast and his image, and whosoever
receiveth the mark of his name."

Some say that the "smoke" goes up forever, but that the "torment"
doesn't go on forever. But the passage says, "The smoke of their
T‑0‑R‑M‑E‑N‑T ascendeth up for ever and ever" (vs. 11), and "He shall be
T‑O‑R‑M‑E‑N‑T‑E‑D with fire and brimstone" (vs. 10).

Revelation 19:20---"And the beast was taken, and with him the false
prophet that wrought miracles before him, with which he deceived them
that had received the mark of the beast, and them that worshipped his
image. These both were cast alive into a lake of fire burning with
brimstone."

Notice, this is a "lake of fire burning with brimstone."

Revelation 20:10---"And the devil that deceived them was cast into the
lake of fire and brimstone, where the beast and the false prophet are,
and shall be tormented day and night for ever and ever."

The Devil is cast into this same "lake of fire," and the beast and the
false prophet are still there, "And shall be T‑O‑R‑M‑E‑N‑T‑E‑D day and
night for ever and ever" (cf. Rev. 14:11).

Revelation 21:8---"But the fearful, and unbelieving, and the abominable,
and murderers, and whoremongers, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all
liars, shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and
brimstone: which is the second death."

It does not say that they are going to burn up in the fire; it says that
they are going to "have their part" in it. The smoke of this lake, which
burns with fire and brimstone, goes up "for ever and ever." The people
in it are tormented forever and ever. The Devil is in it and is
tormented forever and ever. The beast and the false prophet are in it
and are tormented forever and ever. The smoke of their torment goes up
"day and night," forever and ever. "They have no rest" day or night
forever and ever. It's very clear, if you believe what you read, as you
read it, where you find it (which many of you do not, will not, and
cannot, for various reasons. Most of these reasons are summed up in one
word---sin.)

Matthew 25:30---"And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer
darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth."

This brings up an interesting problem for a Jehovah's Witness. If "hell"
is the grave, then where, Judge Russell, is "outer darkness"? If people
are wiped out in hell as "the grave" then what happens to the people who
weep and wail and gnash teeth in outer darkness? Nothing was ever said
that their torment was going to end, nor was anything said about their
getting back from outer darkness!

Jude 13---"Raging waves of the sea, foaming out their own shame;
wandering stars, to whom is reserved the blackness of darkness for
ever."

There's no statement in Jude 13 about anybody going to the grave and
being obliterated; there's no statement about anybody going to hell and
being annihilated.

2 Peter 2:17---"These are wells without water, clouds that are carried
with a tempest; to whom the mist of darkness is reserved for ever."

This passage has no connection with the grave because it is dealing with
outer darkness.

Matthew 25:41---"Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand,
Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil
and his angels."

You just read in Revelation 20:10 where the Devil was cast into the
"lake of fire and brimstone, where the beast and the false prophet are,
and shall be tormented day and night for ever and ever." In Matthew
25:41, the Lord is saying to somebody, "Depart from me, ye cursed, into
everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels."

Notice the word "everlasting." It's the same word that is found in John
3:16.

John 3:16---"For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten
Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have
everlasting life."

Then the "everlasting fire" lasts as long as the "everlasting life."
Everlasting damnation lasts as long as everlasting salvation. The day
God expires (I'm speaking reverently) will be the day that your
damnation will end, and it won't end one minute before.

Matthew 25:46---"And these shall go away into everlasting punishment:
but the righteous into life eternal."

"Everlasting punishment." This means that there is no end to it. You may
think that it means "a long time." If that's so, is that what
"everlasting" means in John 3:16? There isn't a Jehovah's Witness who
ever lived that doesn't believe in "everlasting life," but when they
find the same word dealing with hell, they don't believe that it is
"everlasting." Aren't people strange? Why do you suppose that a man
would believe in an everlasting, eternal life which has no end, but in
an everlasting, eternal hell that was only there for awhile? (It's wild,
man!) Because all lost people have a sinful disposition and moral
reasons for rejecting a literal hell, and these reasons are pretty
apparent to somebody who has been saved from it.

Matthew 25:46---"And these shall go away into everlasting punishment:
but the righteous into life eternal."

Notice the contrast: "life eternal," and "everlasting punishment." It
does not say everlasting death, but "everlasting punishment." You can't
punish a corpse! This "punishment" is spoken of as something that they
feel with a particular emotion attached to it.

Daniel 12:2---"And many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth
shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting
contempt."

Notice that the lost have a response to this punishment. A corpse
doesn't have a response to anything! "Everlasting shame" is a feeling,
and "everlasting contempt" is an emotion. As sure as a "born-again"
believer has an everlasting life, an unsaved sinner has an everlasting
damnation with shame and contempt and punishment.

Did you ever stop to think that if the grave is "hell," then all
Christians go to "hell" when they die? All Christians go to the grave
when they die, don't they? Lazarus was saved, and they buried him; Paul
was saved, and they buried him; Peter was saved, and they buried him;
Martin Luther and John Wesley were saved, and they buried them. Do you
mean to tell me that they are in "hell"? (You see how wild people get?
They get a Greek lexicon and try to prove that one word is another word
before the poor idiots even have time to see what the Bible says about
the word.) Common sense would tell you that "the grave" is not hell. If
the grave is hell, then all dogs, cats, birds, bats, bees, Christians,
Buddhists, atheists, gophers, Catholics, Protestants, Jews, and even
Jehovah's Witnesses go to hell. They do bury Jehovah's Witnesses, don't
they? If you told a Jehovah's Witness that he was going to hell, he
would be highly indignant!

Luke 12:4--5---"And I say unto you my friends, Be not afraid of them
that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do. But I
will forewarn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, which after he hath
killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him."

You don't have to fear them that can only kill the body, because all
they can do with the body is bury it in a grave. "Fear him, which after
he hath killed hath power to cast into hell." You see, there is more to
it than just "killing the body" and burying it in the grave. God can
cast you into hell after you're dead! Any ditch digger has the power to
cast you into the grave, and the funeral director has the power to do
that too. Don't you see the absurdity of teaching that hell is "the
grave"? Going to hell is not "going to the grave." Your body goes to the
grave, and your soul goes to hell---everlasting shame, everlasting
torment, everlasting punishment. The Bible is very clear on this over
and over and over and over again. Why is it that people keep getting the
thing confused? Common sense would tell you that hell is something worse
than the grave. There's weeping and wailing and gnashing of teeth in
hell, but there's none in the grave; there's praying in hell, but
there's none in the grave. You can dig up every graveyard in this
country and you won't find any "weeping and wailing and gnashing of
teeth." You won't find any "furnace of fire" when you dig up a grave
either.

Look at the words of our Lord Jesus Christ if you are so stuck on "His
gospel."

Matthew 13:42---"And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall
be wailing and gnashing of teeth."

"Cast them." You just read in Luke 12:5 that you are to fear Him that is
able to "cast into hell." God does not chuck you into a "Woodlawn"
cemetery!

Matthew 13:50---"And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there
shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth."

"The furnace of fire." This is a particular one. Somebody said that it
was just a parable, but this is one place where you are badly mistaken
because the parable had just been explained.

Matthew 13:36--40---"Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into
the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the
parable of the tares of the field. He answered and said unto them, \[1\]
He that soweth the good seed IS the Son of man; \[2\] The field IS the
world; \[3\] the good seed ARE the children of the kingdom; \[4\] but
the tares ARE the children of the wicked one; \[5\] The enemy that sowed
them IS the devil; \[6\] the harvest IS the end of the world; \[7\] and
the reapers ARE the angels. As therefore the tares are gathered and
burned in \[8\] the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world."

The fire is what? Oh, it's the fire! Don't you see what He just did? He
gave a rule, or a meaning, for everything in the parable except "the
fire." Do you know why? Because "the fire" is f‑i‑r‑e! Is that clear? Do
you know what the tares are? They're the children of the wicked one. Do
you know who the enemy is? He's the Devil. Do you know what the field
is? It's the world. Do you know who the sower is? It's Jesus. Do you
know what the good seed is? It's the children of the kingdom. Do you
know what the harvest is? It's the end of the world. Do you know who the
reapers are? They're the angels. Do you know what the fire is?

It's fire, doctor, just unsheeted flame!

There isn't any meaning given for "fire" because "the fire" is "the
fire."

Matthew 3:11--12---"I indeed baptize you with water unto repentance: but
he that cometh after me is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not worthy
to bear: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with fire: Whose
fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and gather
his wheat into the garner; but he will burn up the chaff with
unquenchable fire."

You hear Christians praying that they'll get the "baptism of the Holy
Ghost and fire." You hadn't better get that baptism of fire, friend, or
you'll fry like a hot dog in hell for notice, the "fire" you are
baptized with here is "unquenchable." It's hell‑fire! It's not a fire
that you put out. A fellow says, "I've got the baptism of the Holy Ghost
and fire." Well, how come the fire isn't sitting on your head? Matthew
3:12 is "unquenchable" fire. The "wheat" gets the baptism of the Holy
Ghost and goes into the garner. The "chaff" is the part that is no good
and gets the "baptism of fire."

Acts 1:5---"For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized
with the Holy Ghost not many days hence."

Notice that there is no "fire." The Holy Spirit is very careful to point
this out. (People who hate the Lord Jesus Christ and defy the Holy
Spirit never can read too carefully.) Somebody will say, "But, in Acts
2, etc."

Acts 2:3---"And there appeared unto them cloven tongues like as of fire,
and it sat upon each of them."

They were not baptized with fire, but "like as of fire." Not fire, but
like fire. You see how careful the Holy Spirit is to correct the
Pentecostal groups. Pentecostal convictions mean absolutely nothing to
the Holy Spirit at all. Regardless of what you may think about me for
criticizing other religions, where the Holy Spirit writes the word of
God, He doesn't show any respect for anybody's "convictions."

Notice that when Simon Peter quotes Matthew 3:12, that he omits, "and
with fire."

Acts 11:16---"Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said,
John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy
Ghost."

Notice---no fire!! Nobody in the Book of Acts was ever baptized with
fire, except the lost sinners who went to hell. Baptism with fire is the
wrong baptism.

Now to digress for a few minutes, let us take up the verses used by the
Jehovah's Witnesses, Seventh-day Adventists, Campbellites, Catholics,
etc. to prove that a man does not burn in hell. (Of course, the Catholic
is kind of a chameleon; he splits the middle and says that you burn for
awhile and then get out!)

Malachi 4:1, 3---"For, behold, the day cometh, that shall burn as an
oven; and all the proud, yea, and all that do wickedly, shall be
stubble: and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of
hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch." "And ye shall
tread down the wicked; for they shall be ashes under the soles of your
feet in the day that I shall do this, saith the Lord of hosts."

The statement in Malachi 4:1 is that the wicked are going to be burned
up, and nothing left but ashes (Mal. 4:3). This is the proof text for
the Jehovah's Witnesses' teaching of "annihilation." It is typical of
the fouled up exegesis carried on in our country by people who think
that they are "believers." Look at the passage. (You don't have to have
any education to get it.)

"For, behold, the DAY cometh, that shall burn as an oven." "In the DAY
that I shall do this." "Before the coming of the great and dreadful DAY
of the Lord" (Mal. 4:1, 3, 5).

There's nothing in the passage on hell. There's nothing about a man
dying now and going to hell. It's talking about the Second Coming of
Christ in the "day of the Lord."

Malachi 4:1---"For, behold, the DAY cometh, that shall burn as an oven;
and all the proud, yea, and all that do wickedly, shall be stubble: and
the DAY that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of hosts, that it
shall leave them neither root nor branch."

Their bodies are "the ashes," in Malachi 4:3. When the saints come back
at the Second Coming of Jesus Christ and go over that pile of dead
corpses, they literally step on the ashes. But there is nothing about a
place called "hell" under the earth, or a lake of fire in eternity.
There is not one single mention of hell in Malachi 4.

Proverbs 2:22---"But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the
transgressors shall be rooted out of it."

Notice how very careful the Holy Spirit is to show you that the place
where the wicked are "cut off," and become "stubble," has to do with
their being consumed off the face of this earth. It has no reference to
any conditions in the life hereafter.

Christ says, "And now also the ax is laid unto the root of the trees."

Out goes the root! They are going to be "stubble" under your feet, and
"it shall leave them neither root nor branch" (Mal. 4:1). No root, no
branch. Why? They've been rooted out (Prov. 2:22). The wicked shall be
cut off; the wicked shall be as stubble; the wicked shall be rooted out;
the wicked shall have no root or branch. Nothing could be plainer. In
every context it is talking about the wicked disappearing off the face
of this earth by being killed.

You'll notice that these favorite passages to prove "annihilation" are
found in the Old Testament. You don't often find any man damning himself
to hell with a passage after the resurrection, although a Campbellite
will do it occasionally!

Now, let's look at a typical passage that a man will use to prove that
one is unconscious in hell, or annihilated in hell, or knows nothing in
hell, and therefore couldn't feel any flame if it did burn him. (In
order to get this passage, you have to go back on the other side of
Calvary again, and pretend that you don't have any Pauline Epistles to
read!)

Ecclesiastes 9:4--6---"For to him that is joined to all the living there
is hope: for a living dog is better than a dead lion. For the living
know that they shall die: but the dead know not any thing, neither have
they any more a reward; for the memory of them is forgotten. Also their
love, and their hatred, and their envy, is now perished; neither have
they any more a portion for ever in any thing that is done under the
sun."

The statement is that "the dead know not anything," and if the dead
"know not anything," how can they feel the fires of hell burning them?
That's the kind of exegesis a no‑heller would give on the passage.
Again, this is a rather stupid way to go about things. After all, the
verses are the expression of a man's opinion "under the sun." This man,
who is giving his opinion, tells you that it is just his opinion.

Ecclesiastes 1:1---"The words of the Preacher, the son of David, king in
Jerusalem."

The Book of Ecclesiastes never professes to be Christian doctrine, given
by the Holy Spirit. It is the "words of the Preacher, the son of David,
king in Jerusalem." It is not a series of statements on New Testament
Christian doctrine. The Book of Ecclesiastes is the book of Solomon, the
wisest man who ever lived, who tried to figure out life and death from
what had been revealed to him. At the time that Solomon tried to figure
things out, half of the Bible had not even been written. Appeal to such
a book is typical of people who wrest the Scriptures "unto their own
destruction" (2 Pet. 3:16).

The Book of Ecclesiastes is not a book of Christian doctrine. The
expression "under the sun" occurs twenty-nine times in the book. In
Ecclesiastes 1:3, 9, 14, 2:11, 17, 18, 19, 20, 22, 3:16, 4:1, 3, 7, 15,
5:13, 18, 6:1, 12, 8:9, 15 (twice), 17, 9:3, 6, 9 (twice), 11, 13, 10:5,
you are told that this book is describing life "under the sun." There's
no reference in the book about somebody burning under the earth or out
in eternity. This is a man (the natural man) trying to figure out things
by the amount of natural revelation which he has. Anybody could tell
this by looking at the conclusion of the book.

Ecclesiastes 12:13--14---"Let us hear the conclusion of the whole
matter: Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty
of man. For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret
thing, whether it be good, or whether it be evil."

The summation of the whole book, "Fear God, and keep his commandments,"
is not the gospel. The gospel is that "Christ died for our sins
according to the scriptures; And that he was buried, and that he rose
again the third day according to the scriptures" (1 Cor. 15:1--6).
Therefore, if you want to get your doctrines on heaven, hell, and New
Testament salvation straightened out, you'd better believe the New
Testament first and Brother Solomon second. Don't you go to Solomon for
an opinion on New Testament truth that he's just getting by partial
revelation.

This brings up the problem of progressive revelation again, one of the
greatest problems found in the religious world today. There are two
extremes to which a man will go: the first position is the position of
the liberal who says, "I don't believe the Bible is the word of God."
But, suppose he gets saved and believes that it is the word of God. Now,
do you know what the Devil will do with him? He'll push him over the
other way. The man will say, "I believe all of it is the word of God."
So, he'll try to keep all of it, and you cannot do this without making
it contradict. Then you'll be right back where you were as an unsaved
liberal because all unsaved liberals believe that it contradicts! To
illustrate this further:

1\. "I believe everything in the Book is what God said, and all the
Bible is for us, and you've got to keep all of it or none." That's the
talk of a demented man, who is deceived by the Devil, although he may be
saved.

2\. "It's just man's opinion. The word of God may be found in it, but it
is man's ideas, philosophy, and legends." That's the talk of an unsaved
man. The Devil pushes you one way or the other.

Now, the saved, solid, Christian approach is this:

Everything in the Bible God wanted me to read, but not everything in the
Bible is aimed at me. For example: Jesus said that the twelve Apostles
should go preach "to the lost sheep of the house of Israel." That's not
for me! All the Bible is for me to read, all the Bible is to help me,
all of it is for exhortation, all of it is for comfort, all of it is for
me to learn from, but not all of it is aimed at me. The Bible is a book
that took more than 2,000 years to write, and it is written on three
different continents by thirty-eight different authors. So don't you
think for a minute that you're going to get a final revelation on hell
in Ecclesiastes or 2 Chronicles or Jeremiah! You're not going to have
the completed revelation until you finish reading the Book of
Revelation. You don't have the complete, absolute, final, thorough
doctrine right until you have the whole thing.

The truth remains that if you are going to turn to the Book of
Ecclesiastes or Proverbs or Malachi to prove that a soul burns up in
hell, all you've proved is that you yourself are going to hell like a
bullet, and you're not going to burn up when you get there. The New
Testament revelation is very clear and very plain about both of these
facts.

John 5:28--29---"Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the
which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice, And shall come
