forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they
that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation."

"The resurrection of damnation." Is that clear? Jesus said, "How can ye
escape the damnation of hell?" (Matt. 23:33). If the "damnation of hell"
was being "annihilated in the grave," there wouldn't be much to it. The
"damnation of hell" is not being buried in a hole in the ground.

Matthew 5:22---"But I say unto you, That whosoever is angry with his
brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment: and
whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the
council: but whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell
fire."

Why did he say "hell fire"? There's no fire in the grave! If the grave
and hell are the same, what is the fire doing there?

Matthew 5:29---"And if thy right eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast
it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members
should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell."

If hell is the grave, it means that it would be better for you to cut
off arms and legs than to die and go into the grave. But wait a minute!
Everybody dies and goes to the grave. Why isn't everybody pulling out
their eyes and cutting off their limbs? You see how absurd and
ridiculous this is? You'll go to the grave, friend, whether you cut
something off or not! Jesus is saying here that there is such a
terrible, hell‑fire-and-damnation, horrible, burning, eternal,
punishment ahead that you would do better to go through life as a saved
person with one eye and one arm, than to go through life healthy and
lost and wind up in hell.

How can "hell" be the "grave"? The answer is that it isn't, never has
been, and never will be.

In the final analysis, there is no difference between a Jehovah's
Witness and a Roman Catholic in regard to hell. The only difference is
the manner in which they get rid of hell. The Roman Catholic pretends
that he is going to purgatory instead of hell, and the Jehovah's Witness
pretends that he is going to the grave instead of hell. I don't care
what kind of LSD you use to have a pipe‑dream with, it'll deceive you
and hurt you and ruin you and harm you. You'd better admit you are a
sinner and receive Jesus Christ (John 1:12), or you're going to go to a
place where there is everlasting shame, everlasting torment, everlasting
smoke, everlasting punishment, everlasting damnation; and you're going
to burn, burn, burn, burn, burn, and never get out.

When the Devil said to Eve, "Ye shall not surely die," that's not the
truth. That's Scripture, but it is not the truth. When Job says, "The
tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure;
into whose hand God bringeth abundantly," it is Scripture (Job 12:46);
but there isn't a word of truth in it. It is only true that what is
recorded is what God wanted recorded, and He moved men to record it and
sort it out; and what He wanted in, He preserved in the Bible. But it is
not true that every statement of every man in the Bible is Christian
doctrine, applied by the Holy Spirit.

The Bible is very carefully written. The Bible is a wonderful Book, and
one of the great proofs of the infallibility and the inspiration of the
Scriptures is the fact that it is so written that if any man comes to it
trying to find a verse to teach something that is not Scriptural (which
the other verses don't bear witness to), he has to violate the rules of
common sense in order to get his teaching. The Bible is a "two‑edged
sword." It can save you, or it can ruin you. It is the only Book ever
penned that is so written that it anticipates the thoughts of the reader
before he thinks them, and then lays a trap for him if his thoughts
aren't right and sincere and sound (Ezek. 14:1--11). It is a moral Book
written by a Holy God, and it is designed for the express purpose of
upsetting and destroying and damning and ruining every man, church,
bishop, priest, pope, preacher, teacher, and professing Christian, who
in any way distorts any part of it to make it match what he thinks is
right (2 Thess. 2:1--13). It's composition is finer than the structure
of a Swiss watch, and it is put together more wonderfully than the human
body.

14:13 "And I heard a voice from heaven saying unto me, Write, Blessed
are the dead which die in the Lord from henceforth: Yea, saith the
Spirit, that they may rest from their labours; and their works do follow
them."

Notice very carefully: people who die in the Tribulation have their
works follow them, and they are judged for these works later. Your
situation in the Church Age is entirely different. Your works go ahead
of you, and you'll meet your works at the Judgment Seat of Christ. (When
the Judgment Seat of Christ is over, your judgment will be past, as a
Christian.) These saints in the Tribulation, have to get judged sometime
in the future. Since the Judgment Seat of Christ takes place during the
Tribulation, it is very apparent that the saints who are saved in the
Tribulation are not judged the same way those saved in the Church Age
are, and they are not judged at the same time and place as those in the
Church Age. Tribulation saints are judged at the Great White Throne
Judgment (see Rev. 11:18).

14:14 "And I looked, and behold a white cloud, and upon the cloud one
sat like unto the Son of man, having on his head a golden crown, and in
his hand a sharp sickle."

The reference, of course, is to the Lord Jesus Christ. This is the Lord
Jesus Christ, represented here as the "Angel of the Lord." The statement
is not just "the Son of man," but "one sat like unto the Son of man."
(See Rev. 10:1, the angel appearing in a cloud.) Then this is an
appearance of the Lord Jesus thrist in the Tribulation sometime before
the actual Advent, or His landing on the earth. When He actually comes
to land on earth, He comes on horseback with an army (Rev. 19:11, Joel
2:1--11). This army of horsemen stops their charge at the Mount of
Olives, Christ gets off His horse, stands on the ground, the earth
splits under Him, then He gets back on His white Arabian steed and
marches through the eastern gate into Jerusalem (Zech. 14:1--21).

But in the appearance found in Revelation 14:14, the Lord is pictured as
seated on a throne, or beside a throne. This is not the appearance of
Revelation 19:11, where He is sitting on a horse with "many crowns" on
His head. This is a Pre‑Advent appearance. The exact time of this
appearance is not given, and I don't profess to know when it's going to
be. I do know that there is an appearance of Christ before the end of
the Tribulation, just like Joseph appeared to his brethren before the
seven years of famine were finished. This will be like Paul's
conversion, who was saved in a Post‑Resurrection appearance of Jesus
Christ on the road to Damascus, and then began to preach to the
Gentiles. The 144,000 are going to see Jesus Christ during the
Tribulation, and they will preach to the Gentiles before the Advent. (I
do not profess to know the time element of Rev. 14:14. It is highly
possible that it will not be revealed until the end time, and then it
will be revealed to the Jews during the Tribulation.)

14:15 "And another angel came out of the temple, crying with a loud
voice to him that sat on the cloud, Thrust in thy sickle, and reap: for
the time is come for thee to reap; for the harvest of the earth is
ripe."

The cross references here are Isaiah 17:6, 26:20--21; Jeremiah 6:9;
James 5:7; and Matthew 13:39. It is generally taught that Revelation
14:20 is one complete picture of the Second Coming. But this is not the
case. What you actually have here is a picture of Jesus Christ appearing
before the end of the Tribulation, and then an interval followed by the
actual Second Coming at the end of the seven years. This truth is
learned by noticing the difference in the wording---notice that two
different types of agricultural activities are described.

"Thrust in thy sickle, and reap: for the time is come for thee to reap;
for the h‑a‑r‑v‑e‑s‑t of the earth is ripe."

14:16 "And he that sat on the cloud thrust in his sickle on the earth;
and the e‑a‑r‑t‑h was reaped."

This is the passage in Matthew 13:39 and Isaiah 66:8. Notice in
Revelation 14:18--19 that it is not the harvest of the earth, and it is
not the earth that is being reaped.

14:17--18 "And another angel came out of the temple which is in heaven,
he also having a sharp sickle. And another angel came out from the
altar, which had power over fire; and cried with a loud cry to him that
had the sharp sickle, saying, Thrust in thy sharp sickle, and gather the
clusters of the vine of the earth; for her grapes are fully ripe."

The harvest of the earth is reaped and gathered into the barn, as in
Matthew 13, and this harvest, which has to do with fire, burns
something, as in Matthew 13. Note the difference: one is the "harvest of
the earth"---time for the earth to be reaped, but the other is the
"clusters of the vine of the earth." The "vine of the earth" is a very,
very important statement.

14:19 "And the angel thrust in his sickle into the earth, and gathered
the vine of the earth, and cast it into the great winepress of the wrath
of God."

In Revelation 14:15--16 a sickle is put into the "harvest of the earth,"
like a wheat crop. The grain is harvested and reaped for God and put
into the barn (Deut. 16:9; Isa. 17:6; Matt. 13:39; James 5:7), but here
in Revelation 14:18--19, we find something that has incurred the wrath
of God, and God wishes it to be destroyed; it is called the "vine of the
earth." It is not "gathered into the barn," but it is cast into "the
winepress of God," and then it is stamped on until blood comes out of it
three feet high for a space of about 160 miles! (This shows us clearly
that the Second Advent is found in Rev. 14:18--19, while a
post‑Tribulation Rapture is found in Rev. 14:15--16.)

"The vine of the earth" is a reference to a passage in Deuteronomy
32:31, and notice that the reference is to the Roman Catholic Church.
(So the reference has been removed in the Roman Catholic Bible!)

Deuteronomy 32:31---"For their rock is not as our Rock, even our enemies
themselves being judges."

Notice the first "rock," small "r"---Simon Peter; the next "Rock,"
capital "R"---Jesus Christ. The context is Israel; therefore, the
enemies referred to are the enemies of Israel.

Deuteronomy 32:32---"For their vine is of the vine of Sodom, and of the
fields of Gomorrah: their grapes are grapes of gall, their clusters are
bitter."

Compare Jeremiah 24:2, and see commentary on Genesis, Genesis 4:3--6.

The reference is to a "vine" that God has no use for, and He is going to
curse it and wipe it out.

Deuteronomy 32:33---"Their wine is the poison of dragons, and. the cruel
venom of asps." (Compare Psa. 16:4 and Deut. 32:38.)

The thing that characterizes this "vine of the earth" (Rev. 14) is the
fact that this "vine of the earth" produces grapes which turn out to be
fermented liquor (like the intoxicating wine used by the Roman Catholic
Church in its imitation of the blood of the Lord Jesus Christ). This is
the vine that God intends to wipe out in Revelation 14:18--19. This wine
is also connected with "Babylon the Great" in Revelation 17:4, 6 and
Revelation 18:3, and the woman, who is a wino (a liquorhead, a lush),
who professes to be the "one true church" that Jesus Christ founded! She
is a chronic alcoholic, a dipsomaniac. This "vine of the earth" in
Revelation 14 pictures the entire mass of unsaved religious people, who
will be literally stamped out under His feet until the blood is all over
His clothes!

Isaiah 63:1--4, 6---"Who is this that cometh from Edom \[south of the
Dead Sea\], with dyed garments from Bozrah \[south of the Dead Sea\],
this that is glorious in his apparel, travelling in the greatness of his
strength? \[answer\] I that speak in righteousness, mighty to save.
\[question\] Wherefore art thou red in thine apparel, and thy garments
like him that treadeth in the winefat? \[answer\] I have trodden the
winepress \[cf. Rev. 14:20\] alone; and of the people there was none
with me: for I will tread them in mine anger, and trample them in my
fury; and their blood shall be sprinkled upon my garments, and I will
stain all my raiment. For the day of vengeance is in mine heart, and the
year of my redeemed is come." "And I will tread down the people in mine
anger, and make them D‑R‑U‑N‑K in my fury, and I will bring down their
strength to the earth."

There is no doubt about this passage at all. This is not a loving,
kindly, heavenly Saviour who comes saying, "Blessed are the pure in
heart: for they shall see God." This is a roaring lion, coming for
vengeance, who stamps on his enemies until their blood is splashed all
over His clothes.

Jeremiah 25:30---"Therefore prophesy thou against them all these words,
and say unto them, The Lord shall R‑O‑A‑R \[There goes the lion of the
tribe of Judah!\] from on high, and utter his voice from his holy
habitation; he shall mightily R‑O‑A‑R upon his habitation; he shall give
a shout, as they that tread the grapes, against all the inhabitants of
the earth."

Revelation 19:13, 15---"And he was clothed with a vesture dipped in
blood: and his name is called The Word of God." "And out of his mouth
goeth a sharp sword, that with it he should smite the nations: and he
shall rule them with a rod of iron: and he T‑R‑E‑A‑D‑E‑T‑H \[stomp down,
walk on it\] the W‑I‑N‑E‑P‑R‑E‑S‑S of the fierceness and wrath of
Almighty God."

14:20 "And the winepress was trodden without the city, and blood came
out of the winepress, even unto the horse bridles, by the space of a
thousand and six hundred furlongs."

Notice that this is outside Jerusalem, "without the gate" or "without
the camp"---Hebrews 13. Now let's get it all together and see what these
verses teach. They present a picture so horrible and so bloody that
preachers, en masse, have finally rejected the literal interpretation of
the Book of Revelation. They portray a "Christ" so foreign to the
concept of this modern milk‑sop age of ecclesiastical Communism and
Liberal Ecumenism that the modern minister cannot stomach the Bible
presentation. The Bible picture, here, is so terrible in its implication
that it would freeze your blood in your veins if you took it literally,
but that is exactly how we Bible‑believing Christians are going to take
it! The passages that deal with the Second Coming of the Lord Jesus
never deal with the times of:

"Gentle Jesus, meek and mild,

Hearken to a little child.

Fain would I to Thee be brought

Gracious Lord, forbid it not."

These things are all true of His first coming as the "Lamb of God, which
taketh away the sin of the world," but His Second Coming is something
else. Joel calls it the "day of darkness," the "day of gloominess," a
"day of clouds," and "thick darkness." There is nothing positive about
this description.

Joel 1:15---"Alas for the day! for the day of the Lord is at hand, and
as a destruction from the Almighty shall it come."

This great, tremendous, negative preaching, which makes up more than
half of the word of God, has been rejected by preachers today because
they are in sympathy with the people against whom the verses are aimed.

Nahum 1:2--6---"God is jealous, and the Lord revengeth; the Lord
revengeth, and is furious; the Lord will take vengeance on his
adversaries, and he reserveth wrath for his enemies. The Lord is slow to
anger, and great in power, and will not at all acquit the wicked: the
Lord hath his way in the whirlwind and in the storm, and the clouds are
the dust of his feet. He rebuketh the sea, and maketh it dry, and drieth
up all the rivers: Bashan languisheth, and Carmel, and the flower of
Lebanon languisheth. The mountains quake at him, and the hills melt, and
the earth is burned at his presence, yea, the world, and all that dwell
therein. Who can stand before his indignation? and who can abide in the
fierceness of his anger? his fury is poured out like fire, and the rocks
are thrown down by him."

This is the Bible photograph of the Second Coming of Jesus Christ in the
"day of the Lord." If you want to know the real reason why the Roman
Catholic Church, the Episcopal Church, the Presbyterian Church, the
Lutheran Church, the Baptist Church, and the Methodist Church have
rejected the Premillennial Second Coming of Jesus Christ, literally and
visibly, to reign on this earth, it is because the Scriptural prophecies
that accompany this particular event in history are all negative. They
all speak of wrath, fierceness, vengeance, rage, fury, jealousy,
fighting, killing, stomping, and blood coming out of someone till it is
running three feet deep on the ground. In plainer words, the Bible is
not rejected by "modern man" because it is "hard to understand," the
Bible is not revised because it is "obscure," the Bible is not laid
aside because it is "apocalyptic and symbolical;" but it is rejected
because it is against man, and, therefore, man is against it! (The
quicker you understand that, the straighter you'll get your Bible  
doctrines laid out.)

Let us examine a few more references to go with these passages---Psalm
68:23; Judges 5:21; Joel 3:13; Genesis 49:11; Isaiah 63:1, 34:5;
Habakkuk 3:15. These passages all make it abundantly clear that the
Second Coming of Jesus Christ is like an Old Testament Jew putting
grapes into a winepress, and then walking around, crushing out the
grapes with his bare feet, until the juice comes up to his ankles and
knees, and stains the short‑skirt robe which he is holding up. Back in
those days, they had a cement circle on the ground, about six feet or
eight feet in diameter, hollow in the middle, like an angel‑food cake
pan. The grapes were dumped into the circular part of this foundry,
which had spigots at intervals through which the juice would run into
leather bottles. Then, the men who would tread the grapes would take off
their shoes, wash their feet and legs, walk around and around the
foundry on the grapes until the grapes were all mashed under their feet,
and the whole foundry was running two feet or three feet in grape juice,
and the juice was running out the spigots on the side. This is "the
winepress."

In the passages you've just read, the Second Coming of Jesus Christ, or
the "day of the Lord" is not described as Christ dying on the cross; the
"day of the Lord" is not pictured as Christ being virgin‑born and
resurrected; the "day of the Lord" is not a picture of the "Sermon on
the Mount" or the "destruction of Jerusalem." The "day of the Lord" is a
picture of Jesus Christ coming back to Palestine and landing on the
heads of 200 million United Nations cavalrymen, and squashing them so
flat under His feet that their blood runs three feet deep down the land
of Palestine, through the rivers, for a space of about 160 miles. When
Christ comes back on His white horse (which we'll study later), this
blood comes up so high that the horses' bridles are dipped in this
blood, and the saints who return with Christ dip their feet in this
blood (Psa. 68:23). A truly terrifying picture! But this is the picture
of the Second Advent of Jesus Christ. The first time He came, He came as
a Lamb; but the next time He comes, He comes as a raging, roaring lion
(Isa. 31:4)!

When the song writer wrote, "He is tramping out the vintage where the
grapes of wrath are stored," she uttered one of the biggest lies a woman
ever uttered since the lie of Genesis 3. No man or woman on this earth
but John the Apostle has ever seen "the glory of the coming of the
Lord." And it will take place in the future. The song might be called
the "Battle Hymn of the Northern Republic Army," but judged by the Book
from which its lyrics were extracted, it remains as one of the most
monstrous perversions of Scripture ever given, and it is quite typical
of the Postmillennial and Amillennial systems which relegate the events
of the Book of Revelation to the past. The passages you've just read
(Rev. 14) all lie in the immediate future. I hope you are on the right
side.

CHAPTER 15

In the study of the Book of Revelation, we have seen already three
pictures of the Second Coming of Christ: (1) Revelation 6:12--17; (2)
Revelation 11:13--16; and (3) Revelation 14:18--20. We have seen, also,
three pictures of the Tribulation: (1) Seals, Revelation 5--7; (2)
Trumpets, Revelation 8--11; (3) Antichrist, Revelation 11--14. We are
now ready to study the Tribulation for the fourth and final time, under
the figure of "vials." (A "vial" is like a small perfume bottle, a
bottle with a large rounded base and a slim neck.)

15:1 "And I saw another sign in heaven, great and marvellous, seven
angels having the seven last plagues; for in them is filled up the wrath
of God."

This probably indicates that the Tribulation saints have gone when these
events take place. Certainly, the Church has disappeared, because "God
hath not appointed us to wrath" according to 1 Thessalonians 5:8--10. We
are told that the Christian is not reserved for this wrath, but the
people on earth at the time of Revelation 15 are.

It would seem that the vials match the trumpets, but the only trouble is
that the vials do not begin until the middle of Daniel's Seventieth
Week, or later.

Revelation 16:2 "And the first went, and poured out his vial upon the
earth; and there fell a noisome and grievous sore upon the men which had
the mark of the beast, and upon them which worshipped his image."

When these vials of wrath begin to be poured out, men have already
received the "mark of the beast." Since the beast does not begin to give
his mark until the middle of the Tribulation (Rev. 13), then the vials
are not poured out until the last three and one-half years. (If they are
simultaneous with the trumpets, then the trumpets are not until the last
three and one-half years.)

15:2 "And I saw as it were a sea of glass mingled with fire: and them
that had gotten the victory over the beast, and over his image, and over
his mark, and over the number of his name, stand on the sea of glass,
having the harps of God."

We have discussed this "sea of glass" before. (See comments on Rev.
4:6.) Our present text shows us very plainly that there is a
post‑Tribulation Rapture. These saints (whom John sees) match those in
Revelation 14:2--3, 4:6, 7:13--15, and this shows conclusively that
these people (who "get the victory" over the beast in the Tribulation)
are "caught up" to the third heaven, and stand on the sea of glass
before the Advent takes place.

15:3 "And they sing the song of Moses the servant of God, and the song
of the Lamb, saying, Great and marvellous are thy works, Lord God
Almighty; just and true are thy ways, thou King of saints."

"The song of Moses" is the song found in the Old Testament in
Deuteronomy 32. This is very important because it means that Deuteronomy
32 awaits future fulfillment, and that it was not fulfilled at the first
coming of Jesus Christ. (As we have said before, and we will keep
repeating until the Lord comes, there isn't any system of interpretation
that is sane, solid, or even "Christian" except the Premillennial
system. A man who does not believe in a literal, physical, visible
coming of Jesus Christ to set up a one thousand year kingdom upon this
earth is not a Bible‑believing Christian. He may be a saved sinner, but
a Bible‑believing Christian he is not.) Anybody, even a
Postmillennialist or an Amilliennialist, will admit that Revelation 15
is written after the resurrection and ascension of Christ. Yet, the
people who "get the victory" in this book cannot be "Church Age"
Christians, for they sing the "song of Moses," who was a Jew, who lived
and died under the law and the Ten Commandments.

Notice again in Revelation 15:3 the combination of faith and works. Not
only do they sing the "song of Moses," but the "song of the Lamb."
(Compare Rev. 14:12.) Salvation in the Tribulation is "by grace through
faith," but a man is kept (in the Tribulation) by keeping the
commandments of God given to Moses in the Old Testament.

"Thou King of saints." Jesus Christ, in this dispensation, the Church
Age, is never called a "King," and He is never referred to as a "King."
Don't let the Campbellite fool you with his talk about "translated us
into the kingdom,"(Col. 1:13.) They say that a kingdom has to have a
king, so Christ is king over the Christian. Christ is the Head of a
spiritual body of born‑again believers, but He is King over the Kingdom
of Heaven, a body of Jews. He is called "the King of the Jews," and He
does not become "King of Kings and Lord of Lords" (Rev. 19) until the
Second Advent. To teach that He is King now, over the Christians, is to
pervert the word of God. You can be born‑again into the kingdom of God,
but you do not come into subjection under a Jewish King, who is King
over the Kingdom of Heaven. You become part of a body in the Kingdom of
God that is subject to Jesus Christ, as Head of the Body (Eph. 5). The
Christian in this age is part of a living body. I'm not the subject of a
king in a kingdom---I belong to a body of which Jesus Christ is my Head,
my Lord, and my Saviour. My "kingdom" doesn't come until He comes, and
when He comes as "King of kings and Lord of lords" to set up His
kingdom, then I shall reign.

Those of us who have been "baptized into one body" (1 Cor. 12:13) by the
Holy Spirit, know a King when we see one. But you little religious
politicians who are trying to reign now "as kings," need to get your
theology straightened out. (You're no king; you're a mess!) The Bible
says that the Christian is a "king" and a "priest" when Christ comes
(Rev. 1); he is a "joint‑heir" with Christ (Gal. 3). When Christ comes
back, the Christian will reign over this earth with Him (Luke 19; 2 Tim.
2; Rom. 8). If you think you're reigning now, brother, you're mistaken.
You don't even look like a king, much less act like one or talk like
one! You have no authority. I doubt if some of you even have any
calling, and if you're reigning now, you're reigning in the wrong
dispensation. This dispensation is not for "kings"; it's for servants
carrying crosses.

15:4 "Who shall not fear thee, O Lord, and glorify thy name? for thou
only art holy: for all nations shall come and worship before thee; for
thy judgments are made manifest."

Notice the reference to the Second Coming, "all nations shall come and
worship before thee." This is the theme of Zechariah 14; this is the
theme of Isaiah 2:1--4; this is the theme of Micah 4:1--2; this is the
theme of the Old Testament, where the nations have to come to Jerusalem
to worship, and the Jew is established as head over the whole earth.
This is the entire burden of Psalm 72; this is also the meaning of Psalm
110. About three‑fourths of the Old Testament deals with this subject,
when all the nations are gathered to Israel and to Jerusalem to worship
the God of Jacob---the Second Coming! Revelation 15:4 is not a reference
to the first coming of Jesus to die on Calvary! Never! This is the
burden of Psalm 96; this is the entire teaching of Psalm 97. This is the
time when Jesus Christ comes as "King of the Jews" to reign over the
earth on the "throne of David," not the right hand of the heavenly
throne. He will be in Jerusalem, when He comes, not in the third heaven.
He will sit on the throne, and not at the right hand of the throne. He
reigns over the House of Jacob, not the church. As "King of kings and
Lord of lords," Russia, Germany, the United States, South America, etc.
are subject to Him, which they never have been.

15:5--8 "And after that I looked, and, behold, the temple of the
tabernacle of the testimony in heaven was opened: And the seven angels
came out of the temple, having the seven plagues, clothed in pure and
white linen, and having their breasts girded with golden girdles. And
one of the four beasts gave unto the seven angels seven golden vials
full of the wrath of God, who liveth for ever and ever. And the temple
was filled with smoke from the glory of God, and from his power; and no
man was able to enter into the temple, till the seven plagues of the
seven angels were fulfilled."

This is the "temple" in heaven which was referred to in Revelation
14:17, 11:19, 8:3--5. These are the seven last plagues, which fall in
judgment upon the inhabitants of this earth for rejecting the Lord Jesus
Christ. They will take place some time near the end of the Tribulation.

CHAPTER 16

There is no doubt about this chapter as far as the placement of time is
concerned. Nothing like this has ever taken place yet. These events are
clearly relegated to Daniel's Seventieth Week---the seven years of the
Tribulation, following the Rapture of the Church. These events deal with
the last half of this particular time, and perhaps, even the last few
months. They refer to the very end of time, immediately preceding the
Second Coming of Jesus Christ. When a man approaches the Bible humbly,
in the fear of God, believing that it is God's word, submitting himself
to its authority, and accepting it above his church, then that man can
compare Scripture with Scripture and get the truth without interpreting
anything. As you can see in this commentary, that is the attitude by
which we have approached the Scriptures. We have approached the
scriptures from the start assuming that whatever the AV 1611 Bible says
is the final authority, regardless of anything or anybody connected with
it directly or indirectly. The only presupposition we've made is to
suppose that God knows what He is doing, and He has given us what He
intended us to have, the way He intended us to have it.

16:1 "And I heard a great voice out of the temple saying to the seven
angels, Go your ways, and pour out the vials of the wrath of God upon
the earth."

These are the final vials of wrath which are "poured out" at the end of
the Tribulation, and they sum up the wrath of God against man for
rejecting His Son, the Lord Jesus Christ. This truth may be distasteful
to the liberal ear, or to a nation raised on Life, Look, Time, etc.;
nevertheless, God has a controversy with men and "His wrath abides" on
the unbeliever (John 3:36). God is not going to remain silent forever
about the course of this age which has rejected His Son. Out come the
"vials of the wrath of God" upon the earth; they are dumped out in this
present planetary system, on this particular planet on which you live.

16:2 "And the first went, and poured out his vial upon the earth; and
there fell a noisome and grievous sore upon the men which had the mark
of the beast, and upon them which worshipped his image."

The picture here is of a perfume bottle, a small bottle with a round
bottom and a thin neck, with something inside it being dumped upside
down and being poured out. These events have to begin in the second half
of Daniel's Seventieth Week because there is no "beast" or any "image"
until forty-two months before the end of the Tribulation.

Revelation 13:5---"And there was given unto him a mouth speaking great
things and blasphemies; and power was given unto him to continue forty
and two months."

The Antichrist takes over "forty‑two months."

Revelation 11:2--3---"But the court which is without the temple leave
out, and measure it not; for it is given unto the Gentiles: and the holy
city shall they tread under foot forty and two months. And I will give
power unto my two witnesses, and they shall prophesy a thousand two
hundred and threescore days, clothed in sackcloth."

The Antichrist is opposed by the "two witnesses" for "forty and two
months."

Revelation 12:6---"And the woman fled into the wilderness, where she
hath a place prepared of God, that they should feed her there a thousand
two hundred and threescore days."

Notice, the Antichrist persecutes Israel "a thousand two hundred and
threescore days" (forty-two months).

"Noisome" is an Old English word meaning "malignant," "grievous." It
means "very deep," "painful," or "hard." So the black spot which men
received is paid back by another kind of spot---"a noisome and grievous
sore."

Every indication in Scripture is that the "sore" they received for
taking the black spot is a white spot! The disease that matches this is
leprosy (Lev. 13), which is a plague; it is an Egyptian, North African
plague. Leprosy is the plague that fell upon the children of Israel in
Egypt, and they were promised that if they kept the law of the Lord and
kept His commandments that He would bring upon them none of the
"diseases of Egypt" (Exod. 15:26). This North African disease, leprosy,
like the North African animal, the leopard, is the disease of the
Tribulation.

Leviticus 13:2, 4, 19, 24, 28---"When a man shall have in the skin of
his flesh a rising, a scab, or BRIGHT SPOT, and it be in the skin of his
flesh like the plague of leprosy; then he shall be brought unto Aaron
the priest, or unto one of his sons the priests." "If the BRIGHT SPOT be
WHITE in the skin of his flesh, and in sight be not deeper than the
skin, etc." "And in the place of the boil there be a WHITE rising, or a
bright SPOT, WHITE, and somewhat reddish, and it be shewed to the
priest." "Or if there be any flesh, in the skin whereof there is a hot
burning, and the quick flesh that burneth have a WHITE BRIGHT SPOT,
somewhat reddish, or WHITE." "And if the BRIGHT SPOT stay in his place,
and spread not in the skin, etc."

These references to the "spot" are talking about leprosy. Leviticus 13
is talking about a disease or plague that is characterized by a "bright
white spot." We have already identified the "mark of the beast" as a
"black spot" in the tribulation.

There is one man in particular, in the Bible, who is healed of leprosy.
He has the "bright spot," and has to go under the water before he is
cleansed, s‑e‑v‑e‑n times---one time for each year of the Tribulation (2
Kings 5)!

Moses has an Ethiopian wife. (Notice the judgment that falls on Aaron
and Miriam is not just for criticizing his wife, but for criticizing the
way in which God had spoken to Moses in spite of the fact that he had
married an "Ethiopian woman.") Miriam gets hit by leprosy, and she is
shut out of the camp s‑e‑v‑e‑n days (Num. 12). In Leviticus 13:12--13,
you are told that if a leper turns "white all over" that he is clean.
Hence, you find Lot's Hamitic, North African wife (black) turning into a
pillar of salt. Salt is white! Jesus Christ says, "Remember Lot's wife"
(Luke 17:32). (The context is the Tribulation. The Book is so
constructed that it foretells every detail of the future, even to the
types of diseases people will have 2,000 years after the Book is
written, and the length of time in which they will have them, and the
cure for them.)

This "grievous sore" is mentioned in a Tribulation epistle.

Jude 23---"And others save with fear, pulling them out of the fire;
hating even the garment spotted by the flesh."

There is only one disease that can "spot" your flesh and your clothes
and your house---leprosy (Lev. 13). If some skeptical person is reading
this, who doesn't believe that a disease can get into a house, let him
be reminded that they used to quarantine houses to keep diseases from
spreading. In Leviticus 14:33--53 you are told that leprosy was in the
walls. Let me remind you again that radioactive cobalt is used for
finding flaws in metals, and it is sent in lead‑walled shipping
containers weighing 800 pounds for 10 pellets, the size of a matchhead.
Two of these shipping containers broke one time, and radioactive dust
got loose (in Houston, Texas) and went through a steel locker into a
worker's clothes. He took it home with him in his clothes, and it got in
the furniture, in the car, on the children, on their toys, etc. (None of
it got on the children's shoes, but some was on the left sock of one
child!) This was the McVay family from Houston, Texas; Mr. McVay was a
worker with radioactive material. Therefore, a man who doesn't believe
the Authorized Version's account of leprosy could be true is
non‑scientific, medieval, and out‑of‑step with the times.

The "sore" gets on them; it gets in their clothes; it gets in their
houses. (There is an indication---I don't say this for certain---that
they can be saved. The indication is \[I know a Campbellite will love
this.\] that they have to be baptized in water to be saved!) In the
Tribulation when a man takes the "mark of the beast" or worships his
image, a "sore" falls upon that man. But, there is an indication that if
that man hearkens to the witness of the "two witnesses," Elijah and
Moses, and is "baptized for the remission of sins" (Acts 2:38), that he
is cleansed from that disease (see 2 Kings 5).

Now the facts behind this are: Moses---who will show up again (Rev.
11)---baptized, and you'll find his baptism in 1 Corinthians 10:12.
Elijah, although he did not baptize, went down under water crossing the
Jordan (2 Kings 2). When John the Baptist showed up, one of the first
things the people asked him was, "Art thou Elias?" "Art thou that
prophet?" (like unto Moses, Acts 7:37). They recognized
baptism---submersion in water---as being connected with Elijah and
Moses. Baptism is preached by Simon Peter in Acts 2, to Israel, as part
of the proof of their repentance and belief on the Messiah, and they are
told that something will happen to them when they get baptized---they
will "receive the gift of the Holy Ghost." (This passage has been the
stumbling block of heretics for 2,000 years in the church. Roman
Catholics and Campbellites, foremost, never can get it through their
heads that Acts 2:38 is an address to Israel by an Israelite with the
Old Testament dispensation in effect to everybody's knowledge. When
Peter preaches in Acts 2, all he has for a Bible is Genesis to Malachi.
Acts 2 is an address to Israel by Peter the Apostle, and baptism is part
of Israel's regeneration in the passage \[see Exod. 37\]. The Roman
Catholics and the Campbellites step in and say that the way to get into
the one "true church" is to be baptized in water. The Campbellite makes
his church the "one true church" by putting a sign out in front saying
"Church of Christ." The Roman Catholic makes his church the "one true
church" by claiming apostolic succession to Simon Peter, which is the
biggest joke that ever hit the boards since Red Skelton and Jack Benny.
Then, they claim that you have to be baptized in water to get the Holy
Ghost to get into these churches. Of course, this is madness that goes
even beyond the bounds of private interpretation.)

Acts 2 is addressed to the men of "Israel," "dwelling at Jerusalem and
Judea," and "Jews" from the four corners of the earth, who meet as a
nation responsible for the rejection of a "Jewish" Messiah. These events
will take place again in the Tribulation because, when Peter quotes
Scripture in Acts 2, he is quoting Joel 2, and you will find that Joel 2
is dealing with the Tribulation and the Second Coming of Jesus Christ.
So this baptism of Acts 2 figures again during the Tribulation. It
figures in the pouring out of the Spirit upon Israel, the 144,000, and
it figures in the Tribulation message, which is the "everlasting
gospel."

So, you will find people taking the "mark of the beast" and getting the
"spot." Then they have a "sore" from God falling on them for taking the
"mark of the beast." Then these people will be cleansed from this "sore"
by being baptized. Of course, the case in the Old Testament is Naaman,
who is baptized in the Jordan to be cured of leprosy. He is baptized
s‑e‑v‑e‑n times, or one time for each year in the Tribulation, which
indicates that water baptism in the Tribulation can cleanse a man from
"filthiness of the flesh."

So Simon Peter, bless his heart, is careful to tell every Christian in
this dispensation:

1 Peter 3:21---"The like figure whereunto even baptism doth also now
save us (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer
of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Jesus Christ."

The passage indicates that the water baptism of Acts 2:38 and the
Tribulation is different from water baptism in this present
dispensation. In this present dispensation, water baptism is a "figure"
of your salvation, the "answer of a good conscience toward God." In Acts
2, baptism is a means of receiving the Holy Ghost. In the Tribulation,
baptism is a means of getting rid of "the filth of the flesh"---leprosy!
(This revelation describes a doctrine you won't find in print in any
book, anywhere, anytime, anyplace, except a King James AV 1611.)

"And there fell a noisome and grievous sore upon the men which had the
mark of the beast, and upon them which worshipped his image."

The picture, here, is Elijah and Moses baptizing in the Tribulation.
People who have accepted the "mark of the beast" can only get rid of
this "sore" one way---Jewish proselyte water baptism of Acts 2, "in the
name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins." To repeat the truth
again---"Every heresy in this age is a Tribulation truth!"

16:3 "And the second angel poured out his vial upon the sea; and it
became as the blood of a dead man: and every living soul died in the
sea."

This catastrophe takes place under the preaching of Moses, because you
are told that he had power to "turn water into blood" (Rev. 11:6).

"And it became as the blood of a dead man: and every living soul died in
the sea."

This is "self‑conscious life" which dies in the sea. (The passage is
interpreted by Rev. 8:9.) "Living soul" proves that animals have souls,
but that the soul is merely a bodily shape. When an animal dies, his
soul dies; the soul is stuck to the body. Animals are spoken of as
having souls in Numbers 31:28.

Numbers 31:28---"And levy a tribute unto the Lord of the men of war
which went out to battle: one soul of five hundred, both of the persons,
and of the beeves, and of the asses, and of the sheep."

Animals are counted as souls, a soul being the natural, bodily shape
inside the physical body. The soul is shaped exactly as the body.

So "living soul" can refer to animals or men who "died in the sea." This
has brought up numerous speculations as to whether or not civilizations
will be built under the sea during the Tribulation. This would be as
protection from the radiation bombardment and the plagues during the
Tribulation. There has been speculation about the calamities of the
Tribulation being so great in the atmosphere that men will have to build
under the ocean in order to survive; therefore, the speculators say that
the remark in Revelation 16:3 ("in the sea") is a reference to men. It
may be true, but this is rather a wild shot. The word "soul" here is
used like "every living creature." It is not used in the sense of an
immortal soul of a living man (See commentary on Genesis, Gen. 2:7.).

16:4 "And the third angel poured out his vial upon the rivers and
fountains of waters; and they became blood."

You can imagine how terror‑stricken the populace of the world must be at
this time. A fellow goes out to his well, pumps up water, and it comes
out blood. A man goes out fishing on a river, casts out his line, and
the waters underneath him turn to blood. There is blood all over the
sides of the boat, and if you don't wash the blood off, it stinks and
corrupts and putrefies the boat. A man goes to the faucet, turns it on
to get a drink of water, and he gets a stream of blood!

16:5 "And I heard the angel of the waters say, Thou art righteous, O
Lord, which art, and wast, and shalt be, because thou hast judged thus.

6 For they have shed the blood of saints and prophets, and thou hast
given them blood to drink; for they are worthy."

Again we are impressed by the fact that these events take place after
the first part of the Tribulation, for the "saints and prophets" begin
to get slaughtered right in the middle of the Tribulation. "Have shed"
is past tense.

"Thou hast given them blood to drink." The indication is that many of
them will drink it; of course, this is nothing new. The woman in
Revelation 17 drinks blood. Although blood is forbidden before the law
(Gen. 9), forbidden under the law (Lev. 17), and forbidden after the law
(Acts 15), we have a church today which professes to drink blood every
Sunday morning. This church not only sins against God by violating all
three Testaments in sixty-six books, but it then brags about it and
claims that if you don't drink this blood with them, you are not a
Christian! (By the way, this bloody clan is the Mafia who killed "the
saints and prophets;" so God says that if they like to drink blood, let
them drink it, Rev. 17:1--7.)

"For they are worthy." In plainer words, they deserve it. Notice, this
is given in sarcasm. "They earned it." They've been bragging about
drinking blood every Sunday morning at 11 o'clock for so long, and
saying that you are not saved if you don't drink Christ's blood, and
that their fermented liquor is Christ's blood, and that Christ's blood
is hooch, and they have been saying "Blood! Blood!" for so long, that
God says, "They are worthy." (You earned it, sonny!) Drink it! Let them
have it! Give it to them!

16:7 "And I heard another out of the altar say, Even so, Lord God
Almighty, true and righteous are thy judgments.

8 And the fourth angel poured out his vial upon the sun; and power was
given unto him to scorch men with fire."

This is not hard to understand because in the Millennium, when Christ
comes back, the sun is going to be seven times brighter than it is now
(Isa. 30:26). If the sun were to become seven times brighter than it is
now, with the atmosphere not conditioned for it, men on this earth would
be roasting with sunburn. They would be suffering first and second
degree burns from the sunlight. That's the case here; the atmosphere is
all torn up and the rays are reaching men directly, scorching and
burning them.

16:9 "And men were scorched with great heat, and blasphemed the name of
God, which hath power over these plagues: and they repented not to give
him glory."

Instead of getting right, they "blasphemed the name of God." They were
"scorched"---barbecued, burned. They "repented not to give him glory" in
spite of all these things that were going on. A man gets up in the
morning, and all he had to drink is blood. He goes outside, and he's
scorched with first and second degree burns from the sunlight. Yet in
all this agony, instead of repenting, turning to God, and getting right,
the man curses God and blasphemes His name.

16:10 "And the fifth angel poured out his vial upon the seat of the
beast; and his kingdom was full of darkness; and they gnawed their
tongues for pain."

(This is nearing the end of the Tribulation, getting over near the last
year, or perhaps, even the last six months.) The same phenomena occur
back in the Book of Exodus, where it is dark in all the dwellings except
those of the children of Israel (Exod. 10:20--23). Again, this indicates
the work of Moses, who will be returning with Elijah.

"The seat of the beast." This is the place where the beast sits (Rev.
2:13). It is located at Rome: "the papal chair," St. Peter's chair,
ex‑cathedra, literally---"out of the chair." This vial is poured out on
Rome, the "seat of the beast," and Rome's kingdom.

They "gnawed their tongues for pain," as those who have extreme thirst,
and it is not hard to figure out why they do. In Revelation 11, Elijah
was prophesying; the text says he "shut up heaven, that it rain not in
the days of their prophecy" (which was forty-two months). Immediately
following that, you read: "and have power over waters to turn them to
blood, and to smite the earth with all plagues, as often as they will"
(Rev. 11:6). So when you get to Revelation 16, you find all three taking
place---the plagues (Rev. 16:2), the blood (Rev. 16:3), and no water
throughout. Therefore in Revelation 16:10, it is no wonder that men are
gnawing their tongues. A man has been in scorching hot sunlight for
months and months, with the disease of leprosy on his body for months
and months, and nothing to drink but blood for months and months. So he
sits in the darkness with a swollen tongue turning black and chews on
it! Instead of getting right, they:

16:11 "And blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and their
sores, and repented not of their deeds."

This proves conclusively that the heart is "deceitful above all things,
and desperately wicked" (Jer. 17:9); this proves that "every man at his
best state is altogether vanity" (Psa. 39:5); this proves that man is so
depraved and so far from God, that even when God whips him, chastens
him, beats him, and almost kills him, that man does not repent. These
scenes do not prove the total depravity of man and his inability to be
saved as much as they show the strength of man's will against God's
will, determining not to be saved no matter what God does to him.

16:12 "And the sixth angel poured out his vial upon the great river
Euphrates; and the water thereof was dried up, that the way of the kings
of the east might be prepared."

Notice the same thing happens in Revelation 9:13--16, when the sixth
angel blows the sixth trumpet, as that which happened when the sixth
angel pours out his vial in Revelation 16:12. (This is why I have said
that the vials and the trumpets are probably simultaneous, and this
accounts for my saying that the trumpets probably do not begin until the
middle of the Tribulation. It is certain that the vials do not begin
until at least the middle of the Tribulation.) This brings us over to
the last year or six months, or maybe even to the last month of the
Tribulation in Revelation 16:12.

There is a new element entering---"the kings of the east." They are from
"east of the Euphrates." The indication is that a cavalry attack is
coming against Palestine from the east, and that it crosses the
Euphrates River dry‑shod. The number of the cavalrymen is given as 200
million in Revelation 9:16. They are probably the souls of the unsaved
dead coming out of hell, and these demoniac "centaur horsemen" come from
the east! The nearest thing to them is Tamerlane or Baibars (the
Panther) or Ghengis Kahn. (This accounts for the legends in China and
Tibet that someday the ground is going to open, and Ghengis Kahn and his
hordes of Mongolians and Tartars are going to come out again to harass
the world. All legends and fiction have an element of truth in them.)
The truth of the passage is that the "kings of the east," at the head of
200 million cavalry troops, figure in the end‑time. This is why you hear
folks out in the country say, "Doesn't the Bible say something about the
future predominance of the yellow race?" It is a half‑truth. The Bible
speaks of the predominance of Israel, the Jew (and he is an Asiatic),
over the entire world at the Second Coming of Christ, but it also speaks
here of the "kings of the east"---Mongolian, Asiatic kings, as mustering
the final attack on Jerusalem before the Second Coming of Jesus Christ.
Notice further:

Revelation 16:14---"For they are the spirits of devils, working
miracles, which go forth unto the kings of the earth and of the whole
world, to gather them to the battle of that great day of God Almighty."

The United Nations probably figures in this. The old saying (1500--1900)
that all the nations couldn't be gathered and that all the world
couldn't be contacted is absolutely ridiculous now. You can get troops
from every civilized nation on the face of the earth mustered in less
than forty-eight hours, due to modern transportation and communications.
The "whole world" figures in the passage. Therefore, we can see how the
Ecumenical Council and the United Nations will be the greatest
instruments of Satan in the end‑time. As these organizations---the
Ecumenical Council and the United Nations---were set up primarily for
bringing peace and prosperity to men, without belief in, or obedience
to, the word of God, they will be Satan's most useful tools in the end
time. Neither the Ecumenical Council or the United Nations has any
intention whatsoever of believing one word in the Bible that contradicts
their own organizational aims. You put that down and remember it the
next twenty years. If they ever profess to believe the Bible, it will be
for commercial reasons; if they adhere to the "fundamentals" or
principles or statements found in the Apostle's Creed, which match the
Bible, it is purely for political expediency. It has nothing to do with
loyalty to the Holy Spirit or the God who wrote the sixty-six books. It
is purely arbitrary.

So the Ecumenical Council and the United Nations figure in this last
final attack on the Jew, to wipe him off the face of the earth, and they
use the "kings of the east." (These kings probably are ten in number, as
we will study later in Rev. 17:10--12.) These kings lead the troops into
battle; the troops are 200 million in number; they come from China,
Japan, Russia, India, and parts of what was formerly known as Persia.

16:13 "And I saw three unclean spirits like frogs come out of the mouth
of the dragon, and out of the mouth of the beast, and out of the mouth
of the false prophet."

The Holy Spirit is like a dove (Matt. 13); an unclean spirit is likened
to a frog---it croaks, hangs out in wet, swampy places, and eats flies!
Revelation 16:13 should settle, once and for all, forever the arguments
about the nature and person of the Antichrist. In the passage you are
told that Satan is a trinity: he is a "dragon," to imitate God the
Father; he is a "beast," to imitate God the Son, the Lamb; he is a
"false prophet," to imitate God the Holy Spirit, foreshadowed by John
the Baptist, who was "filled with the Holy Ghost, even from his mother's
womb." Then, the Satanic trinity is represented by a dragon, a beast,
and a false prophet. The Antichrist is Satan the father, the "dragon";
he is Satan the son, in the flesh, the "beast"; he is Satan the unclean
spirit, like a "frog." This Satanic trinity is typified in the Old
Testament by three men, whose names, strangely enough, all begin with
"B." (Hence, no book in the Bible begins with a "B"!)

Numbers 22:41---"And it came to pass on the morrow, that Balak (type of
Antichrist) took Balaam (type of the false prophet), and brought him up
into the high places of Baal (type of false god), that thence he might
see the utmost part of the people."

Baal is a picture of the dragon; Balak is a picture of the beast; Balaam
is a picture of the false prophet.

"And I saw three unclean spirits like frogs come out of the mouth of the
dragon, and out of the mouth of the beast, and out of the mouth of the
false prophet" (Rev. 16:13).

These are spirits sent forth to deceive (2 Thess. 2:9); they are
amphibious, and must be connected with water, since the dragon is called
a "sea monster" (Job 41), and he is typified by the whale which
swallowed Jonah. (Rev. 16:13 is quite a picture, with a description like
a man is belching a frog out of his mouth. You can't imagine anything
much more unclean or revolting!)

16:14 "For they are the spirits of devils, working miracles, which go
forth unto the kings of the earth and of the whole world, to gather them
to the battle of that great day of God Almighty."

"For they are the spirits of devils, working M‑1‑R‑A-C‑L‑E‑S." Unclean
spirits can heal and speak in tongues and raise the dead. They can cause
people to see "Mary," Lady Fatima, Bernadette (and God knows what)
floating around with beads, rosaries, and candlesticks.

Revelation 13:3---"And I saw one of his heads as it were wounded to
death; and his deadly wound was h‑e‑a-l‑e‑d: and all the world wondered
after the beast."

Revelation 13:14---"And deceiveth them that dwell on the earth by the
means of those m‑i‑r‑a‑c-l‑e‑s which he had power to do in the sight of
the beast; saying to them that dwell on the earth, that they should make
an image to the beast, which had the wound by a sword, and did live."
Healed, brother, healed!

Healing can be Satanic. A man said, "All sickness comes from the Devil."
A man said, "All healing comes from God." Don't move too quickly!! When
Paul "gloried in his infirmities," and carried Luke the physician with
him, he carried Luke with him by the permissive will of God Almighty (2
Cor. 12:1--8). Although Jeroboam was healed, he was one of the most
wicked men who ever lived, and he still did not get right after he was
healed (1 Kings 13). If there is still any doubt in your mind, don't
forget Judas (who was a devil) was also a healer (Matt. 10:1--8).

"That great day of God Almighty." This is not the destruction of
Jerusalem by Rome; this is not some little military maneuver like World
War I or World War II; this is not some little rear guard delaying
action like the Korean War, Civil War, Hundred Years War, etc. This is
the "battle of that great day of God Almighty." When this one cuts
loose, you'll know that something has flat cut loose! If you had to face
the fury of a German "88," you had problems; if you had to face the fury
of a Japanese Banzai charge, you had plenty on your mind; if you faced
the fury of "gooks" and "Red Chinks" running up the ridges at night,
infiltrating your trenches, blowing you, blasting you, it put you in a
sweat; if you had to face Rommel coming at you across the desert with
his Panzer units, you would have something to pray about. But wait until
you face the unsheeted wrath of an outraged, Divine, Sovereign, Almighty
God (who knew where every hair on every head of every soldier that ever
died was, and who made all the materials from which every bullet was
manufactured!). You wait until you meet Him! He's called a "man of war"
(Exod. 15); He's called a "captain" (Heb. 2).

16:15 "Behold, I come as a thief. Blessed is he that watcheth, and
keepeth his garments, lest he walk naked, and they see his shame."

Now, this verse is inserted into the events taking place as an
intermission. (You will notice that Rev. 16:16 is a continuation of Rev.
16:14.) Revelation 16:15 is the post‑Tribulation Rapture which takes
place before the Battle of Armageddon. (We discussed this in Rev. 14.)
While the troops are being gathered together and brought to Armageddon
to be crushed under the feet of Jesus Christ, we find, "Behold, I come
as a thief."

Matthew 24:43, 50---"But know this, that if the goodman of the house had
known in what watch the thief could come, he would have watched, and
would not have suffered his house to be broken up." "The lord of that
servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour
that he is not aware of."

The context is the Second Coming of Christ, in a time when faith is
connected with works, and after the Tribulation.

Matthew 24:29--30---"Immediately after the tribulation of those days
shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and
the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be
shaken: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and
then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son
of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory."

Notice, the post‑Tribulation Rapture immediately follows the verse.

Matthew 24:31---"And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a
trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds,
from one end of heaven to the other."

So you definitely have a post‑Tribulation Rapture, immediately preceding
the Second Advent itself. Nothing could be any plainer or clearer in the
order of events the way the word has laid them out.

"Keepeth his garments." "Fine linen" is said to be "the righteousness of
saints." It is here connected with the appearance of a group of people
whose works have figured in their salvation. (See Rev. 6:11, 7:14,
12:11, 14:12.) (The Rapture is mentioned in the Old Testament in Psa.
50:3--5 and Isa. 26:12--21.)

16:16 "And he gathered them together into a place called in the Hebrew
tongue Armageddon."

Armageddon means literally "the hill of the crowded." We speak of the
Valley of Armageddon, but that is not an exact case. The Valley of
Megiddo is mentioned, which is the "valley of the crowded." But
Armageddon, in Hebrew, is literally "the hill of the crowded." This
means that 200 million troops will be crowded into this particular
space, or passage. The area can be located on a New Testament Bible map.

Zechariah 12:11---"In that day shall there be a great mourning in
Jerusalem, as the mourning of Hadadrimmon in the valley of Megiddon."

The reference is to the Second Coming of Jesus Christ in the passage,
and the place is the "valley of Megiddon." The location is spoken of as
the "valley" here, and the "hill" in the other passage.

Joel 3:11--16---"Assemble yourselves, and come, all ye heathen, and
gather yourselves together round about: thither cause thy mighty ones to
come down, O Lord. Let the heathen be wakened, and come up to the valley
of Jehosha--phat: for there will I sit to judge all the heathen round
about. Put ye in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe: come, get you
down; for the press is full, the fats overflow; for their wickedness is
great. Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision: for the day of
the Lord is near in the valley of decision. The sun and the moon shall
be darkened, and the stars shall withdraw their shining. The Lord also
shall roar out of Zion, and utter his voice from Jerusalem; and the
heavens and the earth shall shake: but the Lord will be the hope of his
people, and the strength of the children of Israel."

There's no doubt about the passage at all; there's no doubt about the
interpretation; there's no doubt about the placement. There's nothing
"obscure," symbolic, apocalyptic, or "figurative" about one thing
connected with anything in the passage. The Bible is a textbook on
history and future history, and nobody to speak of will be ready for the
Second Coming of Christ who doesn't believe that the Bible is an actual
historical record of the past, the present, and the future.

In the passage that we have just studied, you learned that Jesus Christ
is going to come back to this earth in wrath and vengeance upon His
enemies. His enemies are going to be constituted by a United Nations
assembly, a World Council of people gathered together against Israel,
headed up by ten kings of the east with 200 million cavalrymen behind
them. They are going to gather in Palestine, and the troops will be
coming down the northwest valley toward Jerusalem. They will be stomped
upon by the Lord Jesus Christ until their blood runs nearly three feet
deep for 160 miles down the Kishon to the Jordan River. The horses'
bridles and the men's feet on the horses are dipped in the blood. That's
more prophetic detail than you have in the entire Bible on the first
coming of Christ. And this passage only deals with the Battle of
Armageddon!

16:17--18 "And the seventh angel poured out his vial into the air; and
there came a great voice out of the temple of heaven, from the throne,
saying, It is done. And there were voices, and thunders, and lightnings;
and there was a great earthquake, such as was not since men were upon
the earth, so mighty an earthquake, and so great."

That must be some earthquake! That will be greater than the one in San
Francisco (1906); it will be greater than the one in Alaska (1917). It
will make all other earthquakes look like nothing. This terrible shaking
is also described in Isaiah 24:1--23.

16:19 "And the great city was divided into three parts, and the cities
of the nations fell: and great Babylon came in remembrance before God,
to give unto her the cup of the wine of the fierceness of his wrath."

The reason I have taught you throughout this book that the book contains
four simultaneous accounts of the Tribulation, from different aspects,
now becomes apparent by the presentation of a perfect case. You were
just told that Babylon is going to be destroyed. (This takes place in
Rev. 18:2.) Yet you were told that Babylon fell in Revelation 14:8. You
are told that it is falling in Revelation 16:19! This is perfect proof
that these are three simultaneous accounts, and are not one
chronologically following another. In the destruction of Babylon under
the Antichrist, Revelation 12--14, Babylon falls in Revelation 14:8. In
the account given of the destruction under the vials, wrath being poured
out, the destruction is mentioned in Revelation 16:19. Then the details
of this event are found in Revelation 17--18.

You will notice that so far, in the account of the vials, the Second
Coming of Christ has not yet occurred. The Bible has mentioned the
gathering together of the troops (Rev. 16:13--16), the post‑Tribulation
Rapture (Rev. 16:15), the destruction of Babylon (Rev. 16:19), and then
the Second Advent takes place in Revelation 19:11. So Revelation 17--18
is a description of Revelation 16:19.

16:20 "And every island fled away, and the mountains were not found.

21 And there fell upon men a great hail out of heaven, every stone about
the weight of a talent: and men blasphemed God because of the plague of
the hail; for the plague thereof was exceeding great."

This plague of hailstones comes down with stones weighing about eight
pounds apiece. A veritable rain of bowling balls! If some skeptic is
reading this, let him investigate the accounts of hailstones falling at
L'aigle, France, in Normandy on April 26, 1803, where some weighed ten
pounds. Let him read the reports on the Bikini Atoll bomb test, where a
great deal of damage that was caused to the armour‑plated vessels (that
were bombed as an experiment) was done by falling hailstones. The atom
bomb changed the atmosphere and down came hailstones weighing between
ten and twenty pounds and dented the armour‑plate on the decks of those
ships! They didn't let too much of that information out to the public
because it indicates that atomic experiments in the air can cause
changes in the weather! That's the last thing the government wanted to
have the farmers in the United States find out! If your weather gets all
out of kilter in the next ten or fifteen years, it can be due to
atmospheric disturbances caused by radiation and atomic fallout and
explosions.

This hail was found in the Old Testament in the battles of Joshua (Josh.
10:8--13), and thus indicates clearly that the Book of Joshua is a
picture of the Second Coming of Jesus Christ. (The word "Joshua" means
"Jesus.")

"And men blasphemed God because of the plague of the hail; for the
plague thereof was exceeding great." (Compare Psa. 18:13; Lev. 24:16;
Rev. 11:19; 2 Pet. 3:9.)

So throughout the Tribulation, men don't get any better; they get worse.
The blood of God (Acts 20:28) manifested by the bleeding Lamb, hanging
on Calvary's cross, did not change hearts. The Holy Spirit, the Blessed
Comforter, who is purer than the fairest woman that ever walked across
the face of this earth, has been wooing the hearts of men and women for
2,000 years, and He yet is unable to do the work. Every gentle, refined
influence of Bible Christianity, every gentle reminder of a Christian
mother, a Christian home and even church bells on Sunday morning,
flowers at funerals and weddings, with the preacher presiding with a
Bible in his hand: all these gentle and refined Western Christian
influences for 2,000 years have failed to change the hearts of
unbelieving men, or renovate human nature.

And now in the end time, as God pours out unsheeted hell upon the
inhabitants of a Christ‑rejecting, Bible‑despising, religious world (and
all of them are religious---don't let them fool you, because they all
are as religious as Judas), man still does not repent; he does not get
right. With hailstones weighing eight pounds apiece destroying his
gardens, crops, houses, automobiles, etc., with the sun scorching the
skin off his back, with his tongue swelling with pain from thirst, with
nothing to drink but blood, with half‑drugged populations reeling across
the earth (Rev. 9:21), and rabid animals (Hitchcock's "The Birds!" Rev.
6:8) killing children, man uses the tongue God gave him to blast Jesus'
name, and goes right on worshipping sex, education, money, and
"science."

CHAPTER 17

As we have already studied Babylon the Great thoroughly in Chapter 13, I
will not take a great deal of time going through the material again.
Briefly: the "woman" is "that great city, which reigneth over the kings
of the earth" (Rev. 17:18); "that great city, which reigneth over the
kings of the earth" is built on "seven mountains" (Rev. 17:9); the
"woman" is "drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of
the martyrs of Jesus" (Rev. 17:6); the colors of the "woman" are "purple
and scarlet" (Rev. 17:4); the symbol of the "woman" is a "golden cup"
(Rev. 17:4). So, there is no doubt about the identification of the
"woman." You don't have to "interpret" anything in the passage; you know
who the "woman" is. (There isn't anybody reading this who doesn't know
who the "woman" is, and if you pretend you don't, you are probably one
of her kin‑folk.)

In 1825, Pope Leo XII struck a medal with his own image on one side, and
a woman with a cup on the other side. On the side which had the image of
the woman was written, "Sedet Super Universum." The Latin meaning is
"The whole world is her seat." In plainer words, he struck out a medal
with the image of a woman sitting on the whole world, and then his own
image on the other side of it. The Catholic Church has already said that
the teaching that a man is free to embrace and profess whatever religion
he believes to be true is a false teaching. The Catholic Church claims
the power of employing force and exercising direct power in regard to a
man choosing his own religion. (This is Pope Pius IX's decree in The
Encyclical Letter and the Syllabus of the Eighty Errors, published in
1864, quoted by Peterson in The Rise and Fall of the Roman Catholic
Church, page 125.)

There is no doubt about it, the "woman" is Rome! It is the old Roman
Religion that specialized in "mysteries" and is "unable to understand
the Bible!" In Revelation 17:5 she is called "MYSTERY, BABYLON THE
GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH." (Compare
Rev. 16:13 and Isa. 57:3.)

John is marvellously shocked at this apparition. He would expect pagan
Rome to kill saints and martyrs, as he was in exile at this time by
pagan Rome for testifying for Christ. But John is looking into the
future, and he has been caught up to the "day of the Lord" to see these
visions, and he looks down at the professing church of Jesus Christ, the
professing bride of Christ, and finds out that she's the foremost
murderer of Christians! This is not pagan Rome. Why would John marvel
about pagan Rome killing Christians when he was one of the ones they
were trying to kill? The thing he is looking at is that great "mother of
the faith"---Holy Mother Church. Do you know what the Holy Ghost says
she really is? She's a religious whore!! (See Rev. 17:1.) This is why
the Roman Catholic Church does not like the Authorized Version; this is
why Liberal and Orthodox scholars have put pressure on revision
committees for hundreds of years to get rid of the King James Bible
(AV), and replace it with such perversions as the RSV (which is the
official bible approved by the Roman Catholic Church for England and the
U.S.A. The reason that the Roman Catholic Church tells its people that
they cannot "understand the Bible," the reason why Protestant scholars
teach that the Book of Revelation is apocalyptic, symbolical, and
"figurative," the reason why Protestant teachers and preachers do not
teach the Book of Revelation, the reason why God's people think the book
is "hard to understand," is because Revelation 17 tells the world that
the "mother of the faith," "Holy Mother Church," is in reality a
religious prostitute. That is not the interpretation of Dr. Peter S.
Ruckman, and that is not the teaching of the Baptist Church; that is the
infallible, inspired word of the Holy Spirit, in plain Sixth Grade
English, where anybody with an I.Q. of  90 could understand it. (And you
do understand it! The only problem is what to do with it.) Why, today if
a Christian intimated that the Roman Catholic Church was not as good as
other churches, he would have all of Christendom down on his neck! What
do you suppose would happen to you if you got on the radio and said that
the Roman Catholic Church was a religious prostitute? That's what God
said about it! The problem is not "what it means," because you know what
it means. The problem is YOU.

At present, Rome's natural design is going to be against Britain. She
has 12,107,000 square miles, 682,945,000 people, and has indirect
control over more than 8 foreign countries. What nation would Rome
desire to get hold of more than England, and the English‑speaking
people, since English is the universal language? (Naturally, Papa Paul
is going to change the "mass" into English. He isn't going to miss
picking up a fast buck if he can get it! Do you know of any mission bum
who would miss that chance?)

17:1 "And there came one of the seven angels which had the seven vials,
and talked with me, saying unto me, Come hither; I will shew unto thee
the judgment of the great whore that sitteth upon many waters:

2 With whom the kings of the earth have committed fornication, and the
inhabitants of the earth have been made drunk with the wine of her
fornication."

Compare 1 John 2:15 and Jeremiah 51:7. Your eyes are now opened to the
political shenanigans of the "great city" whose primary sphere is
political. She may profess not to be political; she may profess to be
"Christian." She may profess to be the "sole guardian" and "protector of
the word of God." But her only religious activity, from the Bible
standpoint, has been getting rid of born‑again, Bible‑believing
Christians (Rev. 17:6). Her activity is mainly political, and if you
don't know about the politics at the Vatican, I strongly recommend Paul
Blanshard's book called American Freedom and Catholic Power, or The Two
Babylons by Hislop. (It is to be expected that Roman Catholic priests
will not accept the evidence in these books, but there are not very many
chronic liars who will accept tape recordings of their own lies either.)

17:3 "So he carried me away in the spirit into the wilderness: and I saw
a woman sit upon a scarlet coloured beast, full of names of blasphemy,
having seven heads and ten horns.

4 And the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet colour, and decked
with gold and precious stones and pearls, having a golden cup in her
hand full of abominations and filthiness of her fornication:

5 And upon her forehead was a name written, MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT,
THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.

6 And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the
blood of the martyrs of Jesus: and when I saw her, I wondered with great
admiration.

7 And the angel said unto me, Wherefore didst thou marvel? I will tell
thee the mystery of the woman, and of the beast that carrieth her, which
hath the seven heads and ten horns."

Let us now see whom God connects this adulterous, whorish, religious
prostitute with (who professes to be "Holy Mother Church"). Notice how
similar this "beast" (Rev. 17:7) is to the one encountered earlier. In
Revelation 12:3 there was a "red dragon" with seven heads, ten horns,
and seven crowns. In Revelation 17, up shows a beast, which is "scarlet
colored" (bright red), and there is no doubt who this beast is: he has
seven heads and ten horns exactly as the one in Revelation 12. The beast
is not Communism, as Billy James Hargis is teaching, and Billy Graham
and others. In Revelation 12 you were told who the beast was.

Revelation 12:9---"And the great dragon was cast out, that old serpent,
called the Devil, and Satan, which deceiveth the whole world: he was
cast out into the earth, and his angels were cast out with him."

Is that clear? He's the Devil, Satan. Then the Roman Church is a Satanic
church, an imitation bride of Christ, an imitation body of Christ; and
its leader is the god of this world"---Satan!

17:8 "The beast that thou sawest was, and is not; and shall ascend out
of the bottomless pit, and go into perdition: and they that dwell on the
earth shall wonder, whose names were not written in the book of life
from the foundation of the world, when they behold the beast that was,
and is not, and yet is.

9 And here is the mind which hath wisdom. The seven heads are seven
mountains, on which the woman sitteth."

Watch it carefully. "The beast that thou sawest was, and is not." This
is the Devil, and yet, this is the Devil in a certain type of
manifestation. Here Satan, the dragon, is manifested as a man (compare
Isaiah 7:10‑15).

17:10 "And there are seven kings: five are fallen, and one is, and the
other is not yet come; and when he cometh, he must continue a short
space.

11 And the beast that was, and is not, even he is the eighth, and is of
the seven, and goeth into perdition."

Although the "beast" is Satan (the dragon), he also is a man who is
Satan incarnate, and is called the "beast." (We have run into this man
before in Chapter 13. See comments.)

"The beast that thou sawest was." Then he was a man (see Rev. 13:18)
living on this earth. "And is not." This man was not living at the time
that John wrote the Book of Revelation. "And shall ascend out of the
bottomless pit." Then this man is coming back again. He is going to have
a second coming just like Jesus Christ will have a Second Coming. When
this man comes back, he "ascends out of the bottomless pit, and goes
into perdition." Briefly: he is Judas Iscariot, now in the bottomless
pit (see Rev. 11:7). He is the "angel of the bottomless pit," and he is
a devil---not a human being. He has a Hebrew and Greek name (Rev. 9:11),
he is from Kerioth, east of Jordan in Syria. He has Hamitic blood as a
half‑breed Syrian Jew. He is the Antichrist in the Tribulation, the
Devil incarnate, the "son of perdition," that is now in the bottomless
pit, comes out of it, and will go into perdition.

"And they that dwell on the earth shall wonder, whose names were not
written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, when they
behold the beast that was, and is not, and yet is" (Rev. 17:8).

He is not here, and yet he is. This is a "mystery" and the mystery is
simple if you remember the mystery of the Satanic trinity. In Revelation
16:13, you learned that Satan had a trinity: the dragon, the beast, and
the false prophet. (You cannot explain how Jesus Christ could be on the
earth and in heaven at the same time, but He was; you cannot explain how
God the Father was in heaven sending down God the Holy Spirit upon God
the Son at the baptism \[Matt. 3\], but He did. Nor can you explain how
the Antichrist, Judas Iscariot, is going to be the Devil incarnate, and
yet, Judas is no longer on the earth \[he's in the bottomless pit\]. Yet
the dragon \[imitation of God the Father\] is here. Now the Antichrist,
the beast, is gone, yet the beast is here, and the beast is the dragon
\[Rev. 12\].)

"When they behold the beast that was \[on the earth as a man\], and is
not \[Judas is in the bottomless pit.\], and yet is \[The Devil is still
operating, right here, right now.\]."

He is not here, now, as the Antichrist; he's not here incarnate as the
"son of perdition," but he's going to be. But the Devil is here now; the
"beast" is at work. (If you don't know that, you don't have access to a
morning newspaper!)

"And here is the mind which hath wisdom." This is said one other
place---Revelation 13:18---"Here is wisdom. Let him that hath
understanding count the number" etc.

Revelation 17:10---"And there are seven kings: five are fallen, and one
